title: DEVICE FOR BIODEGRADATION OF SANITARY NAPKINS
text: DEVICE FOR BIODEGRADATION OF SANITARY NAPKINS. Disclosed is a device (100) for biodegradation of sanitary napkins. The device (100) is cost effective and provides a complete package for biodegradation of plastic as well as the cellulosic part of the sanitary napkins. The device (100) utilizes a synthetically engineered bacteria that has the capability of releasing extracellular degradation enzymes to deteriorate polyethylene (PE) based sanitary pads. The device (100) facilitates ecofriendly way of dealing with colossal amounts of menstrual waste produced each year.

text: METHOD FOR PRODUCING GUANIDINO ACETIC ACID (GAA). The present invention relates to a microorganism transformed to be capable of producing guanidinoacetic acid (GAA) that has been improved by using a carbamate kinase (2.7.2.2) and L-arginine:glycine amidinotransferase (2.1.4.1) and to a method for the fermentative production of GAA using such microorganism which can be further improved by deleting the central repressor protein argR, expressing glyoxylate aminotransferase (2.6.1.-) or NADH-dependent amino acid dehydrogenase (1.4.1-). text: SCAR-LESS MULTI-PART DNA ASSEMBLY DESIGN AUTOMATION. The present invention provides a method of a method of designing an implementation of a DNA assembly. title: MODULAR METHOD FOR RAPID ASSEMBLY OF DNA
text: MODULAR METHOD FOR RAPID ASSEMBLY OF DNA. The invention is directed to methods, kits and compositions using specially designed nucleic acid components for efficient assembly of a DNA construct. The method involves a) incubating a support with a first form of nucleic acid components under conditions to form support-bound nucleic acid component complexes; b) removing unbound first form nucleic acid components; c) incubating the support-bound first form nucleic acid component complexes with a second form of nucleic acid components under conditions to anneal and link the second form to the first form; d) removing unbound second form nucleic acid components; e) repeating steps c) and d) until the DNA construct is generated; and f) eluting the DNA construct from the support. The first and second forms of the nucleic acid component comprise sticky ends such that each form cannot link to itself but can link to each other to form an alternating head to tail sequence.

title: DNA assembly and cloning method
text: DNA assembly and cloning method. The invention belongs to the field of biotechnology and discloses a simple DNA assembly and cloning method. According to the DNA assembly and cloning method, a polymerase chain reaction between an insert fragment and a linearized vector which are used as a primer and a template mutually is carried out to obtain double-stranded circular DNA, wherein two ends of the insert fragment respectively carry overlapping sequences overlapping with two ends of the linearized vector, and two ends of the linearized vector respectively carry overlapping sequences overlapping with two ends of the insert fragment. The DNA assembly and cloning method has a simple reaction system, is low-cost, is simple to operate, is easy for parallel operation, has obvious advantages for DNA assembly and cloning of a complex gene fragment library, a combined gene fragment library and polygene pathways, can widely be applied in DNA assembly and cloning of a single gene, polygenes and a gene fragment library, and is especially suitable for high-throughput gene synthesis and assembly.

title: METHOD FOR ASSEMBLY OF POLYNUCLEIC ACID SEQUENCES USING PHOSPHOROTHIOATE BONDS WITHIN LINKER OLIGOS
text: METHOD FOR ASSEMBLY OF POLYNUCLEIC ACID SEQUENCES USING PHOSPHOROTHIOATE BONDS WITHIN LINKER OLIGOS. A method for assembling polynucleic acids that introduces phosphorothioate bonds into the linker oligos during the assembly procedure in order to use exonucleases to isolate the DNA of interest, thereby eliminating any cumbersome purification steps, such as gel electrophoresis and significantly increasing the overall selectivity and efficiency of the method. The present invention introduces a phosphorothioate bond by replacing a non-bridging oxygen within the phosphate backbone of the nucleic acid sequence with a sulfur (S) atom. Introduction of this sulfur atom results in an internucleotide linkage that is resistant to nuclease cleavage. Consequently, by adding such modified S linkers to form nuclease resistant ends of part-linker DNA, the present invention allows the use of exonucleases to degrade parts that do not have linkers ligated to their ends to isolate only the part-linker DNA of interest to increase assembly efficiency and selectivity and avoiding the need for purification by gel electrophoresis.

title: HIGH-THROUGHPUT ASSEMBLY OF GENETIC ELEMENTS
text: HIGH-THROUGHPUT ASSEMBLY OF GENETIC ELEMENTS. Disclosed is a method of generating a set of sequence-verified nucleic acid elements for the combinatorial construction of genetic elements. The method includes: providing a plurality of nucleic acid parts; assembling nucleic acid parts to form a one or more nucleic acid elements, wherein the nucleic acid elements include at least two sequences selected from the plurality of parts; and determining the sequence of the nucleic acid elements. title: METHODS AND SYSTEM OF MANAGING RESOURCE COMPETITION IN SYNTHETIC GENE CIRCUITS
text: METHODS AND SYSTEM OF MANAGING RESOURCE COMPETITION IN SYNTHETIC GENE CIRCUITS. Described herein are a synthetic cascading bistable switches (Syn-CBS) circuit. In some aspects, the SynCBS circuit is single-strain circuit with two coupled self-activation modules to achieve two successive cell fate transitions. In other aspects, the SynCBS circuit is a two-strain circuit where the self-activation modules are divided in two cells instead of being expressed in a single cell. Also described are plasmids encoding the Syn-CBS circuits.

title: DNA ASSEMBLY IN MICROFLUIDICS DEVICE HAVING INTEGRATED SOLID-PHASE COLUMNS
text: DNA ASSEMBLY IN MICROFLUIDICS DEVICE HAVING INTEGRATED SOLID-PHASE COLUMNS. Embodiments of the present invention implement DNA assembly using a microfluidics channel having an integrated solid phase column. The presence of a solid phase enables purification of nucleic acid products by selective capture and elution. Enzymatic reactions can be performed in channels or chambers, and the reaction mixture can be passed over the column to selectively capture or selectively elute the desired product. Additional embodiments provide a looped microfluidics channel. When closed, the loop in the channel allows DNA and reagent mixtures to be flowed multiple times over a bead column for efficient capture and enzyme/reagent usage. Integrated valves control the elution volume, meter the reactants, and facilitate mixing. Additional embodiments of the present invention provide a tethered DNA assembly process, where oligonucleotide fragments are tethered to beads during and after assembly.

title: METHODS AND SYSTEMS FOR QUANTIFYING GENE EXPRESSION IN SYNTHETIC GENE CIRCUITS AND FOR TUNING SYNTHETIC GENE CIRUITS
text: METHODS AND SYSTEMS FOR QUANTIFYING GENE EXPRESSION IN SYNTHETIC GENE CIRCUITS AND FOR TUNING SYNTHETIC GENE CIRUITS. A method of tuning the performance of a synthetic gene circuit comprising a plurality of genes is disclosed. The method includes quantifying, for each gene, a relative gene expression metric. The metric is quantified by determining a number of nucleotides in a 5′ adjacent transcriptional region (ATR) of the gene, a number of nucleotides in a 3′ ATR, a minimum free energy of a mRNA secondary structure around a ribosome binding site of the gene, a total folding energy for the 5′ ATR, and a folding energy for the first 100 nucleotides of the 3′ ATR, and then summing sequence-dependent energy changes, the terms including scaling coefficients and an average energy cost for synthesizing a nucleotide. The method further includes simulating, through application of the relative gene expression metrics of the plurality of genes to a deterministic model, the performance of an intended function of the synthetic gene circuit.

title: AMPLIFIED PRODUCTION OF MONOCLONAL ANTIBODIES
text: AMPLIFIED PRODUCTION OF MONOCLONAL ANTIBODIES. Compositions and methods for production of monoclonal antibodies are provided according to aspects of the present invention including a synthetic gene circuit system, comprising: a first expression construct comprising a nucleic acid encoding tetracycline transactivator (tTA) operably linked to a ubiquitous promoter; a second expression construct comprising a nucleic acid encoding tetracycline transactivator (tTA) operably linked to a tetracycline responsive element (TRE); a third expression construct comprising a nucleic acid encoding a heavy chain component of an monoclonal antibody operably linked to a TRE; and a fourth expression construct comprising a nucleic acid encoding a light chain component of an monoclonal antibody operably linked to a TRE, functioning of the system includes a positive feedback loop which amplifies expression of the heavy chain component of the monoclonal antibody and the light chain component of the monoclonal antibody.

title: RAPID ASSEMBLY OF MULTIPLE ARBITRARY LENGTH DNA FRAGMENTS
text: RAPID ASSEMBLY OF MULTIPLE ARBITRARY LENGTH DNA FRAGMENTS. Aspects herein relate to composition, and related methods, for isolating and assembling DNA molecules without intermediate cloning steps.

title: Synthetic gene clusters
text: Synthetic gene clusters. Methods for making synthetic gene clusters are described.

title: Methods and systems for generating complex spatial patterns
text: Methods and systems for generating complex spatial patterns. Synthetic gene circuits and methods for modeling complex spatial patterns (for example, in somitogenesis) and related plasmids are disclosed herein. Also disclosed herein are methods of generating an expression pattern using the synthetic gene circuit described herein.

title: Methods and systems for quantifying gene expression in synthetic gene circuits and for tuning synthetic gene circuits
text: Methods and systems for quantifying gene expression in synthetic gene circuits and for tuning synthetic gene circuits. A method of tuning the performance of a synthetic gene circuit comprising a plurality of genes is disclosed. The method includes quantifying, for each gene, a relative gene expression metric. The metric is quantified by determining a number of nucleotides in a 5′ adjacent transcriptional region (ATR) of the gene, a number of nucleotides in a 3′ ATR, a minimum free energy of a mRNA secondary structure around a ribosome binding site of the gene, a total folding energy for the 5′ ATR, and a folding energy for the first 100 nucleotides of the 3′ ATR, and then summing sequence-dependent energy changes, the terms including scaling coefficients and an average energy cost for synthesizing a nucleotide. The method further includes simulating, through application of the relative gene expression metrics of the plurality of genes to a deterministic model, the performance of an intended function of the synthetic gene circuit.

title: Dynamic characterization of synthetic genetic circuits in living cells
text: Dynamic characterization of synthetic genetic circuits in living cells. The present invention relates to a method for determining one or more intrinsic properties of a DNA component from a plurality of measurements obtained over a time period from a cell culture, with each cell comprising the DNA component, wherein the DNA component is involved in transcription of one or more target signals, wherein the plurality of measurements comprises measurements relating to the density of the cell culture over the time period and measurements relating to the amount of the one or more target signals in the cell culture over the time period.

title: MICROFLUIDIC PLATFORM FOR SYNTHETIC BIOLOGY APPLICATIONS
text: MICROFLUIDIC PLATFORM FOR SYNTHETIC BIOLOGY APPLICATIONS. This invention provides methods and compositions for assembling biological constructs (e.g., plasmids, transformed cells, etc.). In certain embodiments the methods involve encapsulating separate components of said biological construct each in a fluid droplet confined in a fluid channel; optionally mixing droplets from different fluid channels to for a sequenced order of droplets carrying different components of said biological construct in a channel or chamber; and optionally combining two or more droplets each containing different components of said biological construct to permit said components to react with each other in one or more reactions contributing to the assembly of said biological construct.

title: SCAR-LESS MULTI-PART DNA ASSEMBLY DESIGN AUTOMATION
text: SCAR-LESS MULTI-PART DNA ASSEMBLY DESIGN AUTOMATION. The present invention provides a method of a method of designing an implementation of a DNA assembly. In an exemplary embodiment, the method includes (1) receiving a list of DNA sequence fragments to be assembled together and an order in which to assemble the DNA sequence fragments, (2) designing DNA oligonucleotides (oligos) for each of the DNA sequence fragments, and (3) creating a plan for adding flanking homology sequences to each of the DNA oligos. In an exemplary embodiment, the method includes (1) receiving a list of DNA sequence fragments to be assembled together and an order in which to assemble the DNA sequence fragments, (2) designing DNA oligonucleotides (oligos) for each of the DNA sequence fragments, and (3) creating a plan for adding optimized overhang sequences to each of the DNA oligos.

title: SYNTHETIC GENE CLUSTERS
text: SYNTHETIC GENE CLUSTERS. Methods for making synthetic gene clusters are described.

title: Combined water and soil conservation biobrick
text: Combined water and soil conservation biobrick. The utility model discloses a combined type water and soil conservation biobrick which comprises a biobrick body and a through hole, the through hole is formed in the middle of the upper surface of the biobrick body, grooves are formed in the periphery of the upper surface of the biobrick body in a penetrating mode, each groove is in a T shape, a pressing block is movably connected into each groove in an inserted mode, and the through hole is formed in the middle of the upper surface of the biobrick body. The water storage box, the flow guide pipe and the water seepage head are installed, so that a part of a water source entering the connecting pipe can flow into the ground in rainy days, and the water seepage head and the water storage box are arranged in the water storage box, the flow guide pipe and the water seepage head, so that the water seepage head is prevented from leaking out of the water storage box, and the water seepage head is prevented from leaking out of the water storage box. When rainwater enters the water storage box, part of the rainwater enters the water storage box through the extension pipe to be temporarily stored, so that a certain water locking effect is achieved, when the stored rainwater is in dry weather, the rainwater in the water storage box slowly permeates out along the flow guide pipe and the water seepage head, and the moistening effect is achieved.

title: Combined water and soil conservation biobrick
text: Combined water and soil conservation biobrick. The utility model discloses a combined type water and soil conservation biobrick in the technical field of water and soil conservation, which comprises a biobrick body and an inserting rod, the left side and the rear side of the biobrick body are respectively provided with an inserting groove, the right side and the front side of the biobrick body are respectively provided with an inserting block matched with the inserting groove, the biobrick body is provided with an inserting hole I communicated with the inserting groove, and the inserting rod is connected with the inserting block. An insertion hole II is formed in the insertion block; the inserting grooves and the inserting blocks matched with the inserting grooves are formed in the biobricks, so that when the biobricks are laid on the revetment, the inserting blocks and the inserting grooves of the adjacent biobricks can be inserted together, the biobricks can be conveniently laid on the revetment in an aligned mode, workers can conveniently build the biobricks, and the building efficiency of the workers is improved.

title: IMPROVED BIOTECHNOLOGICAL PROCESS TO PRODUCE GUANIDINOACETIC ACID (GAA) BY TARGETED INTRODUCTION OR BY INCREASING THE ACTIVITY OF A TRANSMEMBRANE TRANSPORT PROTEIN BELONGING TO THE AMINO ACID-POLYAMINE-ORGANOCATION SUPERFAMILY
text: IMPROVED BIOTECHNOLOGICAL PROCESS TO PRODUCE GUANIDINOACETIC ACID (GAA) BY TARGETED INTRODUCTION OR BY INCREASING THE ACTIVITY OF A TRANSMEMBRANE TRANSPORT PROTEIN BELONGING TO THE AMINO ACID-POLYAMINE-ORGANOCATION SUPERFAMILY. The present invention concerns a microorganism comprising at least one gene coding for a protein having the function of a L-arginine:glycine amidinotransferase and having an increased activity of a rt protein belonging to the Amino Acid-Polyamine-Organocation Superfamily (TC 2.A.3) and to a method for the fermentative production of guanidino acetic acid (GAA) as well as to a method for the fermentative production of creatine using such microorganism.

title: Combined water and soil conservation biobrick structure
text: Combined water and soil conservation biobrick structure. The utility model discloses a combined water and soil conservation biobrick structure which comprises a first biobrick, and the right side of the first biobrick is movably connected with a second biobrick. The second biobrick is placed on the left side of the first biobrick, then the connecting block is inserted into the first groove and the second groove to limit the first biobrick and the second biobrick, the connecting block is in contact with the front face of the clamping block when inserted, and the clamping block is pushed to move outwards through the chamfer formed in the front face of the clamping block. The clamping blocks drive the baffles to move towards the outer side, after the connecting blocks are inserted, the baffles rebound towards the inner side through the elastic force of the springs, the baffles drive the clamping blocks to rebound and extend into the limiting grooves to limit the connecting blocks, and therefore the advantage of splicing combination is achieved. The problem that most of the existing biobricks are integrated and do not have a splicing and combining function, so that the biobricks are very easy to loosen during laying is solved.

title: Combined water and soil conservation biobrick
text: Combined water and soil conservation biobrick. The utility model provides a combined type water and soil conservation biobrick, which relates to the technical field of biobricks.The combined type water and soil conservation biobrick comprises a biobrick frame, a flat brick is arranged in the biobrick frame, a planting device is arranged between the biobrick frame and the flat brick, a connecting device is arranged on one side of the biobrick frame, and the planting device comprises a rectangular plate. A first rectangular hole is formed in one side of the flat brick, a second rectangular hole is formed in the inner wall of the brick frame, and the size and the shape of the surface of the rectangular plate are matched with the size and the shape of the inner wall of the first rectangular hole and the size and the shape of the inner wall of the second rectangular hole. The flat brick and the brick frame can be independently separated by a person in the later period, plants planted in the circular holes cannot be affected, resource waste caused when the person replaces the biobrick in the later period is reduced, meanwhile, the influence on the plants and soil when the biobrick is replaced is reduced, and the convenience and environmental friendliness of replacing the biobrick by the person are improved.

title: TUMOR IMMUNOTHERAPY
text: TUMOR IMMUNOTHERAPY. Aspects of the present disclosure provide a platform that triggers potent and effective immunotherapy against tumors from within tumors themselves, thus overcoming limitations of existing cancer immunotherapies and tumor-detecting gene circuits.

title: Combined water and soil conservation biobrick
text: Combined water and soil conservation biobrick. The utility model discloses a combined type water and soil conservation biobrick which comprises a biobrick main body, mounting grooves are symmetrically formed in one side of the biobrick main body, fixing assemblies are fixedly connected in the mounting grooves, each fixing assembly comprises a pressing block, the lower end of each pressing block is fixedly connected with a sliding rod, and the lower end of each sliding rod is fixedly connected with a sliding block. A first spring is arranged on the outer surface of the sliding rod, a fixing block is fixedly connected to one side of the sliding rod, a telescopic rod is fixedly connected to the lower end of the fixing block, clamping blocks are symmetrically arranged on one side of the biobrick body, a T-shaped groove is formed in one side of the biobrick body, and a T-shaped block is fixedly connected to one side of the biobrick body. By arranging the clamping blocks, the fixing assemblies, the T-shaped grooves and the T-shaped blocks, a plurality of biobrick main bodies can be conveniently spliced and combined, and the laying integrity and stability of the biobrick main bodies are improved.

title: Combined water and soil conservation biobrick
text: Combined water and soil conservation biobrick. The utility model belongs to the technical field of water and soil conservation, and particularly discloses a combined type water and soil conservation biobrick which comprises a first brick body and a second brick body, combined structures are arranged on the first brick body and the second brick body, first matching grooves are symmetrically formed in the positions, close to one side, of the first brick body, and second matching grooves are symmetrically formed in the positions, close to one side, of the second brick body. First movable grooves are formed in the bottoms of the first matching grooves correspondingly, first clamping pieces are arranged in the first movable grooves correspondingly, first connecting blocks are arranged at the tops of the first clamping pieces correspondingly, first grooves are formed in the first connecting blocks correspondingly, and second mounting grooves are symmetrically formed in one sides of the second brick bodies correspondingly; one end of the first connecting block penetrates through the first movable groove and is embedded into the second mounting groove, second limiting grooves are symmetrically formed in the second brick body, and second clamping nails are arranged on the second limiting grooves; according to the utility model, the biobricks are improved and changed into a combined type, and the biobricks can be mutually fixed, so that the biobricks are laid more orderly, and the position deviation is avoided.

title: Method for preparing biobrick by utilizing mineralization of microorganisms in cotton base material
text: Method for preparing biobrick by utilizing mineralization of microorganisms in cotton base material. The invention discloses a method for preparing a biobrick by utilizing mineralization of microorganisms in a cotton base material, which comprises the following steps: adding a bacterial liquid with microorganisms into the cotton base material, and after the microorganisms are adsorbed on the surface of the cotton base material, adding a cementing liquid into the cotton base material until the cotton base material is submerged; driving the bacterial liquid and the cementing liquid to circularly flow in a container filled with the cotton base material, the bacterial liquid and the cementing liquid, mineralizing microorganisms in the cotton base material, emptying the liquid in the container after the mineralization reaction is completed, namely completing one treatment cycle, and obtaining the biobrick after completing N treatment cycles, N being greater than or equal to 1. The filling degree can be regulated and controlled by controlling the number of treatment cycles, controllability is good, a mold is not needed, demolding operation is not needed, production operation is easy, convenient and efficient, the cotton base material is easily processed into various shapes, biobricks of interlocking structures and various special-shaped structures are conveniently prepared, and the production cost is low. The phenomenon that the strength of a traditional discrete material is reduced in a humid environment is avoided, and the prepared biobrick is more stable in performance.

title: MODIFIED ESCHERICHIA COLI STRAIN BY METABOLIC PATHWAYS ENGINEERING FOR THE PINOSYLVIN BIOSYNTHESIS AND ITS USE.
text: MODIFIED ESCHERICHIA COLI STRAIN BY METABOLIC PATHWAYS ENGINEERING FOR THE PINOSYLVIN BIOSYNTHESIS AND ITS USE.. The present disclosure is related to a novel Escherichia coli strain derived from the W3110 strain, named in the present invention as: W3110?-35fabI::Cm/pSc4CL(M)VvSTS; constructed by metabolic pathways engineering for the pinosylvin biosynthesis. Also refers to an optimized pinosylvin production process. Said process comprises the use of such genetically modified Escherichia coli strain with the capacity to produce pinosylvin that, in a fermentation process, by using LB medium supplemented with glycerol and cinnamic acid, reaches titers of up to 52.67 +/- 2.95 mg/L of pinosylvin.

title: SUPER COMPACTION OF BIOMASS AND OTHER CARBON-CONTAINING MATERIALS TO HIGH ENERGY CONTENT FUELS
text: SUPER COMPACTION OF BIOMASS AND OTHER CARBON-CONTAINING MATERIALS TO HIGH ENERGY CONTENT FUELS

title: RECOMBINASE-BASED LOGIC AND MEMORY SYSTEMS
text: RECOMBINASE-BASED LOGIC AND MEMORY SYSTEMS. The invention provides, inter alia, recombinase-based systems that provide for integrated logic and memory in living cells.

title: Biological ink box, biological ink box assembly, microsphere preparation equipment, shell assembly equipment, biological brick preparation instrument, biological ink preparation instrument and biological ink preparation system
text: Biological ink box, biological ink box assembly, microsphere preparation equipment, shell assembly equipment, biological brick preparation instrument, biological ink preparation instrument and biological ink preparation system. The utility model provides a biological ink box, a biological ink box assembly, microsphere preparation equipment, shell assembling equipment, a biological brick preparation instrument, a biological ink preparation instrument and a biological ink preparation system. The biological ink cartridge comprises: an ink cartridge housing having a cavity; the isolation wall is arranged in the cavity and divides the cavity into a first accommodating cavity and a second accommodating cavity which are isolated from each other; the first ink box inlet and the first ink box outlet are formed in the ink boxshell, communicate with the first containing cavity and are in fluid isolation with the second containing cavity; the second ink box inlet and the second ink box outlet are formed in the ink box shelland are communicated with the second accommodating cavity; and the internal flow channel is located in the second containing cavity and communicates with the first containing cavity and the first inkbox outlet. The preparation method disclosed by the utility model is beneficial to improving the microsphere preparation efficiency, the shell layer assembly efficiency and the biobrick preparation efficiency. Furthermore, the activity of the biobrick intermediate product and the activity of the biobrick can be improved.

title: Method for preparing biobrick containing endothelial cells and biobrick prepared by using same
text: Method for preparing biobrick containing endothelial cells and biobrick prepared by using same. The invention relates to the technical fields of biology, regenerative medicine, biological printing (e.g., 3D biological printing), tissue engineering, etc. Particularly, the invention relates to a biobrick applicable to biological printing (e.g., 3D biological printing) and tissue engineering, a composition (e.g., biological ink) including the biobrick, a preparation method and application of the biobrick and a constructed body (e.g., a three-dimensional constructed body) including the biobrick or constructed by using the biobrick/biological ink. The biobrick comprises cells, a core wrapping cells (e.g., endothelial cells), and a shell encapsulating the core.

title: Compositions and Methods for Pesticide Degradation
text: Compositions and Methods for Pesticide Degradation. Compositions and methods related to anucelate cells (e.g., bacterial minicells) for pesticide degradation applications including related cells, polypeptides, and vectors.

title: Method for preparing constructed body from biobrick containing oxidized alginate
text: Method for preparing constructed body from biobrick containing oxidized alginate. The invention relates to a method for preparing a constructed body from a biobrick containing oxidized alginate, belonging to the technical fields of biology, regenerative medicine, biological printing (e.g., 3D biological printing), tissue engineering, etc. Particularly, the invention relates to a biobrick applicable to biological printing (e.g., 3D biological printing) and tissue engineering, a composition (e.g., biological ink) including the biobrick, a preparation method and application of the biobrick and the constructed body (e.g., a three-dimensional constructed body) including the biobrick or constructed by using the biobrick/biological ink, wherein at least one shell of the biobrick contains oxidized alginate (e.g., oxidized sodium alginate and/or oxidized calcium alginate).

title: Single biobrick with mutual occlusion function
text: Single biobrick with mutual occlusion function. The utility model provides a single biobrick with a mutual occlusion function. A prefabricated biobrick is in the form of a geometric graph with corners of two square blocks being connected together through two mouth-shaped square blocks left by removing two opposite angles of a structure shaped as a Chinese character 'Tian'; one square hole is formed in the middle of each square block; the outer edge of the single biobrick has six exterior angles and two interior angles; a root-growing hole is reserved in each side face of the brick body. Bricks can be occluded by butting the exterior angles and the interior angles. The square holes of biobricks are filled with planting soil, and the brick bodies are covered with the branches and leaves of plants growing in the square hole, thereby realizing slope protection close-to-nature ecological recovery by the biobricks. The root systems of plants are transversely connected in series through the root-growing holes in the side faces of the brick bodies, thereby enhancing the slope protection stability of the biobricks. The brick bodies are occluded together without male-female bricks, namely, occlusion among biobricks can be realized by using only one type of single biobricks, thereby promoting production, transportation and construction.

title: Simple environmental protective green biobrick
text: Simple environmental protective green biobrick. The invention discloses a simple environmental protective green biobrick, and belongs to the field of agricultural and forestry transplanting. The simple environmental protective green biobrick aims to solve the problems that currently green planting is very complicated and watering are frequently needed. The simple environmental protective green biobrick comprises a fixing blank (1) which has a hollow structure, wherein a first substrate layer (2), a second substrate layer (4) and a seed layer (3) are arranged in the hollow space of the fixing blank (1), with the first substrate layer (2) on the uppermost position and the seed layer between the first substrate layer and the second substrate layer.

title: ANTI-SIGMAS FOR PROGRAMMABLE TRANSCRIPTIONAL REGULATION
text: ANTI-SIGMAS FOR PROGRAMMABLE TRANSCRIPTIONAL REGULATION. The invention relates to anti-sigma factors (“anti-sigmas”) that bind to sigma factors and block activation of transcription. Anti-sigmas and their cognate sigma factors provide a highly effective mechanism for regulating gene expression in genetic circuits.

title: METHOD FOR CELL-FREE BACTERIOPHAGE SYNTHESIS
text: METHOD FOR CELL-FREE BACTERIOPHAGE SYNTHESIS. The invention relates to a cell-free method for synthesizing bacteriophages, comprising: (a) in vitro assembling overlapping DNA fragments into a phage genome, in a ligase-free assembling reaction, comprising: (a1) digesting the overlapping DNA fragments by an exonuclease, and (a2) annealing the overlapping DNA fragments to assemble a phage genome, and (b) cell-free transcribing and translating the phage genome. The invention also relates to phages synthesized by the method and to their applications.

title: Biobrick used for biological printing and application thereof
text: Biobrick used for biological printing and application thereof. The invention relates to the technical fields of biology, regenerative medicine, biological printing (e.g., 3D biological printing), tissue engineering, etc. Particularly, the invention relates to a biobrick applicable to biological printing (e.g., 3D biological printing) and tissue engineering, a composition (e.g., biological ink) including the biobrick, a preparation method and application of the biobrick and a constructed body (e.g., a three-dimensional constructed body) including the biobrick or constructed by using the biobrick/biological ink.

title: Biobrick including endothelial cells and application thereof
text: Biobrick including endothelial cells and application thereof. The invention relates to the technical fields of biology, regenerative medicine, biological printing (e.g., 3D biological printing), tissue engineering, etc. Particularly, the invention relates to a biobrick applicable to biological printing (e.g., 3D biological printing) and tissue engineering, a composition (e.g., biological ink) including the biobrick, a preparation method and application of the biobrick and a constructed body (e.g., a three-dimensional constructed body) including the biobrick or constructed by using the biobrick/biological ink. The biobrick comprises cells, a core wrapping cells (e.g., endothelial cells), and a shell encapsulating the core.

title: Combined water and soil conservation biobrick
text: Combined water and soil conservation biobrick. The utility model discloses a combined type water and soil conservation biobrick which comprises a biobrick body, a positioning groove is formed in the edge of the biobrick body, a first through hole is formed in the positioning groove, a butt joint groove is further formed in the edge of the biobrick body, an open groove is formed in the butt joint groove, butt joint blocks are arranged in the butt joint groove and the open groove, and the first through hole is formed in the first through hole. And a second through hole is formed in the butt joint block. According to the combined type water and soil conservation biobrick, the open slots between the two biobrick bodies are aligned, so that the butt joint block can be inserted into the open slots and the butt joint slots between the two biobrick bodies, the two biobrick bodies can be prevented from being separated left and right, then the ground holding nails penetrate through the first through holes and the second through holes, the nuts are screwed, and then the biobrick bodies are fixed. Therefore, front-back and up-down shaking between the two biobrick bodies can be avoided, stable splicing between the biobrick bodies is facilitated, the laying integrity and stability of the biobrick bodies are improved, and the situation that a single biobrick body is prone to shaking is prevented.

title: MATRIX-ASSISTED SPECTROPHOTOMETRY
text: MATRIX-ASSISTED SPECTROPHOTOMETRY. Disclosed herein are multiwell plates suitable for spectrophotometry for low-volume liquid samples. For the multiwell plates, the bottom of at least one well has a layer of porous matrix disposed thereon, or is comprised of a layer of porous matrix. The layer of porous matrix permits a low-volume liquid sample to distribute evenly across the porous matrix. Also disclosed herein are methods of performing a photometric or spectrophotometric measurement on a liquid sample having a small volume, by using a multiwell plate comprising a layer of porous matrix.

title: Biobrick with expansion performance for water and soil conservation
text: Biobrick with expansion performance for water and soil conservation. The utility model provides a water and soil conservation biobrick with expansion performance, which relates to the technical field of environmental protection building materials and comprises a brick body, an upper arc groove is arranged at the top of the brick body, a lower fixed protrusion is fixed at the bottom of the brick body, a plurality of fillet reserved grooves are arranged on the surface of the lower fixed protrusion, and the upper arc groove corresponds to the lower fixed protrusion in position. The mode that the upper arc groove is formed in the top of the brick body and the lower fixed protrusion is fixed to the bottom of the brick body is adopted, so that the biobrick is suitable for early-stage nutrition supply and rhizome development of water and soil conservation plants after the plants are planted through nutrition in the brick, destruction caused by soil depletion is avoided, the biobrick is low in manufacturing cost and good in shaping effect, and an existing biobrick is smooth in surface and not prone to deformation. In the prior art, rainwater retention time is difficult to prolong well, the stacked biobrick is easy to slip and topple, the biobrick is damaged and material loss is caused in the transportation process due to the fact that the surface is smooth, and the transportation cost is increased and the profit rate is reduced due to the fact that the cost of the fixed material is even possibly higher than that of the biobrick if a large number of fixed materials are used.

title: BIOLOGICAL CIRCUIT CHEMOTACTIC CONVERTERS
text: BIOLOGICAL CIRCUIT CHEMOTACTIC CONVERTERS. Described herein are novel biological circuit chemotactic converter that utilize modular components, such as genetic toggle switches and single invertase memory modules (SIMMs), for detecting and converting external inputs, such as chemoattractants, into outputs that allow for autonomous chemotaxis in cellular systems. Flexibility in these biological circuit chemotactic converter is provided by combining individual modular components, i.e., SIMMs and genetic toggle switches, together. These biological converter switches can be combined in a variety of network topologies to create network systems that regulate chemotactic responses based on the combination and nature of input signals received.

title: RATIONAL DESIGN OF UPSTREAM ENHANCEMENT RNA FOR CIRCUIT DYNAMICS REGULATION AND VIRAL DIAGNOSTICS OPTIMIZATION
text: RATIONAL DESIGN OF UPSTREAM ENHANCEMENT RNA FOR CIRCUIT DYNAMICS REGULATION AND VIRAL DIAGNOSTICS OPTIMIZATION. The present invention provides a new class of RNA modules, referred to as degradation tuning RNAs (dtRNAs), which form stabilizing secondary structures. Also provided are methods of using dtRNAs to modulate the stability of RNAs. DNA constructs including a promoter that is operably connected to a sequence encoding the dtRNA are also provided.

title: Combined water and soil conservation biobrick
text: Combined water and soil conservation biobrick. The utility model discloses a combined water and soil conservation biobrick and relates to the technical field of biobricks. Comprising a slope and a biological brick, the biological brick is laid on the outer surface of the slope, the biological brick comprises a base plate and a side plate, a water guide plate is installed on the base plate, a water guide groove is formed in the water guide plate, a nutrient layer is arranged at the interval between the side plate and the water guide plate, and a vegetation layer is cultivated on the nutrient layer; an insertion rod is inserted into the water guide plate in a penetrating manner and is inserted into the slope. According to the combined water and soil conservation biobrick provided by the utility model, the biobrick is laid on the slope, and the water chute and the nutrient layer for planting the vegetation layer are arranged in the middle of the biobrick, so that the greening area on the slope can be ensured, soil loosening at the slope is avoided, water and soil loss is avoided, and the ecological environment at the slope is protected; meanwhile, the biobrick is prepared by the steps of crushing industrial waste, forming and the like, so that the industrial waste can be recycled, the accumulation of the industrial waste is reduced, and the environment is protected.

title: HIGH-THOUGHPUT ASSEMBLY OF GENETIC ELEMENTS
text: HIGH-THOUGHPUT ASSEMBLY OF GENETIC ELEMENTS. Disclosed is a method of generating a set of sequence-verified nucleic acid elements for the combinatorial construction of genetic elements. The method includes: providing a plurality of nucleic acid parts; assembling nucleic acid parts to form a one or more nucleic acid elements, wherein the nucleic acid elements include at least two sequences selected from the plurality of parts; and determining the sequence of the nucleic acid elements. Further disclosed is pool of higher-order nucleic acid constructs or amplification products thereof, comprising one or more nucleic acid elements as well as kits including a the pool of sequence-verified nucleic acid elements of claims and/or a pool of higher-order nucleic acid constructs; and a plurality of primers for retrieving one or more sequence-verified nucleic acid elements and/or higher-order nucleic acid constructs.

title: BIOLOGICAL CIRCUIT CHEMOTACTIC CONVERTERS
text: BIOLOGICAL CIRCUIT CHEMOTACTIC CONVERTERS. Described herein are novel biological circuit chemotactic converter that utilize modular components, such as genetic toggle switches and single invertase memory modules (SIMMs), for detecting and converting external inputs, such as chemoattractants, into outputs that allow for autonomous chemotaxis in cellular systems. Flexibility in these biological circuit chemotactic converter is provided by combining individual modular components, i.e., SIMMs and genetic toggle switches, together. These biological converter switches can be combined in a variety of network topologies to create network systems that regulate chemotactic responses based on the combination and nature of input signals received.

title: SUPER COMPACTION OF BIOMASS AND OTHER CARBON-CONTAINING MATERIALS TO HIGH ENERGY CONTENT FUELS
text: SUPER COMPACTION OF BIOMASS AND OTHER CARBON-CONTAINING MATERIALS TO HIGH ENERGY CONTENT FUELS. A super compactor receives a carbon-containing material and delivers a brick suitable as fuel for power generation. A compaction chamber receives the carbon-containing material and has at least one ram reciprocating along a travel path and exerting a predetermined amount of pressure on the carbon-containing material. The compaction chamber also has an end effector plate or a movable slide gate against which the carbon-containing material is compressed, promoting the removal of moisture from the carbon-containing material, and one or more dewatering apertures or a membrane permitting moisture to pass through but blocking the carbon-containing material. A collector captures the moisture that passes through the dewatering apertures or membrane. A power unit provides power to the at least one ram. A heated extrusion chamber receives the carbon-containing material from the compaction chamber and heats and compacts the carbon-containing material to bind the carbon-containing material into the form of a brick.

title: Genetic indicator and control system and method utilizing split Cas9/CRISPR domains for transcriptional control in eukaryotic cell lines
text: Genetic indicator and control system and method utilizing split Cas9/CRISPR domains for transcriptional control in eukaryotic cell lines. While genetic engineering has undergone rapid advancement with the discovery of CRISPR/Cas9, there is room for improvement for genetic circuit control, precision (reducing circuit ‘leakiness’) and delivery into living systems. The claimed invention offers programmable and precise regulation of dCas9 functions in response to multiple molecular signals by using synthetic gene circuits, greatly expanding applications. Moreover, using the system to greatest therapeutic potential has been greatly limited by the restrictive cargo size of existing viral delivery systems. By splitting dCas9 into multiple sections, the delivery size of synthetic gene circuits is greatly reduced. By exchanging split dCas9 domains, differential regulation on one gene, or activating two different genes in response to cell-type specific microRNAs is illustrated. Practical applications of the illustrative examples include engineered sensory switches including indicators for bladder cancer as well as enhanced systems for adenovirus delivery, cellular regulation, plant cell modification and potential therapeutic applications.

title: NITROGEN FIXATION GENE ISLAND SUITABLE FOR EXPRESSING IN PROKARYOTIC AND EUKARYOTIC SYSTEMS
text: NITROGEN FIXATION GENE ISLAND SUITABLE FOR EXPRESSING IN PROKARYOTIC AND EUKARYOTIC SYSTEMS. Provided is a nitrogen fixation gene island suitable for expressing in prokaryotic and eukaryotic systems, wherein the prokaryotic nitrogen fixation genes are modified using T7 promoters to make them suitable for eukaryotic expression.

title: Combined water and soil conservation biobrick
text: Combined water and soil conservation biobrick. The utility model relates to the field of biological bricks, in particular to a combined water and soil conservation biological brick which comprises a biological brick body, the biological brick body comprises a bottom brick body and a face brick body, the bottom brick body is located above the face brick body, and the bottom brick body is detachably connected with the face brick body; wherein a groove is formed in the side, facing the face brick body, of the bottom brick body, a water absorption sponge is embedded in the groove, and an adjusting assembly used for adjusting the distance between the bottom brick body and the face brick body is arranged between the bottom brick body and the face brick body. The utilization efficiency of the biobrick can be improved, resource waste can be reduced, meanwhile, loss of water and soil resources can be reduced, and the biobrick has the advantages of saving energy and being environmentally friendly.

title: Brick making method and device for microbial cementing biobrick by unsaturated method and biobrick
text: Brick making method and device for microbial cementing biobrick by unsaturated method and biobrick. The invention relates to a method and a device for making a microbial cementing biobrick by an unsaturated method and the biobrick. The method comprises the following steps: firstly, mixing a pre-prepared powder slag raw material and a bacillus pasteurii bacterial solution, adding the mixture into a forming cavity, and compacting the powder slag raw material; then, a pre-prepared cementing liquid is added into the forming cavity for a curing reaction, the volume of the fed cementing liquid is equal to the volume of the bacillus pasteurii liquid, and the sum of the volume of the cementing liquid and the volume of the bacillus pasteurii liquid is smaller than the pore volume of the compacted powder slag raw material; after a curing reaction is carried out for a set time, demolding and drying are carried out, and the biobrick is obtained. The biobrick obtained through the method is high in strength.

title: NOVEL RECOMBINASES AND TARGET SEQUENCES
text: NOVEL RECOMBINASES AND TARGET SEQUENCES. The disclosure provides novel large serine recombinases and their respective recognition sites, as well as libraries of orthogonal recombinase recognition sites. Uses of the large serine recombinases, recognition sites, and libraries of orthogonal recombinase recognition sites also are provided.

title: Method for preparing biobrick by using engineering bacteria
text: Method for preparing biobrick by using engineering bacteria. The invention discloses a method for preparing a biobrick by using engineering bacteria, and belongs to the technical field of biological building materials. Escherichia coli engineering bacteria capable of generating urease are obtained through engineering modification of escherichia coli, an escherichia coli engineering bacteria solution is mixed with a matching solution containing urea and mineral calcium salt, then sand grains and a hydrogel adhesive are added, and under the appropriate environmental condition, urease generated by the escherichia coli engineering bacteria can catalyze urea decomposition, so that the urea decomposition rate is increased, and the urea decomposition rate is increased. Ammonia and carbonate ions are generated and then react with calcium ions to generate calcium carbonate, so that sand grains are cemented together, and the biobrick with certain strength and biological characteristics is formed. The biobrick not only has good strength, but also has certain self-repairing capability and environmental adaptability, and can be applied to the field of civil engineering as a green and sustainable building material. The preparation method disclosed by the invention is simple, efficient and environment-friendly, has important significance on the national double-carbon strategy, and has a wide application prospect.

title: MATRIX-ASSISTED SPECTROPHOTOMETRY
text: MATRIX-ASSISTED SPECTROPHOTOMETRY. Disclosed herein are multiwell plates suitable for spectrophotometry for low-volume liquid samples. For the multiwell plates, the bottom of at least one well has a layer of porous matrix disposed thereon, or is comprised of a layer of porous matrix. The layer of porous matrix permits a low-volume liquid sample to distribute evenly across the porous matrix. Also disclosed herein are methods of performing a photometric or spectrophotometric measurement on a liquid sample having a small volume, by using a multiwell plate comprising a layer of porous matrix.

title: Anti-sigmas for programmable transcriptional regulation
text: Anti-sigmas for programmable transcriptional regulation. The invention relates to anti-sigma factors (“anti-sigmas”) that bind to sigma factors and block activation of transcription. Anti-sigmas and their cognate sigma factors provide a highly effective mechanism for regulating gene expression in genetic circuits.

title: MATRIX-ASSISTED SPECTROPHOTOMETRY
text: MATRIX-ASSISTED SPECTROPHOTOMETRY. Disclosed herein are multiwell plates suitable for spectrophotometry for low-volume liquid samples. For the multiwell plates, the bottom of at least one well has a layer of porous matrix disposed thereon, or is comprised of a layer of porous matrix. The layer of porous matrix permits a low-volume liquid sample to distribute evenly across the porous matrix. Also disclosed herein are methods of performing a photometric or spectrophotometric measurement on a liquid sample having a small volume, by using a multiwell plate comprising a layer of porous matrix.

title: Water and soil conservation biobrick
text: Water and soil conservation biobrick. The utility model discloses a water and soil conservation biobrick, which relates to the technical field of water and soil conservation and comprises a biobrick body, a first mounting groove formed in the outer surface of the biobrick body, a second mounting groove formed in the outer surface of the biobrick body, a mounting mechanism arranged inside the biobrick body and a slot formed in the outer surface of the biobrick body. An insertion block is fixedly connected to the outer surface of the biobrick body, a group of planting holes arranged at equal intervals are formed in the upper surface of the biobrick body, and a water storage mechanism is arranged in the biobrick body. By arranging the mounting mechanisms and the biobrick bodies, two adjacent biobrick bodies can be connected in series by utilizing the arranged mounting mechanisms during use, so that the problem that the biobrick bodies fall off one by one along with loss of a soil layer when an existing device encounters heavy rain weather is solved, the fixing effect of the biobrick bodies is improved, and the service life of the biobrick bodies is prolonged. The practicability of the biobrick body is enhanced.

title: Method of preparing nucleic acid molecules
text: Method of preparing nucleic acid molecules. Provided is a method of preparing nucleic acid molecules comprising: (a) a step of providing nucleic acid fragments constituting at least a portion of the complete sequence of a target nucleic acid; (b) tagging the nucleic acid fragments with barcode sequences; (c) identifying the sequence of the nucleic acid fragments tagged by the barcode sequences; and (d) recovering desired nucleic acid fragments among the sequence-identified nucleic acid fragments using the barcode sequences.

title: Biological analog-to-digital and digital-to-analog converters
text: Biological analog-to-digital and digital-to-analog converters. Described herein are novel biological converter switches that utilize modular components, such as genetic toggle switches and single invertase memory modules (SIMMs), for converting analog inputs to digital outputs, and digital inputs to analog outputs, in cells and cellular systems. Flexibility in these biological converter switches is provided by combining individual modular components, i.e., SIMMs and genetic toggle switches, together. These biological converter switches can be combined in a variety of network topologies to create circuits that act, for example, as switchboards, and regulate the production of an output product(s) based on the combination and nature of input signals received.

title: Super Compaction Of Biomass and Other Carbon-Containing Materials To High Energy Content Fuels
text: Super Compaction Of Biomass and Other Carbon-Containing Materials To High Energy Content Fuels. A super compactor receives a carbon-containing material and delivers a brick suitable as fuel for power generation. A compaction chamber receives the carbon-containing material and has at least one ram reciprocating along a travel path and exerting a predetermined amount of pressure on the carbon-containing material. The compaction chamber also has an end effector plate or a movable slide gate against which the carbon-containing material is compressed, promoting the removal of moisture from the carbon-containing material, and one or more dewatering apertures or a membrane permitting moisture to pass through but blocking the carbon-containing material. A collector captures the moisture that passes through the dewatering apertures or membrane. A power unit provides power to the at least one ram. A heated extrusion chamber receives the carbon-containing material from the compaction chamber and heats and compacts the carbon-containing material to bind the carbon-containing material into the form of a brick.

title: Method for preparing constructed body from biobrick containing endothelial cells
text: Method for preparing constructed body from biobrick containing endothelial cells. The invention relates to a method for preparing a constructed body from a biobrick containing endothelial cells, belonging to the technical fields of biology, regenerative medicine, biological printing (e.g., 3D biological printing), tissue engineering, etc. Particularly, the invention relates to a biobrick applicable to biological printing (e.g., 3D biological printing) and tissue engineering, a composition (e.g., biological ink) including the biobrick, a preparation method and application of the biobrick and the constructed body (e.g., a three-dimensional constructed body) including the biobrick or constructed by using the biobrick/biological ink. The biobrick comprises cells, a core wrapping cells (e.g., endothelial cells), and a shell encapsulating the core.

title: Method for preparing biobrick by using microorganisms to induce calcium carbonate precipitation and solidify rare earth tailing sand
text: Method for preparing biobrick by using microorganisms to induce calcium carbonate precipitation and solidify rare earth tailing sand. The invention discloses a method for preparing a biobrick by using microorganisms to induce calcium carbonate precipitation and solidify rare earth tailing sand, and belongs to the field of geotechnical engineering. The method comprises the following steps: treating the rare earth tailing sand; the preparation of the curing liquid comprises preparation of a bacterial liquid and a cementing liquid; and manufacturing the biobrick. The biobrick is manufactured in a slurry mixing and demolding mode, the process is simple, the cost is low, the manufacturing process is green and free of pollution, the prepared biobrick can meet the requirement for temporary storage of the rare earth tailing sand, the environmental problem caused by accumulation of the rare earth tailing sand is solved, and a foundation is laid for secondary recycling of the rare earth tailing sand.

title: Water and soil conservation biobrick and water and soil conservation device composed thereof
text: Water and soil conservation biobrick and water and soil conservation device composed thereof. The invention discloses a water and soil conservation biobrick and a water and soil conservation device composed thereof. The water and soil conservation biobrick structurally comprises a mounting disc, a bolt, a through hole, a soil breaking cone and a grating; the mounting disc is connected in the through hole through the bolt; the soil breaking cone is connected below the through hole; the grating is arranged on the soil breaking cone; the grating comprises a cross rod, a rubber sleeve, a row clamp, a connecting pipe and a frame; the cross rod is connected in the frame through the rubber sleeve and is connected with the soil breaking cone; the rubber sleeve is indirectly matched with the row clamp through the connecting pipe; a gathering device and a leveling part are arranged on the row clamp; and the gathering device and the leveling part are matched on a column layer. When the grating is slowly expanded to be embedded into soil, the gathering device pushes a row plate to shake in the column layer, and by means of the leveling part, a base slightly extends and is flatly laid in the rubber sleeve, so that the situation that the loose soil is stably compacted within a limited range is guaranteed, and the use level of equipment is maintained.

title: ELECTRONIC READER
text: ELECTRONIC READER. The present invention relates to electronic readers configured to perform optical measurements (e.g., absorbance, fluorescence, luminescence, colorimetric analysis, etc.) on samples present on porous matrices. The electronic readers are particularly useful for measuring paper-based devices.

title: Nitrogen fixation gene island suitable for expressing in prokaryotic and eukaryotic systems
text: Nitrogen fixation gene island suitable for expressing in prokaryotic and eukaryotic systems. Provided is a nitrogen fixation gene island suitable for expressing in prokaryotic and eukaryotic systems, wherein the prokaryotic nitrogen fixation genes are modified using T7 promoters to make them suitable for eukaryotic expression.

title: Super compaction of biomass and other carbon-containing materials to high energy content fuels
text: Super compaction of biomass and other carbon-containing materials to high energy content fuels. A super compactor receives a carbon-containing material and delivers a brick suitable as fuel for power generation. A compaction chamber receives the carbon-containing material and has at least one ram reciprocating along a travel path and exerting a predetermined amount of pressure on the carbon-containing material. The compaction chamber also has an end effector plate or a movable slide gate against which the carbon-containing material is compressed, promoting the removal of moisture from the carbon-containing material, and one or more dewatering apertures or a membrane permitting moisture to pass through but blocking the carbon-containing material. title: DIGITAL MICROFLUIDIC GENE SYNTHESIS AND ERROR CORRECTION
text: DIGITAL MICROFLUIDIC GENE SYNTHESIS AND ERROR CORRECTION. The invention provides methods for gene synthesis and error correction on a droplet actuator. In one embodiment, the invention provides a method for performing polymerase-mediated gene synthesis on a droplet actuator. The polymerase-mediated gene synthesis method uses oligonucleotide hybridization and PCR cycling to generate a pool of synthesized DNA strands, which may be subsequently enriched for the correct gene sequence.

title: Preparation method of sulfur autotrophic denitrification biobrick and biobrick prepared by preparation method
text: Preparation method of sulfur autotrophic denitrification biobrick and biobrick prepared by preparation method. The invention discloses a preparation method of a sulfur autotrophic denitrification biobrick and the biobrick prepared by the preparation method. The preparation method comprises the following steps:mixing sulfur powder, pyrite powder, a granular adsorbent capable of adsorbing nitrate nitrogen and construction waste recycled aggregate to form mixed aggregate; uniformly stirring the sulfur autotrophic denitrifying bacteria liquid, the biological cementing liquid containing the bacillus pasteurii and the mixed aggregate, and then putting the mixture into a porous water-permeable mold for compaction; completely immersing the porous water-permeable mold in a mixed salt solution of urea and CaCl2, and standing for 24 hours; taking out the porous water-permeable mold in the mixed salt solution, and draining water in pores at a ventilated place; and taking out the cured dry brick body from the porous water-permeable mold, and repeating the steps of soaking and draining for 2-4 times to finally obtain the sulfur autotrophic denitrification biobrick. The biobrick is simple in manufacturing process, resources are saved, nitrogen in water can be effectively removed, water pollutants are adsorbed, and the biobrick has important significance in construction of a conservation-minded society.

title: Method for preparing constructed body from biobrick
text: Method for preparing constructed body from biobrick. The invention relates to a method for preparing a constructed body from a biobrick, belonging to the technical fields of biology, regenerative medicine, biological printing (e.g., 3D biological printing), tissue engineering, etc. Particularly, the invention relates to a biobrick applicable to biological printing (e.g., 3D biological printing) and tissue engineering, a composition (e.g., biological ink) including the biobrick, a preparation method and application of the biobrick and the constructed body (e.g., a three-dimensional constructed body) including the biobrick or constructed by using the biobrick/biological ink.

title: Programmable Oligonucleotide Synthesis
text: Programmable Oligonucleotide Synthesis. The invention relates to methods and devices for preparing synthetic nucleic acids.

title: Preparation method for biobricks containing mesenchymal stem cell (MSC) and biobrick prepared by using same
text: Preparation method for biobricks containing mesenchymal stem cell (MSC) and biobrick prepared by using same. The invention provides a biobrick, comprising MSCs, a core layer wrapping the MSCs and a shell layer encapsulating the core layer, wherein the core layer and the shell layer are independently prepared from a biodegradable material and the core layer contains an inducing factor capable of inducing differentiation of MSCs into osteoblasts or bone. The invention also provides another biobrick, comprising MSCs, a core layer wrapping the MSCs and a shell layer encapsulating the core layer, wherein the core layer and the shell layer are independently prepared from a biodegradable material and the core layer contains an inducing factor capable of inducing differentiation of MSCs into chondroblasts or cartilage. The invention further provides a method for preparing a composite structure containing bone and cartilage. The composite structure comprises the above-mentioned two biobricks. The invention also provides the composite structure containing bone and cartilage prepared by using the method.

title: Combined water and soil conservation biobrick
text: Combined water and soil conservation biobrick. The utility model relates to the technical field of water and soil conservation, and discloses a combined type water and soil conservation biobrick which comprises a brick body, evenly-distributed planting blocks are fixedly connected to the top of the brick body in a penetrating mode, an installation cylinder is fixedly connected to the tops of the planting blocks in a penetrating mode, and drainage holes are fixedly connected to the four corners of the top of the inner wall of the installation cylinder in a penetrating mode. A fixing plate is fixedly connected to the bottom of the mounting cylinder, evenly-distributed supporting nets penetrate through and are fixedly connected to the top of the fixing plate, a bottom plate is arranged at the bottoms of the supporting nets, and a supporting plate is fixedly connected to the top of the bottom plate. According to the biobrick disclosed by the utility model, the clamping block connected on the connecting plate is clamped with the groove in the clamping plate on the connecting plate on another biobrick, so that the biobrick can be spliced according to actual requirements, the most effective splicing effect can be achieved, and the biobrick can be simply spliced by the mutual sliding between the clamping block and the groove in the splicing or dismounting process.

title: Combined water and soil conservation biobrick
text: Combined water and soil conservation biobrick. The utility model discloses a combined type water and soil conservation biobrick which comprises a box body, a water injection hole is formed in one side of the front face of the box body, a partition plate is arranged in the box body, a water passing pipe is arranged in the partition plate, an atomizing nozzle is arranged on one side of the water passing pipe, a ventilation net is arranged on one side of the box body, and a water outlet is formed in the other side of the box body. A hub is arranged in the middle of the bottom of the box body, universal wheels are arranged on one side of the bottom of the box body, and a first pushing handle is fixedly connected to the top of the other side of the box body. According to the combined water and soil conservation biobrick, through the arrangement of the water injection hole and the atomization nozzle, when the biobrick is placed in the box body for a long time, water is injected into the water through pipe through the water injection hole, the atomization nozzle atomizes the water and sprays the water on the biobrick in the box body, so that the biobrick keeps the due humidity, and through the arrangement of the ventilation net, the interior of the box body can be ventilated; and the reaction of the biobrick caused by long-time staying in a stuffy and humid environment is prevented.

text: Recombinase-based logic and memory systems. The invention provides, inter alia, recombinase-based systems that provide for integrated logic and memory in living cells.

title: NITROGEN FIXATION GENE ISLAND SUITABLE FOR EXPRESSING IN PROKARYOTIC AND EUKARYOTIC SYSTEMS
text: NITROGEN FIXATION GENE ISLAND SUITABLE FOR EXPRESSING IN PROKARYOTIC AND EUKARYOTIC SYSTEMS. Provided is a nitrogen fixation gene island suitable for expressing in prokaryotic and eukaryotic systems, wherein the prokaryotic nitrogen fixation genes are modified using T7 promoters to make them suitable for eukaryotic expression.

title: Combined water and soil conservation biobrick
text: Combined water and soil conservation biobrick. The utility model relates to the technical field of water and soil conservation, and discloses a combined type water and soil conservation biobrick which comprises a biological face brick, a biological bottom brick is fixedly installed at the bottom of the biological face brick, a plant groove is formed in the bottom of the biological bottom brick, a clamping groove is formed in one side of the plant groove, and a water inlet is formed in the other side of the plant groove. And a base material frame is clamped in the clamping groove through a first clamping block. According to the combined type water and soil conservation biobrick, the biological bottom brick, the plant groove, the clamping groove, the first clamping block, the base material frame, the base material and the rhizome plant seeds are arranged in a matched mode, the biological bottom brick is attached to the surface of soil when the combined type water and soil conservation biobrick is used, then the rhizome plant seeds in the base material root and germinate through rainwater or manual irrigation, and then water and soil conservation is conducted through plant rhizomes; and through the arrangement of the growth openings, the effect of facilitating the growth of plants in the using process is achieved.

title: Compositions and methods for pesticide degradation
text: Compositions and methods for pesticide degradation. Compositions and methods related to anucelate cells (e.g., bacterial minicells) for pesticide degradation applications including related cells, polypeptides, and vectors.

title: METHOD OF PREPARING NUCLEIC ACID MOLECULES
text: METHOD OF PREPARING NUCLEIC ACID MOLECULES. Provided is a method of preparing nucleic acid molecules comprising: (a) a step of providing nucleic acid fragments constituting at least a portion of the complete sequence of a target nucleic acid; (b) tagging the nucleic acid fragments with barcode sequences; (c) identifying the sequence of the nucleic acid fragments tagged by the barcode sequences; and (d) recovering desired nucleic acid fragments among the sequence-identified nucleic acid fragments using the barcode sequences.

title: Environment-friendly biobrick making machine
text: Environment-friendly biobrick making machine. The utility model discloses an environment-friendly biobrick making machine. The device comprises a tray conveying platform and a pushing platform, a pressing through hole, a lower die frame and an upper die are arranged on the pushing platform; the upper die is connected with a die pressing oil cylinder; a base soil pushing bin and a mixed seed soil pushing bin are respectively arranged on two sides of the pressing through hole of the pushing platform; a base soil metering platform and a seed soil metering platform are respectively arranged above two sides of the pressing through hole on thepushing platform; a base soil stirring bin and a seed soil mixing and stirring bin are respectively arranged above the base soil metering platform and the seed soil metering platform; a base soil metering frame is arranged between the bottom face of the base soil stirring bin and the upper face of the base soil metering platform, a mixed seed soil metering frame is arranged between the bottom faceof the seed soil mixing and stirring bin and the upper face of the seed soil metering platform, and the tray conveying platform is provided with a tray pushing frame and a tray pushing oil cylinder.According to the environment-friendly biobrick making machine, the environment-friendly biobrick is automatically pressed in a full-hydraulic mode, and the pressing efficiency is high.

text: TUMOR IMMUNOTHERAPY. Aspects of the present disclosure provide a platform that triggers potent and effective immunotherapy against tumors from within tumors themselves, thus overcoming limitations of existing cancer immunotherapies and tumor-detecting gene circuits.

title: Combined water and soil conservation biobrick
text: Combined water and soil conservation biobrick. The combined type water and soil conservation biobrick comprises a biobrick body, a positioning column and a water storage tank, the positioning column is installed on the outer side of the biobrick body, movable cavities are formed in the front face and the back face of the positioning column, a positioning plate is installed on the side, away from the positioning column, of the biobrick body, and the water storage tank is installed on the positioning plate. A water accumulation groove is formed in the top of the biobrick body, and a water storage groove is formed in the biobrick body. The movable cavities are formed in the front faces and the back faces of the positioning columns, the locking blocks are pressed when the biobricks are combined, the locking blocks move on the inner sides of the movable grooves to extrude the reset springs, and the biobricks are transversely spliced so that the mounting blocks can be mounted on the inner sides of the mounting grooves in an embedded mode; and the reset springs enable the locking blocks to be clamped on the inner sides of the locking holes due to the elasticity of the reset springs, so that stable connection between the two groups of biobricks is realized, convenience is provided for users for convenient mounting and dismounting of the biobricks, and the practical performance is enhanced.

title: Compositions and Methods for Pesticide Degradation
text: Compositions and Methods for Pesticide Degradation. Compositions and methods related to anucelate cells (e.g., bacterial minicells) for pesticide degradation applications including related cells, polypeptides, and vectors.

title: Combined water and soil conservation biobrick
text: Combined water and soil conservation biobrick. The utility model discloses a combined type water and soil conservation biobrick which comprises a connecting shell, a connecting mechanism is arranged in an inner cavity of the connecting shell, an anti-rust sleeve is arranged on the upper end face of the connecting shell, and brick bodies are arranged on the left side and the right side of the connecting shell. The combined type water and soil conservation biobrick solves the problems that existing water and soil conservation biobricks are single in structure and inconvenient to be connected in series, and water and soil loss cannot be limited due to the poor fixing effect in heavy rain weather.

title: Biological circuit chemotactic converters
text: Biological circuit chemotactic converters. Described herein are novel biological circuit chemotactic converter that utilize modular components, such as genetic toggle switches and single invertase memory modules (SIMMs), for detecting and converting external inputs, such as chemoattractants, into outputs that allow for autonomous chemotaxis in cellular systems. Flexibility in these biological circuit chemotactic converter is provided by combining individual modular components, i.e., SIMMs and genetic toggle switches, together. These biological converter switches can be combined in a variety of network topologies to create network systems that regulate chemotactic responses based on the combination and nature of input signals received.

title: Biobrick with plant drought resisting function and production process of biobrick
text: Biobrick with plant drought resisting function and production process of biobrick. The invention discloses a biobrick with a plant drought resisting function and a production process thereof, and relates to the technical field of water and soil conservation, the biobrick comprises brick bodies, interlocking bayonets are symmetrically arranged on the upper side and the lower side of each brick body, clamping blocks are arranged on the brick bodies located on the two sides of the interlocking bayonets, and the brick bodies are in pairwise vertical interlocking clamping connection through the interlocking bayonets and the clamping blocks. The middle of the brick body is hollow and forms a planting area, longitudinal holes are formed in the bottom of the brick body on the front side and the rear side of the planting area, and transverse holes are formed in the bottom of the brick body on the left side and the right side of the planting area. The production process comprises the following steps: S1, selecting raw materials; s2, preparing a brick body and a limiting slide block; s3, preparing a biological glue seal; and S4, combining and assembling. The biobrick disclosed by the invention can be laid on soil of a river bank, the brick bodies are stably connected, the stability of the slope is improved, water and soil loss of a river bank area is favorably prevented, and the pressure of pollution treatment of rivers and reservoirs is relieved.

title: Movable ecological purification island
text: Movable ecological purification island. The utility model discloses a movable ecological purification island, including frame, stock bin, biobrick, active filler and aeration device, the frame is square, the stock bin is arranged in the frame, both sides of the stock bin are divided into anoxic zone, the middle is aerobic zone, the biobrick is fixed or hoisted in the stock bin, the active filler is dispersed in the stock bin, and the aeration device is arranged at the lower end of the frame. An aeration pipe of the aeration device is arranged at the lower end of the aerobic zone of the stock bin. According to the utility model, the frame and the stock bin are arranged, and aeration treatment is carried out, so that aerobic and anoxic treatment on sewage is realized, and the sewage is fully treated; according to the sewage treatment device disclosed by the invention, the active filler in the stock bin is used for treating pollutants in a water body, meanwhile, nutrients are provided for microorganisms on the active filler through slow release of the biobrick, the sewage can be quickly treated without pre-culture, the active filler can be turned over through aeration treatment and is more fully contacted with the sewage, the treatment effect is improved, and the treatment speed is increased; and nutrients of the biobrick can be better contacted and absorbed.
