title: Preparation method of biobrick with mechanical property enhanced by admixture
text: Preparation method of biobrick with mechanical property enhanced by admixture. The invention discloses a preparation method of a biobrick with mechanical properties enhanced by admixtures, which is characterized in that clay, cementing liquid and bacillus pasteurii liquid are used as main raw materials, active magnesium oxide and fiber materials are used as the admixtures, the fiber materials are polypropylene fibers or basalt fibers, the main raw materials and the admixtures are uniformly mixed to form a mixed material, and the mixed material is used as a raw material for preparing the biobrick with the mechanical properties enhanced by the admixtures. And preparing the biobrick by using the obtained mixed material. According to the preparation method of the biobrick with the mechanical property enhanced by the admixture, the compressive strength and the breaking strength of the biobrick are improved by adding the active magnesium oxide; by adding the polypropylene fiber or basalt fiber, the compressive strength and breaking strength of the biobrick are further improved, the brittleness of the biobrick is improved, and the ductility of the biobrick is improved.

title: Combined biobrick
text: Combined biobrick. The utility model relates to the technical field of water and soil conservation, and discloses a combined biobrick which comprises a brick body, a top plate is arranged at the top of the brick body, a water permeable mechanism is fixedly connected to the rear side of the brick body, and a combination mechanism is fixedly connected to the bottom of the top plate. The permeable mechanism comprises clamping blocks, the clamping blocks are fixedly connected to the front sides of the bricks, the clamping blocks are linearly distributed on the front sides of the bricks at equal intervals, and clamping grooves are formed in the rear sides of the bricks. According to the combined biobrick, by arranging the water permeable mechanism, in the using process, after rainwater in rainy days falls on the road surface, the rainwater directly enters the ground through the water permeable holes in the top of the top plate and the through holes in the clamping blocks, and it is avoided that too much rainwater stays on the road surface to affect walking of pedestrians; impurities entering the water permeable holes can be filtered under the action of the filter screen block, the water permeable holes are prevented from being blocked by the impurities, the filter screen block is conveniently taken out under the action of the pull ring and the connecting strip to be cleaned, and the water permeability of the biobrick is ensured.

title: METHODS FOR OBTAINING CORRECTLY ASSEMBLED NUCLEIC ACIDS
text: METHODS FOR OBTAINING CORRECTLY ASSEMBLED NUCLEIC ACIDS. Methods, systems, and devices are provided for identification and selective amplification of a correctly assembled nucleic acid comprising a sequence of interest from a mixture of assembled nucleic acids. In some aspects, the inventions use split-pool enzymatic DNA synthesis to add a bead-specific index sequence to bead-bound clonally amplified assembled nucleic acids. High-throughput sequencing is used to identify beads comprising correctly assembled, sequence-perfect nucleic acids. A correctly assembled nucleic acid may be selectively amplified using a primer comprising a sequence that is complementary to the bead-specific index sequence that is associated with the correctly assembled nucleic acid.

title: IMPROVED BIOTECHNOLOGICAL METHOD FOR PRODUCING GUANIDINO ACETIC ACID (GAA) BY INACTIVATION OF AN AMINO ACID EXPORTER
text: IMPROVED BIOTECHNOLOGICAL METHOD FOR PRODUCING GUANIDINO ACETIC ACID (GAA) BY INACTIVATION OF AN AMINO ACID EXPORTER. The present invention relates to a microorganism transformed to be capable of producing guanidinoacetic acid (GAA) having an inactivated amino acid exporter and to a method for the fermentative production of GAA using such microorganism. The present invention also relates to a method for the fermentative production of creatine.

title: BIOLOGICAL ANALOG-TO-DIGITAL AND DIGITAL-TO-ANALOG CONVERTERS
text: BIOLOGICAL ANALOG-TO-DIGITAL AND DIGITAL-TO-ANALOG CONVERTERS. Described herein are novel biological converter switches that utilize modular components, such as genetic toggle switches and single invertase memory modules (SIMMs), for converting analog inputs to digital outputs, and digital inputs to analog outputs, in cells and cellular systems. Flexibility in these biological converter switches is provided by combining individual modular components, i.e., SIMMs and genetic toggle switches, together. These biological converter switches can be combined in a variety of network topologies to create circuits that act, for example, as switchboards, and regulate the production of an output product(s) based on the combination and nature of input signals received.

title: MATRIX-ASSISTED SPECTROPHOTOMETRY
text: MATRIX-ASSISTED SPECTROPHOTOMETRY. Disclosed herein are multiwell plates suitable for spectrophotometry for low-volume liquid samples. For the multiwell plates, the bottom of at least one well has a layer of porous matrix disposed thereon, or is comprised of a layer of porous matrix. The layer of porous matrix permits a low-volume liquid sample to distribute evenly across the porous matrix. Also disclosed herein are methods of performing a photometric or spectrophotometric measurement on a liquid sample having a small volume, by using a multiwell plate comprising a layer of porous matrix.

title: Biobrick containing oxidized alginate and application thereof
text: Biobrick containing oxidized alginate and application thereof. The invention relates to the technical fields of biology, regenerative medicine, biological printing (e.g., 3D biological printing), tissue engineering, etc. Particularly, the invention relates to a biobrick applicable to biological printing (e.g., 3D biological printing) and tissue engineering, a composition (e.g., biological ink) including the biobrick, a preparation method and application of the biobrick and a constructed body (e.g., a three-dimensional constructed body) including the biobrick or constructed by using the biobrick/biological ink, wherein at least one shell layer of the biobrick contains oxidized alginate (e.g., oxidized sodium alginate and/or oxidized calcium alginate).

title: Biobricks containing mesenchymal stem cell (MSC) and application thereof
text: Biobricks containing mesenchymal stem cell (MSC) and application thereof. The invention provides a biobrick, comprising MSCs, a core layer wrapping the MSCs and a shell layer encapsulating the core layer, wherein the core layer and the shell layer are independently prepared from a biodegradable material and the core layer contains an inducing factor capable of inducing differentiation of MSCs into osteoblasts or bone. The invention also provides another biobrick, comprising MSCs, a core layer wrapping the MSCs and a shell layer encapsulating the core layer, wherein the core layer and the shell layer are independently prepared from a biodegradable material and the core layer contains an inducing factor capable of inducing differentiation of MSCs into chondroblasts or cartilage. The invention further provides a method for preparing a composite structure containing bone and cartilage. The composite structure comprises the above-mentioned two biobricks. The invention also provides the composite structure containing bone and cartilage prepared by using the method.

title: Combined water and soil conservation biobrick
text: Combined water and soil conservation biobrick. The utility model relates to the technical field of combined type water and soil conservation biobrick, and discloses a combined type water and soil conservation biobrick which comprises a biobrick body, a connecting assembly is arranged outside the biobrick body, and the connecting assembly comprises a placing frame and a ground surface attaching plate. A worker can fixedly connect the two biobrick bodies through the bolts and the threaded holes according to the adaptive relation between the connecting blocks and the connecting grooves, so that the adjacent biobrick bodies can be tightly connected; the biobrick body can be prevented from displacing or misplacing under the action of external force (such as water flow impact, wind power blowing or ground vibration), and then the earth surface attaching plate is fixed on the ground through the inserting rod, so that the biobrick body is firmly fixed on the ground, the scouring of rainwater, runoff and the like to soil can be effectively prevented, and the service life of the biobrick body is prolonged. The soil loss is prevented.

title: NOVEL RECOMBINASES AND TARGET SEQUENCES
text: NOVEL RECOMBINASES AND TARGET SEQUENCES. The disclosure provides novel large serine recombinases and their respective recognition sites, as well as libraries of orthogonal recombinase recognition sites. Uses of the large serine recombinases, recognition sites, and libraries of orthogonal recombinase recognition sites also are provided.

title: Biological brick sample surface permeation bacterium injection device
text: Biological brick sample surface permeation bacterium injection device. The utility model discloses a biobrick sample surface permeation bacterium injection device which comprises a bottom box and a top cover, the top cover covers the bottom box, a plurality of bacterium injection holes are distributed in the top cover, the bacterium injection holes penetrate through the top cover, and a connector is inserted in each bacterium injection hole; a dispersed sponge layer is adhered to the lower surface of the top cover, and the lower end of the fungus injection hole faces the dispersed sponge layer. The device has the advantages that bacterial liquid can be continuously, evenly and stably injected to the surfaces of the biobricks to be reinforced, influences of human factors are avoided, variables can be accurately controlled in the research stage, and the stability of the biobricks is guaranteed.

title: Agricultural biobrick as well as preparation method and use method thereof
text: Agricultural biobrick as well as preparation method and use method thereof. The invention discloses an agricultural biobrick which is prepared from soil, crop straws, livestock manure and complete rice straws, wherein the weight ratio of the soil to the crop straws to the livestock manure to the complete rice straws is 10: 7: 2: 1. The agricultural biobrick has two shapes, a first agricultural biobrick is in a cuboid shape and is called a common biobrick, and a second agricultural biobrick is in a cuboid shape, is provided with a through hole in the middle, and is called a biobrick for direct seeding. According to the agricultural biobrick disclosed by the invention, the crop straws and the complete rice straws can be used as fertilizers and also can be used for improving the strength of a mixture of the soil and the livestock manure, so that the soil and the livestock manure can be prefabricated into regular objects and are convenient to store, transport and use; and farmers only need to flatly lay the agricultural biobrick in a field, the effects of heat preservation, moisture preservation, weed inhibition and fertilizer slow release can be achieved, the use of agricultural plastic films and pesticides is reduced, and the labor input of fertilization is reduced.

title: Method for preparing biobrick by inducing calcium carbonate deposition through microorganisms
text: Method for preparing biobrick by inducing calcium carbonate deposition through microorganisms. The invention belongs to the technical field of novel building materials, and particularly relates to a method for preparing a biobrick by inducing calcium carbonate deposition through microorganisms.The method is used for preparing an ecological and environmentally friendly building brick having good strength characteristics and physical characteristics. The method comprises the steps: taking bacillus pasteurii and bacillus pseudofirmus as a composite bacterial liquid; mixing the composite bacterial liquid with a mixed saline solution and a urea aqueous solution to form a curing liquid; uniformly mixing the curing liquid with sand and quick lime; pouring the mixture into a brick making mold and carrying out curing reaction for 5-7 days; and supplementing a nutrient solution once every 12hours during period, supplementing the bacterial solution once every 1 day, and demolding and drying after curing is finished, to obtain the biobrick. The method has the advantages that the operationis simple, fast and efficient; and the prepared biobrick is non-toxic and pollution-free, has no adverse effect on the environment, also has high compressive strength and good environment tolerance,and can replace a traditional concrete brick.

title: RATIONAL DESIGN OF UPSTREAM ENHANCEMENT RNA FOR CIRCUIT DYNAMICS REGULATION AND VIRAL DIAGNOSTICS OPTIMIZATION
text: RATIONAL DESIGN OF UPSTREAM ENHANCEMENT RNA FOR CIRCUIT DYNAMICS REGULATION AND VIRAL DIAGNOSTICS OPTIMIZATION. The present invention provides a new class of RNA modules, referred to as degradation tuning RNAs (dtRNAs), which form stabilizing secondary structures. Also provided are methods of using dtRNAs to modulate the stability of RNAs. DNA constructs including a promoter that is operably connected to a sequence encoding the dtRNA are also provided.

title: RECOMBINASE-BASED LOGIC AND MEMORY SYSTEMS
text: RECOMBINASE-BASED LOGIC AND MEMORY SYSTEMS

title: BIOLOGICAL ANALOG-TO-DIGITAL AND DIGITAL-TO-ANALOG CONVERTERS
text: BIOLOGICAL ANALOG-TO-DIGITAL AND DIGITAL-TO-ANALOG CONVERTERS. Described herein are novel biological converter switches that utilize modular components, such as genetic toggle switches and single invertase memory modules (SIMMs), for converting analog inputs to digital outputs, and digital inputs to analog outputs, in cells and cellular systems. Flexibility in these biological converter switches is provided by combining individual modular components, i.e., SIMMs and genetic toggle switches, together. These biological converter switches can be combined in a variety of network topologies to create circuits that act, for example, as switchboards, and regulate the production of an output product(s) based on the combination and nature of input signals received.

title: Microfluidic-based Gene Synthesis
text: Microfluidic-based Gene Synthesis. A method for synthesizing long DNA constructs from oligonucleotide precursors directly within a microfluidic device uses several oligonucleotides at once. A precursor mix containing at least two oligonucleotide precursors with at least partial base complementarity is introduced into an input of a microfluidic chip and at least one cycle of at least one gene synthesis protocol is applied to fabricate a DNA construct containing the sequence of at least two oligonucleotide precursors. A method for the synthesis of a modified DNA construct includes electroporating at least one oligonucleotide encoding for at least one point mutation and having homology with at least one DNA region of a target cell into the target cell and incorporating the oligonucleotide into the target cell DNA through the action of recombination protein beta or a recombination protein beta functional homolog.

title: Method for preparing biobrick containing oxidized alginate and biobrick prepared by using same
text: Method for preparing biobrick containing oxidized alginate and biobrick prepared by using same. The invention relates to the technical fields of biology, regenerative medicine, biological printing (e.g., 3D biological printing), tissue engineering, etc. Particularly, the invention relates to a biobrick applicable to biological printing (e.g., 3D biological printing) and tissue engineering, a composition (e.g., biological ink) including the biobrick, a preparation method and application of the biobrick and a constructed body (e.g., a three-dimensional constructed body) including the biobrick or constructed by using the biobrick/biological ink, wherein at least one shell of the biobrick contains oxidized alginate (e.g., oxidized sodium alginate and/or oxidized calcium alginate).

title: Water and soil conservation biobrick
text: Water and soil conservation biobrick. The utility model discloses a water and soil conservation biobrick which comprises an earth surface, a biobrick body is arranged at the top of the earth surface, a mounting notch is formed in the top of the biobrick body, a positioning groove is formed in the bottom of the inner wall of the mounting notch, a fixing block is arranged in the mounting notch in a clamped and matched mode, and a positioning insertion rod is fixedly connected to the bottom of the fixing block. A planting notch is formed in the middle of the biobrick body, planting soil is arranged in the planting notch, plants are planted on the planting soil, the biobrick body is provided with a mounting block, a limiting block is arranged on the mounting block, a mounting groove is formed in the biobrick body, and a limiting groove is formed in the inner wall of the mounting groove. According to the utility model, the whole brick body can be firmly and stably positioned and fixedly mounted on the earth surface through the matching of the fixing blocks, the positioning insertion rods, the mounting notches, the positioning grooves and other structures, so that the positioning stability of the brick body can be effectively ensured, and the brick body is not easy to deviate and slide.

title: COMPOSITIONS AND METHODS FOR PESTICIDE DEGRADATION
text: COMPOSITIONS AND METHODS FOR PESTICIDE DEGRADATION. Compositions and methods related to anucelate cells (e.g., bacterial minicells) for pesticide degradation applications including related cells, polypeptides, and vectors.

title: Low-carbon energy-saving biobrick forming device
text: Low-carbon energy-saving biobrick forming device. The utility model discloses a low-carbon energy-saving biobrick forming device which comprises a box body, a sand box with the top and the bottom open is arranged in a forming cavity, a rack is fixed on the upper surface of the box body, a telescopic pipe in butt joint with a discharging hole is arranged at the bottom of the rack, a plurality of filtering holes are formed in a bottom plate, and the lower end of the telescopic pipe is connected with the middle of a leveling plate. A positive electrode is arranged on the upper surface of the leveling plate, a negative electrode is arranged at the bottom of the forming cavity, and the output end of the downward-pressing air cylinder is connected with the upper surface of the leveling plate. According to the utility model, the rock-soil particles, the bacterial liquid and the cementing liquid are injected into the mold, the mechanical property of the brick body is improved by utilizing the mineralization effect of microorganisms, the clay crushing and firing steps in the traditional brick making process are not needed, and only the sand particles with proper sizes are selected to be mixed with the bacterial liquid and the cementing liquid, and then the drying process is carried out; the preparation of the biobrick in the low-carbon and energy-saving environment is completed.

title: Biobrick preparation device for slope microorganism mineralization support
text: Biobrick preparation device for slope microorganism mineralization support. The utility model discloses a biobrick preparation device for slope microorganism mineralization support, which comprises a plurality of forming boxes, two guide rails are arranged above the plurality of forming boxes, and grating plates are arranged in the forming boxes; the upper end of the sleeve movably penetrates through the middle of the bearing plate, an oil cylinder is inversely arranged on the upper surface of the bearing plate, a rotating motor is arranged at the output end of the oil cylinder, a universal ball penetrates through the outer circumferential wall of the lower section of the sleeve, and a plurality of jacking blocks are arranged at the bottom of the jacking plate. Driving motors are arranged at the two ends of the bearing plate correspondingly, and gears are arranged at the output ends of the driving motors. A bottom plate is arranged in the middle of the grating plate, and a butt joint mechanism matched with the lower end of the connecting rod is arranged on the bottom plate. According to the device disclosed by the utility model, only cementing particles and two-phase solutions (bacterial liquid and curing liquid) for inducing to generate calcium carbonate need to be put and immersed in the forming cavity, the traditional destructive clay mining and the use of chemical reagents are abandoned, and the device is really friendly to the ecological environment.

title: PROGRAMMABLE OLIGONUCLEOTIDE SYNTHESIS
text: PROGRAMMABLE OLIGONUCLEOTIDE SYNTHESIS. The invention relates to methods and devices for preparing synthetic nucleic acids.

title: Method for improving expression level of recombinant protein in corynebacterium glutamicum and application thereof
text: Method for improving expression level of recombinant protein in corynebacterium glutamicum and application thereof. The invention provides a method for improving the expression level of recombinant protein in corynebacterium glutamicum and application thereof. The expression of the corynebacterium glutamicum foreign protein can be effectively improved. An RBS sequence is introduced into the upstream of a gene through a universal RBS primer and a PCR (Polymerase Chain Reaction) technology, then a gene multi-copy vector is constructed by utilizing a biobrick assembly technology, and high-efficiency expression of protein is realized by utilizing the technology.

title: A CONSTRUCTION, A VECTOR AND A METHOD PRODUCTION OF A TARGET MOLECULE IN A CYANOBACTERIUM
text: A CONSTRUCTION, A VECTOR AND A METHOD PRODUCTION OF A TARGET MOLECULE IN A CYANOBACTERIUM

title: PCR-BASED METHOD OF SYNTHESIZING A NUCLEIC ACID MOLECULE
text: PCR-BASED METHOD OF SYNTHESIZING A NUCLEIC ACID MOLECULE. There is provided a method of synthesizing a nucleic acid molecule and in one aspect, the method comprises assembling a full length template nucleic acid molecule by PCR in a PCR reaction mixture comprising a set of assembly oligonucleotides having a first average melting temperature and a set of outer amplification primers having a second average melting temperature that is lower than the first average melting temperature, wherein said assembling comprises subjecting the PCR reaction mixture to a first annealing temperature that is higher than the second average melting temperature and; amplifying the full length template nucleic acid molecule by PCR in the PCR reaction mixture wherein said amplifying comprises subjecting the PCR reaction mixture to a second annealing temperature that permits annealing of the outer amplification primers to the full length template nucleic acid molecule.

title: Saccharomyces rouxii engineering bacterium with high yield of D-arabitol as well as construction method and application of saccharomyces rouxii engineering bacterium
text: Saccharomyces rouxii engineering bacterium with high yield of D-arabitol as well as construction method and application of saccharomyces rouxii engineering bacterium. The invention provides a high-yield D-arabitol yeast rouxii engineering bacterium as well as a construction method and application thereof, and belongs to the technical field of biological engineering. Saccharomyces rouxii is used as a starting strain, a synthetic biological technology is adopted, and metabolic pathway engineering and coenzyme engineering are integrated to construct the engineering strain of the Saccharomyces rouxii of which the production performance of D-arabitol is greatly improved; the engineered yeast rouxii solves the prominent problems of low production performance, low yield of D-arabitol, difficulty in downstream separation and purification and the like of a D-arabitol production strain in the prior art; the saccharomyces rouxii engineering bacterium has good application in high-yield D-arabitol by fermenting glucose.

title: Combined water and soil conservation biobrick
text: Combined water and soil conservation biobrick. The utility model relates to the technical field of environmental science and constructional engineering, in particular to a combined water and soil conservation biobrick which comprises a stabilizing frame, a planting block, a nutrient block and a fixing block are arranged in the stabilizing frame, a first clamping groove is formed in the bottom of the stabilizing frame, and a reserved opening is formed in the side face of the stabilizing frame. A water outlet is formed in the side face of the planting block, an anti-skid piece is arranged on the planting block, grass planting holes are formed in the planting block, and a fastener is fixedly connected to the lower portion of the water outlet. The utility model has the advantages that water and soil loss can be effectively prevented through the arrangement of the water outlet and the grass planting holes; by arranging the fixing blocks, the stability of the brick body can be enhanced, and loosening is prevented; by arranging the nutrient pool, the survival rate of plants can be increased, and compressed kitchen garbage is put into the nutrient pool, so that environmental pollution can be effectively reduced; the stabilizing frame is arranged to be a cross-shaped boss and can be connected in a buckling mode, construction consumables are reduced, and therefore the production cost is reduced.

title: SYNTHETIC GENE CIRCUIT MODEL AND CONSTRUCTION METHOD
text: SYNTHETIC GENE CIRCUIT MODEL AND CONSTRUCTION METHOD. A method for constructing a synthetic gene circuit model. The method comprises: establishing a gene circuit constrained resource allocation flux balance analysis method, and simulating a cell growth rate; constructing a growth rate regulated gene circuit model by combining the cell growth rate with a gene circuit dynamic model, and obtaining a dynamic behavior result of a gene circuit; and performing a quantitative experiment on the dynamic behavior of the gene circuit to obtain an experiment result, and correcting the model by means of a comparison result. According to the method, Escherichia coli is taken as a research object, the physiological state of cells is fully characterized using the method of combining flux balance analysis and a bacterial growth law on the basis of an Escherichia coli genome metabolic network model, the growth rate of the cells is simulated, and in conjunction with quantitative experiment data for standardizing a typical gene circuit, a gene circuit dynamic model depending on the cell growth rate is constructed, thereby accurately predicting the influence of the cell growth rate on the function of the synthetic gene circuit.

title: A CONSTRUCT, A VECTOR AND A METHOD OF PRODUCTION OF A TARGET MOLECULE IN A CYANOBACTERIUM
text: A CONSTRUCT, A VECTOR AND A METHOD OF PRODUCTION OF A TARGET MOLECULE IN A CYANOBACTERIUM

title: SYNTHETIC GENE CIRCUITS AND USES THEREOF
text: SYNTHETIC GENE CIRCUITS AND USES THEREOF. Aspects of the invention is directed to a high-throughput method for screening a gene circuit library. The pace of gene circuit engineering can thereby be increased by expanding the number of circuits tested in each cycle. By measuring large collections of circuits in a single experiment, such an approach could be used for rapidly profiling complex circuit design spaces to identify part compositions with desired quantitative behaviors.

title: SOLID PHASE TRANSFERS OF DNA AND OTHER REAGENTS
text: SOLID PHASE TRANSFERS OF DNA AND OTHER REAGENTS. Disclosed are methods and systems for solid phase transfer of a reagent to a substrate. In some embodiments, the method comprises providing at least a first composition comprising a solid phase, wherein the solid phase comprises a reagent; and dispensing a first sample from the first composition onto a first coordinate on a substrate, whereby the first reagent is transferred to the substrate from the solid phase.

title: Engineered phagemids
text: Engineered phagemids. Provided herein, in some embodiments, are engineered phagemids that comprise at least one synthetic genetic circuit, wherein the at least one synthetic genetic circuit comprises gene sequences encoding at least one non-lytic antimicrobial peptides (AMPs) and/or antibacterial toxin proteins, a stable origin of replication, and a bacteriophage-packaging signal, wherein the engineered phagemid does not comprise some or all gene sequences encoding bacteriophage proteins required for assembly of a bacteriophage particle.

title: SYNTHETIC REPRESSION OF GENE EXPRESSION IN PLANTS
text: SYNTHETIC REPRESSION OF GENE EXPRESSION IN PLANTS. The present disclosure provides synthetic repressor constructs and the proteins encoded therein, as well as synthetic repressible promoter constructs for use in combination with the synthetic repressor constructs/synthetic repressors disclosed herein. Various combinations of synthetic repressor constructs and synthetic repressible promoter constructs are also provided in synthetic genetic circuits for modifying expression of a protein of interest in a plant cell.

title: ANTI-SIGMAS FOR PROGRAMMABLE TRANSCRIPTIONAL REGULATION
text: ANTI-SIGMAS FOR PROGRAMMABLE TRANSCRIPTIONAL REGULATION. The invention relates to anti-sigma factors ("anti-sigmas") that bind to sigma factors and block activation of transcription. Anti-sigmas and their cognate sigma factors provide a highly effective mechanism for regulating gene expression in genetic circuits.

title: METHOD OF PREPARING NUCLEIC ACID MOLECULES
text: METHOD OF PREPARING NUCLEIC ACID MOLECULES. Disclosed is a method of preparing nucleic acid molecules, including: providing a pool of oligonucleotides, each containing restriction enzyme digestion sequences and generic flanking sequences; cleaving the restriction enzyme digestion sequence portions to provide a pool of mixtures comprising the oligonucleotides, each containing the generic flanking sequences at one end, and the oligonucleotides, each containing none of the generic flanking sequences at one end, and; assembling the oligonucleotides using the generic flanking sequences to randomly synthesize nucleic acid fragments.

title: Water and soil conservation biobrick with drainage system
text: Water and soil conservation biobrick with drainage system. The utility model discloses a water and soil conservation biobrick with a drainage system in the technical field of water and soil conservation, which comprises a prefabricated cement brick, two sides of the front end face of the prefabricated cement brick are fixedly connected with mounting blocks, and two sides of the rear end face of the prefabricated cement brick are provided with mounting grooves matched with the mounting blocks. The two sets of mounting grooves and the two sets of mounting blocks are arranged in the same vertical direction, two sets of water accumulation grooves are formed in the prefabricated cement brick, second drainage holes which are formed in a penetrating mode are formed in an inner cavity of the prefabricated cement brick in the vertical direction, and first drainage holes which are formed in a penetrating mode are formed in the inner cavity of the prefabricated cement brick in the transverse direction; the first drainage holes are communicated with the second drainage holes, drainage pipes are installed in the first drainage holes and the second drainage holes, drainage pipes are communicated with the bottom ends of the two water accumulation grooves, and the bottom ends of the drainage pipes are communicated with the drainage pipes. The practicability is high.

title: PROGRAMMABLE OLIGONUCLEOTIDE SYNTHESIS
text: PROGRAMMABLE OLIGONUCLEOTIDE SYNTHESIS. The invention relates to methods and devices for preparing synthetic nucleic acids.

title: GENETICALLY ENGINEERED SENSORS FOR IN VIVO DETECTION OF BLEEDING
text: GENETICALLY ENGINEERED SENSORS FOR IN VIVO DETECTION OF BLEEDING. Provided herein are microorganisms engineered with heme-responsive transcription factors and genetic circuits. Also provided are methods for using engineered microorganisms to sense bleeding events and treat bleeding in vivo.

title: Combined water and soil conservation biobrick
text: Combined water and soil conservation biobrick. The utility model discloses a combined water and soil conservation biobrick. The slope bricks are laid on a slope layer of the waste residue field through concrete. The horizontal bricks are laid on the horizontal layer of the waste residue field through concrete; concrete layers are arranged at the bottoms of the slope bricks and the horizontal bricks and are poured on the surface of the waste residue field; the concrete layer wraps the surface of the clay layer, and a vegetation layer is arranged on the surface of the clay layer. The vegetation layer is arranged at the top of the clay layer,and the runoff groove is formed in the surface of the clay layer in the length direction of the clay layer. The runoff grooves between every two adjacent slope bricks are communicated, the surfaces of the runoff grooves are coated with concrete, a flow collecting pit is formed in the slope layer of the waste residue field, a flow collecting barrel is arranged in each flow collecting pit, the bottoms of the runoff grooves are communicated to the flow collecting pits, and the runoff grooves in the horizontal bricks are communicated with the runoff grooves in the slope bricks. The device effectively prevents water and soil loss of the waste residue field.

title: Water and soil conservation biobrick
text: Water and soil conservation biobrick. The utility model relates to the technical field of water and soil conservation, in particular to a water and soil conservation biobrick which comprises a first brick body and a second brick body, the inner side of the first brick body is provided with a first groove, the inner side of the second brick body is provided with a second groove matched with the first groove, and the second brick body is provided with a second groove matched with the second groove. The bottoms of the first brick body and the second brick body are provided with the same reinforcing assembly; the reinforcing assembly comprises a first partition net fixedly connected with the bottom of the first brick body, the first partition net is matched with the first groove, the bottom of the second brick body is fixedly connected with a second partition net matched with the first partition net, and the second partition net is matched with the second groove. According to the utility model, through the splicing of the first groove and the second groove, rainwater can flow into the first groove and the second groove through the water flowing groove, the flowing groove and the sliding groove, and part of the rainwater can flow into the first groove and the second groove along the through holes, so that timely drainage can be realized, and the drainage performance is better.

title: SOLID PHASE TRANSFERS OF DNA AND OTHER REAGENTS
text: SOLID PHASE TRANSFERS OF DNA AND OTHER REAGENTS. Disclosed are methods and systems for solid phase transfer of a reagent to a substrate. In some embodiments, the method comprises providing at least a first composition comprising a solid phase, wherein the solid phase comprises a reagent; and dispensing a first sample from the first composition onto a first coordinate on a substrate, whereby the first reagent is transferred to the substrate from the solid phase.

title: Programmable oligonucleotide synthesis
text: Programmable oligonucleotide synthesis. The invention relates to methods and devices for preparing synthetic nucleic acids.

title: Methods for in vitro joining and combinatorial assembly of nucleic acid molecules
text: Methods for in vitro joining and combinatorial assembly of nucleic acid molecules. The present invention relates to methods of joining two or more double-stranded (ds) or single-stranded (ss) DNA molecules of interest in vitro, wherein the distal region of the first DNA molecule and the proximal region of the second DNA molecule of each pair share a region of sequence identity. The method allows the joining of a large number of DNA fragments, in a predetermined order and orientation, without the use of restriction enzymes. It can be used, e.g., to join synthetically produced sub-fragments of a gene or genome of interest. Kits for performing the method are also disclosed. The methods of joining DNA molecules may be used to generate combinatorial libraries useful to generate, for example, optimal protein expression through codon optimization, gene optimization, and pathway optimization.

title: ENGINEERING DNA ASSEMBLY IN VIVO AND METHODS OF MAKING AND USING THE REVERSE TRANSCRIPTASE TECHNOLOGY
text: ENGINEERING DNA ASSEMBLY IN VIVO AND METHODS OF MAKING AND USING THE REVERSE TRANSCRIPTASE TECHNOLOGY. Cells that can synthesize oligonucleotides in vivo to produce a nucleic acid nanostructure are described. Methods for producing oligonucleotide nanostructures for use in regulating gene expression and altering biological pathways are provided. Methods of performing multiplex automated genome editing (MAGE) are also provided.

title: DNA ASSEMBLY
text: DNA ASSEMBLY. The invention relates to a nucleic acid for use in DNA assembly, wherein the nucleic acid comprises at least one methylation-protectable restriction element, the methylation-protectable restriction element comprising: a restriction enzyme recognition sequence that is recognised by a restriction enzyme that cleaves outside of the recognition sequence; and a DNA methylase recognition sequence, wherein the restriction enzyme recognition sequence and the DNA methylase recognition sequence overlap such that the base modified by the DNA methylase lies within the restriction enzyme recognition sequence, wherein the DNA methylase recognition sequence is not identical to or enclosed by the restriction enzyme recognition sequence, and wherein the DNA methylase recognition sequence does not overlap with the sequence that would form the overhang end sequence generated by the restriction enzyme. The invention further relates to asscociated methods and kits.

title: STRAINS OF ESCHERICHIA COLI MODIFIED BY GENETIC ENGINEER OF METABOLIC PATHWAYS FOR THE RESVERATROL BIOSYNTHESIS AND ITS USE IN A NEW PROCESS IN CO-CULTIVATION.
text: STRAINS OF ESCHERICHIA COLI MODIFIED BY GENETIC ENGINEER OF METABOLIC PATHWAYS FOR THE RESVERATROL BIOSYNTHESIS AND ITS USE IN A NEW PROCESS IN CO-CULTIVATION.. The present invention relates to new strains of Escherichia coli derived from the W3110 strain, named W/Sc4CL-AhSTS and W/Sc4CL-VvSTS, constructed by engineering metabolic pathways for resveratrol biosynthesis, which is an exclusive compound of some plants with extensive pharmaceutical potential. Also, it refers to an optimized method of resveratrol production based on metabolic pathway engineering strategies. Said process comprises the use of the strain of E. coli W/Sc4CL-VvSTS genetically modified to produce resveratrol from coumaric acid, which a fermentation process using minimal medium is supplemented with glycerol and coumaric acid reaching titers of up to 74.3 mg /L of resveratrol. Additionally, it comprises an innovative method for the production of resveratrol from a co-culture system from the strain W/Sc4CL-VvSTS capable of producing coumaric acid from glucose or glycerol, thereby achieving titles of up to 22.6 mg/L without adding the said acid.

title: BIOLOGICAL CARTRIDGE, BIOLOGICAL CARTRIDGE ASSEMBLY, MICROSPHERE PREPARATION DEVICE, SHELL ASSEMBLING DEVICE, BIOBRICK PREPARATION INSTRUMENT, BIOLOGICAL INK PREPARATION INSTRUMENT, AND BIOLOGICAL INK PREPARATION SYSTEM
text: BIOLOGICAL CARTRIDGE, BIOLOGICAL CARTRIDGE ASSEMBLY, MICROSPHERE PREPARATION DEVICE, SHELL ASSEMBLING DEVICE, BIOBRICK PREPARATION INSTRUMENT, BIOLOGICAL INK PREPARATION INSTRUMENT, AND BIOLOGICAL INK PREPARATION SYSTEM. A biological cartridge, a biological cartridge assembly, a microsphere preparation device, a shell assembling device, a biobrick preparation instrument, a biological ink preparation instrument, and a biological ink preparation system. A biological cartridge (131) comprises: a cartridge housing (1311), having a cavity; an isolation wall (1312), provided in the cavity, and dividing the cavity into a first accommodating cavity (1313) and a second accommodating cavity (1314) isolated from each other; a first cartridge inlet (13151) and a first cartridge outlet (13153), provided on the cartridge housing (1311), and communicated with the first accommodating cavity (1313) and fluidly isolated from the second accommodating cavity (1314); a second cartridge inlet (13152) and a second cartridge outlet (13154), provided on the cartridge housing (1311), and communicated with the second accommodating cavity (1314); and an internal flow channel (1316), located in the second accommodating cavity (1314), and communicated with the first accommodating cavity (1313) and the first cartridge outlet (13153).

title: manufacture method of infra-red rays emission bricks
text: manufacture method of infra-red rays emission bricks. PURPOSE: Provided are biobricks with good appearance and far infrared emission by mixing waste ceramic products(pottery, tiles, red bricks, etc.) with lava mortar and forming. CONSTITUTION: The manufacturing method of biobricks is as follows: crushing pottery, tiles, red bricks, pots into an effective size; mixing crushed ceramics with lava mortar emitting far infrared ray; dry forming for 2-3hrs. in a steam room; and cutting to a shape of brick.

title: METHODS OF ENGINEERING PLATELETS FOR TARGETING CIRCULATING TUMOR CELLS
text: METHODS OF ENGINEERING PLATELETS FOR TARGETING CIRCULATING TUMOR CELLS. Disclosed herein are nucleic acid constructs that can be used to build genetic circuits for producing antibodies comprising split toxins. Also disclosed herein are methods of producing platelets comprising the antibodies. The platelets produced by the methods disclosed herein can be used to target circulating tumor cells.

title: METHODS FOR IN VITRO JOINING AND COMBINATORIAL ASSEMBLY OF NUCLEIC ACID MOLECULES
text: METHODS FOR IN VITRO JOINING AND COMBINATORIAL ASSEMBLY OF NUCLEIC ACID MOLECULES. The present invention relates to methods of joining two or more double-stranded (ds) or single-stranded (ss) DNA molecules of interest in vitro, wherein the distal region of the first DNA molecule and the proximal region of the second DNA molecule of each pair share a region of sequence identity. The method allows the joining of a large number of DNA fragments, in a predetermined order and orientation, without the use of restriction enzymes. It can be used, e.g., to join synthetically produced sub-fragments of a gene or genome of interest. Kits for performing the method are also disclosed. The methods of joining DNA molecules may be used to generate combinatorial libraries useful to generate, for example, optimal protein expression through codon optimization, gene optimization, and pathway optimization.

title: Bio-field programmable gate array and bio-programmable logic array: reconfigurable chassis construction
text: Bio-field programmable gate array and bio-programmable logic array: reconfigurable chassis construction. Aspects of the invention relate to reconfigurable chassis that allow for rapid construction and optimization of biocircuits.

title: Protease K multi-copy strain construction method
text: Protease K multi-copy strain construction method. The invention discloses a protease K multi-copy strain construction method, and belongs to the technical field of biology. According to the protease K multi-copy strain construction method, protease Kmulti-copy strains is artificially constructed in vitro by utilizing a Biobrick method, and the protease K multi-copy strain construction method mainly comprises the following specific steps: S1, constructing a multi-copy vector; S2, screening multi-copy strains; and S3, carrying out multi-copy strain large-scale fermentation. A protease K multi-copy tandem expression cassette is artificially constructed in vitro by using a Biobrick method; the construction method has the advantages that the copy number of recombinant plasmids is controllable, dependence on screening of high-concentration resistant drugs is not needed, large-scale screening is also not needed, the gene dose effect is considered as the most important factor for expression of foreign protein by pichia pastoris, and the expression quantity of the multi-copy strains is many times higher than that of single-copy strains; therefore, the yield of the protease K is increased, the price is lower, and the protease K can be applied to various fields.

title: ENGINEERED BACTERIA FOR BIOSYNTHESIS OF PEPTIDE-BASED QUORUM SENSING INHIBITORS
text: ENGINEERED BACTERIA FOR BIOSYNTHESIS OF PEPTIDE-BASED QUORUM SENSING INHIBITORS. Described herein are engineered bacterial cells capable of producing an autoinducing peptide (AIP) or non-native AIP analog (including AIP inhibitors), methods for producing the AIP or non-native AIP analog, plasmids and kits. The bacterial cells are transformed with at least one plasmid into gram-positive bacterial cells expressing a mutation in the argD gene manipulating the AIP biosynthesis system via the AgrB/D to produce inhibitors of the S. aureus arg quorum sensing.

title: Water and soil conservation biobrick
text: Water and soil conservation biobrick. The utility model discloses a water and soil conservation biobrick which comprises a planting pool with a circular section, the planting pool is formed by enclosing concrete solid bricks which are integrally rectangular, and planting soil is filled in the planting pool; the lower portion of the concrete solid brick is supported on four feet through four fixing pins, limiting strips are arranged on the upper portions of the fixing pins, buckles are arranged on the limiting strips, pointed ends are arranged at the bottoms of the fixing pins, and a plurality of barbs are arranged on main rods of the fixing pins. A separation net is arranged between every two adjacent fixing pins, and the separation nets are sequentially fixed through the buckles of the limiting strips and then connected into a circle to enclose the lower portions of the concrete solid bricks to form a cavity communicated with the planting pool. The separation net has the effects of enhancing the water exchange effect and improving the water and soil conservation, due to the arrangement of the separation net, root systems can freely penetrate through the net holes, the root systems of all plants are connected to form a tight net, and the effect of enhancing the water and soil conservation is achieved.

title: PCR-BASED METHOD OF SYNTHESIZING A NUCLEIC ACID MOLECULE
text: PCR-BASED METHOD OF SYNTHESIZING A NUCLEIC ACID MOLECULE. There is provided a method of synthesizing a nucleic acid molecule and in one aspect, the method comprises assembling a full length template nucleic acid molecule by PCR in a PCR reaction mixture comprising a set of assembly oligonucleotides having a first average melting temperature and a set of outer amplification primers having a second average melting temperature that is lower than the first average melting temperature, wherein said assembling comprises subjecting the PCR reaction mixture to a first annealing temperature that is higher than the second average melting temperature and; amplifying the full length template nucleic acid molecule by PCR in the PCR reaction mixture wherein said amplifying comprises subjecting the PCR reaction mixture to a second annealing temperature that permits annealing of the outer amplification primers to the full length template nucleic acid molecule.

title: Microbial mineralization cementation biobrick production device
text: Microbial mineralization cementation biobrick production device. The invention discloses a microorganism mineralization cementation biobrick production device which comprises a forming box, a plurality of transverse partition plates divide the interior of the forming box into a plurality of cavities, the output end of an oil cylinder movably penetrates through a transverse arm and then extends downwards, and a cover plate is arranged on the extending section of the output end of the oil cylinder; a plurality of liquid discharging grooves are formed in the bottom of each cavity, a plurality of baffles are arranged on the lower surface of the cover plate, and when the oil cylinder drives the cover plate to move downwards to the bottoms of the baffles to make contact with the liquid discharging grooves, the cavities can be divided into a plurality of forming cavities; the upper end of each baffle is connected with the lower surface of the cover plate, and a discharging mechanism and a spraying mechanism are arranged between the two connecting plates. According to the invention, slag, solid construction waste particles and the like are used as raw materials, and the composite material for construction is generated after shaping and consolidation of the molding cavity, that is, the raw material particles and a two-phase solution for inducing generation of calcium carbonate are laid and sprayed in a shaping area, and traditional clay mining and brick making processes of a plurality of chemical reagents are abandoned; and the method is really friendly to the ecological environment.

title: MODIFIED ESCHERICHIA COLI STRAINS BY MEANS OF METABOLIC ENGINEERING FOR THE PRODUCTION OF (R)-3-HYDROXYBUTYRATE
text: MODIFIED ESCHERICHIA COLI STRAINS BY MEANS OF METABOLIC ENGINEERING FOR THE PRODUCTION OF (R)-3-HYDROXYBUTYRATE. The present disclosure is related to new strains of Escherichia coli and derivatives that produce (R)-3-hydroxybutyrate (R3HB) with a high performance and selectivity; said strains were genetically modified by metabolic pathway engineering to express two genes of Azotobacter vinelandii (phvA and phpbB genes) together with the overexpression of Escherichia coli (tesB gene). Also including new strains that have eliminated the pathways that compete for carbon flux for the accumulation of acetyl-CoA, and the availability of NADPH was increased by means of a Streptococcus mutans glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate dehydrogenase dependent on NADP+. Also comprises the technical solution, not obvious, to the nonspecific generation of acetic acid by thioesterase II native to Escherichia coli; requiring the generation-regeneration pathway of acetate by the phosphotransacetylase enzymes and acetate kinase, to regenerate acetyl-CoA from the acetic acid generated by TesB, in order to obtain a higher produc tion of R3HB in genetic backgrounds of Escherichia coli that allow the consumption of acetic acid. Particularly comprising fermentation processes with culture media with glucose and/or xylose, including lignocellulose hydrolysates, as a carbon source and under moderate oxygen limitation conditions, using a transformed Escherichia coli strain with the production plasmid, expressing the phbB, phbA and tesB genes in the operon. The invention also comprises novel methods of producing R3HB by overexpressing the gapNopt gene in strains of Escherichia coli with a plasmid system.

title: CONSTRUCTS AND THEIR USE IN METABOLIC PATHWAY ENGINEERING
text: CONSTRUCTS AND THEIR USE IN METABOLIC PATHWAY ENGINEERING. The present invention relates generally to methods and techniques for the expression of metabolic pathways, novel gene fusion constructs encoding multi-functional enzymatic domains, and related hybrid proteins.

title: Programmable oligonucleotide synthesis
text: Programmable oligonucleotide synthesis. The invention relates to methods for the preparation of synthetic nucleic acids, such as double-stranded nucleic acids. The methods involve the preparation of a multiplicity of different nucleic acid fragments by solid-phase synthesis, and the joining together of at least two of the multiplicity of the nucleic acid fragments by binding to one another or by covalent linkage. In the methods at least some of the nucleic acid fragments have a high AT content and are used in an increased amount relative to other fragments for the joining step.

title: IMPROVED BIOTECHNOLOGICAL METHOD FOR PRODUCING GUANIDINO ACETIC ACID (GAA) BY INACTIVATION OF AN AMINO ACID EXPORTER
text: IMPROVED BIOTECHNOLOGICAL METHOD FOR PRODUCING GUANIDINO ACETIC ACID (GAA) BY INACTIVATION OF AN AMINO ACID EXPORTER. A microorganism is transformed to be capable of producing guanidinoacetic acid (GAA) having an inactivated amino acid exporter. The microorganism is used in a method for the fermentative production of GAA. Moreover, creatine is produced by a method of fermentative production.

title: A CONSTRUCT, A VECTOR AND A METHOD OF PRODUCTION OF A TARGET MOLECULE IN A CYANOBACTERIUM
text: A CONSTRUCT, A VECTOR AND A METHOD OF PRODUCTION OF A TARGET MOLECULE IN A CYANOBACTERIUM. The present disclosure relates to constructs for the integration of DNA into the genome of cyanobacteria, vectors comprising the constructs, cyanobacteria comprising the constructs or transformed by the vector and a method of transforming cyanobacteria. The current disclosure described herein employs the use of five previously unidentified neutral sites into which foreign DNA can be integrated into the chromosome of Synechocystis without deleterious effects upon the growth and viability of the host cells. It is further described that foreign DNA may be integrated at such neutral sites so that Synechocystis expresses genes, which encode proteins that enable production of commercially important chemicals. The identification of a number of neutral sites which are spread throughout the Synechocystis chromosome, enables implementation of more complex synthetic circuits i.e. multiple genes may be expressed. New integrative vectors have been produced and are also disclosed herein. The present disclosure therefore relates to constructs for the integration of DNA into the genome of cyanobacteria, vectors comprising the constructs, cyanobacteria comprising the constructs or transformed by the vector and a method of transforming cyanobacteria.

title: Microfluidic-based Gene Synthesis
text: Microfluidic-based Gene Synthesis. A method for synthesizing long DNA constructs from oligonucleotide precursors directly within a microfluidic device uses several oligonucleotides at once. A precursor mix containing at least two oligonucleotide precursors with at least partial base complementarity is introduced into an input of a microfluidic chip and at least one cycle of at least one gene synthesis protocol are applied to fabricate a DNA construct containing the sequence of at least two oligonucleotide precursors. A method for the synthesis of a modified DNA construct includes electroporating at least one oligonucleotide encoding for at least one point mutation and having homology with at least one DNA region of a target cell into the target cell and incorporating the oligonucleotide into the target cell DNA through the action of recombination protein beta or a recombination protein beta functional homolog.

title: BIO-FIELD PROGRAMMABLE GATE ARRAY AND BIO-PROGRAMMABLE LOGIC ARRAY: RECONFIGURABLE CHASSIS CONSTRUCTION
text: BIO-FIELD PROGRAMMABLE GATE ARRAY AND BIO-PROGRAMMABLE LOGIC ARRAY: RECONFIGURABLE CHASSIS CONSTRUCTION. Aspects of the invention relate to reconfigurable chassis that allow for rapid construction and optimization of biocircuits.

title: TARGETED REMODELING OF PROKARYOTIC GENOMES USING CRISPR-NICKASES
text: TARGETED REMODELING OF PROKARYOTIC GENOMES USING CRISPR-NICKASES. The present invention relates to kits and methods of modifying the prokaryotic genome a Clustered Regularly Interspaced Short Palindromic Repeats (CRISPR)-Cas system that utilized one nicking Cas nuclease and crRNAs. The kid and methods delete or replace portions of the prokaryotic genome. In some embodiments, an entire gene or multiple genes may be deleted or replaced.

title: METHOD FOR HIGH EFFICIENCY BACTERIAL IN VIVO OR IN VITRO CLONING
text: METHOD FOR HIGH EFFICIENCY BACTERIAL IN VIVO OR IN VITRO CLONING. The present invention relates to a method for producing a desired linear combined DNA molecule, linear or circular DNA plasmid using at least one genetically altered E. coli strain, wherein linear DNA fragments are converted into a linear combined DNA molecule or circular DNA plasmid. The method can be performed in vivo or in vitro, in particular as a single step method. The invention further relates to a method for cloning a nucleic acid fragment of interest, comprising the method according to the present invention, specific genetically altered E. coli strains, a kit for performing the method according to the present invention, and the use of the genetically altered E. coli strains, or the kit for producing a linear combined DNA molecule or circular DNA plasmid or cloning a nucleic acid fragment of interest.

title: Efficient activation device for aerobic microorganisms
text: Efficient activation device for aerobic microorganisms. The utility model provides a high-efficiency aerobic microorganism activating device, which is characterized in that a liquid inlet and a liquid outlet are arranged on the opposite side surfaces of a box body, slow-release biobricks are hung at the upper part of the box body at intervals, fluidized bed filler is filled in the box body, and a micro-nano bubble ion generating mechanism is arranged on the liquid outlet side in the box body and is arranged at the bottom; the aeration mechanism is arranged on the side face of the liquid inlet in the box body, and the PLC is arranged on the surface of the box body. According to the device, sewage is treated through the slow-release biobrick, the fluidized bed filler and the compound functional bacteria, on one hand, the sewage is purified, on the other hand, the content of microorganisms, growth factors and a carbon source in the sewage is increased, and further through treatment of the micro-nano bubble ion generation mechanism, the activated water is subjected to micro-nano bubble treatment, so that the sewage treatment efficiency is improved. The oxygen content in the water body is increased, and the retention time of bubbles in the water body can be prolonged, so that the sewage treatment time is prolonged, and the sewage treatment effect is improved.

title: FERMENTATIVE PRODUCTION GUANIDINOACETIC ACID (GAA) FROM SERINE USING A MICROORGANISM HAVING AN ENHANCED L-SERINE HYDROXYMETHYLTRANSFERASE ACTIVITY
text: FERMENTATIVE PRODUCTION GUANIDINOACETIC ACID (GAA) FROM SERINE USING A MICROORGANISM HAVING AN ENHANCED L-SERINE HYDROXYMETHYLTRANSFERASE ACTIVITY. The present invention provides a microorganism which has been modified to express genes encoding proteins having arginine:glycine amidinotransferase (AGAT) activity and to increase the production of glycine from L-serine by an enhanced L-serine hydroxymethyltransferase activity and a method of producing guanidinoacetic acid (GAA) through the fermentation of such microorganism as well as a method for producing creatine.

title: COMPOSITIONS AND METHODS FOR ANALYTE DETECTION
text: COMPOSITIONS AND METHODS FOR ANALYTE DETECTION. The present disclosure provides compositions and methods related to the detection of an analyte-of-interest. In particular, the present disclosure provides compositions and methods related to the detection and/or quantification of an analyte-of-interest using a signal detection component in combination with a signal amplification component. By combining these components in a modular format, cell-free synthetic gene circuits can be generated or improved to address a specific biological or biomedical diagnostic need.

title: Synthetic gene network system with specificity and sensitivity for treating malignant tumors
text: Synthetic gene network system with specificity and sensitivity for treating malignant tumors. The invention belongs to the technical field of gene therapy, and particularly relates to a synthetic gene network system with specificity and sensitivity for treating malignant tumors, the gene network system can treat various cancers such as ovarian cancer and breast cancer, and each gene network system comprises a signal identification module, a signal output module and a signal amplification module. And the combination of two promoters in two parallel paths in the signal identification module is adjustable, so that the purpose of identifying and killing various cancers with specificity and sensitivity is realized.

title: Method of preparing nucleic acid molecules
text: Method of preparing nucleic acid molecules. Disclosed is a method of preparing nucleic acid molecules, including: providing a pool of oligonucleotides, each containing restriction enzyme digestion sequences and generic flanking sequences; cleaving the restriction enzyme digestion sequence portions to provide a pool of mixtures comprising the oligonucleotides, each containing the generic flanking sequences at one end, and the oligonucleotides, each containing none of the generic flanking sequences at one end, and; assembling the oligonucleotides using the generic flanking sequences to randomly synthesize nucleic acid fragments.

text: METABOLIC ENGINEERING OF LIPID METABOLISM BY IMPROVING FATTY ACID BINDING AND TRANSPORT. CHI like fatty acid binding proteins and genes, recombinant cells and organisms, methods of metabolic pathway engineering to improve lipid production in cells, Crystal structures of CHI like fatty acid binding proteins, methods of engineering CHI like fatty acid binding proteins and systems thereof are provided.

title: Directed evolution of synthetic gene cluster
text: Directed evolution of synthetic gene cluster. The invention relates to methods and products for generating diverse libraries of genetic material. The products include libraries and constructed nucleic acids as well as kits and databases and systems thereof.

title: Combined water and soil conservation biobrick
text: Combined water and soil conservation biobrick. The utility model discloses a combined type water and soil conservation biobrick which comprises a main box body, a control mechanism is arranged at the front end of the main box body, the control mechanism comprises a fixed plate fixedly arranged at the front end of the main box body, and a moving groove is formed in the front end of the fixed plate. A limiting frame is fixedly arranged on the side, close to the moving groove, of the front end of the fixing plate, a connecting frame is fixedly arranged on the side, close to the limiting frame, of the front end of the fixing plate, a moving block is slidably arranged on the inner side of the moving groove, a control frame is fixedly arranged at the front end of the moving block, and a supporting rod is fixedly arranged at the upper end of the control frame. According to the combined type water and soil conservation biobrick, the threaded shaft is rotated by rotating the hand wheel, so that the control frame moves along the threaded shaft, the control frame drives the gear plate to move, the gear plate drives the gear shaft, and meanwhile, the gear shaft drives the box cover to rotate, so that the purpose of convenient use is achieved.

title: Floating island type ecological purification device
text: Floating island type ecological purification device. The utility model discloses a floating island type ecological purification device, which is characterized in that a frame is provided with an inflatable bag, at least one side surface of the frame is provided with a driving propeller mechanism, and the top of the frame is provided with an inflation and deflation mechanism connected with an inflatable bag pipeline; the bin is arranged in the frame, partition plates are arranged on two sides of the bin to divide the bin into anoxic zones, the middle of the bin is an aerobic zone, the biobrick is fixed or hung in the bin, the active filler is dispersed in the bin, the aeration device is arranged at the lower end of the frame, and an aeration pipe of the aeration device is arranged at the lower end of the aerobic zone of the bin. According to the device, aerobic and anoxic treatment of sewage is realized, the purification device can be conveniently, quickly and accurately moved to an area needing purification treatment by combining an inflatable bag and a driving propeller mechanism, pollutants in a water body are treated through active filler in a stock bin, and meanwhile, nutrients are provided for microorganisms on the active filler through slow release of biological bricks, so that the treatment effect is improved. The aeration treatment can enable the active filler to roll over, so that nutrients of the biobrick can be better contacted and absorbed, and the treatment effect and speed are improved.

text: METHODS FOR IN VITRO JOINING AND COMBINATORIAL ASSEMBLY OF NUCLEIC ACID MOLECULES. The present invention relates to methods of joining two or more double-stranded (ds) or single-stranded (ss) DNA molecules of interest in vitro, wherein the distal region of the first DNA molecule and the proximal region of the second DNA molecule of each pair share a region of sequence identity. The method allows the joining of a large number of DNA fragments, in a predetermined order and orientation, without the use of restriction enzymes. It can be used, e.g., to join synthetically produced sub-fragments of a gene or genome of interest. Kits for performing the method are also disclosed. The methods of joining DNA molecules may be used to generate combinatorial libraries useful to generate, for example, optimal protein expression through codon optimization, gene optimization, and pathway optimization.

title: Synthesis of polyhydroxyalkanoates in the cytosol of yeast
text: Synthesis of polyhydroxyalkanoates in the cytosol of yeast. Transgenic yeast strains and methods for producing polyhydroxyalkanoate (PHA). A genetically engineered Pseudomonas oleovorans polyhydroxyalkanoate (PHA) polymerase was expressed in the cytosol of some wild type yeast strains, the pex5 mutants and a fox3 mutant. The composition of the PHA was influenced by the genetic background of the yeast host, the monomer specificity of the polymerase, the cellular compartment in which the polymerase was active, and the substrate supplied in the medium. The culture strategies and further metabolic pathway engineering technologies were provided. This platform provides a basis for controlling the composition and thus the properties of the synthesized PHA.

title: A CONSTRUCTION, A VECTOR AND A METHOD PRODUCTION OF A TARGET MOLECULE IN A CYANOBACTERIUM
text: A CONSTRUCTION, A VECTOR AND A METHOD PRODUCTION OF A TARGET MOLECULE IN A CYANOBACTERIUM. The present disclosure relates to constructs for the integration of DNA into the genome of cyanobacteria, vectors comprising the constructs, cyanobacteria comprising the constructs or transformed by the vector and a method of transforming cyanobacteria. The current disclosure described herein employs the use of five previously unidentified neutral sites into which foreign DNA can be integrated into the chromosome of Synechocystis without deleterious effects upon the growth and viability of the host cells. It is further described that foreign DNA may be integrated at such neutral sites so that Synechocystis expresses genes, which encode proteins that enable production of commercially important chemicals. The identification of a number of neutral sites which are spread throughout the Synechocystis chromosome, enables implementation of more complex synthetic circuits i.e. multiple genes may be expressed. New integrative vectors have been produced and are also disclosed herein. The present disclosure therefore relates to constructs for the integration of DNA into the genome of cyanobacteria, vectors comprising the constructs, cyanobacteria comprising the constructs or transformed by the vector and a method of transforming cyanobacteria.

title: Combined water and soil conservation biobrick
text: Combined water and soil conservation biobrick. The utility model discloses a combined water and soil conservation biobrick and relates to the technical field of ecological restoration. The ecological brick comprises a brick body, the brick body comprises a clay layer, a water storage layer and a vegetation layer, a water storage groove and a drainage groove are formed in the water storage layer, a screen is arranged on the upper surface of the drainage groove, a soil fixing net is arranged on the upper surface of the vegetation layer, and a plurality of sets of through holes, first clamping grooves, second clamping grooves, first clamping columns and second clamping columns are arranged on the outer surface of the brick body. Guide grooves are formed in the upper surfaces of the ecological bricks. By arranging the clay layer, the water storage layer, the drainage tank, the water storage tank, the through hole, the vegetation layer, the soil fixation net and the guide groove, water can be stored through the water storage tank, water can be drained through the drainage tank when the water amount is large, soil can be fixed through the soil fixation net, and the guide groove can guide the outer surface of the ecological brick when the water amount is large; and the effect of water drainage is achieved while water and soil conservation is carried out.

title: METHODS OF ENGINEERING PLATELETS FOR TARGETING CIRCULATING TUMOR CELLS
text: METHODS OF ENGINEERING PLATELETS FOR TARGETING CIRCULATING TUMOR CELLS. Disclosed herein are nucleic acid constructs that can be used to build genetic circuits for producing antibodies comprising split toxins. Also disclosed herein are methods of producing platelets comprising the antibodies. The platelets produced by the methods disclosed herein can be used to target circulating tumor cells.

title: ENGINEERED PHAGEMIDS
text: ENGINEERED PHAGEMIDS. Provided herein, in some embodiments, are engineered phagemids that comprise at least one synthetic genetic circuit, wherein the at least one synthetic genetic circuit comprises gene sequences encoding at least one non-lytic antimicrobial peptides (AMPs) and/or antibacterial toxin proteins, a stable origin of replication, and a bacteriophage-packaging signal, wherein the engineered phagemid does not comprise some or all gene sequences encoding bacteriophage proteins required for assembly of a bacteriophage particle.

title: STABLE SYNTHETIC PHYTASE VARIANTS
text: STABLE SYNTHETIC PHYTASE VARIANTS. The present invention relates to a synthetic phytase which is a shuffling product comprising biobricks of at least two reference phytases of Obesumbacterium and/or Hafnia (Fig. 1).

title: Targeted remodeling of prokaryotic genomes using CRISPR-nickases
text: Targeted remodeling of prokaryotic genomes using CRISPR-nickases. The present invention relates to kits and methods of modifying the prokaryotic genome a Clustered Regularly Interspaced Short Palindromic Repeats (CRISPR)-Cas system that utilized one nicking Cas nuclease and crRNAs. The kid and methods delete or replace portions of the prokaryotic genome. In some embodiments, an entire gene or multiple genes may be deleted or replaced.

title: BIOLOGICAL ANALOG-TO-DIGITAL AND DIGITAL-TO-ANALOG CONVERTER SWITCHES
text: BIOLOGICAL ANALOG-TO-DIGITAL AND DIGITAL-TO-ANALOG CONVERTER SWITCHES. Described herein are novel biological converter switches that utilize modular components, such as genetic toggle switches and single invertase memory modules (SIMMs), for converting analog inputs to digital outputs, and digital inputs to analog outputs, in cells and cellular systems. Flexibility in these biological converter switches is provided by combining individual modular components, i.e., SIMMs and genetic toggle switches, together. These biological converter switches can be combined in a variety of network topologies to create circuits that act, for example, as switchboards, and regulate the production of an output product(s) based on the combination and nature of input signals received.

title: GENETIC CIRCUIT SIMULATING ARTIFICIAL NEURAL NETWORK AND CONSTRUCTION METHOD THEREFOR
text: GENETIC CIRCUIT SIMULATING ARTIFICIAL NEURAL NETWORK AND CONSTRUCTION METHOD THEREFOR. A genetic circuit simulating an artificial neural network, comprising at least one input layer, at least two hidden layers, and at least one output layer. The input layer comprises several input layer nodes, each of the hidden layers comprises several hidden layer nodes, the output layer comprises several output layer nodes, input vectors of each input layer node comprise a promoter and an input gene, each hidden layer node comprises a hidden layer gene, and each hidden layer gene is regulated and controlled by the promoter of the gene of that layer or a gene product of the gene of the previous layer; the output node outputs a genetic circuit result; the regulation and control to the hidden layer gene and the output layer node by the product of the gene of the previous layer conforms with an activation function; the regulation and control to the input layer node by the input promoter conforms with an activation function. A genetic circuit structure is used to simulate an artificial neural network, so that complex operation is simulated at the molecular level, and complex application in the fields of biology, medicine, chemistry, electronics and the like is achieved.

title: Method and system for modeling cellular metabolism
text: Method and system for modeling cellular metabolism. This invention relates to methods and systems for in silico or bioinformatic modeling of cellular metabolism. The invention includes methods and systems for modeling cellular metabolism of an organism, comprising constructing a flux balance analysis model, and applying constraints to the flux balance analysis model, the constraints selected from the set consisting of: qualitative kinetic information constraints, qualitative regulatory information constraints, and differential DNA microarray experimental data constraints. In addition, the present invention provides for computational procedures for solving metabolic problems.

title: GENE SYNTHESIS PROCESS, GENE CHIP AND KIT
text: GENE SYNTHESIS PROCESS, GENE CHIP AND KIT. Provided is a chip process of gene synthesis, and the process comprises incorporating the whole procedure, which comprises amplifying oligonucleotides and assembling the oligonucleotides into a gene in parallel, onto a single chip. A specific mismatch endonuclease is also used in the process to establish an error repair system in gene synthesis, and the error rate is decreased to about 0.19 mismatched bases/kb. The high-throughput, high-fidelity and low-cost chip process of gene synthesis provided in the present invention can meet the requirements of gene synthesis and the optimization and screening of protein expression on a large scale at the frontier of life sciences such as synthetic biology, genomics, and systems biology.

title: Construction method of glucose metabolic pathway in shewanella strain
text: Construction method of glucose metabolic pathway in shewanella strain. The invention relates to a construction method of a glucose metabolic pathway in shewanella strains. A glucose transmembrane transport module is synthesized into a key gene for biological modification and optimization (Ompc, galp, glf and glk) and combination of the glucose transport module and an ED module to enhance glucose metabolism (zwf, pgl, edd, eda, pfkA and pykA) by means of a heterologous expression technology and genetic engineering and the like, recombinant plasmids are constructed through Biobrick and are introduced into wild shewanella MR-1, a metabolic pathway of shewanella is reconstructed, and the recombinant shewanella MR-1 is obtained. Growth metabolism can be carried out by taking glucose as a unique carbon source, and the available substrate spectrum of shewanella is widened. 13 engineering strains capable of performing glucose growth metabolism can be obtained. A practical basis is provided for the design of co-utilization of the substrate of the microbial fuel cell, and the extracellular electron transfer efficiency of the strain is favorably improved.

title: SPLIT INTEIN MEDIATED POLYMERIZATION AND PRODUCTION OF MUSSEL FOOT ADHESIVE PROTEIN MATERIALS
text: SPLIT INTEIN MEDIATED POLYMERIZATION AND PRODUCTION OF MUSSEL FOOT ADHESIVE PROTEIN MATERIALS. Mussels strongly adhere to a variety of surfaces by secreting byssal threads that contain mussel foot proteins (Mfps). Recombinant production of Mfps presents an attractive route for preparing advanced adhesive materials. Using synthetic biology strategies, Mfp5 together with Mfp5 oligomers containing two or three consecutive, covalently-linked Mfp5 sequences (named Mfp52 and Mfp53) were synthesized. Positive correlations were found between Mfp5 molecular weight and underwater adhesive properties, including adhesion force, adhesion work, protein layer thickness, and recovery distance. Dopa-modified Mfp53 displayed a high adhesion force (201±36 nN μm1) and a high adhesion work (68±21 fJ μm−1) for 200 s cure times, higher than previously reported Mfp-mimetic adhesives. Results disclosed herein highlight the power of synthetic biology in producing biocompatible and highly adhesive Mfp-based materials.

title: CIRCADIAN PROBIOTIC SYSTEMS
text: CIRCADIAN PROBIOTIC SYSTEMS. Among the various aspects of the present disclosure is the provision of a circadian probiotic system. The present teachings include compositions for a probiotic supplement to deliver a drug to a subject. The supplement can be modified to include a synthetic gene circuit to drive a therapeutic controlled gene output that can be delivered to the gut of the subject on a prescribed time basis, and the synthetic gene circuit can include at least one circadian clock gene that drives expression of at least one prescribed therapeutic or diagnostic gene target. The present teachings also include therapeutic methods to deliver at least one diagnostic and therapeutic gene target to a subject time-sensitively. The method can include modifying a probiotic supplement to include a synthetic circadian gene circuit that drives expression of the at least one gene target. In another aspect, the method can include delivering the probiotic supplement to the subject.

text: MARINE DNA POLYMERASE I. The present invention relates to DNA polymerases.
