title: Engineering DNA assembly in vivo and methods of making and using the reverse transcriptase technology
text: Engineering DNA assembly in vivo and methods of making and using the reverse transcriptase technology. Cells that can synthesize oligonucleotides in vivo to produce a nucleic acid nanostructure are described. Methods for producing oligonucleotide nanostructures for use in regulating gene expression and altering biological pathways are provided. Methods of performing multiplex automated genome editing (MAGE) are also provided.

title: Gene synthesis using pooled DNA
text: Gene synthesis using pooled DNA. A method and system for synthesizing one or more pieces of DNA with desired sequences using pooled DNA, the method comprising a hierarchical division phase and a hierarchical assembly phase. In the division phase, the sequences of one or more pieces of DNA with desired nucleic acid sequences are recursively: divided into partially overlapping resulting pieces of DNA, and the resulting pieces of DNA assigned to a plurality of pools except after the after the final division step, wherein overlapping, adjacent resulting pieces of DNA are assigned to different pools. In the assembly phase, pools of oligonucleotides are obtained corresponding to the pools of the resulting pieces of DNA, and one or more pieces of DNA with desired sequences are assembled by overlap extension in the reverse order of the hierarchical division. Embodiments of the method combine the advantages of hierarchical assembly with the advantages of pooled oligonucleotides.

title: High-throughput gene synthesis method based on digital micro-fluidic chip
text: High-throughput gene synthesis method based on digital micro-fluidic chip. The invention provides a high-throughput gene synthesis method based on a digital micro-fluidic chip, and belongs to the technical field of biological chips and gene synthesis. The chip comprises an upper polar plate and a lower polar plate, the surface of the upper polar plate is spin-coated with a hydrophobic layer and is modified with a gene array, the upper polar plate and the lower polar plate form the bipolar plate digital micro-fluidic chip, and liquid drops are controlled to move based on an EWOD technology. Synthesis of high-throughput genes by using the high-throughput gene synthesis chip based on the digital microfluidic system comprises the steps of Oligo pool capturing, cleaning, enzyme digestion reaction, gene synthesis and product amplification. The gene array chip based on the digital micro-fluidic system is designed, the gene array chip is used for improving the flux of gene synthesis, improving the synthesis efficiency and reducing the synthesis cost, non-specific adsorption of enzyme and the surface of the hydrophobic layer is avoided in the synthesis process, and the application range of the digital micro-fluidic chip in the field of gene synthesis is widened.

title: Gene synthesis primer group of macrobrachium rosenbergii male reproduction related protein and application of gene synthesis primer group
text: Gene synthesis primer group of macrobrachium rosenbergii male reproduction related protein and application of gene synthesis primer group. According to the gene synthesis primer group of the macrobrachium rosenbergii male reproduction related protein and the application of the gene synthesis primer group, the amino acid sequence of the macrobrachium rosenbergii male reproduction related protein is shown as SEQ ID NO: 18, and the nucleotide sequence of the coding gene is shown as SEQ ID NO: 17; the coding gene synthesis primer group comprises a linker primer, an intermediate sequence amplification primer, a 3 'RACE primer and a 5' RACE primer, the invention also provides a method for cloning the macrobrachium rosenbergii male reproduction related protein gene by using the coding gene synthesis primer group, and the coding gene synthesis primer group has important application value in guiding the sex control of macrobrachium rosenbergii.

title: Ligation and/or assembly of nucleic acid molecules
text: Ligation and/or assembly of nucleic acid molecules. The present invention relates to ligation and/or assembly of nucleic acid molecules. Particularly, a double-stranded target nucleic acid having overhangs of at least one nucleotide is ligated with another nucleic acid molecule capable of forming a stem-loop structure with an overhang of at least one nucleotide. The invention is suitable for tagging nucleic acid molecules. In specific embodiments, the overhangs can be produced by chemical cleavage of phosphorothioate-modified nucleic acid molecules. The invention further relates to the amplification of the ligated product, and using the resultant amplicon for assembly of multiple nucleic acid fragments.

title: DIRECTED EVOLUTION OF SYNTHETIC GENE CLUSTER
text: DIRECTED EVOLUTION OF SYNTHETIC GENE CLUSTER. The invention relates to methods and products for generating diverse libraries of genetic material. The products include libraries and constructed nucleic acids as well as kits and databases and systems thereof.

title: METHOD FOR DETECTING GENE AND CHIP FOR DETECTING GENE
text: METHOD FOR DETECTING GENE AND CHIP FOR DETECTING GENE. PROBLEM TO BE SOLVED: To provide a method for detecting a gene and a chip for detecting the gene easy to operate, allowing miniaturizing the whole apparatus and enhancing generality and further allowing detecting a target gene even when the concentration of the target gene in an assayed sample is low. SOLUTION: This method of detection comprises comparing an electrochemical response in the presence of an electrochemically active gene-binding substance capable of specifically binding to a gene in relation to a mixed solution comprising the assayed sample and a liquid for gene synthesis reaction and not subjected to the gene synthesis reaction and an electrochemical response in the presence of the gene-binding substance in relation to the generated solution obtained by carrying out the gene synthesis reaction to the mixed solution comprising the assayed sample and the liquid for gene synthesis reaction. The chip for detection comprises a part 1 for the gene synthesis reaction, a part 2 for assaying the electrochemical responses equipped with electrodes 7 and 8 and a passage 4 capable of transporting a liquid in the part for the gene synthesis reaction from the part for the gene synthesis reaction to the part for assaying.

title: METHOD OF GENE SYNTHESIS AND DRY COMPOSITION FOR GENE SYNTHESIS AND METHOD OF PRODUCING THE SAME
text: METHOD OF GENE SYNTHESIS AND DRY COMPOSITION FOR GENE SYNTHESIS AND METHOD OF PRODUCING THE SAME. PURPOSE: A method for gene synthesis and a dry composition for gene synthesis are provided to minimize mutation and to enhance synthesis rate. CONSTITUTION: A dry composition for gene synthesis contains kinase, ligase, reaction buffer, and stabilizing agent. The stabilizing agent is one or more material selected from carbohydrate including arbinose, xylose, lysoxe, ribose, xylulose, ligrose, galatose, glucose, mannose, sorbose, fructose, arabitol, xylitol, galactitol, sorbitol, mannitol, or methyl glucopyranoside. A method for preparing the composition comprises: a step of mixing oligonucleotide, kinase, ligase, and reaction buffer; a step of adding stabilizing agent; and a step of drying at -70°C to 50°C and -500mmHg to -760mmHg for 40-120 minutes.

title: PROGRAMMABLE SYNTHETIC LYSIS SYSTEM FOR CONTROLLED RELEASE OF MACROMOLECULES
text: PROGRAMMABLE SYNTHETIC LYSIS SYSTEM FOR CONTROLLED RELEASE OF MACROMOLECULES. The present invention relates to an expression system or a recombinant cell comprising one or more nucleic acid constructs, wherein the one or more nucleic acid constructs comprise: (i) a first gene encoding for one or more protein(s) that activate a quorum sensing system; and (ii) a second gene encoding for one or more lytic protein(s) capable of forming a lesion in a host cell's membrane; wherein the first gene is under control of a first promoter and the second gene is under control of a second promoter, wherein the first promoter controlling the first gene is a carbon starvation-induced promoter and the second promoter is a quorum sensing system promoter induced by the quorum sensing system activated by one or more protein(s) encoded by the first gene as well as recombinant cells hosting such an expression system. Further encompassed is the use of the expression systems and cells of the invention for the expression of gene products of interest and the respective methods of use.

title: Top-speed gene synthesis method
text: Top-speed gene synthesis method. The invention discloses a top-speed gene synthesis method, which belongs to the technical field of biosynthesis, and comprises the following steps: designing and synthesizing oligonucleotides, then assembling through an overlap extension PCR technology, then adding primers, polymerase and the like to carry out a second round of PCR, analyzing and purifying a final PCR product to obtain a purified DNA fragment, cloning the purified DNA fragment into a pUC19 vector, and carrying out high-speed gene synthesis. After escherichia coli is transformed, monoclonal colonies are selected and subjected to colony PCR, plasmids are extracted, and sequencing verification is carried out; purifying the plasmid, verifying a clone product of the purified plasmid by adopting a gene sequencing technology, comparing whether mutation, SNPs, insertion or deletion exist or not, and performing QC enzyme digestion verification to complete gene synthesis; the plasmid copy number of the improved escherichia coli adopted in the transformation and cloning process is increased; the adopted polymerase has DNA synthesis activity and small fragment connection activity under normal-temperature and high-temperature conditions, so that the gene synthesis time is shortened.

title: INTEGRATED MICROFLUIDIC DEVICE FOR GENE SYNTHESIS
text: INTEGRATED MICROFLUIDIC DEVICE FOR GENE SYNTHESIS. We report making an integrated micro-fluidic device for synthesizing double stranded DNA from short oligo-nucleotides. We demonstrate successful synthesis of a 760 bp gene segment from a pool of 39 oligonucleotides on a micro-fluidic device using both the one-step and two-step synthesis processes. We also describe purifying the double stranded DNA PCR product and filtering out sequence errors in the double stranded DNA product, all on the same device.

text: ENGINEERED PROBIOTICS FOR TREATMENT AND IMMUNITY AGAINST VIRUSES. The present invention involves an engineered probiotic bacterium comprising a heterologous nucleic acid, where the heterologous nucleic acid comprises a nucleic acid sequence encoding an anti-spike glycoprotein nanobody of a coronavirus. In one embodiment, the bacterium is Escherichia coli Nissle 1917. In another embodiment, the anti-spike glycoprotein nanobody appears on the surface of the probiotic bacteria.

title: Construction method for utilization and metabolism of xylose in shewanella
text: Construction method for utilization and metabolism of xylose in shewanella. The invention provides a construction method for utilizing metabolism of xylose in shewanella, which comprises the following steps of: constructing xylose transporter gene and xylose Weimberg metabolic pathway related gene on the same recombinant plasmid expression vector under the action of T4 ligase by adopting a biobrick construction strategy and utilizing isocaudarners SpeI and XbaI to generate the same cohesive end after treatment, and constructing the xylose transporter gene and the xylose Weimberg metabolic pathway related gene on the same recombinant plasmid expression vector under the action of T4 ligase. According to the construction method, recombinant plasmids are introduced into two host bacteria MR-1 and S114 in a transformation and conjugational transfer manner to obtain recombinant shewanella engineering strains S129 and S135. After aerobic growth fermentation is performed for 60 hours, a growth plateau stage is reached, and the biomass of S135 and S129 is 21.03 times and 12.94 times that of WT respectively; the utilization rate of the xylose in the culture medium is that the xylose consumed by S135 after 48 hours is about 47.69%, and the xylose consumed by S129 after 96 hours is about 45.03%.

title: LIGATION AND/OR ASSEMBLY OF NUCLEIC ACID MOLECULES
text: LIGATION AND/OR ASSEMBLY OF NUCLEIC ACID MOLECULES. The present invention relates to ligation and/or assembly of nucleic acid molecules. Particularly, a double-stranded target nucleic acid having overhangs of at least one nucleotide is ligated with another nucleic acid molecule capable of forming a stem-loop structure with an overhang of at least one nucleotide. The invention is suitable for tagging nucleic acid molecules. In specific embodiments, the overhangs can be produced by chemical cleavage of phosphorothioate-modified nucleic acid molecules. The invention further relates to the amplification of the ligated product, and using the resultant amplicon for assembly of multiple nucleic acid fragments.

title: GENE SYNTHESIS PROCESS, GENE CHIP AND KIT
text: GENE SYNTHESIS PROCESS, GENE CHIP AND KIT. Provided is a chip process of gene synthesis, and the process comprises incorporating the whole procedure, which comprises amplifying oligonucleotides and assembling the oligonucleotides into a gene in parallel, onto a single chip. A specific mismatch endonuclease is also used in the process to establish an error repair system in gene synthesis, and the error rate is decreased to about 0.19 mismatched bases/kb. The high-throughput, high-fidelity and low-cost chip process of gene synthesis provided in the present invention can meet the requirements of gene synthesis and the optimization and screening of protein expression on a large scale at the frontier of life sciences such as synthetic biology, genomics, and systems biology.

title: Inducible Ammonia Production from a Symbiotic Diazotroph, Methods of Creation and Uses Thereof
text: Inducible Ammonia Production from a Symbiotic Diazotroph, Methods of Creation and Uses Thereof. The present disclosure describes systems and methods capable of fixing atmospheric nitrogen into bio-available nitrogenous compounds, including ammonia. Embodiments of the present disclosure are directed to synthetic DNA constructs encoding genes to allow release of bio-available nitrogenous compounds in nitrogen fixing diazotrophic organisms. Many of these constructs encode these genes in inducible and constitutive means, such that inducible embodiments can be activated at select times. Additional embodiments are directed to genetically engineered diazotrophs utilizing these constructs to produce bio-available nitrogenous compounds. Further embodiments are directed to methods to create these constructs and organisms as well as to use these constructs and organisms.

title: DEGENERATE DROPSYNTH GENE SYNTHESIS
text: DEGENERATE DROPSYNTH GENE SYNTHESIS. Methods of multiplex gene synthesis of a plurality of variant target nucleic acids in a single reaction are provided.

title: ENGINEERED MAMMALIAN GENETIC CIRCUITS AND METHODS OF USING THE SAME
text: ENGINEERED MAMMALIAN GENETIC CIRCUITS AND METHODS OF USING THE SAME. The present disclosure relates generally to genetic engineering of cells to perform specific and complex functions. In particular, the present disclosure relates to engineered mammalian cells and methods of engineering mammalian cells, as well as novel multi-functional proteins integrating both transcriptional and post-translational control effectively linking genetic circuits with sensors for multi-input evaluations.

title: ENGINEERING DNA ASSEMBLY IN VIVO AND METHODS OF MAKING AND USING THE REVERSE TRANSCRIPTASE TECHNOLOGY
text: ENGINEERING DNA ASSEMBLY IN VIVO AND METHODS OF MAKING AND USING THE REVERSE TRANSCRIPTASE TECHNOLOGY. Cells that can synthesize oligonucleotides in vivo to produce a nucleic acid nanostmcture are described. Methods for producing oligonucleotide nano structures for use in regulating gene expression and altering biological pathways are provided. Methods of performing multiplex automated genome editing (MAGE) are also provided. Methods of synthesizing single stranded DNA (ssDNA) comprising expressing a reverse transcriptase in a cell and a functional template comprising a non-coding tRNA structure at the 3' end and a coding RNA sequence at the 5' end, wherein the non-coding tRNA structure is capable of initiating transcription of the coding RNA sequence using the reverse transcriptase to produce the ssDNA oligonucleotide in the cell are also provided.

title: GENE SYNTHESIS METHOD
text: GENE SYNTHESIS METHOD. The present invention relates to polymerase chain reaction (PCR)-based methods for the one-step synthesis of nucleic acid molecules, wherein the amplification primers used in said methods are designed such that they have two distinct melting temperatures in order to minimize the competition between polymerase cycling assembly (PCA) and polymerase chain reaction (PCR) amplification in the one-step nucleic acid synthesis and to maximize the emerging full-length amplification, as well as kits for use in such methods.

title: METHOD FOR HIGH EFFICIENCY BACTERIAL IN VIVO OR IN VITRO CLONING
text: METHOD FOR HIGH EFFICIENCY BACTERIAL IN VIVO OR IN VITRO CLONING. The present invention relates to a method for producing a desired linear combined DNA molecule, linear or circular DNA plasmid using at least one genetically altered E. coli strain, wherein linear DNA fragments are converted into a linear combined DNA molecule or circular DNA plasmid. The method can be performed in vivo or in vitro, in particular as a single step method. The invention further relates to a method for cloning a nucleic acid fragment of interest, comprising the method according to the present invention, specific genetically altered E. coli strains, a kit for performing the method according to the present invention, and the use of the genetically altered E. coli strains, or the kit for producing a linear combined DNA molecule or circular DNA plasmid or cloning a nucleic acid fragment of interest.

title: CRISPR LOGIC CIRCUITS FOR SAFER AND CONTROLLABLE GENE THERAPIES
text: CRISPR LOGIC CIRCUITS FOR SAFER AND CONTROLLABLE GENE THERAPIES. Aspects of the disclosure relate to synthetic regulatory systems composed of a multifunctional Cas nuclease, at least two guide RNAs (gRNAs) configured to target distinct nucleotide sequences, and a multilayered regulatory control element comprising ribozyme-based safety switches providing spatial and temporal control over the synthetic circuit in vivo.

title: METHOD OF ASSEMBLING MULTI-FRAGMENT DNA MOLECULE AND USE THEREOF
text: METHOD OF ASSEMBLING MULTI-FRAGMENT DNA MOLECULE AND USE THEREOF. A method of assembling multi-fragment DNA molecule, the method including: 1) constructing a vector system including an acceptor vector A1, a first donor vector B1, and a second donor vector B2, and each of the acceptor vector A1, the second donor vector B2, and the second donor vector B2 including a multiple cloning site; 2) introducing a first target sequence Lm to the first donor vector B1, m being an odd number starting from 1, and introducing a second target sequence Ln to the second donor vector B2, n being an even number starting from 2; and 3) repeating 2) to assemble multiple fragments to yield a multi-fragment DNA molecule.

title: Primer-free gene synthesis method
text: Primer-free gene synthesis method. The invention discloses a primer-free gene synthesis method and belongs to the field of artificial synthesis of genes. The invention discloses a pNew carrier plasmid, and the pNew carrier plasmid has a nucleotide sequence shown in the formula of SEQ ID NO.33. The invention also discloses a carrier bank which is constructed from pNew carrier plasmids and contains 16384(47) plasmids. The invention further discloses a primer-free gene synthesis method. The primer-free gene synthesis method comprises the following steps of 1, carrying out grouping on targeted gene DNA sequences to be synthesized according to a ratio of 82bp per fragment, 2, finding plasmids corresponding to the grouped fragments in the constructed plasmid bank, 3, carrying out enzyme digestion on the corresponding plasmids, carrying out screening by antibiotic and carrying out reconstruction to obtain a plasmid containing 82bp of the targeted gene sequences, and 4, splicing the gene fragments by a golden-gate cloning reaction to obtain a complete targeted gene. The primer-free gene synthesis method is free of primers, has a low synthesis cost, a very low mutation rate and high accuracy, can synthesize special gene sequences such as a highly repetitive sequence and Poly A, has simple processes and can realize automatic operation.

title: Method for in vitro efficient construction of multi-copy Pichia expression vector
text: Method for in vitro efficient construction of multi-copy Pichia expression vector. The present invention provides a method for in vitro efficient construction of a multi-copy Pichia expression vector. The method comprises: 1) based on a pPIC9K expression vector, constructing a vector pHBM905A; 2) constructing a recombinant vector pHBM905BDM; 3) selecting exogenous gene protein requiring being expressed; 4) constructing exogenous gene protein-containing single-copy recombinant vector pHBM905BDM-gene-1; and 5) based on a Biobrick method, constructing a new multi-copy vector pHBM905BDM-gene-n having an artificial in vitro tandem duplication expression cassette type structure. According to the present invention, based on a series of transformations on the pPIC9K expression vector, the multi-copy recombinant vector pHBM905BDM suitable for in vitro construction is constructed; construction of the exogenous gene-containing single-copy recombinant vector pHBM905BDM-gene-1 and the multi-copy recombinant vector pHBM905BDM-gene-n is achieved; and the high-copy recombinant plasmids are obtained through digestion and enzyme linkage on the basis of the copy 1 with right sequencing, such that re-sequencing is not required so as to save cost, save time, save labor, and substantially increase expression ability of exogenous gene protein.

title: PAPER-BASED SYNTHETIC GENE NETWORKS
text: PAPER-BASED SYNTHETIC GENE NETWORKS

text: CRISPR logic circuits for safer and controllable gene therapies. title: Gene synthesis difficulty evaluation method and system
text: Gene synthesis difficulty evaluation method and system. The invention provides a gene synthesis difficulty evaluation method and system and a gene synthesis automatic quotation method and system, the gene synthesis automatic quotation method is convenient for a demand side to timely obtain price details, and the communication efficiency between the demand side and a supplier is greatly improved.

title: BIOTECHNOLOGICAL PRODUCTION OF COLLAGEN PROTEINS AND BACTERIAL COLLAGEN-LIKE PROTEINS BY RECOMBINANT MICROORGANISMS
text: BIOTECHNOLOGICAL PRODUCTION OF COLLAGEN PROTEINS AND BACTERIAL COLLAGEN-LIKE PROTEINS BY RECOMBINANT MICROORGANISMS. The present invention relates to polynucleotides encoding an amino acid sequence encoding a collagen protein or a bacterial collagen-like protein, comprising an N-terminal signal sequence as well as a fermentative process for secreting bacterial collagen-like proteins in a host.

title: Gene synthesis device and gene synthesis method
text: Gene synthesis device and gene synthesis method. The invention relates to a gene synthesis device and a gene synthesis method. The gene synthesis device comprises a controller, a bearing assembly, a first air pressure adjusting assembly, an upper cover assembly and a second air pressure adjusting assembly. The bearing assembly is provided with a first bearing cavity and used for bearing a composite plate. The first air pressure adjusting assembly is electrically connected with the controller, the first air pressure adjusting assembly communicates with the first bearing cavity, and the controller is used for controlling the first air pressure adjusting assembly to adjust the air pressure in the first bearing cavity; the upper cover assembly is provided with a second bearing cavity; the upper cover assembly is arranged on the side, away from the first bearing cavity, of the composite plate. The first bearing cavity and the second bearing cavity are separated through a composite plate; the second air pressure adjusting assembly is electrically connected with the controller and communicates with the second bearing cavity, and the controller is used for controlling the second air pressure adjusting assembly to adjust the air pressure in the second bearing cavity. When the gene synthesis device and method are used for synthesizing the gene, the synthesis cost is low, and the deviation of the synthesis result is small.

title: NOVEL MICROORGANISM HAVING MINIMAL GENOME AND METHOD OF PRODUCING THE SAME
text: NOVEL MICROORGANISM HAVING MINIMAL GENOME AND METHOD OF PRODUCING THE SAME. Provided is a method of producing a novel microorganism having a minimal genome. In particular, provided are a method of producing a novel microorganism having a smaller genome size than a wild-type, the method including the step of culturing the microorganism in a medium containing LB broth with stepwise decreasing concentrations of the LB broth; and a novel microorganism having a smaller genome size than a wild-type, produced by the above method.Despite the small genome size, the novel microorganism of the present disclosure may have a high growth rate, pyruvate production capacity, and protein translation efficiency thereby being usefully applied to industry such as production of recombinant proteins or pyruvate.

title: DIRECTED EVOLUTION OF SYNTHETIC GENE CLUSTER
text: DIRECTED EVOLUTION OF SYNTHETIC GENE CLUSTER. The invention relates to methods and products for generating diverse libraries of genetic material. The products include libraries and constructed nucleic acids as well as kits and databases and systems thereof.

title: INTELLIGENT GENE SYNTHESIS AND MONOCLONAL SELECTION SYSTEM
text: INTELLIGENT GENE SYNTHESIS AND MONOCLONAL SELECTION SYSTEM. An intelligent system for gene synthesis and monoclonal picking, comprising: a core operation module for performing operations associated with gene synthesis and monoclonal picking; and a control system for controlling at least a part of operations of corresponding compartments of the core operation module. The core operation module at least includes: a liquid operation compartment for performing operations of mixing gene fragments and mixing synthetizing reagents with biological samples in the process of gene synthesis; a purification compartment for performing purification operations on nucleic acid products created after the mixing; an amplification compartment for performing amplification operations and error correction operations on fragments of the nucleic acid products and performing culturing and amplification operations on vectors; and a monoclonal picking compartment for performing monoclonal picking operations on purified and cultured nucleic acid products.

title: MODULAR CONSTRUCTION OF SYNTHETIC GENE CIRCUITS IN MAMMALIAN CELLS USING MATHEMATICAL MODELING OF CLOSED LOOP TRANSCRIPTIONAL REPRESSORS
text: MODULAR CONSTRUCTION OF SYNTHETIC GENE CIRCUITS IN MAMMALIAN CELLS USING MATHEMATICAL MODELING OF CLOSED LOOP TRANSCRIPTIONAL REPRESSORS. We hereby disclose and claim a mathematically based system for design and implementation synthetic biology genetic circuitry architecture which uses genetic expression circuits which are linked together to achieve a therapeutic or diagnostic effect. The genetic architecture that enables the reversible and tunable desired gene product output is achieved by genetic control elements upstream and downstream of a promoter region. Reversible and tunable control is achieved through the computational design and pairing of transcriptional repression elements with their cognate transcriptional promoters to enable a broad range of therapeutic and diagnostic applications.

title: Novel Genetic Tools
text: Novel Genetic Tools. An expression cassette for expression in Lactobacillus includes a promoter operatively linked to a nucleic acid encoding a protein to be expressed, the promoter being substantially identical to the PtlpA promoter.

text: MODULAR, CELL-FREE PROTEIN EXPRESSION VECTORS TO ACCELERATE BIOLOGICAL DESIGN IN CELLS. Disclosed are compositions, methods, and kits for performing cell-free protein synthesis (CFPS) and for expressing proteins in cells. Particularly disclosed are vectors comprising Golden Gate sites for cloning, methods for preparing such vectors, and the use thereof for performing CFPS and for expressing proteins in cells such as in naturally occurring or recombinant species of Clostridia, including Clostridium autoethanogenum.

title: Novel microorganism having minimal genome and method of producing the same
text: Novel microorganism having minimal genome and method of producing the same. Provided is a method of producing a novel microorganism having a minimal genome. In particular, provided are a method of producing a novel microorganism having a smaller genome size than a wild-type,the method including the step of culturing the microorganism in a medium containing LB broth with stepwise decreasing concentrations of the LB broth; and a novel microorganism having a smaller genomesize than a wild-type, produced by the above method. Despite the small genome size, the novel microorganism of the present disclosure may have a high growth rate, pyruvate production capacity, and protein translation efficiency thereby being usefully applied to industry such as production of recombinant proteins or pyruvate.

title: Programmable synthetic lysis system for controlled release of macromolecules
text: Programmable synthetic lysis system for controlled release of macromolecules. The present invention relates to an expression system or a recombinant cell comprising one or more nucleic acid constructs, wherein the one or more nucleic acid constructs comprise: (i) a first gene encoding for one or more protein(s) that activate a quorum sensing system; and (ii) a second gene encoding for one or more lytic protein(s) capable of forming a lesion in a host cell's membrane; wherein the first gene is under control of a first promoter and the second gene is under control of a second promoter, wherein the first promoter controlling the first gene is a carbon starvation-induced promoter and the second promoter is a quorum sensing system promoter induced by the quorum sensing system activated by one or more protein(s) encoded by the first gene as well as recombinant cells hosting such an expression system. Further encompassed is the use of the expression systems and cells of the invention for the expression of gene products of interest and the respective methods of use.

title: METHODS AND COMPOSITIONS FOR THE PRODUCTION OF GUIDE RNA
text: METHODS AND COMPOSITIONS FOR THE PRODUCTION OF GUIDE RNA. Various aspects and embodiments of the present disclosure relate to methods and compositions that combine multiple mammalian RNA regulatory strategies, including RNA triple helix structures, introns, microRNAs, and ribozymes with Cas-based CRISPR trascription factors and ribonuclease -based RNA processing in human cells. The methods and compositions of the present disclosure, in some embodiments, enable multiplexed production of proteins and multiple guide RNAs from a single compact RNA-polymerase-II-expressed transcript for efficient modulation of synthetic constructs and endogenous human promoters.

title: Solar river microorganism activation and domestication system
text: Solar river microorganism activation and domestication system. The utility model provides a solar river microorganism activation and domestication system which comprises a primary filter box body, a secondary filter box body and a plurality of water pumps, river water is pumped from a river by the water pumps, sequentially passes through the primary filter box body and the secondary filter box body and then is sent back to the river by the water pumps; the first-stage filtering box body comprises a first box body, a plurality of biobrick wall bodies arranged in the first box body at intervals and a biological rope; original microorganisms are activated while nitrogen and phosphorus contents in river water are circularly reduced, and the microorganisms can survive on a large scale after returning to the river; in order to make the biological rope neat and easy to replace, the upper end and the lower end of the biological rope are fixed on the fixing frame respectively, the fixing frame is a frame strip with a plurality of mutually parallel caulking grooves formed in the upper end face, and the caulking grooves are mainly used for fixing the head end or the tail end of the biological rope and preventing the biological rope from changing due to water flow impact.

title: Split intein mediated polymerization and production of mussel foot adhesive protein materials
text: Split intein mediated polymerization and production of mussel foot adhesive protein materials. Mussels strongly adhere to a variety of surfaces by secreting byssal threads that contain mussel foot proteins (Mfps). Recombinant production of Mfps presents an attractive route for preparing advanced adhesive materials. Using synthetic biology strategies, Mfp5 together with Mfp5 oligomers containing two or three consecutive, covalently-linked Mfp5 sequences (named Mfp52 and Mfp53) were synthesized. Positive correlations were found between Mfp5 molecular weight and underwater adhesive properties, including adhesion force, adhesion work, protein layer thickness, and recovery distance. Dopa-modified Mfp53 displayed a high adhesion force (201±36 nN μm−1) and a high adhesion work (68±21 fJ μm−1) for 200 s cure times, higher than previously reported Mfp-mimetic adhesives. Results disclosed herein highlight the power of synthetic biology in producing biocompatible and highly adhesive Mfp-based materials.

title: METHODS AND COMPOSITIONS FOR THE PRODUCTION OF GUIDE RNA

title: Electrowetting chip for high-throughput gene synthesis and sample adding card box thereof
text: Electrowetting chip for high-throughput gene synthesis and sample adding card box thereof. The invention belongs to the technical field of digital micro-fluidic chips, and provides an electrowetting chip for high-throughput gene synthesis and a sample adding card box thereof.The electrowetting chip comprises ITO glass, a sample adding device and the electrowetting chip for high-throughput gene synthesis, the bottom of the ITO glass is fixedly connected with a dielectric layer through a flow cavity, the sample adding device is arranged at the top of the ITO glass, and the sample adding device is arranged at the bottom of the ITO glass; the sample adding device is provided with an operation hole group, the ITO glass is provided with a plurality of sample outlet holes, and the operation hole group is communicated with the flowing chamber through the plurality of sample outlet holes. The electrowetting chip for high-throughput gene synthesis comprises a PCB (Printed Circuit Board) substrate, wherein a conductive component is arranged in the PCB substrate; the chip substrate is arranged at the top of the PCB substrate, a plurality of electrode layers are arranged at the top of the chip substrate, and the plurality of electrode layers are electrically connected with the conductive assembly; and the dielectric layer is laid above the plurality of electrode layers. The synthesis precision and the synthesis flux of gene synthesis can be improved, and meanwhile, the synthesis cost is reduced.

title: Method for producing polymers
text: Method for producing polymers

title: IMPROVED BIOTECHNOLOGICAL PROCESS TO PRODUCE GUANIDINOACETIC ACID (GAA) BY TARGETED INTRODUCTION OR BY INCREASING THE ACTIVITY OF A TRANSMEMBRANE EXPORTER PROTEIN
text: IMPROVED BIOTECHNOLOGICAL PROCESS TO PRODUCE GUANIDINOACETIC ACID (GAA) BY TARGETED INTRODUCTION OR BY INCREASING THE ACTIVITY OF A TRANSMEMBRANE EXPORTER PROTEIN. The present invention concerns a microorganism comprising at least one gene coding for a protein having the function of a L-arginine:glycine amidinotransferase and having an increased activity of a transmembrane exporter protein and to a method for the fermentative production of guanidino acetic acid (GAA) as well as to a method for the fermentative production of creatine using such microorganism.

title: Industrialized gene synthesis method
text: Industrialized gene synthesis method. The invention discloses an industrialized gene synthesis method. The industrialized gene synthesis method comprises the following steps of (1) analyzing and optimizing a DNA sequence for synthesis to obtain a target DNA sequence; (2) designing an oligodeoxynucleotide chain for extension according to the target DNA sequence; (3) synthesizing and purifying the oligodeoxynucleotide chain; (4) splicing the oligodeoxynucleotide chain to form a DNA fragment; (5) cloning the DNA fragment to a vector to obtain a recombinant plasmid; (6) carrying out sequencing analysis of the recombinant plasmid; and(7) carrying out a quality validation process on the recombinant plasmid with a correct sequence. The industrialized gene synthesis method has the characteristics of high success rate, high throughput, high speed, low cost, simple design, wide application scope and standardized operation. Therefore, the industrialized gene synthesis method has feasibility of industrial scale popularization, and is beneficial for reduction of costs, shortening of a synthesis cycle and improvement of synthesis quality.

title: MODULAR, CELL-FREE PROTEIN EXPRESSION VECTORS TO ACCELERATE BIOLOGICAL DESIGN IN CELLS

title: Method and system for modeling cellular metabolism
text: Method and system for modeling cellular metabolism. This invention relates to methods and systems for in silico or bioinformatic modeling of cellular metabolism. The invention includes methods and systems for modeling cellular metabolism of an organism, comprising constructing a flux balance analysis model, and applying constraints to the flux balance analysis model, the constraints selected from the set consisting of: qualitative kinetic information constraints, qualitative regulatory information constraints, and differential DNA microarray experimental data constraints. In addition, the present invention provides for computational procedures for solving metabolic problems.

title: A DNA Assembly Mix And Method Of Uses Thereof
text: A DNA Assembly Mix And Method Of Uses Thereof. Disclosed is a DNA assembly mix, comprising a 3′-5′ exonuclease enzyme which is XthA; and a buffer. Also disclosed is a DNA assembly mix, comprising a polymerase and ligase free composition comprising a 3′-5′ exonuclease enzyme; and a buffer. Also disclosed is a method of assembling a plurality of DNA fragments, comprising: (a) mixing the plurality of DNA fragments with the DNA assembly mix as disclosed herein; and (b) incubating the mixture from step (a) at a temperature for a period of time suitable for assembling the plurality of DNA fragments. Further disclosed is use of the DNA assembly mix as disclosed herein in high-throughput DNA assembly, wherein the DNA assembly mix is used in a microfluidic platform to assemble DNA.

title: GENE SYNTHESIS USING POOLED DNA
text: GENE SYNTHESIS USING POOLED DNA. A method and system for synthesizing one or more pieces of DNA with desired sequences using pooled DNA, the method comprising a hierarchical division phase and a hierarchical assembly phase. In the division phase, the sequences of one or more pieces of DNA with desired nucleic acid sequences are recursively: divided into partially overlapping resulting pieces of DNA, and the resulting pieces of DNA assigned to a plurality of pools except after the after the final division step, wherein overlapping, adjacent resulting pieces of DNA are assigned to different pools. In the assembly phase, pools of oligonucleotides are obtained corresponding to the pools of the resulting pieces of DNA, and one or more pieces of DNA with desired sequences are assembled by overlap extension in the reverse order of the hierarchical division. Embodiments of the method combine the advantages of hierarchical assembly with the advantages of pooled oligonucleotides.

title: GENE SYNTHESIS METHOD
text: GENE SYNTHESIS METHOD. The present invention relates to polymerase chain reaction (PCR)-based methods for the one-step synthesis of nucleic acid molecules, wherein the amplification primers used in said methods are designed such that they have two distinct melting temperatures in order to minimize the competition between polymerase cycling assembly (PCA) and polymerase chain reaction (PCR) amplification in the one-step nucleic acid synthesis and to maximize the emerging full-length amplification, as well as kits for use in such methods.

title: METHOD OF GENE SYNTHESIS AND DRY COMPOSITION FOR GENE SYNTHESIS AND METHOD OF PRODUCING THE SAME
text: METHOD OF GENE SYNTHESIS AND DRY COMPOSITION FOR GENE SYNTHESIS AND METHOD OF PRODUCING THE SAME. PURPOSE: A gene synthesis method is provided to minimize mutant of gene synthesis and enhance the rate of synthesis. CONSTITUTION: A gene synthesis method comprises: a step of adding a kinase and ligase into an oligonucleotide for gene synthesis; and a step of performing kination and LCR (ligase chain reaction) at the same time. A composition for gene synthesis comprises a kinase, ligase, reaction buffer and stabilizing agent. The stabilizing agent is selected among carbonhydrate.

title: GENE CIRCUIT SIMULATING ARTIFICIAL NEURAL NETWORK AND CONSTRUCTION METHOD THEREFOR
text: GENE CIRCUIT SIMULATING ARTIFICIAL NEURAL NETWORK AND CONSTRUCTION METHOD THEREFOR. A genetic circuit simulating an artificial neural network, comprising at least one input layer, at least two hidden layers, and at least one output layer. The input layer comprises several input layer nodes, each of the hidden layers comprises several hidden layer nodes, the output layer comprises several output layer nodes, input vectors of each input layer node comprise a promoter and an input gene, each hidden layer node comprises a hidden layer gene, and each hidden layer gene is regulated and controlled by the promoter of the gene of that layer or a gene product of the gene of the previous layer; the output node outputs a genetic circuit result; the regulation and control to the hidden layer gene and the output layer node by the product of the gene of the previous layer conforms with an activation function; the regulation and control to the input layer node by the input promoter conforms with an activation function. A genetic circuit structure is used to simulate an artificial neural network, so that complex operation is simulated at the molecular level, and complex application in the fields of biology, medicine, chemistry, electronics and the like is achieved.

title: INDUCIBLE AMMONIA PRODUCTION FROM A SYMBIOTIC DIAZOTROPH, METHODS OF CREATION AND USES THEREOF
text: INDUCIBLE AMMONIA PRODUCTION FROM A SYMBIOTIC DIAZOTROPH, METHODS OF CREATION AND USES THEREOF. The present disclosure describes systems and methods capable of fixing atmospheric nitrogen into bio-available nitrogenous compounds, including ammonia. Embodiments of the present disclosure are directed to synthetic DNA constructs encoding genes to allow release of bio-available nitrogenous compounds in nitrogen fixing diazotrophic organisms. Many of these constructs encode these genes in inducible and constitutive means, such that inducible embodiments can be activated at select times. Additional embodiments are directed to genetically engineered diazotrophs utilizing these constructs to produce bio-available nitrogenous compounds. Further embodiments are directed to methods to create these constructs and organisms as well as to use these constructs and organisms.

title: Kit for constructing synthetic gene circuits by utilizing modularization of TALE transcriptional suppressors in mammal animals
text: Kit for constructing synthetic gene circuits by utilizing modularization of TALE transcriptional suppressors in mammal animals. The invention aims at providing a kit for constructing synthetic gene circuits by utilizing modularization of TALE transcriptional suppressors in mammal animals. The invention protects the kit for realizing adjustable type expression of two types of proteins. The kit comprises a recombinant carrier A with an expression box A, a recombinant carrier B with an expression box B and a recombinant carrier C with an expression box C, wherein the expression box A comprises a coding sequence of a feedback element, a stater A, protein A and coding gene of TALER protein A which are connected by self-cleavable polypeptide, and a target sequence A (including a target sequence of shRNA1); the expression box B comprises a coding sequence of a feedback element, a stater B, protein B and coding gene of TALER protein B which are connected by self-cleavable polypeptide, and a target sequence B (including a target sequence of shRNA2); and the expression box C comprises coding sequences forming the stater and an activating element. The kit provided by the invention has the advantages that the recombinant carrier A, the recombinant carrier B and the recombinant carrier C are introduced into host cells, and the expression of the protein A and the protein B is regulated by adding the shRNA1 or the shRNA2.

title: INGESTIBLE AND MODULAR CAPSULE FOR SAMPLE COLLECTION, MONITORING, AND/OR DATA DETECTION
text: INGESTIBLE AND MODULAR CAPSULE FOR SAMPLE COLLECTION, MONITORING, AND/OR DATA DETECTION. The present disclosure provides for a modular ingestible capsule that may be biodegradable. In one example, the ingestible capsule includes a selectively expandable material and a genetic circuit positioned within the selectively expandable material. The selective expansion of the selectively expandable material exposes the genetic circuit to bodily molecules or fluids at a predicable location within the gastrointestinal tract. The genetic circuit can then detect characteristics and relay those characteristics to a device or be retrieved and analyzed.

title: METHOD AND SYSTEM FOR MODELING CELLULAR METABOLISM
text: METHOD AND SYSTEM FOR MODELING CELLULAR METABOLISM. This invention relates to methods and systems for in silico or bioinformatic modeling of cellular metabolism. The invention includes methods and systems for modeling cellular metabolism of an organism, comprising constructing a flux balance analysis model, and applying constraints to the flux balance analysis model, the constraints selected from the set consisting of: qualitative kinetic information constraints, qualitative regulatory information constraints, and differential DNA microarray experimental data constraints. In addition, the present invention provides for computational procedures for solving metabolic problems.

title: DIRECTED EVOLUTION OF SYNTHETIC GENE CLUSTER
text: DIRECTED EVOLUTION OF SYNTHETIC GENE CLUSTER. The invention relates to methods and products for generating diverse libraries of genetic material. The products include libraries and constructed nucleic acids as well as kits and databases and systems thereof.

title: A HIGH-THROUGHPUT AUTOMATED GENE SYNTHESIS DEVICE BASED ON CLUSTER ARRAY
text: A HIGH-THROUGHPUT AUTOMATED GENE SYNTHESIS DEVICE BASED ON CLUSTER ARRAY. A high-throughput automated gene synthesis device based on a cluster array includes a substrate and a microwell plate; the substrate is provided with a plurality of clusters of micropores; the inner wall surface of the micropores is chemically modified as a solid phase carrier for nucleic acid synthesis, or the micropore is filled with solid phase carriers; the clusters of micropores are arranged in a cluster array and each cluster of micropores has the same size and corresponding position as each well on the microwell plate. When using the device to synthesize oligonucleotides, by automatically recovering the synthesized oligonucleotides into a standard SBS plate of the corresponding size under the device, the oligonucleotide pool for each gene is formed. The yield of oligonucleotides is in picomole level, which is used for subsequent polymerase-mediated gene assembly (PCA) or ligase-mediated gene assembly (LCR) without amplification.

title: Whole gene synthesis method
text: Whole gene synthesis method. The invention relates to a whole gene synthesis method. The whole gene synthesis method is named an STRP (single tube reverse PCR-based gene synthesis, STRP) method. The method can be completed withinone day only through a one-round PCR reaction, DpnI enzyme digestion and Escherichia coli transformation. Oligonucleotides covering a target gene are synthesized firstly, the ends of the adjacent oligonucleotides overlap each other, and the outermost oligonucleotide is homologous to sequences at two sides of the insertion site of a plasmid vector. All the oligonucleotides are mixed with the plasmid vector, the target gene is synthesized in the one-round PCR reaction, and the target gene is integrated into the plasmid vector. The oligonucleotides are purified by polyacrylamide gel electrophoresis (SDS-PAGE), and PCR amplification using high-fidelity DNA polymerase can reduce mutations in the synthetic gene. The method can be used to synthesize 1638 bp DNA. Experiments prove that the methodis a simple and effective whole gene synthesis tool, and is suitable for high-throughput gene synthesis and vector construction.

title: METHOD FOR THE FERMENTATIVE PRODUCTION OF GUANIDINOACETIC ACID
text: METHOD FOR THE FERMENTATIVE PRODUCTION OF GUANIDINOACETIC ACID

title: Multi-angle overturning reaction bin device for gene synthesis
text: Multi-angle overturning reaction bin device for gene synthesis. The invention discloses a multi-angle overturning reaction bin device for gene synthesis. The multi-angle overturning reaction bin device for gene synthesis comprises a first sleeve and a mounting seat, wherein the mounting seat is rotatably mounted at one end of the top of a loading box; the first sleeve is rotatably mounted at one end of the mounting seat; a driving gear is mounted outside the first sleeve in the circumferential direction; a plurality of bearing rods are rotatably mounted outside the first sleeve, at one end of the mounting seat; a driven gear is mounted outside each bearingrod in the circumferential direction; and the driven gears are in meshing connection with the driving gear. Before the multi-angle overturning reaction bin device for gene synthesis is used, the number of annular blocks in each clump weight is adjusted, so that the weights of the clump weights are different, the stress of the mounting seat in all directions can be imbalanced through the rotationof the clump weights of different weights, and during rotation, a reaction bin can be swayed, and can be overturned to different angles; and besides, rotating rods can drive the mounting seat to rotate, so that the overturning angle of the reaction bin can be changed, the angle changing range is extended, and the multi-angle overturning reaction bin device for gene synthesis can be adapted to different working requirements.

title: Directed evolution of synthetic gene cluster
text: Directed evolution of synthetic gene cluster. The invention relates to methods and products for generating diverse libraries of genetic material. The products include libraries and constructed nucleic acids as well as kits and databases and systems thereof.

title: PROGRAMMABLE SYNTHETIC LYSIS SYSTEM FOR CONTROLLED RELEASE OF MACROMOLECULES
text: PROGRAMMABLE SYNTHETIC LYSIS SYSTEM FOR CONTROLLED RELEASE OF MACROMOLECULES. The present invention relates to an expression system or a recombinant cell comprising one or more nucleic acid constructs, wherein the one or more nucleic acid constructs comprise: (i) a first gene encoding for one or more protein(s) that activate a quorum sensing system; and (ii) a second gene encoding for one or more lytic protein(s) capable of forming a lesion in a host cell's membrane; wherein the first gene is under control of a first promoter and the second gene is under control of a second promoter, wherein the first promoter controlling the first gene is a carbon starvation-induced promoter and the second promoter is a quorum sensing system promoter induced by the quorum sensing system activated by one or more protein(s) encoded by the first gene as well as recombinant cells hosting such an expression system. Further encompassed is the use of the expression systems and cells of the invention for the expression of gene products of interest and the respective methods of use.

title: GENE SYNTHESIS PROCESS, GENE CHIP AND KIT
text: GENE SYNTHESIS PROCESS, GENE CHIP AND KIT. Provided is a chip process of gene synthesis, and the process comprises incorporating the whole procedure, which comprises amplifying oligonucleotides and assembling the oligonucleotides into a gene in parallel, onto a single chip. A specific mismatch endonuclease is also used in the process to establish an error repair system in gene synthesis, and the error rate is decreased to about 0.19 mismatched bases/kb. The high-throughput, high-fidelity and low-cost chip process of gene synthesis provided in the present invention can meet the requirements of gene synthesis and the optimization and screening of protein expression on a large scale at the frontier of life sciences such as synthetic biology, genomics, and systems biology.

title: CHIP PRIMER SURFACE EXTRACTION-BASED HIGH-THROUGHPUT GENE SYNTHESIS METHOD
text: CHIP PRIMER SURFACE EXTRACTION-BASED HIGH-THROUGHPUT GENE SYNTHESIS METHOD. Provided is a chip primer surface extraction-based high-throughput gene synthesis method. The method relates to sorting primers by cluster, then synthesizing same to a fixed area of a chip, performing cutting, and then using a sampler to dissolve primers of different clusters and transferring same to a 96 pore plate for gene synthesis.

title: UNIVERSAL DNA ASSEMBLY
text: UNIVERSAL DNA ASSEMBLY. The invention relates to a nucleic acid comprising at least one methylation-protectable restriction element, the methylation-protectable restriction element comprising: (i) a type IIS restriction enzyme recognition sequence, or a partial type IIS restriction enzyme recognition sequence, that is recognised by a type IIS restriction enzyme that cleaves outside of the recognition sequence; (ii) a DNA methylase recognition sequence that is recognised and methylated by a DNA methylase, wherein the DNA methylase recognition sequence is identical to, or is encompassed within, the type IIS restriction recognition sequence, such that methylation of the nucleic acid by the DNA methylase methylates the type IIS restriction enzyme recognition sequence and protects the nucleic acid from cleavage by the type IIS restriction enzyme; and (iii) a recognition sequence for a sequence-specific DNA-binding protein, wherein the recognition sequence is positioned such that the binding of the sequence-specific DNA-binding protein overlaps with the DNA methylase recognition sequence such that binding of the sequence-specific DNA-binding protein is capable of preventing methylation of the type IIS restriction enzyme recognition sequence by the DNA methylase such that it is not protected from cleavage by the type IIS restriction enzyme. The invention further relates to associated methods of nucleic acid assembly.

title: Fluid control device for gene synthesis and operation method thereof
text: Fluid control device for gene synthesis and operation method thereof. The invention discloses a fluid control device for gene synthesis. The fluid control device comprises a supporting mechanism, a liquid inlet mechanism and a control mechanism, wherein the supporting mechanism comprises a supporting platform, a first water pump motor, a second water pump motor, a connecting pipe, a vertical fixed plate, an L-shaped fixed bracket and a current divider box; the liquid inlet mechanism comprises a pressure lever, a supporting baffle, a fixed rod, a bleeding pipe, a pressure meter, a first liquid inlet pipe, a liquid inlet valve and a second liquid inlet pipe; the control mechanism comprises a fixed platform, flow meters, a rotary plug, a liquid discharge pipe, a controller and a liquid discharge head; and under the action of the rotary plug and the controller,the liquid discharge capacity is controlled, and liquid is discharged from the liquid discharge head finally. The technical problems that an existing fluid control device for gene synthesis cannot premix fluid while implementing the control function, has the single function and cannot realize simultaneous mixing and control are solved.

title: METHODS OF USING MICROFLUIDIC POSITIONAL ENCODING DEVICES
text: METHODS OF USING MICROFLUIDIC POSITIONAL ENCODING DEVICES. Embodiments relate to methods and compositions useful for routing and tracking multiple mobile units within a microfluidic device. Mobile units may be routed through a plurality of chemical environments, and the mobile units may be tracked to determine the path and/or environments that the mobile units have routed through. Mobile units may be routed in accordance with a predetermined algorithm. Mobile units may be routed through microfluidic devices in ordered flow. Mobile units routed through the microfluidic device can be used to perform various chemical reactions uniquely associated to the units, including without limitation peptide synthesis, enzymatic gene synthesis and gene assembly.

title: Method for improving long gene synthesis efficiency
text: Method for improving long gene synthesis efficiency. The invention discloses a method for improving long gene synthesis efficiency, and belongs to the technical field of gene synthesis, the method comprises the following steps: 1, gene segmentation; 2, synthesizing a 2-3kb intermediate fragment gene; 3, performing full-length synthesis of the 10kb gene; the rapid and efficient synthesis of the 10k gene is realized. A CmR gene segment is added during multi-segment recombination, a double-resistant plate is used for screening, assembled segments including CI gene segments cannot grow on the double-resistant plate, carriers which are not subjected to enzyme digestion cannot grow on the double-resistant plate, background pollution from the carriers and insert segment templates is discharged, and the success rate of assembling and screening is increased. In addition, the gene synthesis process is improved, in the construction process of an intermediate fragment, phi29 DNA polymerase is used for amplifying and recombining a single colony, sequencing analysis is carried out after purification, the single colony serves as a template for next-round assembly, escherichia coli culture is not needed, and time and cost are saved.

title: METHODS OF USING MICROFLUIDIC POSITIONAL ENCODING DEVICES
text: METHODS OF USING MICROFLUIDIC POSITIONAL ENCODING DEVICES. Embodiments relate to methods and compositions useful for routing and tracking multiple mobile units within a microfluidic device. Mobile units may be routed through a plurality of chemical environments, and the mobile units may be tracked to determine the path and/or environments that the mobile units have routed through. Mobile units may be routed in accordance with a predetermined algorithm. Mobile units may be routed through microfluidic devices in ordered flow. Mobile units routed through the microfluidic device can be used to perform various chemical reactions uniquely associated to the units, including without limitation peptide synthesis, enzymatic gene synthesis and gene assembly.

title: Multifunctional integrated device for high-throughput gene synthesis and synthesizer
text: Multifunctional integrated device for high-throughput gene synthesis and synthesizer. The invention relates to the technical field of DNA synthesis, in particular to a multifunctional integrated device for high-throughput gene synthesis and a synthesizer. The integrated device comprises a reaction pore plate, a preservation pore plate, a liquid beating mechanism, a moving module, a reaction cover plate and a multi-axis mechanical arm, a liquid discharging module, a cover plate clamping module and a preservation liquid adding module are arranged at the output end of the multi-axis mechanical arm, and efficient and accurate reagent injection and removal and reagent rod preservation operation are achieved through linkage of all the devices. Through the structural design, the use of protective gases such as argon is canceled, the cost is reduced, meanwhile, normal operation of DNA synthesis can be ensured, a round of DNA synthesis can be quickly and efficiently performed in combination with the use of the liquid discharge module, and the efficiency, precision and stability in the high-throughput gene synthesis process are remarkably improved.

title: Method of assembling multi-fragment DNA molecule and use thereof
text: Method of assembling multi-fragment DNA molecule and use thereof. A method of assembling multi-fragment DNA molecule, the method including: 1) constructing a vector system including an acceptor vector A1, a first donor vector B1, and a second donor vector B2, and each of the acceptor vector A1, the second donor vector B2, and the second donor vector B2 including a multiple cloning site; 2) introducing a first target sequence Lm to the first donor vector B1, m being an odd number starting from 1, and introducing a second target sequence Ln to the second donor vector B2, n being an even number starting from 2; and 3) repeating 2) to assemble multiple fragments to yield a multi-fragment DNA molecule.

title: Microfluidic-based gene synthesis
text: Microfluidic-based gene synthesis. A method for synthesizing long DNA constructs from oligonucleotide precursors directly within a microfluidic device uses several oligonucleotides at once. A precursor mix containing at least two oligonucleotide precursors with at least partial base complementarity is introduced into an input of a microfluidic chip and at least one cycle of at least one gene synthesis protocol is applied to fabricate a DNA construct containing the sequence of at least two oligonucleotide precursors. A method for the synthesis of a modified DNA construct includes electroporating at least one oligonucleotide encoding for at least one point mutation and having homology with at least one DNA region of a target cell into the target cell and incorporating the oligonucleotide into the target cell DNA through the action of recombination protein beta or a recombination protein beta functional homolog.

title: Method for improving sensitivity of gene synthesis technology based on PCR by means of integration host factor
text: Method for improving sensitivity of gene synthesis technology based on PCR by means of integration host factor. The invention discloses a method for improving the sensitivity of a gene synthesis technology based on PCR by means of an integration host factor. The method comprises the steps that an IHF stock solution is added to a reaction solution which contains desoxyribose nucleotides, DNA polymerase, an oligonucleotide primer and hybrid overlapped oligonucleotides serving as templates, the three steps of unwinding, annealing and extension of conventional PCR are cycled repeatedly, and therefore a target nucleic acid fragment is synthesized and amplified. The method for improving the sensitivity of the gene synthesis technology based on the PCR by means of the integration host factor can specially and efficiently synthesize and amplify the target nucleic acid sequence.

title: Biosensors in human gut organoids
text: Biosensors in human gut organoids. Ex vivo monolayer models of human interstinal epithelia that express sensors, and methods of use thereof for evaluation of the effects of test compounds on the human gut.

title: Sheet gene synthesis substrate with high throughput and low reagent dosage
text: Sheet gene synthesis substrate with high throughput and low reagent dosage. The utility model relates to a slice gene synthesis substrate with high flux and low reagent dosage, which comprises a substrate plate, the substrate plate comprises a body and a skirt edge body, a plurality of through micropores are arranged on the body, the body comprises a first surface and a second surface along the thickness direction, the skirt edge body is formed by extending the periphery of the body along the thickness direction, and the first surface and the second surface are opposite to each other. The skirt body extends to exceed the first surface and/or the second surface; the number of the solid-phase synthesis carriers corresponds to that of the micropores, and the solid-phase synthesis carriers are fixedly arranged in the micropores in a one-to-one correspondence manner. The sheet gene synthesis substrate can be fixed through the thickened skirt body structure, so that the stress of the substrate plate is reduced, and the deformation of the substrate plate is effectively prevented.

title: Method for synthesizing gene using high-depth oligonucleotide tiling
text: Method for synthesizing gene using high-depth oligonucleotide tiling. Provided is a method for stably synthesizing an error-free gene using a high-depth oligonucleotide tiling, which includes designing an oligonucleotide fragment by an over-overlapping method, synthesizing the oligonucleotide fragment using DNA microarray, retrieving error-free oligonucleotide fragments retrieved by next generation sequencing, and assembling the error-free oligonucleotide fragments.

title: Error correction method for gene synthesis
text: Error correction method for gene synthesis. The invention discloses an error correction method for gene synthesis, and belongs to the technical field of gene detection analysis, the error correction method for gene synthesis comprises the following steps: A1, annealing: taking a PCR (Polymerase Chain Reaction) product, keeping at 95 DEG C for 5 minutes, and cooling to 25 DEG C at a cooling rate of 0.1 DEG C/S; a2, incubation: taking an annealed product, adding Muts protein and a buffer solution, and incubating at 30 DEG C for 20 minutes; and A3, electrophoresis: carrying out gel electrophoresis after incubation, and carrying out PCR amplification on a target strip in a blue light gel cutting instrument. The mismatch binding protein Muts involved in the invention is an important component of a prokaryote mismatch repair system, and can identify and bind a plurality of different DNA mismatches. According to the method, experimental conditions are further optimized, so that the accuracy can reach 95%.

title: DNA BACKBONE EDITING FOR DNA DATA STORAGE
text: DNA BACKBONE EDITING FOR DNA DATA STORAGE. Methods of writing data to a DNA strand by inserting data-encoding oligos or symbols into a DNA backbone. One particular method of synthesizing a DNA strand encoding data includes cleaving a DNA backbone into multiple segments, and pasting a plurality of data-encoding oligo symbols between the multiple segments, with the terminal ends of the segments joining homologous terminal ends of the symbols, resulting in the DNA strand encoding data comprising alternating segments and symbols.

title: METHOD FOR SYNTHESIZING GENE USING HIGH-DEPTH OLIGONUCLEOTIDE TILING
text: METHOD FOR SYNTHESIZING GENE USING HIGH-DEPTH OLIGONUCLEOTIDE TILING. Provided is a method for stably synthesizing an error-free gene using a high-depth oligonucleotide tiling, which includes designing an oligonucleotide fragment by an over-overlapping method, synthesizing the oligonucleotide fragment using DNA microarray, retrieving error-free oligonucleotide fragments retrieved by next generation sequencing, and assembling the error-free oligonucleotide fragments.

title: Sheep stem ubiquitin protein as well as preparation method and application thereof
text: Sheep stem ubiquitin protein as well as preparation method and application thereof. The invention discloses a preparation method of sheep stem ubiquitin protein, and belongs to the technical field of bioengineering. The method specifically comprises the following steps: S1, determining a target protein: determining a target molecule of an active component in the sheep stem element; s2, gene synthesis and cloning: synthesizing a target gene sequence by using a DNA synthesis technology, cloning the synthesized gene into an expression vector, and introducing the constructed expression vector into a proper host cell. The sheep stem ubiquitin protein can be applied to the fields of cosmetics for skin care and medical treatment. The preparation method of the sheep stem ubiquitin protein replaces a traditional extraction method through gene synthesis, active ingredients can be prevented from being extracted from human placenta amniotic membranes, and ethical and legal problems are avoided. Efficient production can be achieved, a large amount of target protein can be efficiently produced through gene synthesis and cell expression, and the production cost is reduced.

title: CRISPR-BASED SYNTHETIC GENE CIRCUITS AS NEXT GENERATION GENE THERAPY OF INNER EAR
text: CRISPR-BASED SYNTHETIC GENE CIRCUITS AS NEXT GENERATION GENE THERAPY OF INNER EAR. Aspects of the disclosure relate to synthetic regulatory systems comprising a multifunctional Cas nuclease and at least two guide RNAs (gRNAs) including a truncated gRNA and an multilayered regulatory control element. The synthetic regulatory system modulates endogenous gene expression, including transcriptional repression and transcriptional activation of one or more endogenous genes of a mammalian inner ear cell with multiple safety switches. Also provided herein are methods for modulating hearing sensitivity in damaged cells of the organ of corti.

title: Solid-phase carrier microfluidic gene synthesis system and method
text: Solid-phase carrier microfluidic gene synthesis system and method. The invention discloses a solid-phase carrier micro-fluidic gene synthesis system and method. The solid-phase carrier micro-fluidic gene synthesis system comprises a substrate, a micro-fluidic control pipeline and a micro-fluidic valve group. The inlet end of the main pipeline is provided with the input port, so that a buffer solution can be injected into the main pipeline for cleaning through the input port before various reagents or solid-phase carriers are input, and cross contamination is avoided. For various synthesis units needing to be prepared, only corresponding solid-phase carriers and synthesis reagents need to be selected to be injected into the main pipeline along respective corresponding input ports in sequence for synthesis reaction, and preparation of various synthesis units is achieved. In addition, the channels are switched through the micro-fluidic valve group, so that collection of various synthesis units is realized.

title: Method of gene synthesis and dry composition for gene synthesis and method of producing the same
text: Method of gene synthesis and dry composition for gene synthesis and method of producing the same

title: Biosensors in Human Gut Organoids
text: Biosensors in Human Gut Organoids. Ex vivo monolayer models of human interstinal epithelia that express sensors, and methods of use thereof for evaluation of the effects of test compounds on the human gut.

title: BIOTECHNOLOGICAL PRODUCTION OF COLLAGEN PROTEINS AND BACTERIAL COLLAGEN-LIKE PROTEINS BY RECOMBINANT MICROORGANISMS
text: BIOTECHNOLOGICAL PRODUCTION OF COLLAGEN PROTEINS AND BACTERIAL COLLAGEN-LIKE PROTEINS BY RECOMBINANT MICROORGANISMS. Polynucleotides with an N-terminal signal sequence encode an amino acid sequence encoding a collagen protein or a bacterial collagen-like protein. Fusion products of collagen-like protein with various N-terminal signal peptides lead to increased production of collagen-like protein and secretion in a fermentative process for secreting bacterial collagen-like proteins in a host.

title: COMPOSITIONS AND METHODS FOR ELECTRONIC CONTROL OF GENE EXPRESSION
text: COMPOSITIONS AND METHODS FOR ELECTRONIC CONTROL OF GENE EXPRESSION. Provided are electrogenetic methods, devices and systems that use redox biomolecules to carry electronic information to engineered bacterial cells in order to control transcription from a synthetic gene circuit. Method include providing electrical stimulation to one or more living cells, the cells comprising a promoter operably linked to a DNA segment, wherein the promoter is controllable by reversible redox dependent activation, whereby redox dependent activation and transcription of the DNA segment occurs. Devices can be biologic -based sensors that contain living cells that are maintained in a housing capable of sustaining the cells. The devices are configured to provide electrical stimulation to the cells, and to change the electrical stimulation in response to signals. The devices can be implantable or wearable. Also provided is a system that contains a biologic-based sensor that includes a processor running software configured to adjust the electrical stimulation based on communication with a sensing component.

title: METHODS AND COMPOSITIONS FOR REWRITABLE DIGITAL DATA STORAGE IN LIVE CELLS
text: METHODS AND COMPOSITIONS FOR REWRITABLE DIGITAL DATA STORAGE IN LIVE CELLS. A rewriteable recombinase addressable data (RAD) module that reliably stores digital information within a chromosome is provided. The disclosed RAD modules use serine integrase and excisionase functions to invert and restore specific DNA sequences. The RAD memory element is capable of passive information storage in the absence of heterologous gene expression over multiple generations, and can be switched repeatedly without performance degradation to support combinatorial data storage.

title: Methods and Compositions for Rapid Assembly of Genetic Modules
text: Methods and Compositions for Rapid Assembly of Genetic Modules. Provided herein are methods and compositions for rapid assembly of genetic modules, as well as seamless transition from in vitro to in vivo testing of genetic constructs.

title: HIGH-THROUGHPUT AUTOMATED GENE SYNTHESIS APPARATUS BASED ON CLUSTER ARRAY
text: HIGH-THROUGHPUT AUTOMATED GENE SYNTHESIS APPARATUS BASED ON CLUSTER ARRAY. A high-throughput automated gene synthesis apparatus based on a cluster array. The high-throughput gene synthesis apparatus comprises a substrate and a microwell plate, wherein the substrate is provided with several clusters of microwells (1); a sidewall surface of the microwell (1) is chemically modified and is then used as a solid-phase carrier for nucleic acid synthesis, or the microwell (1) is filled with solid-phase carriers; and the several clusters of microwells (1) are arranged in a cluster array, each cluster of microwells (1) has the same size as that of each hole (2) on the microwell plate, and the position of each cluster of microwells (1) corresponds to the position of each hole (2). When oligonucleotides are synthesized using the apparatus, the synthesized oligonucleotides are automatically recovered into a standard SBS plate (a 96-well plate, a 384-well plate, a 1536-well plate, etc.) having a corresponding size under the apparatus, thereby forming oligonucleotide pools required for each gene. The yield of the oligonucleotides is at a pmoL level, and is sufficient for direct subsequent gene assembly by means of polymerase cycling assembly (PCA) or ligase chain reaction (LCR) without amplification. The oligonucleotides which have been subjected to error correction can thus be used for full-length gene splicing, thereby realizing high-throughput automated gene synthesis.

title: BIOPOLYMER AND PROTEIN PRODUCTION USING TYPE III SECRETION SYSTEMS OF GRAM NEGATIVE BACTERIA
text: BIOPOLYMER AND PROTEIN PRODUCTION USING TYPE III SECRETION SYSTEMS OF GRAM NEGATIVE BACTERIA. Proteins, polynucleotide, expression cassette, vector and bacterium compositions for obtaining proteins of interest by expression of same in gram negative bacteria having a Type III secretion system are provided. Uses of the proteins so obtained in the manufacture of isolated proteins, silks, fibers threats, and pharmaceutical compositions are also provided.

title: IN VIVO DNA ASSEMBLY AND ANALYSIS
text: IN VIVO DNA ASSEMBLY AND ANALYSIS. Provided herein, inter alia, are methods and compositions for assembling oligonucleotide fragments in vivo. The methods bypass inefficient cloning methods and the requirement for expensive enzymes. The methods may further be used to assemble long fragments of DNA. The methods may be used to generate variant libraries and combinatorial libraries and may be used to trace biological processes. Also provided herein, inter alia, are methods for in vivo DNA barcoding of oligonucleotide sequences. Methods provided herein are contemplated to produce unique barcode-oligonucleotide fusion sequences for identifying and isolating the oligonucleotide sequences, e.g., from a mixture. Accordingly, also provided are methods of identifying an oligonucleotide from a mixture of oligonucleotides.

title: Gene synthesis method, device and equipment based on intelligent algorithm and medium
text: Gene synthesis method, device and equipment based on intelligent algorithm and medium. The invention provides a gene synthesis method, device and equipment based on an intelligent algorithm and a medium, and the method comprises the following steps: obtaining gene synthesis information, inputting the gene synthesis information to an algorithm model, and obtaining a primer set; dividing the DNA sequence into a preset number of oligonucleotide fragments through a greedy algorithm according to the input DNA sequence, the chemical parameters and the length range; performing an iterative algorithm on the obtained oligonucleotide fragments with the preset number to obtain a segmentation result with the minimum dissolution temperature standard deviation of each pool; performing depth-first search on the obtained segmentation results, constructing a two-dimensional array for the segmentation results of each pool, taking one oligonucleotide from each column to enable the dissolution temperature standard deviation of the obtained oligonucleotide to be minimum, and obtaining a new oligonucleotide set; and assembling the new oligonucleotide set through an input result type to obtain a primer set. And the melting temperature of the corresponding overlapping region of the designed primer is lower than 3 DEG C, so that the assembly accuracy is greatly improved, and the assembly error is reduced.

title: INTELLIGENT SYSTEM FOR GENE SYNTHESIS AND MONOCLONAL PICKING
text: INTELLIGENT SYSTEM FOR GENE SYNTHESIS AND MONOCLONAL PICKING. An intelligent system for gene synthesis and monoclonal picking, comprising: a core operation module for performing operations associated with gene synthesis and monoclonal picking; and a control system for controlling at least a part of operations of corresponding compartments of the core operation module.
