The core operation module at least includes: a liquid operation compartment for performing operations of mixing gene fragments and mixing synthetizing reagents with biological samples in the process of gene synthesis; a purification compartment for performing purification operations on nucleic acid products created after the mixing; an amplification compartment for performing amplification operations and error correction operations on fragments of the nucleic acid products and performing culturing and amplification operations on vectors; and a monoclonal picking compartment for performing monoclonal picking operations on purified and cultured nucleic acid products.

title: Chicken interferon-alpha gene by total gene synthesis and protein expression
text: Chicken interferon-alpha gene by total gene synthesis and protein expression. The invention relates to chicken interferon-alpha gene by the total gene synthesis technology and prokaryotic expression of interferon proteins, and belongs to the field of biological pharmacy. The invention comprises codon modification on a chicken interferon-alpha gene sequence and total gene synthesis of the sequence, construction of a chicken interferon-alpha gene-containing prokaryotic expression vector, conversion and induction expression of colibacillus by the chicken interferon-alpha gene-containing prokaryotic expression vector, protein extraction and denaturation and renaturation, and study on antiviral activity. The interferon obtained by experiments can resist the attack of vesicular stomatitis virus, and has the specific activity of 1.06*105U/mg. The interferon produced by the method has the advantages of simple process, high expression level and high bioactivity.

title: GENE SYNTHESIS METHOD
text: GENE SYNTHESIS METHOD

title: ONE-POT MULTIPLEX GENE SYNTHESIS
text: ONE-POT MULTIPLEX GENE SYNTHESIS. The present invention provides methods for generating a library of synthetic polynucleotides. The present invention also provides methods for generating proteins encoded by the library of synthetic polynucleotides. In addition, provided herein are methods for determining the soluble expression of said proteins. This invention is based, in part, on the discovery of a method for selecting optimal oligonucleotides in combination with performing a phosphorylation reaction, ligation reaction and PCR amplification in a single reaction vessel to produce synthetic polynucleotides in a multiplex manner.

title: MINIMAL BACTERIAL GENOME
text: MINIMAL BACTERIAL GENOME. The present invention relates, e.g., to a minimal set of protein-coding genes which provides the information required for replication of a free-living organism in a rich bacterial culture medium, wherein (1) the gene set does not comprise the 101 genes listed in Table 2; and/or wherein (2) the gene set comprises the 381 protein-coding genes listed in Table 3 and, optionally, one of more of: a set of three genes encoding ABC transporters for phosphate import (genes MG410, MG411 and MG412; or genes MG289, MG290 and MG291); the lipoprotein-encoding gene MG185 or MG260; and/or the glycerophosphoryl diester phosphodiesterase gene MG293 or MG385.

title: Gene synthesis of wild boar alpha-interferon, vector construction and method for producing outcome
text: Gene synthesis of wild boar alpha-interferon, vector construction and method for producing outcome. The invention discloses a gene synthesis of Alpha-interferon of wild boars and vector construction thereof as well as a production method of a product. A number of problems of low expression capacity, products expressed in a fusion state, products with purification tags or no fermentation technology with high density exist in the domestic recombinant strains. The invention includes a method that a codon and codon pairs, preferred by Escherichia coli, are used for synthesizing an Alpha-interferon gene of the wild boar, establishing a high-efficiency expression vector and transforming a high-efficiency expression strain as well as methods of high-density fermentation of engineering bacteria, separation and purification of inclusion bodies, the modification, the renaturation and the purification of target protein and the determination of biological activity of the expressed product. The gene synthesis, the vector construction and the production method pertain to the technical field of the production of polypeptide drugs by genetic engineering in biopharmaceuticals.

title: Inducible ammonia production from a symbiotic diazotroph, methods of creation and uses thereof
text: Inducible ammonia production from a symbiotic diazotroph, methods of creation and uses thereof. The present disclosure describes systems and methods capable of fixing atmospheric nitrogen into bio-available nitrogenous compounds, including ammonia. Embodiments of the present disclosure are directed to synthetic DNA constructs encoding genes to allow release of bio-available nitrogenous compounds in nitrogen fixing diazotrophic organisms. Many of these constructs encode these genes in inducible and constitutive means, such that inducible embodiments can be activated at select times. Additional embodiments are directed to genetically engineered diazotrophs utilizing these constructs to produce bio-available nitrogenous compounds. Further embodiments are directed to methods to create these constructs and organisms as well as to use these constructs and organisms.

title: Method, system and equipment for analyzing synthesis difficulty of gene synthesis sequence
text: Method, system and equipment for analyzing synthesis difficulty of gene synthesis sequence. A gene synthesis sequence synthesis difficulty analysis system comprises a foreground end page management unit and a background end analysis unit, the foreground end page management unit comprises an input data judgment module and a sequence difficulty analysis result output module, and the background end analysis unit comprises a synthesis difficulty analysis model module. The input data judgment module is used for judging whether data input by a user on a page meets input analysis conditions or not, if the data input by the user on the page meets the conditions, all the input data are put into an array, the array is serialized into a Json character string array, the Json character string array is sent to the synthesis difficulty analysis model module through an ajax method of Jquery, and the synthesis difficulty analysis model module is used for analyzing the synthesis difficulty. The synthesis difficulty analysis model module is used for analyzing the synthesis difficulty of the gene synthesis sequence, and the sequence difficulty analysis result output module is used for outputting a sequence difficulty analysis result of the synthesis difficulty analysis model module.

title: DNA BACKBONE EDITING FOR DNA DATA STORAGE
text: DNA BACKBONE EDITING FOR DNA DATA STORAGE. Methods of writing data to a DNA strand by inserting data-encoding oligos or symbols into a DNA backbone. One particular method of synthesizing a DNA strand encoding data includes cleaving a DNA backbone into multiple segments, and pasting a plurality of data-encoding oligo symbols between the multiple segments, with the terminal ends of the segments joining homologous terminal ends of the symbols, resulting in the DNA strand encoding data comprising alternating segments and symbols.

title: INTEGRATION OF MESA RECEPTORS AND PROMOTERS TO IMPLEMENT CUSTOMIZED CELLULAR FUNCTION
text: INTEGRATION OF MESA RECEPTORS AND PROMOTERS TO IMPLEMENT CUSTOMIZED CELLULAR FUNCTION. Disclosed are systems and methods for detecting extracellular ligands and/or inducing expression of an exogenous or endogenous gene. The disclosed systems and methods typically include and/or utilize (i) first and second exogenous extracellular sensors, and third and fourth exogenous extracellular sensors; and (ii) an expression vector comprising a target gene operably linked to a hybrid promoter sequence. The hybrid promoter sequence of the expression vector includes a minimal promoter for inducing transcription and the hybrid promoter sequence further includes interspaced transcription regulator binding sites upstream of the minimal promoter that bind two or more transcription regulators of the extracellular sensors that are released from the extracellular sensors when the extracellular sensor bind an extracellular ligand.

title: METHOD AND KIT FOR ASSEMBLY OF MULTIPLE DNA FRAGMENTS AT ROOM TEMPERATURE
text: METHOD AND KIT FOR ASSEMBLY OF MULTIPLE DNA FRAGMENTS AT ROOM TEMPERATURE. The present invention relates to recombinant DNA technology, in particular to methods for assembling two or more double stranded (ds) nucleic acid molecules with overlapping terminal sequences. In particular, the present invention relates to the use of a thermolabile DNA polymerase II derived 3′-5′ exonuclease isolated from Moritella viscoa and a thermolabile DNA polymerase I of marine origin in multi DNA assembly processes.

title: A GENE CASSETTE AND USES THEREFOR
text: A GENE CASSETTE AND USES THEREFOR. Described herein is a recombinant cell genetically modified to include a gene cassette comprising a promoter and one or more of a sequence for a recombinase followed by a terminator, the recombinase-terminator sequence being flanked by recognition sites specific for the recombinase, and the cassette being activatable by an external stimulus. Also described are a method for determining the number of times the cell has been activated by an external stimulus, and plastic or rubber or a mixture of plastics or rubber including the recombinant cell, as well as a method for determining the number of times a plastic or rubber has been recycled. [Figure 1]

title: Gene synthesis device
text: Gene synthesis device. The utility model provides a gene synthesis device which comprises a synthesis assembly, a liquid collecting box, a sealing plate and a driving assembly, the synthesis assembly comprises a synthesis seat and a plurality of synthesis columns, the plurality of synthesis columns are arranged on the synthesis seat, the bottoms of the synthesis columns are opened and located on the same plane, and the liquid collecting box is arranged on the synthesis seat. The bottom of the synthesis column is flush with the bottom of the synthesis seat or protrudes out of the bottom of the synthesis seat; the liquid collecting box is arranged on the lower side of the synthesis seat and is connected and hermetically matched with the synthesis seat; the sealing plate is arranged in the liquid collecting box, one end of the sealing plate is provided with a rotating shaft which is rotatably matched with the liquid collecting box, and the sealing plate is suitable for rotating around the rotating shaft and hermetically attached to or separated from the bottom of the composite column; the power output end of the driving assembly is connected with the sealing plate and used for driving the sealing plate to rotate around the rotating shaft. According to the gene synthesis device provided by the utility model, the problem of liquid leakage of a limited synthesis column is solved; when the liquid discharging action is carried out, the sealing plate is overturned downwards and is separated from the bottom of the synthetic column, so that liquid discharging is completed, and a reagent in the synthetic column is prevented from being polluted.

title: High-throughput automatic gene synthesis device based on cluster array
text: High-throughput automatic gene synthesis device based on cluster array. The invention discloses a high-throughput automatic gene synthesis device based on a cluster array. The high-throughput gene synthesis device comprises a substrate and a microporous plate; a plurality of clusters of micropores are formed in the substrate; the side wall surfaces of the micropores are chemically modified to serve as solid-phase carriers for nucleic acid synthesis, or the micropores are filled with the solid-phase carriers; the plurality of clusters of micropores are arranged in a cluster array, and each cluster of micropores is consistent with each hole in the microporous plate in size and corresponds to each hole in position. When the device is used for synthesizing oligonucleotide, the synthesized oligonucleotide is automatically recovered into standard SBS plates (96-pore plates, 384-pore plates, 1536-pore plates and the like) with corresponding sizes below the device to form an oligonucleotide pool required by each gene, and the yield of the oligonucleotide is at a pmoL level; the yield can meet the requirement that subsequent polymerase-mediated (PCA) or ligase-mediated (LCR) genome assembly can be directly carried out without amplification, and full-length splicing of the gene is completed by the oligonucleotide subjected to error correction so that high-throughput automatic synthesis of the gene is realized.

title: Gene synthesis of wild boar alpha-interferon, vector construction and method for producing outcome
text: Gene synthesis of wild boar alpha-interferon, vector construction and method for producing outcome. The invention discloses a gene synthesis of Alpha-interferon of wild boars and vector construction thereof as well as a production method of a product. A number of problems of low expression capacity, products expressed in a fusion state, products with purification tags or no fermentation technology with high density exist in the domestic recombinant strains. The invention includes a method that a codon and codon pairs, preferred by Escherichia coli, are used for synthesizing an Alpha-interferon gene of the wild boar, establishing a high-efficiency expression vector and transforming a high-efficiency expression strain as well as methods of high-density fermentation of engineering bacteria, separation and purification of inclusion bodies, the modification, the renaturation and the purification of target protein and the determination of biological activity of the expressed product. The gene synthesis, the vector construction and the production method pertain to the technical field of the production of polypeptide drugs by genetic engineering in biopharmaceuticals.

title: Methods of production of products of metabolic pathways
text: Methods of production of products of metabolic pathways. A plurality of isolated polynucleotide sequences encoding enzymes of the astaxanthin pathway is disclosed. The polynucleotides include: (i) a polynucleotide which encodes Phytoene dehydrogenase (crtI) and a first transcriptional regulatory sequence; (ii) a polynucleotide which encodes Beta-lycopene cyclase (lcy-B) and a second transcriptional regulatory sequence; (iii) a polynucleotide which encodes Beta-carotene ketolase (crtW) and a third transcriptional regulatory sequence; and wherein the first, second and third regulatory sequence are selected such that the expression of the Icy-B and the crtW is greater than a level of expression of the crtI. Methods of generating astaxanthin using the plurality of polynucleotide are also disclosed as well as bacterial cells comprising high levels of astaxanthin.

title: METHODS OF PRODUCTION OF PRODUCTS OF METABOLIC PATHWAYS
text: METHODS OF PRODUCTION OF PRODUCTS OF METABOLIC PATHWAYS. A plurality of isolated polynucleotide sequences encoding enzymes of the astaxanthin pathway is disclosed. The polynucleotides include: (i) a polynucleotide which encodes Phytoene dehydrogenase (crtI) and a first transcriptional regulatory sequence; (ii) a polynucleotide which encodes Beta-lycopene cyclase (lcy-B) and a second transcriptional regulatory sequence; (iii) a polynucleotide which encodes Beta-carotene ketolase (crtW) and a third transcriptional regulatory sequence; and wherein the first, second and third regulatory sequence are selected such that the expression of the Icy-B and the crtW is greater than a level of expression of the crtI. Methods of generating astaxanthin using the plurality of polynucleotide are also disclosed as well as bacterial cells comprising high levels of astaxanthin.

title: PRIMER-FREE GENE SYNTHESIS METHOD
text: PRIMER-FREE GENE SYNTHESIS METHOD. The present invention relates to the field of artificial synthesis of genes. Disclosed is a primer-free gene synthesis method. First, disclosed is a pNew carrier plasmid, wherein the pNew carrier plasmid has a nucleotide sequence shown in SEQ ID NO.33. Also disclosed is a carrier bank that is constructed by using pNew carrier plasmids and contains 16384(47) plasmids. Further disclosed is a primer-free gene synthesis method. The method comprises the following steps: (1) grouping DNA sequences of target genes to be synthesized according to a length of 82bp per fragment; (2) finding plasmids corresponding to the grouped fragments in the constructed plasmid bank; (3) carrying out enzyme digestion on the corresponding plasmids, carrying out screening by using an antibiotic and carrying out reconstruction to obtain a plasmid containing a target gene sequence of 82bp; and (4) splicing the gene fragments by means of a golden-gate cloning reaction to obtain a complete target gene. The gene synthesis method of the present invention is free of primers, has low synthesis costs, very low mutation rate and high accuracy, can synthesize special gene sequences such as a highly repetitive sequence and Poly A, has simple operations and can realize automatic operations.

title: MATERIALS AND METHODS FOR NUCLEIC ACID SYNTHESIS
text: MATERIALS AND METHODS FOR NUCLEIC ACID SYNTHESIS. The present invention relates to methods and kits for synthesizing a DNA sequence which employ a series of overlapping template oligonucleotides having sequences corresponding to one strand of a DNA sequence, these template oligonucleotides being incapable of extension in the synthesis reaction. The method uses one primer that primes synthesis of one strand of the DNA sequence, using a series of template oligonucleotides corresponding solely to the complementary strand as template. A second primer that primes synthesis of the complementary strand using the newly synthesised strand as template can be used. In one embodiment, the template oligonucleotides have modified 3' termini to prevent DNA polymerase mediated strand extension and nucleotide backbone modifications between one or more 3' nucleotides, to inhibit or prevent removal of the 3' modified nucleotides by the 3'-5' exonuclease activity of proof-reading polymerases, i.e. to confer some resistance to the exonuclease.

title: Gene synthesis method
text: Gene synthesis method. The invention provides a gene synthesis method. The gene synthesis method comprises the steps of segmenting a plurality of target genes into a plurality of overlapped oligonucleotide sequences, addingenzyme digestion site sequences and tag sequences at the end of the overlapped oligonucleotide sequence, wherein the tag sequence are different based on different target genes; enabling the overlapped oligonucleotide sequence connected with the tag sequences to be specifically linked with magnetic beads that are connected with reverse complementary sequences of the tag sequences through a double-chain structures in a one-chain mode, then carrying out enzyme digestion in a water-in-oil system to obtain single-chain overlapped oligonucleotide, and next carrying out polymerase chain type assembling to obtain multiple target gene segments.

title: Protein YNA15 for efficiently preparing nano-silver based on whole-gene synthesis as well as coding gene and application of protein YNA15
text: Protein YNA15 for efficiently preparing nano-silver based on whole-gene synthesis as well as coding gene and application of protein YNA15. The invention discloses a protein YNA15 for efficiently preparing nano-silver based on whole-gene synthesis as well as a coding gene and application of the protein YNA15. The nucleotide sequence of the gene is as shown in SEQ ID NO.1, and the amino acid sequence of the protein is as shown in SEQ ID NO.2. Protein obtained through functional modification of a protein engineering technology can efficiently prepare nano-silver with biological activity, recombinant protein YNA15 for synthesizing the nano-silver is obtained through large-scale mutation and whole-gene synthesis of an amino acid sequence of the protein, the prepared nano-silver solution is stable in property, the nano-silver solution is brown, and the nano-silver solution can be used for preparing the nano-silver with biological activity. According to the present invention, the modified protein has characteristics of high nano-silver synthesis efficiency and good biological function, and the synthesized nano-silver has broad-spectrum antibacterial ability, and has excellent antibacterial effect on eukaryotic organisms (S.cerevisiae), prokaryotic organisms (E.coli, S.aureus and B.subtilis), and can be used for preparing the antibacterial nano-silver product, such that the antibacterial nano-silver product can be used for preparing the antibacterial nano-silver product, and the antibacterial nano-silver product can be used for preparing the antibacterial nano-silver product, such that the antibacterial nano-silver product can be used for preparing the antibacterial nano-silver product.

title: NS1 Recombinant pangenotype complex NS1 antigen pool of dengue virus and endemic flaviviruses designed by gene synthesis
text: NS1 Recombinant pangenotype complex NS1 antigen pool of dengue virus and endemic flaviviruses designed by gene synthesis. The present invention relates to a recombinant genotypic complex NS1 antigen of dengue virus and a control group of recombinant genotypic complex NS1 antigen pool of endemic flavivirus, designed by gene synthesis. More particularly, the present invention can provide a control group of recombinant genotypic complex NS1 antigen pool of endemic flavivirus and a recombinant genotypic complex NS1 antigen pool of dengue virus, designed by high-sensitivity gene synthesis, which can be combined with antibodies of the dengue virus in a biological sample to diagnose the infection of the dengue virus.

title: Gene synthesis lethal association prediction method
text: Gene synthesis lethal association prediction method. The invention relates to a gene synthesis lethal association prediction method. The method comprises the steps of S1, determining and using a multi-label learning loss function; S2, setting a graph regularization penalty term for the multi-label learning loss function and obtaining a graph regularization multi-label learning model; S3, reconstructing the graph regularization multi-label learning model in the step S2 by using the multi-view information, and determining an adaptive weighted multi-view model; and S4, solving the adaptive multi-view model to realize prediction of gene synthesis lethal association. The method provided by the invention can be used for fusing multiple view angle data, breaks through the limitation of a single view angle, has relatively high flexibility, and can improve the accuracy of gene synthesis lethal association prediction.

title: TAILED PRIMER FOR CLONED PRODUCTS USED IN LIBRARY CONSTRUCTION
text: TAILED PRIMER FOR CLONED PRODUCTS USED IN LIBRARY CONSTRUCTION. This invention pertains to improved methods for the synthesis of long, double stranded nucleic acid sequences containing difficult to clone or variable regions.

title: High-throughput gene synthesis method
text: High-throughput gene synthesis method. The invention relates to a high-throughput gene synthesis method which comprises the following steps: treating the surface of a chip to obtain a micro-structure chip with a grid shape, cleaning and drying the micro-structure chip, and then carrying out coating treatment to obtain a hydrophilic and hydrophobic grid chip; dNA is synthesized in grids of the hydrophilic and hydrophobic grid chip by adopting an ink-jet printing method; and carrying out ammonolysis and transfer on the synthesized DNA, and then splicing and amplifying to obtain the high-throughput synthetic gene. The splicing and amplification products can be further assembled into recombinant plasmids. The synthesis method disclosed by the invention is simple, low in cost, high in flux and relatively high in efficiency. Theoretically, the gene synthesis cost can be reduced to 0.35-0.4 yuan/bp and is only about half of the current mainstream synthesis cost, the oligonucleotide chain synthesized at one time can meet the splicing of 100-1000 genes, and a large number of genes can be synthesized in a short time.

title: Electronic devices for polymer synthesis and assembly
text: Electronic devices for polymer synthesis and assembly. The present disclosure discloses a polymer synthesis and assembly chip that includes a semiconductor integrated circuit device that contains a plurality of pixels, wherein each pixel of the plurality of pixels contains an electrode; and wherein a set of pixels of the plurality of pixels is capable of synthesizing a polymer; and wherein a set of pixels of the plurality of pixels is capable of assembling a synthesized polymer, either independently or in concert with one or more pixels of the plurality of pixels; and control circuitry capable of applying voltages to the electrode. The present disclosure includes related methods of use, such as methods for error correction, for use with the described polymer synthesis and assembly chip.

title: INTELLIGENT GENE SYNTHESIS AND MONOCLONAL SELECTION SYSTEM
text: INTELLIGENT GENE SYNTHESIS AND MONOCLONAL SELECTION SYSTEM. An intelligent gene synthesis and monoclonal selection system, comprising: a core operation module, the core operation module being used for performing operations related to gene synthesis and monoclonal selection; and a control system, the control system being used for controlling at least some operations of corresponding compartments of the core operation module. The core operation module at least comprises: a liquid operation compartment, the liquid operation compartment being used for performing a mixing operation of gene fragments and a mixing operation of a synthetic reagent and a biological sample during the process of gene synthesis; a purification compartment, the purification compartment being used for performing a purification operation on a mixed nucleic acid product; an amplification compartment, the amplification compartment being used for performing an amplification operation and an error correction operation on fragments of the nucleic acid product, and performing a culture and amplification operation on a carrier; and a monoclonal selection compartment, the monoclonal selection compartment being used for performing a monoclonal selection operation on the purified and cultured nucleic acid product.

title: GUIDE RNA ASSEMBLY VECTOR
text: GUIDE RNA ASSEMBLY VECTOR

title: SYNTHESIS OF ERROR-MINIMIZED NUCLEIC ACID MOLECULES
text: SYNTHESIS OF ERROR-MINIMIZED NUCLEIC ACID MOLECULES

title: Methods and Compositions for Rapid Assembly of Genetic Modules
text: Methods and Compositions for Rapid Assembly of Genetic Modules. Provided herein are methods and compositions for rapid assembly of genetic modules, as well as seamless transition from in vitro to in vivo testing of genetic constructs.

title: Automatic cotton assorting method and device and readable storage medium
text: Automatic cotton assorting method and device and readable storage medium. The invention provides an automatic cotton assorting method and device and a readable storage medium, and relates to the technical field of cotton spinning.The method comprises the steps that k resultant yarn quality prediction models for predicting different yarn characteristics are established according to multiple cotton fiber indexes, and k is larger than or equal to 1; taking the established k resultant yarn quality prediction models as one of main constraint conditions, taking cotton assorting cost minimization as a target function, and establishing a nonlinear cotton assorting model; an orthogonal genetic algorithm is used for optimizing the cotton assorting proportion of the nonlinear cotton assorting model, a cotton assorting scheme is obtained, the orthogonal genetic algorithm uses orthogonal experimental design to improve a generation strategy of an initial population of a basic genetic algorithm, and generated initial solutions are evenly distributed in the whole solution space. According to the method and device and the readable storage medium, the problems that it is difficult to guarantee that the quality of spun yarn meets the requirement, the algorithm convergence precision is poor, the time complexity is high, and the optimization speed is low in an existing automatic cotton assorting method can be solved.

title: One-pot multiplex gene synthesis
text: One-pot multiplex gene synthesis. The present invention provides methods for generating a library of synthetic polynucleotides. The present invention also provides methods for generating proteins encoded by the library of synthetic polynucleotides. In addition, provided herein are methods for determining the soluble expression of said proteins. This invention is based, in part, on the discovery of a method for selecting optimal oligonucleotides in combination with performing a phosphorylation reaction, ligation reaction and PCR amplification in a single reaction vessel to produce synthetic polynucleotides in a multiplex manner.

title: Long gene synthesis method
text: Long gene synthesis method. The invention discloses a long gene synthesis method. The method includes the following steps: 1) two-stage segmentation is performed on a long gene according to the typeIIs restriction sites within agene fragment; 2) a two-stage fragment can be obtained through traditional gene synthesis; 3) the two-stage fragments are spliced into primary fragments through Golden Gate; and 4) the one-stage fragment is spliced into a full-length by utilizing an enzyme digestion-LCR method or a PCR-LCR method. The method uses the typeIIs restriction sites to perform segmentation on large gene fragments, manytypeIIs restriction sites can be chosen, and thus, the method is suitable for most of the sequence features. Short fragments can be rapidly spliced into long fragments. Splicing the two-stage fragmentinto the full-length gene can be achieved within 5 days. The whole technical scheme is streamlined, automation design can be performed by using software, and production can be carried out by using automation platforms.

title: Tailed primer for cloned products used in library construction
text: Tailed primer for cloned products used in library construction. This invention pertains to improved methods for the synthesis of long, double stranded nucleic acid sequences containing difficult to clone or variable regions.

title: Method and kit for assembly of multiple DNA fragments at room temperature
text: Method and kit for assembly of multiple DNA fragments at room temperature. The present invention relates to recombinant DNA technology, in particular to methods for assembling two or more double stranded (ds) nucleic acid molecules with overlapping terminal sequences. In particular, the present invention relates to the use of a thermolabile DNA polymerase II derived 3′-5′ exonuclease isolated from Moritella viscoa and a thermolabile DNA polymerase I of marine origin in multi DNA assembly processes.

title: Gene synthesis device
text: Gene synthesis device. The utility model relates to a gene synthesis device. The gene synthesis device comprises a bearing assembly and a first air pressure adjusting assembly, the bearing assembly is provided with a first bearing cavity and used for bearing a composite plate. The first air pressure adjusting assembly communicates with the first bearing cavity and adjusts the air pressure in the first bearing cavity. When genes are synthesized through the device, the synthesis plate is firstly borne on the bearing assembly, and then preset reagents are injected into the first cavities of the multiple synthesis columns respectively. In the process of injecting the preset reagent, by adjusting the air pressure in the first bearing cavity, the air pressure in the first bearing cavity can form back pressure larger than the atmospheric pressure, and then the air pressure in the first bearing cavity can apply upward lifting force to the preset reagent in the first cavity; therefore, the possibility that the preset reagent leaks downwards relative to the carrier in the synthesis column before the synthesis reaction is reduced, the liquid drainage time required by the whole synthesis process is short, meanwhile, the excessive preset reagent does not need to be additionally added, and the synthesis cost is low.

title: PCC1-Brick vector construction and application in field of gene synthesis
text: PCC1-Brick vector construction and application in field of gene synthesis. The invention discloses pCC1-Brick vector construction and application in the field of gene synthesis. The vector is obtained by loading a CEN/ARS sequence and an LEU2 sequence of a segment of key component of yeast into a pCC1 plasmid by using a PCR cloning method and constructing. The toxicity problem of dengue virus can be solved by using a pCC1-Brick vector, and further the whole-gene combination of the dengue virus can be completed in cell bodies, so that the problem that a dengue virus genome is unstable or cannot be obtained is solved. Meanwhile, the vector is also proved to be effective for the whole-gene synthesis of other viruses, so that the synthesized vector greatly benefits the synthesis of large genes such as toxic genes, unstable genes and viral sequences, and the competition status of an applicant in the field of gene synthesis is promoted.

title: DNA ASSEMBLY
text: DNA ASSEMBLY. The invention relates to a nucleic acid for use in DNA assembly, wherein the nucleic acid comprises at least one methylation-protectable restriction element, the methylation-protectable restriction element comprising: a restriction enzyme recognition sequence that is recognised by a restriction enzyme that cleaves outside of the recognition sequence; and a DNA methylase recognition sequence, wherein the restriction enzyme recognition sequence and the DNA methylase recognition sequence overlap such that the base modified by the DNA methylase lies within the restriction enzyme recognition sequence, wherein the DNA methylase recognition sequence is not identical to or enclosed by the restriction enzyme recognition sequence, and wherein the DNA methylase recognition sequence does not overlap with the sequence that would form the overhang end sequence generated by the restriction enzyme. The invention further relates to asscociated methods and kits.

title: Methods and compositions for rapid assembly of genetic modules
text: Methods and compositions for rapid assembly of genetic modules. Provided herein are methods and compositions for rapid assembly of genetic modules, as well as seamless transition from in vitro to in vivo testing of genetic constructs.

title: A HIGH-THROUGHPUT PLATFORM TO SELECT FOR REGULATORS OF CRISPR-CAS ASSOCIATED ACTIVITY
text: A HIGH-THROUGHPUT PLATFORM TO SELECT FOR REGULATORS OF CRISPR-CAS ASSOCIATED ACTIVITY. The invention relates to a method for in vivo detection of small molecules or polypeptides that act as regulators of CRISPR-Cas associated activity, in particular the method is for use in the screening of molecule libraries and non-host DNA libraries. The method employs cells comprising genes expressing CRISPR-Cas associated activity and a reporter protein to allow the detection of said regulators. The invention further relates to cells comprising genes expressing of CRISPR-Cas associated activity and a reporter protein for use in said in vivo screening and detection methods.

title: Chip primer surface extraction-based high-throughput gene synthesis method
text: Chip primer surface extraction-based high-throughput gene synthesis method. Provided is a chip primer surface extraction-based high-throughput gene synthesis method. The method relates to sorting primers by cluster, then synthesizing same to a fixed area of a chip, performing cutting, and then using a sampler to dissolve primers of different clusters and transferring same to a 96 pore plate for gene synthesis.

title: CHIP PRIMER SURFACE EXTRACTION-BASED HIGH-THROUGHPUT GENE SYNTHESIS METHOD
text: CHIP PRIMER SURFACE EXTRACTION-BASED HIGH-THROUGHPUT GENE SYNTHESIS METHOD. Provided is a chip primer surface extraction-based high-throughput gene synthesis method. The method relates to sorting primers by cluster, then synthesizing same to a fixed area of a chip, performing cutting, and then using a sampler to dissolve primers of different clusters and transferring same to a 96-pore plate for gene synthesis.

title: Lentivirus packaging system comprising a synthetic positive feedback loop
text: Lentivirus packaging system comprising a synthetic positive feedback loop. Novel lentivirus packaging systems engineered with a synthetic gene network having a positive feedback loop to amplify the expression of virus genes are provided. When co-transfected into a host cell with a transfer plasmid and envelope vector, extremely high viral titers are achieved when compared to transfection of a host cell with conventional third generation packaging systems. Methods for enhancing production of lentivirus, compositions comprising high titer lentivirus, and therapeutic methods based on delivery of lentiviral nucleic acid to target cells are also provided.

title: Method for improving sensitivity of gene synthesis technology based on PCR by means of non-specific binding protein
text: Method for improving sensitivity of gene synthesis technology based on PCR by means of non-specific binding protein. The invention discloses a method for improving the sensitivity of a gene synthesis technology based on PCR by means of non-specific binding protein. The method comprises the following steps that S1, DNA sequences needed to be synthesized are designed, and hybrid overlapped oligonucleotides are obtained, wherein each sequence comprises 25bp overlapped oligonucleotides to cover the whole DNA sequence with the basic group number ranging from 200 to 1500; S2, an HU protein stock solution is added to a reaction solution which contains desoxyribose nucleotides, DNA polymerase, an oligonucleotide primer and the hybrid overlapped oligonucleotides serving as templates; S3, the three steps of unwinding, annealing and extension of conventional PCR are cycled repeatedly, and a target nucleic acid fragment is synthesized and amplified. In this way, the method for improving the sensitivity of the gene synthesis technology based on the PCR by means of the non-specific binding protein can specially and efficiently synthesize and amplify the target nucleic acid sequence.

title: INTEGRATED MICROFLUIDIC DEVICE FOR GENE SYNTHESIS
text: INTEGRATED MICROFLUIDIC DEVICE FOR GENE SYNTHESIS. We report making an integrated micro-fluidic device for synthesizing double stranded DNA from short oligo-nucleotides. We demonstrate successful synthesis of a 760 bp gene segment from a pool of 39 oligonucleotides on a micro-fluidic device using both the one-step and two-step synthesis processes. We also describe purifying the double stranded DNA PCR product and filtering out sequence errors in the double stranded DNA product, all on the same device.

title: PCR-DIRECTED GENE SYNTHESIS FROM LARGE NUMBER OF OVERLAPPING OLIGODEOXYRIBONUCLEOTIDES
text: PCR-DIRECTED GENE SYNTHESIS FROM LARGE NUMBER OF OVERLAPPING OLIGODEOXYRIBONUCLEOTIDES. The present invention provides methods of PCR-directed gene synthesis that may be used for all genes, including those with a high G+C content and/or a long sequence. The invention relates to methods of gene synthesis using overlapping oligonucleotides and polymerase chain reaction (PCR), wherein several PCR parameters, e.g., the concentration of overlapping oligonucleotides, the type of DNA polymerase used, and the number of PCR amplification cycles, are optimized. Additionally, the invention relates to oligonucleotide design that allows for increased protein expression of synthesized genes.

title: Inducible dimerization of recombinases
text: Inducible dimerization of recombinases. The technology described herein relates to controlled chemically- or light-induced rejoinder of split-recombinases. In some embodiments, compositions, methods, kits and systems are provided that relate to a split-recombinase system, whereby protein complementation or rejoinder of split-recombinases is mediated by chemical-induced dimerization domains (CIDDs) or light-induced dimerization domains (LIDD) and rejoinder of the split-recombinases occur in the presence of one or more chemical inducers or light inducers, respectively. The split-recombinases systems as disclosed herein can be used in gene therapy, integrated logic and memory in living cells such as mammalian cells. The nucleic acid cassettes, switches, and systems described herein allow for controlled gene expression or gene regulation. The controlled chemically- or light-induced rejoinder of split-recombinases can be used in, for example, adopted T-cell therapy.

title: Gene synthesis plasmid for expressing RGD-targeted elastin-like polypeptide
text: Gene synthesis plasmid for expressing RGD-targeted elastin-like polypeptide. The invention belongs to the technical field of biological medicine, and provides a gene synthesis plasmid for expressing RGD-targeted elastin-like polypeptide, the gene synthesis plasmid comprises a carrier, the carrier is RGD-targeted amphiphilic elastin-like polypeptide, the amino acid sequence of the carrier is G (VPGYG) n (VPGXG) n (RGD) m, X is aqueous amino acid, n is equal to 20-50, and m is equal to 1-3; a gene synthesis plasmid for expressing RGD-targeted elastin-like polypeptide is constructed, so that tyrosine in a hydrophobic end repetitive unit of the polypeptide can be coupled with radioactive iodine nuclide, and a pET-25b (+) plasmid serving as a carrier has relatively stable hereditary characteristics, can be stably copied and inherited in host cells, and can be used for preparing a recombinant plasmid for expressing the RGD-targeted elastin-like polypeptide. And moreover, the DNA molecule of the plasmid is small, so that various molecular biology operations are easy to carry out. The patent is subsidized by Key Technology and Industrial Demonstration for Green Biological Manufacturing of Medicinal Polypeptides, and the project number is 2021YFC2103900.

title: Rapid and simple gene synthesis method
text: Rapid and simple gene synthesis method. The invention discloses a rapid and simple gene synthesis method comprising the following steps: dividing a to-be-synthesized gene sequence into a plurality of forward oligonucleotide chains and corresponding reverse complementary oligonucleotide chains, mutually staggering every two adjacent reverse complementary oligonucleotide chains by 4-8 nt, and making the rest parts form completely reversecomplementation; synthesizing forward and reverse oligonucleotide chains; carrying out enzyme endonuclease by using a restriction enzyme or amplifying a linearized vector by using PCR; respectively mixing and annealing the forward oligonucleotide chains and the reverse complementary oligonucleotide chains to obtain double-chain DNA fragments containing multiple incisions; mixing the incised double-chain DNA fragments with the linearized vector, and connecting the plurality of double-chain DNA fragments with a target vector by utilizing a T4 ligase to form rings; and transferring a connected product in the fifth step to a competent cell, and carrying out bacterial detection, sequencing and verification. By using the rapid and simple gene synthesis method, the processes of PCR and glue recovery and purification are not needed, so that human errors and cross pollution brought by various steps are greatly reduced, and the time and cost are reduced.

title: Insulin gene synthesis protein releases extracellular model
text: Insulin gene synthesis protein releases extracellular model. The utility model discloses an insulin gene synthesis protein releases extracellular model, including model, base and extension board, the top of base is equipped with the extension board, and the both sides of model all link to each other with first bull stick through the joint block, and the outer wall of first bull stick has cup jointed the bearing, and the inner wall of bearing is equipped with the ball, and the bearing links to each other with the second loop bar through first loop bar, and the both sides of second loop bar all are equipped with first nut, and the spout passes through theslide and links to each other with the extension board. This insulin gene synthesis protein releases extracellular model can place the model on the desktop through the base, through first loop bar and second loop bar can the pair model highly adjust, has greatly solved teaching personnel and the student has appeared can't see the model scheduling problem when imparting knowledge to students the operation, through bull stick and bedding -in of bearings, can make the model rotate, formula teaching personnel is explanation convenient more when imparting knowledge to students the operation.

title: Preparation device of stationary phase chip for enzymatic gene synthesis
text: Preparation device of stationary phase chip for enzymatic gene synthesis. The utility model provides a preparation device of a stationary phase chip for enzymic method gene synthesis, the preparation device of the stationary phase chip for enzymic method gene synthesis comprises a chip modification box and a modification box cover, the upper side of the chip modification box is provided with a plurality of modification grooves, the bottom of each modification groove is provided with a chip clamping groove, a substrate is suitable for being placed in the modification grooves, and the modification box cover is arranged on the substrate. The bottom is clamped in the chip clamping groove; the modification box cover covers the chip modification box and is suitable for being in sealing fit with the chip modification box. According to the preparation device of the stationary phase chip for enzymatic gene synthesis, the substrate is stably clamped through the chip clamping groove in the bottom of the modification groove and prevented from falling off, and the modification box cover matched with the chip modification box in a sealing mode is arranged, so that a stable environment is provided for manufacturing of the stationary phase chip, and preparation of the stationary phase chip is facilitated; and a plurality of modification grooves are formed in the chip modification box, so that a small batch of stationary phase chips can be prepared at one time.

title: Traffic signal timing optimization method based on principal component analysis and local search improvement orthogonality genetic algorithm
text: Traffic signal timing optimization method based on principal component analysis and local search improvement orthogonality genetic algorithm. Provided is a traffic signal timing optimization method based on principal component analysis and a local search improvement orthogonality genetic algorithm. The algorithm is provided by analyzing internal relation among the genetic algorithm, image processing and mode recognition and can be used for solving various function optimization problems. By means of the algorithm, an improvement orthogonality cross operator based on principal component analysis is provided. The operator first conducts PCA projection on the population before cross, individual length is reduced during cross, orthogonal cross operation is implemented on the projection area, the projection is projected to the original space after cross, redundant individual number and calculation expenses caused by redundancy are reduced, algorithm convergence speed is further improved, and the local search strategy is further introduced. The algorithm is applied to single-crossing signal timing optimization. By means of testing comparison with the existing algorithm, the method improves algorithm generality and efficiency, effective timing time is acquired, and the number of the queuing vehicles in front of a crossing is reduced.

title: METHOD FOR THE FERMENTATIVE PRODUCTION OF GUANIDINOACETIC ACID
text: METHOD FOR THE FERMENTATIVE PRODUCTION OF GUANIDINOACETIC ACID. The present invention relates to a microorganism transformed with L-arginine:glycine amidinotransferase and a glyoxylate aminotransferase and reduced malate synthase to be capable of producing guanidinoacetic acid (GAA) and to a method for the fermentative production of GAA using such microorganism. The present invention also relates to a method for the fermentative production of creatine.

title: Purification method of single-stranded binding protein and application of single-strand binding protein in gene synthesis
text: Purification method of single-stranded binding protein and application of single-strand binding protein in gene synthesis. The invention relates to a purification method of single-stranded binding protein, an application of the single-stranded binding protein in gene synthesis, and a sequential assembly method of a groupof single-stranded or double-stranded DNAs with homologous sequences. The method is applied to assembly of single-stranded nucleic acid molecules with homologous sequences, the single-stranded nucleicacid molecules serve as an additive in a polymerase chain reaction, and the method is applied to assembly of double-stranded DNA molecules with homologous sequences. The specific scheme comprises plasmid cloning of the high-temperature-resistant SSB protein, protein purification and an application in gene synthesis. By applying the ssb protein, the splicing assembly of the single-stranded or double-stranded DNAs is accelerated, and the assembly accuracy is improved.

title: Intelligent gene synthesis and monoclonal selection system
text: Intelligent gene synthesis and monoclonal selection system. An intelligent gene synthesis and monoclonal selection system comprises: a core operation module, which is used for implementing operations related to gene synthesis and monoclonal selection; and the control system is used for controlling at least one part of operation of the corresponding cabin of the core operation module. The core operation module at least comprises a liquid operation cabin, and the liquid operation cabin is used for carrying out mixing operation of gene segments and mixing operation of synthetic reagents and biological samples in the gene synthesis process; the purification cabin is used for carrying out purification operation on the mixed nucleic acid product; the amplification cabin is used for carrying out amplification operation and error correction operation on fragments of the nucleic acid product and carrying out culture amplification operation on a carrier; and the monoclonal selection cabin is used for carrying out monoclonal selection operation on the purified and cultured nucleic acid products.

title: Selective integration face identification method based on CSJOGA
text: Selective integration face identification method based on CSJOGA. The invention discloses a selective integration face identification method based on a CSJOGA, belongs to the technical field of face identification, and particularly relates to a face identification new method based on a Combined Sparse Projection Improved Hybrid Orthogonal Genetic Algorithm (CSJOGA), a bagging method, a selective integration technology and a multi-classification SVM. The method combines minimum spanning tree clustering, combined sparse projection and orthogonal experimental design, and proposes an orthogonal crossover operator based on combined sparse projection. Then an orthogonal experimental method is utilized to design crossover, a clustering local search strategy is introduced, and a hybrid orthogonal genetic algorithm based on combined sparse projection is proposed. The method can ensure that the accuracy of face identification can be further improved to a higher level on the premise of substantially reducing the number of integrated classifiers, reducing storage and calculation overheads and effectively improving face identification speed, and ensure that classification precision in practical application reaches an ideal requirement.

title: METHOD AND KIT FOR ASSEMBLY OF MULTIPLE DNA FRAGMENTS AT ROOM TEMPERATURE
text: METHOD AND KIT FOR ASSEMBLY OF MULTIPLE DNA FRAGMENTS AT ROOM TEMPERATURE. The present invention relates to recombinant DNA technology, in particular to methods for assembling two or more double stranded (ds) nucleic acid molecules with overlapping terminal sequences. In particular, the present invention relates to the use of a thermolabile DNA polymerase II derived 3'-5' exonuclease isolated from Moritella viscoa and a thermolabile DNA polymerase I of marine origin in multi DNA assembly processes.

title: DNA backbone editing for DNA data storage
text: DNA backbone editing for DNA data storage. Methods of writing data to a DNA strand by inserting data-encoding oligos or symbols into a DNA backbone. One particular method of synthesizing a DNA strand encoding data includes cleaving a DNA backbone into multiple segments, and pasting a plurality of data-encoding oligo symbols between the multiple segments, with the terminal ends of the segments joining homologous terminal ends of the symbols, resulting in the DNA strand encoding data comprising alternating segments and symbols.

title: ANALOG AND MIXED-SIGNAL COMPUTATION AND CIRCUITS IN LIVING CELLS
text: ANALOG AND MIXED-SIGNAL COMPUTATION AND CIRCUITS IN LIVING CELLS. Provided herein are molecular analog gene circuits that exploit positive and negative feedback to implement logarithmically linear sensing, addition, subtraction, and scaling thus enabling multiplicative, ratiometric, and power-law computations. The circuits exhibit Weber's Law behavior as in natural biological systems, operate over a wide dynamic range of up to four orders of magnitude, and can be architected to have tunable transfer functions. The molecular circuits described herein can be composed together to implement higher-order functions that are well-described by both intricate biochemical models and by simple mathematical functions. The molecular circuits described herein enable logarithmically linear analog computation within in-vitro and in-vivo systems with a broad class of molecules, all of which obey the Boltzmann exponential equations of thermodynamics that govern molecular association, attenuation, transformation, and degradation.

title: GENOME-WIDE CONSTRUCTION OF SCHIZOSACCHAROMYCES POMBE HETEROZYGOUS DELETION MUTANTS CONTAINING GENE-SPECIFIC BARCODES BY THE METHODS OF 4-ROUND SERIAL OR BLOCK PCR, OR TOTAL GENE SYNTHESIS THEREOF
text: GENOME-WIDE CONSTRUCTION OF SCHIZOSACCHAROMYCES POMBE HETEROZYGOUS DELETION MUTANTS CONTAINING GENE-SPECIFIC BARCODES BY THE METHODS OF 4-ROUND SERIAL OR BLOCK PCR, OR TOTAL GENE SYNTHESIS THEREOF. PURPOSE: A method for producing gene targeting heterozygote fission yeast strain is provided to detect drug active point at the genetic level. CONSTITUTION: A method for producing gene targeting heterozygote fission yeast strain containing a strain-specific bar code comprises gene synthesis, 4-round serial PCR, and 4-round block PCR. In the method, a selection marker gene, gene-specific microarray bar code sequence, and gene targeting deletion cassette.

title: Synthesis method of trace ultra-long low mutation nucleotide
text: Synthesis method of trace ultra-long low mutation nucleotide. The invention relates to a trace ultra-length low mutation nucleotide synthesis method, and belongs to the field of whole-gene synthesis long-chain O < l > i < go > production processes, and the method comprises the following steps: 1, examination and preparation of a synthesizer; 2, preparing a synthetic reagent according to a formula; 3, compiling a reaction sequence and steps of the synthetic program reagent; step 4, synthesizing Fr < i > tsamp; and synthesizing the board: installing the synthesizing board, covering an instrument cover plate, clicking to operate the synthesizer, automatically synthesizing, and finally unloading from the machine. According to the invention, the reaction rate of each basic group can be increased, the reaction rate is closer to 100% than that of a 60bp coupling reaction, primers for whole gene synthesis can be prolonged to 120bp or above, and the mutation rate can be reduced to five ten thousandths or below. The proportion of the overlap region of a pair of primers is reduced to 8.1-11.1%, and the total number of production strips of the primers for whole-gene use is greatly reduced, so that the cost of the primers and the pressure on environmental protection are reduced.

title: RAPID MODULATION OF DROPLET COMPOSITION WITH MEMBRANE MICROVALVES
text: RAPID MODULATION OF DROPLET COMPOSITION WITH MEMBRANE MICROVALVES. The disclosure provides a method of modulating droplet composition using a microfluidic device. Also provided is a method of combining two or more fluids for droplet formation using a microfluidic device, and devices for practicing the subject methods.

title: Biosensors for detecting and/or neutralizing bioavailable uranium and related u-sensitive genetic molecular components, gene cassettes, vectors, genetic circuits, compositions, methods and systems
text: Biosensors for detecting and/or neutralizing bioavailable uranium and related u-sensitive genetic molecular components, gene cassettes, vectors, genetic circuits, compositions, methods and systems. U biosensors, and related U-sensing genetic molecular components, genetic circuits, compositions, methods and systems are described, which in several embodiments can be used to detect and/or neutralize uranium and in particular bioavailable U.

title: COMPLETE OXIDATION OF SUGARS TO ELECTRICITY BY USING CELL-FREE SYNTHETIC ENZYMATIC PATHWAYS
text: COMPLETE OXIDATION OF SUGARS TO ELECTRICITY BY USING CELL-FREE SYNTHETIC ENZYMATIC PATHWAYS. 본 발명은 생체전기 (bioelectricity)의 분야에 속한다. 본 발명은 당에 저장된 화학적 에너지를 유용한 전기로 전환시킬 수 있는 에너지 생성 시스템, 방법, 및 장치를 제공한다.

title: NOVEL SCREENING ASSAYS FOR THE DETECTION OF PROTEIN HOMODIMERIZATION
text: NOVEL SCREENING ASSAYS FOR THE DETECTION OF PROTEIN HOMODIMERIZATION. The present invention provides plasmids, recombinant bacteria and uses thereof which enables rapid and simple detection and quantification of homodimerization of soluble proteins. The present invention relates to e.g. a plasmid for the detection of protein homodimerization of the protein in soluble state comprising: a nucleotide sequence encoding the expression of a chimeric protein comprising a protein of interest (POI) fused to a DNA-binding domain (DBD) via a flexible linker, said DBD being from the bacterial transcription factor LexA or ToxR; a nucleotide sequence encoding a reporter protein which is under transcriptional control being activated or repressed by said chimeric protein comprising a protein of interest (POI) fused to a DNA-binding domain (DBD) via a flexible linker once said protein of interest (POI) associates such that said chimeric protein homodimerizes; an origin of replication, and a selection system.

title: Nucleic acid reactive dye coupling labeling, detection and purification method thereof
text: Nucleic acid reactive dye coupling labeling, detection and purification method thereof. The invention relates to a nucleic acid reactive dye coupling labeling method. The method comprises the following steps: DNA molecules can be obtained through a gene cloning or gene synthesis mode, and the RNA molecules can be obtained through the gene synthesis mode; a Tris-HCl solution is prepared, a quantitative reactive dye is weighed, and is added in the Tris-HCl solution to prepare dye liquor, the dye liquor quantitative and the DNA molecules or the RNA molecules are placed in an EP pipe and mixed, a mixture is slightly and uniformly beaten by a tool, and the mixture is incubated for 20 min in a water-bath pan to prepare the reactive dye coupling labeled nucleic acid. The method breaks up a traditional excitation chromogenic method by using a fluorescence dye, and the nucleic acid double-chain is embedded with the dye, and the method has the characteristics of high combination stability, visual color developing, safety and high efficiency, and economic performance.

title: Artificial synthesis gene acting on TME immune effector cells, verification method and applications thereof
text: Artificial synthesis gene acting on TME immune effector cells, verification method and applications thereof. The invention relates to an artificial synthesis gene acting on TME immune effector cells, a verification method and applications thereof, wherein the structure of the artificial synthesis gene is formed by combining an inhibition signal receptor gene and an intracellular activation signal functional region gene through a gene synthesis method. According to the present invention, the inhibition function signal can be converted into the immune activation function signal by the target cells expressing the structure of the artificial synthesis gene, such that the immune effector cells can be activated in the immunosuppressive environment, the cell failure can be prevented, and the immune response can be promoted; the method comprises that an immune checkpoint PD-1 and other intracellular activation pathway genes are combined through gene synthesis, the combined material is transfected into antigen-specific effector cells by a vector, and the transfected effector cells can convert the inhibition signal into an activation signal, such that the effector cells can be amplified, and the inhibition on immune cells can be reduced; and the effector cells transfected with the structure of theartificial synthesis gene can overcome the function inhibition of the immune checkpoint on the immune cells, and have functions of activating and enhancing of the activity of immune cells.

title: Minimal bacterial genome
text: Minimal bacterial genome. The present invention relates, e.g., to a minimal set of protein-coding genes which provides the information required for replication of a free-living organism in a rich bacterial culture medium, wherein (1) the gene set does not comprise the 101 genes listed in Table 2; and/or wherein (2) the gene set comprises the 381 protein-coding genes listed in Table 3 and, optionally, one of more of: a set of three genes encoding ABC transporters for phosphate import (genes MG410, MG411 and MG412; or genes MG289, MG290 and MG291); the lipoprotein-encoding gene MG185 or MG260; and/or the glycerophosphoryl diester phosphodiesterase gene MG293 or MG385.

title: Construction and identification method of influenza B DNA vaccine
text: Construction and identification method of influenza B DNA vaccine. The invention discloses a construction and identification method of an influenza B DNA vaccine, which comprises the following steps: step 1, downloading sequence information of an influenza B recommended strain B/Austria/1359417/2021 in vaccine strain recommended components in 2022-2023, carrying out codon optimization on an HA gene of the strain, and then carrying out gene synthesis, and naming the gene as BHA; 2, highly conservative sequences of HA protein, M2e protein and NA protein are selected for series combination, gene synthesis is performed after codon optimization, and the gene is named as G3; 3, respectively extracting plasmid DNAs of the synthetic fragments for later use; 4, amplifying a target fragment by using a PCR technology so as to connect a carrier; and 5, carrying out a target gene and vector connection experiment. According to the present invention, the HA protein sequence of the DNA vaccine can be rapidly replaced by using the restriction enzyme cutting site, the preparation method is simple, the immunogenicity is good, the cross protection is good, the preparation cost is low, the preparation requirement of the large-scale influenza vaccine can be met, and the important scientific significance and the important social value are provided.

title: - COMPLETE OXIDATION OF SUGARS TO ELECTRICITY BY USING CELL-FREE SYNTHETIC ENZYMATIC PATHWAYS
text: - COMPLETE OXIDATION OF SUGARS TO ELECTRICITY BY USING CELL-FREE SYNTHETIC ENZYMATIC PATHWAYS. 본 발명은 바이오전기 (bioelectricity)의 분야에 속한다. 본 발명은 당에 저장된 화학적 에너지를 유용한 전기로 전환시킬 수 있는 에너지 생성 시스템, 방법, 및 장치를 제공한다.

title: Pressure system, pressure system use method and gene synthesis equipment
text: Pressure system, pressure system use method and gene synthesis equipment. The invention discloses a pressure system, a pressure system using method and gene synthesis equipment, the pressure system comprises a first gas source, a second gas source, a first conveying pipeline, a second conveying pipeline, a first switching piece and an input pipe; the first gas source is communicated with an inlet of the first conveying pipeline and used for conveying first gas to the first conveying pipeline, and the second gas source is communicated with an inlet of the second conveying pipeline and used for conveying second gas to the second conveying pipeline; a first connector of the first switching piece is communicated with an outlet of the first conveying pipeline, a second connector of the first switching piece is communicated with an outlet of the second conveying pipeline, a third connector of the first switching piece is communicated with one end of the input pipe, and the other end of the input pipe is communicated with the reaction container. According to the invention, the cost of the first gas source is lower than that of the second gas source, the first gas source is used during press-drying operation, and the second gas source is used during synthesis operation. The use amount of the second gas is saved, and the cost is reduced.

title: Synthesis of long nucleic acid sequences
text: Synthesis of long nucleic acid sequences. The invention provides methods for the synthesis of long oligonucleotides, genes and gene fragments. The methods include the manufacture of genes or gene fragments that can be then inserted into a variety of vectors.

title: BIOSENSORS FOR DETECTING AND/OR NEUTRALIZING BIOAVAILABLE URANIUM AND RELATED U-SENSITIVE GENETIC MOLECULAR COMPONENTS, GENE CASSETTES, VECTORS, GENETIC CIRCUITS, COMPOSITIONS, METHODS AND SYSTEMS
text: BIOSENSORS FOR DETECTING AND/OR NEUTRALIZING BIOAVAILABLE URANIUM AND RELATED U-SENSITIVE GENETIC MOLECULAR COMPONENTS, GENE CASSETTES, VECTORS, GENETIC CIRCUITS, COMPOSITIONS, METHODS AND SYSTEMS. U biosensors, and related U-sensing genetic molecular components, genetic circuits, compositions, methods and systems are described, which in several embodiments can be used to detect and/or neutralize uranium and in particular bioavailable U.

title: CRISPR-BASED SYNTHETIC GENE CIRCUITS AS NEXT GENERATION GENE THERAPY OF INNER EAR
text: CRISPR-BASED SYNTHETIC GENE CIRCUITS AS NEXT GENERATION GENE THERAPY OF INNER EAR. Aspects of the disclosure relate to synthetic regulatory systems comprising a multifunctional Cas nuclease and at least two guide RNAs (gRNAs) including a truncated gRNA and an multilayered regulatory control element. The synthetic regulatory system modulates endogenous gene expression, including transcriptional repression and transcriptional activation of one or more endogenous genes of a mammalian inner ear cell with multiple safety switches. Also provided herein are methods for modulating hearing sensitivity in damaged cells of the organ of corti.

title: METHODS AND COMPOSITIONS FOR RAPID ASSEMBLY OF GENETIC MODULES
text: METHODS AND COMPOSITIONS FOR RAPID ASSEMBLY OF GENETIC MODULES. Provided herein are methods and compositions for rapid assembly of genetic modules, as well as seamless transition from in vitro to in vivo testing of genetic constructs.

title: BIOSENSORS FOR DETECTING AND/OR NEUTRALIZING BIOAVAILABLE URANIUM AND RELATED U-SENSITIVE GENETIC MOLECULAR COMPONENTS, GENE CASSETTES, VECTORS, GENETIC CIRCUITS, COMPOSITIONS, METHODS AND SYSTEMS
text: BIOSENSORS FOR DETECTING AND/OR NEUTRALIZING BIOAVAILABLE URANIUM AND RELATED U-SENSITIVE GENETIC MOLECULAR COMPONENTS, GENE CASSETTES, VECTORS, GENETIC CIRCUITS, COMPOSITIONS, METHODS AND SYSTEMS. U biosensors, and related U-sensing genetic molecular components, genetic circuits, compositions, methods and systems are described, which in several embodiments can be used to detect and/or neutralize uranium and in particular bioavailable U.

title: BISPECIFIC SINGLE CHAIN ANTIBODIES WITH SPECIFICITY FOR HIGH MOLECULAR WEIGHT TARGET ANTIGENS
text: BISPECIFIC SINGLE CHAIN ANTIBODIES WITH SPECIFICITY FOR HIGH MOLECULAR WEIGHT TARGET ANTIGENS. The present invention provides a method for the selection of bispecific single chain antibodies comprising a first binding domain capable of binding to an epitope of CD3 and a second binding domain capable of binding to the extracellular domain cell surface antigens with a high molecular weight extracellular domain. Moreover, the invention provides bispecific single chain antibodies produced by the use of the method of the invention, nucleic acid molecules encoding these antibodies, vectors comprising such nucleic acid molecules and methods for the production of the antibodies. Furthermore, the invention provides pharmaceutical compositions comprising bispecific single chain antibodies of the invention, medical uses of the same and methods for the treatment of diseases comprising the administration of bispecific single chain antibodies of the invention.

title: Application of EBV BRLF1 and functional small peptide thereof in inhibition of immune inflammation
text: Application of EBV BRLF1 and functional small peptide thereof in inhibition of immune inflammation. The invention discloses an application of BRLF1 in preparation of herpes virus 5'ppp RNA and downstream interferon related immune gene synthesis inhibitors thereof. The invention also discloses an application of an BRLF1 functional small peptide in preparation of herpes virus 5'ppp RNA and the downstream interferon related immune gene synthesis inhibitors thereof. After the small peptide is coupled with a penetrating signal peptide, the small peptidehas good penetrating property, and the small peptide can directly enter cells, so that the effect of inhibiting the immune response activity of herpes virus activation is better exerted.

title: Novel Nucleic Acid Constructs Containing Orthogonal Site Selective Recombinases (OSSRs)
text: Novel Nucleic Acid Constructs Containing Orthogonal Site Selective Recombinases (OSSRs). The present invention provides for a recombinant nucleic acid comprising a nucleotide sequence comprising a plurality of constructs, wherein each construct independently comprises a nucleotide sequence of interest flanked by a pair of recombinase recognition sequences. Each pair of recombinase recognition sequences is recognized by a distinct recombinase. Optionally, each construct can, independently, further comprise one or more genes encoding a recombinase capable of recognizing the pair of recombinase recognition sequences of the construct. The recombinase can be an orthogonal (non-cross reacting), site-selective recombinase (OSSR).

title: Construction method and application of conditionally replicative Ad5/35 chimeric oncolytic adenovirus
text: Construction method and application of conditionally replicative Ad5/35 chimeric oncolytic adenovirus. The invention provides a construction method and application of conditionally replicative Ad5/35 chimeric oncolytic adenovirus, and belongs to the technical field of genetic engineering. The construction method comprises the following steps: carrying out enzyme digestion on a PoAd5 plasmid by endonuclease AsiSI; the method comprises the following steps: carrying out in-vitro gene synthesis on a chimeric Ad5/35-Hexon (HVR1 + 5) gene segment, carrying out homologous recombination on the chimeric Ad5/35-Hexon (HVR1 + 5) gene segment and linear PoAd5 co-transfected BJ5183 to obtain PoAd5/35-H, carrying out ClaI enzyme digestion, carrying out in-vitro gene synthesis on Ad5/35-Fiber, and carrying out homologous recombination on the Ad5/35-Fiber and linear PoAd5/35-H co-transfected BJ5183 to obtain a final plasmid oAd5/35-HF; and carrying out PacI enzyme digestion, purifying, and transfecting an HEK293 cell to obtain the conditionally replicative Ad5/35 chimeric oncolytic adenovirus. The invention provides an innovative adenovirus vector construction scheme for enhancing the infection ability and the treatment effect of viruses.

title: A NOVEL GENETIC DEVICE TO ENGINEER CELL BEHAVIOR
text: A NOVEL GENETIC DEVICE TO ENGINEER CELL BEHAVIOR. This invention is related to the design and production of novel genetic device to control cell behavior. This device can be used to engineer eukaryotic and prokaryotic cells, so it has a wide variety of application from biotechnological to biomedical science and biomedical engendering. Also this invention is related to the production of novel genetic circuit and recombined DNA to control cell behavior. These genetic circuits and recombined DNA can be used to engineer eukaryotic and prokaryotic cells, so it has a wide variety of application from biotechnological to biomedical science and biomedical engendering. Also this invention is contains novel methods to solve some pharmaceutical, biopharmaceutical, and medical obstacles as miRNA delivery system and develop cancer therapeutics. Also this invention contains novel methods related to the production of synthetic promoters.

title: SYNTHESIS OF LONG NUCLEIC ACID SEQUENCES
text: SYNTHESIS OF LONG NUCLEIC ACID SEQUENCES. The invention provides methods for the synthesis of long oligonucleotides, genes and gene fragments. The methods include the manufacture of genes or gene fragments that can be then inserted into a variety of vectors.

title: Insulin gene synthesis protein releases extracellular model
text: Insulin gene synthesis protein releases extracellular model. The utility model belongs to the technical field of the teaching aid and specifically relates to insulin gene synthesis protein releases extracellular model. Meet " the coordination between the organelle " or " synthesis, processing, the transportation of secretion albumen " for solving the student in learning process, always feel very abstract, be difficult to understand, the problem of having half knowledge. The utility model discloses a conventional material makes the model of insulin building -up process, duplicate module, ribosomal translation module, endoplasmic reticulum including base plate, DNA and tell the module to the thick processing module of peptide chain, dictyosome's whole processing module and born of the same parents. The utility model discloses be easily understood, easily the student starts to make, can temper teenagers' hands -on ability and the mutual cooperative ability between classmates, helps the teaching.

title: Totally-gene-synthesized dog alpha interferon gene and protein expression
text: Totally-gene-synthesized dog alpha interferon gene and protein expression. The invention relates to a dog alpha interferon gene obtained by utilizing total gene synthesis technology and a prokaryotic expression of the interferon protein, which belong to the field of bio-pharmaceuticals. The invention comprises a technical scheme, which comprises: improvement on a codon of a dog alpha interferon gene sequence, the total gene synthesis of the sequence, the construction ofa prokaryotic expression carrier containing the dog alpha interferon gene, the conversion of colon bacillus with the prokaryotic expression carrier containing the dog alpha interferon gene for induction expression, protein extraction, denaturation and renaturation, and research on antiviral activity. Interferon obtained in tests can resist the attack of vesicular stomatitis viruses and has specific activity reaching 1.67*107U/mg. A method for producing the interferon has the advantages of simple process, high expression level and high biological activity.

title: METHOD FOR THE FERMENTATIVE PRODUCTION OF GUANIDINOACETIC ACID
text: METHOD FOR THE FERMENTATIVE PRODUCTION OF GUANIDINOACETIC ACID. A microorganism is transformed to be capable of producing guanidinoacetic acid (GAA). A method can be used for the fermentative production of GAA using such a microorganism. A corresponding method can be used for the fermentative production of creatine.

title: Genome-wide construction of schizosaccharomyces pombe heterozygous deletion mutants containing gene-specific barcodes by the methods of 4-round serial or block pcr, or total gene synthesis thereof
text: Genome-wide construction of schizosaccharomyces pombe heterozygous deletion mutants containing gene-specific barcodes by the methods of 4-round serial or block pcr, or total gene synthesis thereof. A method comprising transforming Schizosaccharomyces pombe with a deletion cassette, constructed by four-round serial PCR, block PCR or total gene synthesis, containing a homologous recombination site is provided for preparing gene-targeted heterozygous deletion Schizosaccharomyces pombe. Also provided are gene-targeted hetero2ygous deletion Schizosaccharomyces pombe mutants prepared by the method, and a library of gene-targeted heterozygous deletion Schizosaccharomyces pombe mutants. Further, the library is useful in constructing a method and a kit for screening a drug's modes of action.

title: COMPLETE OXIDATION OF SUGARS TO ELECTRICITY BY USING CELL-FREE SYNTHETIC ENZYMATIC PATHWAYS
text: COMPLETE OXIDATION OF SUGARS TO ELECTRICITY BY USING CELL-FREE SYNTHETIC ENZYMATIC PATHWAYS

title: Quality inspection method of liquid drop generation device, electronic equipment and storage medium
text: Quality inspection method of liquid drop generation device, electronic equipment and storage medium. The invention relates to the field of bioengineering, and provides a quality inspection method of a liquid drop generation device, electronic equipment and a storage medium, the method comprises the following steps: collecting a spray head image of a liquid drop, and obtaining a reference coordinate of a spray head; setting a liquid drop generation parameter, generating a liquid drop image, and collecting the liquid drop image; extracting quantitative characterization data of liquid drops in the liquid drop image, and generating a characteristic matrix of the liquid drops; normalizing the feature matrix, and calculating the stability feature of the liquid drop; and evaluating the droplet generation device according to the feature matrix and the stability feature data. According to the scheme, quality control can be effectively carried out on the droplet generation link in the gene synthesis process, through the image recognition and analysis technology, precision and stability evaluation of the device is achieved, the evaluation process is automatic and precise, and the efficiency and quality of gene synthesis work can be effectively improved.

title: ELECTRONIC DEVICES FOR POLYMER SYNTHESIS AND ASSEMBLY
text: ELECTRONIC DEVICES FOR POLYMER SYNTHESIS AND ASSEMBLY.
