title: Method for the manufacture of DNA
text: Method for the manufacture of DNA. The present invention relates to a method for manufacturing DNA comprising the steps of preparing n single stranded base-DNA-oligonucleotides which form immediately consecutive parts of the nucleotide sequence of the DNA being manufactured, in which the second to the n-th base-DNA-oligonucleotide is phosphorylated at the 5' end and n is at least 2; preparing at least (n-1) single stranded joint-DNA-oligonucleotides capable of functioning as ligation templates for the base-DNA-oligonucleotides; contacting the base-DNA-oligonucleotides with the joint-DNA-oligonucleotides; subjecting the resultant product DNA-hybrid to a ligation reaction; and finally subjecting the resultant reaction product to an exonuclease reaction, in which the DNA strand of the reaction product formed by ligated base-DNA-oligonucleotides includes at least two cap-structures. Further the invention relates to DNA obtained by the method and a kit for carrying out the method.

title: Phosphatidylinositol proteoglycan 3 monoclonal antibody, hybridoma cell strain and application
text: Phosphatidylinositol proteoglycan 3 monoclonal antibody, hybridoma cell strain and application. The invention discloses a monoclonal antibody, a hybridoma cell strain for generating the monoclonal antibody and application. The monoclonal antibody is generated by GPC3 full-length protein antigen immunization according to a whole-gene synthesis method and can be specifically combined with phosphatidylinositol proteoglycan3 (GPC3). The monoclonal antibody disclosed by the invention is high in specificity and high in sensitivity, can be used for detecting trace expressed GPC3 protein, can be used as a reference index for clinical early diagnosis of liver cancer patients, and can be widely applied to clinical detection and treatment.

title: PROKARYOTIC 2-COMPONENT SIGNALING PATHWAYS FOR USE AS LOGIC GATES IN MAMMALIAN CELLS
text: PROKARYOTIC 2-COMPONENT SIGNALING PATHWAYS FOR USE AS LOGIC GATES IN MAMMALIAN CELLS. The invention relates to mammalian cells comprising at least one prokaryotic two-component signaling (TCS) pathway comprised of an activator protein A, a response regulator (RR) protein B activated by said protein A, such activation leading to an activated RR protein B, and an output gene C operably linked to a promoter. Transcription from said promoter is activated by activated RR protein B, and the expression of output gene C defines at least a first state (0, no transcription) and a second state (1, detectable transcription). The invention further relates to logic gates designed from such cells, and methods for integrating a plurality of output signals based on the cells and logic gates of the invention.

title: Human GLUT5 stable-transfection cell strain and application thereof in screening of GLUT5 inhibitor
text: Human GLUT5 stable-transfection cell strain and application thereof in screening of GLUT5 inhibitor. The invention relates to a human GLUT5 stable-transfection cell strain and application thereof in screening of a GLUT5 inhibitor. On the basis of successfully constructing the human GLUT5 stable-transfection cell strain by using technologies such as gene synthesis, molecular biology and the like, the invention discloses application and a method for screening the GLUT5 inhibitor by using the human GLUT5 stable-transfection cell strain, and a new way is found for searching a GLUT5 selective inhibitor.

title: Method for recombinant production of procambarus clarkii ATP synthase F0 subunit 6 protein
text: Method for recombinant production of procambarus clarkii ATP synthase F0 subunit 6 protein. The invention relates to a method for recombinant production of procambarus clarkii ATP synthase F0 subunit 6 protein, and belongs to the technical field of gene engineering. According to the method, an atp6 gene is obtained through whole-gene synthesis, a recombinant expression vector pColdII-atp6 is constructed and transforms an escherichia coli expression strain BL21(DE3) to achieve efficient expression, and then an important foundation is laid for the following intensive study for the molecular mechanism of the procambarus clarkii ovary 'eyestalk excision effect'.

title: A METHOD FOR PRODUCING PROBIOTIC BACTERIA WITH MAGNETIC CHARACTERISTIC THAT SPECIFICALLY TARGETS CANCER CELLS
text: A METHOD FOR PRODUCING PROBIOTIC BACTERIA WITH MAGNETIC CHARACTERISTIC THAT SPECIFICALLY TARGETS CANCER CELLS. The present invention relates to a method (100) for enabling the deposition of magnetite in a probiotic bacterial strain that can colonize in tumor masses and does not cause septic shock, and the use of magnetite-loaded probiotic bacteria in magnetic resonance imaging and treatment of targeted magnetic resonance hyperthermia.

title: Method for synthesizing and assembling in-vitro large-fragment genome DNA (deoxyribonucleic acid)
text: Method for synthesizing and assembling in-vitro large-fragment genome DNA (deoxyribonucleic acid). The invention discloses an in-vitro large-fragment genome DNA synthesis and assembly method, which adopts a double-stranded DNA cohesive end generation system, and the generation system comprises a DNA assembly sequence, a single-stranded guide DNA, a nicking endonuclease A, a nicking endonuclease B and a nicking endonuclease C, the DNA assembly sequence comprises a first nicking endonuclease recognition site unit, a target DNA sequence and a second nicking endonuclease recognition site unit from the 5'end to the 3 'end, dividing the genome sequence into n target DNA sequences, preparing the DNA assembly sequences, designing and synthesizing single-stranded guide DNA, adding nicking endonuclease A, nicking endonuclease B and nicking endonuclease C into each DNA assembly sequence and single-stranded guide DNA, and carrying out one-stranded guide DNA synthesis to obtain a nicking endonuclease recognition site unit, a nicking endonuclease recognition site unit, a nicking endonuclease recognition site unit and a nicking endonuclease recognition site unit; performing enzyme digestion treatment; and carrying out DNA in-vitro assembly on the DNA assembly sequence after enzyme digestion. The method overcomes the limitation that restriction endonuclease generates a short cantilever and improves the success rate of large DNA molecule assembly.

title: Gas Biosensors
text: Gas Biosensors. Microbial biosensors that generate gas outputs in hard-to-image materials using exogenous methyl halide transferase (WIT) genes. By varying the promoter that is fused to the MHT gene, biosensors for different triggers can be made.

title: MULTI-SECTION DNA MOLECULE ASSEMBLY METHOD AND APPLICATION OF THE METHOD
text: MULTI-SECTION DNA MOLECULE ASSEMBLY METHOD AND APPLICATION OF THE METHOD. Provided is a multi-section DNA molecule assembly method, comprising the following steps: constructing a carrier system; cloning target sequences Lm and Rn and entering a supply carrier B1 and/or a supply carrier B2, and an accepting carrier A1 and the supply carrier B1 and/or the supply carrier B2 for multi-section assembly. Also provided is an application of the method in gene synthesis, large-section DNA cloning, and multi-gene carrier construction.

title: A GENE SYNTHESIS KIT
text: A GENE SYNTHESIS KIT. This disclosure is directed to the field of polynucleotide synthesis, and the embodiments taught herein are generally directed to a kit for use in the design of a desired polynucleotide from information obtained from a polypeptide or another polynucleotide, the generation of a custom set of oligonucleotides that complement the design, and the ordering of the custom set of oligonucleotides. The invention includes systems and methods for producing a desired polynucleotide using the kit.

title: METHOD FOR PRODUCING RECOMBINANT PROTEIN BY USING PDI DERIVED FROM TRICHODERMA SP. STRAIN
text: METHOD FOR PRODUCING RECOMBINANT PROTEIN BY USING PDI DERIVED FROM TRICHODERMA SP. STRAIN. The present invention relates to a method for producing a recombinant protein by using a PDI derived from Trichoderma sp. strain. A variant yeast comprising a gene that encodes a PDI derived from Trichoderma sp. (the genus Trichoderma) strain, of the present invention, highly expresses a target protein, and thus is used to produce a target recombinant protein at a high yield.

title: SYSTEMS FOR CELL PROGRAMMING AND METHODS THEREOF
text: SYSTEMS FOR CELL PROGRAMMING AND METHODS THEREOF. Provided herein are systems of modulating gene expression and methods of use thereof for converting cells of one type to another type (e.g. reprogramming of stem cells into tissue-specific progenitor cells).

title: GENE SYNTHESIS METHOD
text: GENE SYNTHESIS METHOD. The present invention relates to polymerase chain reaction (PCR)-based methods for the one-step synthesis of nucleic acid molecules, wherein the amplification primers used in said methods are designed such that they have two distinct melting temperatures in order to minimize the competition between polymerase cycling assembly (PCA) and polymerase chain reaction (PCR) amplification in the one-step nucleic acid synthesis and to maximize the emerging full-length amplification, as well as kits for use in such methods.

title: METHOD FOR THE FERMENTATIVE PRODUCTION OF GUANIDINOACETIC ACID USING A MICROORGANISM COMPRISING A HETEROLOGOUS L-THREONINE ALDOLASE GENE
text: METHOD FOR THE FERMENTATIVE PRODUCTION OF GUANIDINOACETIC ACID USING A MICROORGANISM COMPRISING A HETEROLOGOUS L-THREONINE ALDOLASE GENE. The present invention concerns a microorganism comprising at least one heterologous gene coding for a L-arginine:glycine amidinotransferase (AGAT) and at least one heterologous L-5 threonine aldolase gene (lta) and a method for the fermentative production of guanidinoacetic acid (GAA) using such microorganism. The present invention also relates to a method for the fermentative production of creatine.

title: GENOME-WIDE CONSTRUCTION OF SCHIZOSACCHAROMYCES POMBE HETEROZYGOUS DELETION MUTANTS CONTAINING GENE-SPECIFIC BARCODES BY THE METHODS OF 4-ROUND SERIAL OR BLOCK PCR, OR TOTAL GENE SYNTHESIS THEREOF
text: GENOME-WIDE CONSTRUCTION OF SCHIZOSACCHAROMYCES POMBE HETEROZYGOUS DELETION MUTANTS CONTAINING GENE-SPECIFIC BARCODES BY THE METHODS OF 4-ROUND SERIAL OR BLOCK PCR, OR TOTAL GENE SYNTHESIS THEREOF

title: NUCLEIC ACID LINKERS AND THEIR USE IN GENE SYNTHESIS
text: NUCLEIC ACID LINKERS AND THEIR USE IN GENE SYNTHESIS. The present invention relates to a single-stranded nucleic acid molecule for use in a method for the production of a nucleic acid, whereby the nucleic acid molecule comprises a part A and a part B, whereby part A comprises a sequence, which corresponds at least to a partial sequence of the recognition site of a type IIS restriction enzyme, and part B comprises an arbitrary but defined sequence of nucleotides. By using such nucleic acid molecules it is possible to assemble different fragments in a sequence-independent manner and thus conduct the synthesis of a nucleic acid with recourse to standardized elements.

title: SoxRS Genetic Circuit Containg SoxRS varient and Screening Method of thereof
text: SoxRS Genetic Circuit Containg SoxRS varient and Screening Method of thereof. The present invention relates to a genetic circuit containing a SoxRS mutant for increasing the accuracy of ultrahigh speed screening technology of target genes. The genetic circuit for the ultrahigh speed screening of an enzyme of the present invention can prevent the expression of target protein early in saturation, thereby significantly expanding the range of results on FACS, and improving the accuracy of the ultrahigh speed screening technology of enzymes through ultrahigh speed flow cell separators (FACS). Therefore, the genetic circuit of the present invention can be effectively used to search for biotechnologically, medically and industrially useful proteins.

title: ENGINEERED DCAS9 WITH REDUCED TOXICITY AND ITS USE IN GENETIC CIRCUITS
text: ENGINEERED DCAS9 WITH REDUCED TOXICITY AND ITS USE IN GENETIC CIRCUITS. Disclosed herein are novel CRISPR/dCas9-based fusion proteins that facilitate the scaling up of genetic circuits, including those with non-linear response curves. The dCas9- based fusion proteins produce significantly less toxicity in comparison to previously described CRISPR/Cas9-based proteins used with logic gates. These improvements enable the generation of complex genetic circuits when both digital response curves and large amounts of dCas9 protein are needed. Also disclosed herein are methods of regulating expression of an output sequence through the introduction of novel CRISPR/dCas-9-based fusion proteins and genetic circuits into a cell.

title: Pseudomonas fluorescens strain and applications thereof
text: Pseudomonas fluorescens strain and applications thereof. The invention discloses a pseudomonas fluorescens strain G7. The strain is preserved in the China Center for Type Culture Collection, and is assigned with the accession number of CCTCC NO:M 2018359. The crude extract of the strain has the inhibiting capacity for various bacteria, meanwhile, the whole genome sequencing result analysis for the strain discloses that two novel ribosome-independent polypeptide synthetase type antibiotic gene synthesis clusters are coded, and therefore, the strain has the potential of being used for preparing bacteriostatic agents and antibiotics.

title: NUCLEIC ACID LINKERS AND USE THEREOF IN GENE SYNTHESIS
text: NUCLEIC ACID LINKERS AND USE THEREOF IN GENE SYNTHESIS. The invention relates to a single-stranded nucleic acid molecule used in a method for the production of a nucleic acid. The nucleic acid molecule comprises part A and part B. Part A comprises a sequence which corresponds at least to one partial sequence of the recognition sequence of a restriction enzyme of type IIS and part B comprises any particular but defined sequence of nucleotides. By using said nucleic molecules it is possible to link various partial sequences in a sequence-independent manner and thus carry out effective synthesis of a nucleic acid by resorting to standardized elements.

title: Derivatized versions of ligase enzymes for constructing DNA sequences
text: Derivatized versions of ligase enzymes for constructing DNA sequences. A method of making very long, double-stranded synthetic poly-nucleotides. A multiplicity of short oligonucleotides is provided. The short oligonucleotides are sequentially hybridized to each other. Enzymatic ligation of the oligonucleotides provides a contiguous piece of PCR-ready DNA of predetermined sequence.

title: NUCLEIC ACID LINKERS AND THEIR USE IN GENE SYNTHESIS
text: NUCLEIC ACID LINKERS AND THEIR USE IN GENE SYNTHESIS. The invention relates to a single-stranded nucleic acid molecule used in a method for the production of a nucleic acid. The nucleic acid molecule comprises part A and part B. Part A comprises a sequence which corresponds a t least to one partial sequence of the recognition sequence of a restriction enzyme of type IIS and part B comprises any particular but defined sequence of nucleotides. By using said nucleic molecules it is possible to link various partial sequences in a sequence-independent manner and thus carry out effective synthesis of a nucleic acid by resorting to standardized elements.

title: CROSS-SPECIES-SPECIFIC BISPECIFIC BINDERS
text: CROSS-SPECIES-SPECIFIC BISPECIFIC BINDERS. The present invention relates to a polypeptide comprising a first human binding domain capable of binding to an epitope of human and non-chimpanzee primate CD3 (epsilon) chain and a second binding domain capable of binding to EGFR, Her2/neu or IgE of a human and/or a non-chimpanzee primate as well as to a process for the production of the mentioned polypeptide. The invention further relates to nucleic acids encoding for the polypeptide, to vectors comprising the same and to host cells comprising the vector. In another aspect, the invention provides for a pharmaceutical composition comprising the mentioned polypeptide and medical uses of the polypeptide.

title: GENE SYNTHESIS BY CONVERGENT ASSEMBLY OF OLIGONUCLEOTIDE SUBSETS
text: GENE SYNTHESIS BY CONVERGENT ASSEMBLY OF OLIGONUCLEOTIDE SUBSETS. The invention provides a system and method for synthesizing polynucleotides by solid phase assembly oligonucleotide precursors, in accordance with the method, a polynucleotide is partitioned into an ordered set of subunits, wherein each subunit is assembled in a single reaction from a subset of oligonucleotide precursors that uniquely anneal together to produce the subunit. The subunits are then assembled to form the desired polynucleotide. An important feature of the invention is the selection of subunits that are free of undesired sequence elements, such as palindromes, repetitive sequences, and the like, which would result in more than one subunit product alter ligating a pool of oligonucleotide precursors.

title: NUCLEIC ACID LINKERS AND USE THEREOF IN GENE SYNTHESIS
text: NUCLEIC ACID LINKERS AND USE THEREOF IN GENE SYNTHESIS. The invention relates to a single-stranded nucleic acid molecule used in a method for the production of a nucleic acid. The nucleic acid molecule comprises part A and part B. Part A comprises a sequence which corresponds at least to one partial sequence of the recognition sequence of a restriction enzyme of type IIS and part B comprises any particular but defined sequence of nucleotides. By using said nucleic molecules it is possible to link various partial sequences in a sequence-independent manner and thus carry out effective synthesis of a nucleic acid by resorting to standardized elements.

title: GENETIC CIRCUIT FOR DNA MUTATION DETECTION AND RESPONSE
text: GENETIC CIRCUIT FOR DNA MUTATION DETECTION AND RESPONSE. The present disclosure generally relates to cancer therapies and, in some embodiments, to using genetic circuits designed for targeted cancer therapies. The genetic circuit, in some embodiments, utilizes a suitable vector capable of delivering DNA sequences comprising functional units comprising a transcriptional promoter, target sequences and a transcriptional terminator. Together, the genetic circuit, in some embodiments, may trigger apoptosis — a target protein activity where cell death occurs — in cancer cells, and protect non-cancer cells from apoptosis. In some embodiments, the genetic circuit may direct target protein activity in cancer cells, while not directing protein activity in non-cancer cells. However, in the presence of certain stimuli, the inhibition is relieved, which may lead to the induction of target protein activity in cancer cells. In addition, some embodiments are generally directed to vectors comprising a first sequence encoding at least a portion of Mdm2 and a promoter, and a second sequence encoding a promoter and a fusion protein comprising at least a portion of p53 and iCasp-9 (or another protein capable of achieving a therapeutic objective), optionally connected by a linker sequence capable of allowing each protein subunit of the fusion protein to be independently active but subject to the same proteolytic pathways as p53 in cancer and non-cancer cells. Other aspects are generally directed to methods of making or using such compositions, kits including such compositions, or the like.

title: Deoxyribonucleic acid (DNA) assembly vector guided by specific nucleotide sequence and mediated by nicking enzyme and application of DNA assembly vector
text: Deoxyribonucleic acid (DNA) assembly vector guided by specific nucleotide sequence and mediated by nicking enzyme and application of DNA assembly vector. The invention discloses a DNA (deoxyribonucleic acid) assembly vector guided by a specific nucleotide sequence and mediated by nicking enzyme and application of the DNA assembly vector. The DNA assembly vector comprises a nicking enzyme assembly element (UNiE) which consists of a specific nucleotide sequence, a gene expression box with a selection marking function and nicking enzyme sites and is guided by the specific nucleotide sequence, and can be used for cloning long-fragment DNA and efficiently assembling multiple gene fragments. According to the invention, the UNiE element is combined with the multi-gene superposition system (TGSII), a novel multi-gene superposition system (TGSII-UNiE) with higher assembly efficiency is constructed, efficient assembly of multiple genes is realized, and the method has the characteristics of high efficiency, simplicity and convenience in operation and low cost.

title: Compound-linker constructs comprising novel compounds useful as STING agonists and uses thereof
text: Compound-linker constructs comprising novel compounds useful as STING agonists and uses thereof. Provided herein are compound-linker constructs and antibody-drug conjugates of formula (Y-1), (Y-2), (Y-3), (A), (B), (C), I, II, III, IV, or V, which are useful as modulators of STING (stimulators of interferon genes). Synthesis, compositions and uses of such compound-linker constructs and antibody-drug conjugates are also provided.

title: SOFTWARE FOR DESIGN AND VERIFICATION OF SYNTHETIC GENETIC CONSTRUCTS
text: SOFTWARE FOR DESIGN AND VERIFICATION OF SYNTHETIC GENETIC CONSTRUCTS. The present invention provides methods for designing and verifying nucleic acid molecules having one or more desired properties. The methods are typically encoded into software, and typically include use of databases and algorithms to determine if nucleic acid molecules designed to have various elements in functional relationships have the intended properties. The result is achieved by determining if the various elements of the designed nucleic acid are in the correct order and physical relationship to other elements, and that the proper elements are selected. Computer systems for implementing the method, as well as business methods for reaping monetary gain from use of the methods, are also disclosed.

title: NOVEL NUCLEIC ACID CONSTRUCTS CONTAINING ORTHOGONAL SITE SELECTIVE RECOMBINASES (OSSRs)
text: NOVEL NUCLEIC ACID CONSTRUCTS CONTAINING ORTHOGONAL SITE SELECTIVE RECOMBINASES (OSSRs). The present invention provides for a recombinant nucleic acid comprising a nucleotide sequence comprising a plurality of constructs, wherein each construct independently comprises a nucleotide sequence of interest flanked by a pair of recombinase recognition sequences. Each pair of recombinase recognition sequences is recognized by a distinct recombinase. Optionally, each construct can, independently, further comprise one or more genes encoding a recombinase capable of recognizing the pair of recombinase recognition sequences of the construct. The recombinase can be an orthogonal (non-cross reacting), site-selective recombinase (OSSR).

title: PROKARYOTIC 2-COMPONENT SIGNALING PATHWAYS FOR USE AS LOGIC GATES IN MAMMALIAN CELLS
text: PROKARYOTIC 2-COMPONENT SIGNALING PATHWAYS FOR USE AS LOGIC GATES IN MAMMALIAN CELLS. The invention relates to mammalian cells comprising at least one prokaryotic two-component signaling (TCS) pathway comprised of an activator protein A, a response regulator (RR) protein B activated by said protein A, such activation leading to an activated RR protein B, and an output gene C operably linked to a promoter. Transcription from said promoter is activated by activated RR protein B, and the expression of output gene C defines at least a first state (0, no transcription) and a second state (1, detectable transcription). The invention further relates to logic gates designed from such cells, and methods for integrating a plurality of output signals based on the cells and logic gates of the invention.

title: Biomarkers of benA and cyp51A gene for the molecular diagnosis of clinical Aspergillus and genetic synthetic control template for the qualitative and quantitative analysis
text: Biomarkers of benA and cyp51A gene for the molecular diagnosis of clinical Aspergillus and genetic synthetic control template for the qualitative and quantitative analysis. The present invention relates to biomarkers of benA and Cyp51A genes for molecular diagnosis of major clinical Aspergillus species, and a gene synthesis composite of a control group for qualitative and quantitative analysis. More specifically, the present invention uses benA and cyp51A gene biomarkers for molecular diagnosis of Aspergillus species to quickly and molecularly diagnose Aspergillus strains causing aspergillosis, identifies azole-resistant Aspergillus strains, so as to enable effective treatment of aspergillosis through the selection of an appropriate antifungal agent.

title: METHOD FOR PREPARATION OF DELETION CASSETTE USING SYNTHESIS OF GENE COMPRISING ARTIFICIAL SEQUENCES LINKERS
text: METHOD FOR PREPARATION OF DELETION CASSETTE USING SYNTHESIS OF GENE COMPRISING ARTIFICIAL SEQUENCES LINKERS. PURPOSE: A method for manufacturing a deletion cassette by gene synthesis, containing an artificial sequence linker is provided to simplify production process and prepare heterozygote gene targeting strain. CONSTITUTION: A method for manufacturing a deletion cassette comprises: a step of designing an oligomer of gene fragment containing an artificial sequence linker; a step of performing oligomer phosphorylation and hybridizing the fragment using LCR(ligase chain reaction); a step of performing PCR using a primer pair; a step of removing the artificial sequence linker existing at both terminals of the deletion cassette.

title: GENOME-WIDE CONSTRUCTION OF SCHIZOSACCHAROMYCES POMBE HETEROZYGOUS DELETION MUTANTS CONTAINING GENE-SPECIFIC BARCODES BY THE METHODS OF 4-ROUND SERIAL OR BLOCK PCR, OR TOTAL GENE SYNTHESIS THEREOF
text: GENOME-WIDE CONSTRUCTION OF SCHIZOSACCHAROMYCES POMBE HETEROZYGOUS DELETION MUTANTS CONTAINING GENE-SPECIFIC BARCODES BY THE METHODS OF 4-ROUND SERIAL OR BLOCK PCR, OR TOTAL GENE SYNTHESIS THEREOF

title: Genetically engineered bacterium for high expression of Procambarus clarkia ATP synthase F0 subunit 8
text: Genetically engineered bacterium for high expression of Procambarus clarkia ATP synthase F0 subunit 8. The invention relates to a method for high expression of a Procambarus clarkia ATP synthase F0 subunit 8 protein by the aid of an Escherichia coli expression system and belongs to the technical field of genetic engineering. An atp8 gene is obtained through total gene synthesis, a recombinant expression vector pColdII-atp8 is constructed and an Escherichia coli expression strain BL21(DE3) is converted for high expression, and an important foundation is laid for subsequent deep research on a molecular mechanism of the Procambarus clarkia ovary eyestalk-ablation effect.

title: METHOD OF ON-CHIP NUCLEIC ACID MOLECULE SYNTHESIS
text: METHOD OF ON-CHIP NUCLEIC ACID MOLECULE SYNTHESIS. A method of synthesizing a nucleic acid molecule, such as a gene, on a substrate or microchip is described. In particular, a method for synthesizing, amplifying, and assembling DNA oligonucleotides into a nucleic acid molecule or gene product, on a single substrate or microchip is described. Also described are a method of correcting a sequence error in a synthesized nucleic acid molecule, as well as a method for synthesizing and screening a library of codon variants to identify a nucleic acid molecule with an optimized level of protein expression.

title: Recombinant bacteria of coding macrotherm phytase gene, synthesis, cloning and expression of said gene
text: Recombinant bacteria of coding macrotherm phytase gene, synthesis, cloning and expression of said gene. The invention relates to a recombination and the gene and its compounding, cloning and expression of a coding high temperature phytase gene that could gain a coded high temperature phytase gene NcpyN, supply nucleotide sequence, recombinant plasmid pUC-NcphyN and yeast expression carrier pNCphyN, and the recombination yeast of pNCphyN to gain recombination by chemical and biology method. The invention could be used to improve the level and quality of phytase and achieve the purpose of improving species quality.

title: NOVEL METHOD FOR SEARCHING AND QUANTIFYING CELLULASE USING REDESIGNED GENETIC CIRCUIT
text: NOVEL METHOD FOR SEARCHING AND QUANTIFYING CELLULASE USING REDESIGNED GENETIC CIRCUIT. The present invention relates to a novel method for searching cellulase using a redesigned genetic circuit and, more specifically, to a novel method for searching cellulase using a redesigned genetic circuit and a method for quantifying cellobiose degraded by the cellulase. When the redesigned genetic circuit of the present invention is used, the cellobiose recognition transcription regulation protein (CelR), which is proportional to the amount of cellobiose, induces the expression of a reporter protein, thereby promptly sensing and quantifying cellobiose with high sensitivity, and thus the present invention can be utilized for cellobiose sensing and quantitative analysis, and enables the measurement of the activity of exo-type cellulase which frees cellobiose and the search of new exo-type cellulase.

title: TRANSCRIPTIONAL CONTROL IN PROKARYOTIC CELLS USING DNA-BINDING REPRESSORS
text: TRANSCRIPTIONAL CONTROL IN PROKARYOTIC CELLS USING DNA-BINDING REPRESSORS. The present disclosure relates generally to methods and compositions for transferring a genetic circuit from one prokaryotic cell (donor cell) to another prokaryotic cell (recipient cell or target cell which are used interchangeably herein). More specifically, the present disclosure relates to prokaryotic donor cells comprising (i) a genetic circuit of interest and (ii) one or more expressed transcriptional repressor proteins and the use of said donor cells in the efficient transfer of the genetic circuit into a prokaryotic recipient cell. The genetic circuit includes nucleic acid sequences encoding a RNA molecule or protein of interest.

title: Microbe Prototyping Genetic Circuit based Novel Microbe Screening System
text: Microbe Prototyping Genetic Circuit based Novel Microbe Screening System. The present invention relates to a genetic circuit including a gene for encoding a transcription regulatory factor expressed by sensing phenol and a lower level reporter gene operated by the transcription regulatory factor; a sensor cell including the genetic circuit; and a method for sensing and searching a novel microorganism strain or a gene source containing the activities of a direct target enzyme in a natural sample by using the sensor cell and a phenol-tag substrate. The genetic circuit and the sensor cell including the same according to the present invention include a fluorescent protein and a nutrition demand-related enzyme for controlling the survival of the sensor cell as reporter genes. Thus, through a simple method for measuring fluorescence by culturing a random microbe strain having the activities of the sensor cell and a desired target enzyme together, a novel microbe strain or a gene source having the activities of a corresponding target enzyme can be easily identified. Also, by introducing such a dual reporter system, the presence of the activity of a target enzyme can be visibly and more clearly confirmed. Moreover, when a useful resource is directly searched in nature, a DNA separating and library constructing step is skipped, and the use of an antibiotic agent is suggested. Thus, a possibility of the expression of a target enzyme gene and the easiness of an experiment are maximized. By directly using a natural high active microbe, a GMO/LMO problem caused by a recombinant microbe can be minimized.

title: SOFTWARE FOR DESIGN AND VERIFICATION OF SYNTHETIC GENETIC CONSTRUCTS
text: SOFTWARE FOR DESIGN AND VERIFICATION OF SYNTHETIC GENETIC CONSTRUCTS. The present invention provides methods for designing and verifying nucleic acid molecules having one or more desired properties. The methods are typically encoded into software, and typically include use of databases and algorithms to determine if nucleic acid molecules designed to have various elements in functional relationships have the intended properties. The result is achieved by determining if the various elements of the designed nucleic acid are in the correct order and physical relationship to other elements, and that the proper elements are selected. Computer systems for implementing the method, as well as business methods for reaping monetary gain from use of the methods, are also disclosed.

title: Cascade genetic circuit useful for regulated expression of cloned genes, comprises several nucleic acid constructs encoding regulators arranged in order and final target promoter responsive in dose-dependent fashion
text: Cascade genetic circuit useful for regulated expression of cloned genes, comprises several nucleic acid constructs encoding regulators arranged in order and final target promoter responsive in dose-dependent fashion. A cascade genetic circuit (I), comprising nucleic acid constructs (II) encoding several transcriptional regulators arranged in a hierarchical order so that expression of an upstream regulator stimulates expression of a downstream regulator, and a final target promoter (III) responsive in a dose-dependent fashion to a terminal downstream regulator, is new. Independent claims are also included for the following: (1) a cell comprising (I) and (2) regulating the expression of a nucleic acid, comprising: (a) establishing a genetic circuit according to the novel method (b) placing the sequence under the control of (III) and (c) inducing the cascade genetic circuit to stimulate expression of the sequence.

title: TRANSCRIPTIONAL CONTROL IN PROKARYOTIC CELLS USING DNA-BINDING REPRESSORS
text: TRANSCRIPTIONAL CONTROL IN PROKARYOTIC CELLS USING DNA-BINDING REPRESSORS. To provide, generally, methods and compositions for transferring a genetic circuit from one prokaryotic cell ("donor cell") to another prokaryotic cell ("recipient cell" or "target cell" used interchangeably herein).SOLUTION: The present disclosure relates to: prokaryotic donor cells comprising (i) a genetic circuit of interest and (ii) one or more expressed transcriptional repressor proteins; and the use of the donor cells in efficient transfer of the genetic circuit into a prokaryotic recipient cell. The genetic circuit includes nucleic acid sequences encoding an RNA molecule or protein of interest.SELECTED DRAWING: None

The invention further relates to nucleic acids encoding for the polypeptide, to vectors comprising the same and to host cells comprising the vector. In another aspect, the invention provides for a pharmaceutical composition comprising the mentioned polypeptide and medical uses of the polypeptide.

text: TRANSCRIPTIONAL CONTROL IN PROKARYOTIC CELLS USING DNA-BINDING REPRESSORS. The present disclosure relates generally to methods and compositions for transferring a genetic circuit from one prokaryotic cell ("donor cell") to another prokaryotic cell ("recipient cell" or "target cell" which are used interchangeably herein). More specifically, the present disclosure relates to prokaryotic donor cells comprising (i) a genetic circuit of interest and (ii) one or more expressed transcriptional repressor proteins and the use of said donor cells in the efficient transfer of the genetic circuit into a prokaryotic recipient cell. The genetic circuit includes nucleic acid sequences encoding a RNA molecule or protein of interest.

title: Method for constructing multi-copy eukaryotic expression vector based on programmable enzyme Argonaute and application of multi-copy eukaryotic expression vector
text: Method for constructing multi-copy eukaryotic expression vector based on programmable enzyme Argonaute and application of multi-copy eukaryotic expression vector. The invention provides a method for constructing a multi-copy eukaryotic expression vector based on a programmable enzyme Argonaute and application of the multi-copy eukaryotic expression vector based on the programmable enzyme Argonaute, and belongs to the technical field of biological medicines.The method comprises the following operation steps that DNA assembly is conducted on recombinant plasmids In-pc-CDs based on Argonaute and resistance gene reconstruction, and expression units in the plasmids obtained through assembly are transferred to plasmids Lamp; in R-Arm, the multi-copy expression vector is obtained. A set of in-vitro seamless DNA assembly technology which is low in mutation rate, convenient to operate, easy to screen, short in assembly period, wide in base sequence universality and low in both economic cost and time cost is developed by starting from a novel programmable enzyme Argonaute and combining a resistance reconstruction strategy, and the in-vitro seamless DNA assembly technology is used as an eukaryotic expression platform DNA assembly tool to realize the in-vitro seamless DNA assembly of the eukaryotic expression platform. The invention provides a method for rapidly constructing a multi-copy expression vector and a signal peptide expression plasmid library, so that the construction of the CHO stable transfection cell strain is realized.

title: HIGH PERFORMANCE MULTI-INPUT MICRORNA SENSORS AND USES THEREOF
text: HIGH PERFORMANCE MULTI-INPUT MICRORNA SENSORS AND USES THEREOF. Provided herein are genetic circuits and cell state classifiers for detecting the microRNA profile of a cell. The cell state classifiers of the present disclosure are designed to incorporate multiple genetic circuits integrated together by transcriptional or translational control. Multiple inputs can be sensed simultaneously by coupling their detection to different portions of the genetic circuit such that the output molecule is produced only when the correct input profile of miRNAs is detected. The genetic circuits and cell state classifiers may be used in various applications (e.g., therapeutic or diagnostic applications).

title: Recombined composite epitope antigen of liver cancer, preparation method, and application of preparing medication for specific immunity treatment of liver cancer
text: Recombined composite epitope antigen of liver cancer, preparation method, and application of preparing medication for specific immunity treatment of liver cancer. This invention discloses recombinant hepatocarcinoma composite epitope antigen, and its preparation method and application in manufacturing hepatocarcinoma-specific immunotherapy drugs. The composite epitope antigen has transduction activity, and contains T cell epitopes of several tumor antigens. The composite epitope antigen is obtained by gene synthesis and molecular cloning, and has such advantages as high safety and stability, and reduced immune evasion. The composite epitope antigen has potential applications in specific immunotherapy of liver cancer.

title: Multi-fragment DNA assembly kit, multi-fragment DNA assembly method and application thereof
text: Multi-fragment DNA assembly kit, multi-fragment DNA assembly method and application thereof. The invention provides a multi-fragment DNA assembly kit, a multi-fragment DNA assembly method and application thereof. The kit comprises a to-be-assembled DNA fragment Fn and a vector. The vector includes an intermediate connection vector and/or an assembly vector; the kit further comprises saccharomyces cerevisiae. According to the invention, a mode of combining in-vitro assembly and in-vivo assembly is adopted; an initial fragment is synthesized through PCR and the initial fragment is added into the intermediate connection vector, a recombinant enzyme system is adopted to assemble large-fragment DNA and the assembly vector in vitro, and then an in-vitro assembly product is converted into saccharomyces cerevisiae for in-vivo assembly. Advantages of in-vitro assembly and in-vivo assemblyare combined and the synthesis success rate of super-large plasmids is remarkably increased.

title: PEG-MEDIATED ASSEMBLY OF NUCLEIC ACID MOLECULES
text: PEG-MEDIATED ASSEMBLY OF NUCLEIC ACID MOLECULES. The present invention discloses methods for assembling a nucleic acid molecule from a set of overlapping oligonucleotides. The method involves contacting a set of overlapping oligonucleotides with a DNA polymerase, a mixture of dNTPs, and a crowding agent to form an assembly mixture. In one embodiment the crowding agent is polyethylene glycol (PEG). The presence of the crowding agent facilitates the nucleic acid assembly process of the invention. The assembly mixture is then subjected to multiple cycles, each cycle comprising an annealing phase, an extension phase, and a denaturation phase, and the desired nucleic acid molecule is thereby assembled. In some embodiments one or more of the phases are time varied.

title: PEG-MEDIATED ASSEMBLY OF NUCLEIC ACID MOLECULES
text: PEG-MEDIATED ASSEMBLY OF NUCLEIC ACID MOLECULES. The present invention discloses methods for assembling a nucleic acid molecule from a set of overlapping oligonucleotides. The method involves contacting a set of overlapping oligonucleotides with a DNA polymerase, a mixture of dNTPs, and a crowding agent to form an assembly mixture. In one embodiment the crowding agent is polyethylene glycol (PEG). The presence of the crowding agent facilitates the nucleic acid assembly process of the invention. The assembly mixture is then subjected to multiple cycles, each cycle comprising an annealing phase, an extension phase, and a denaturation phase, and the desired nucleic acid molecule is thereby assembled. In some embodiments one or more of the phases are time varied.

title: MICROORGANISM FOR PRODUCING L-TRYPTOPHAN AND METHOD FOR PRODUCING L-TRYPTOPHAN USING SAME
text: MICROORGANISM FOR PRODUCING L-TRYPTOPHAN AND METHOD FOR PRODUCING L-TRYPTOPHAN USING SAME. The present disclosure relates to a microorganism of the genus Corynebacterium having an L-tryptophan-producing ability, into which pyruvate, phosphate dikinase derived from Komagataeibacter xylinus or a polynucleotide encoding the same is introduced; a method for producing L-tryptophan, comprising culturing the microorganism in a medium; a composition for producing L-tryptophan, comprising the microorganism, a culture product of the microorganism, a fermented product of the microorganism, or a combination of two or more thereof; and the use of the microorganism for the production of L-tryptophan.

title: BIOSENSORS FOR DETECTING AND/OR NEUTRALIZING BIOAVAILABLE URANIUM AND RELATED U-SENSITIVE GENETIC MOLECULAR COMPONENTS, GENE CASSETTES, VECTORS, GENETIC CIRCUITS, COMPOSITIONS, METHODS AND SYSTEMS
text: BIOSENSORS FOR DETECTING AND/OR NEUTRALIZING BIOAVAILABLE URANIUM AND RELATED U-SENSITIVE GENETIC MOLECULAR COMPONENTS, GENE CASSETTES, VECTORS, GENETIC CIRCUITS, COMPOSITIONS, METHODS AND SYSTEMS. UO2F2 biosensors, and related U-sensing and/or F-sensing genetic molecular components, genetic circuits, compositions, methods and systems are described, which in several embodiments can be used to detect and/or neutralize uranium and in particular bioavailable UO2F2.

title: CROSS-SPECIES-SPECIFIC BISPECIFIC BINDERS
text: CROSS-SPECIES-SPECIFIC BISPECIFIC BINDERS. The present invention relates to a polypeptide comprising a first human binding domain capbable of binding to an epitope of human and non-chimpanzee CD3ε (epsilon) chain and a second binding domain capable of binding to EGFR, Her2/neu or IgE of a human and/or a non-chimpanzee primate as well as to a process for the production of the mentioned polypeptide. The invention further relates to nucleic acid sequences encoding the polypeptide, to vectors comprising the nucleic acid sequences and to host cells comprising the nucleic acid sequences or vectors containing the nucleic acid sequences. In another aspect, the invention provides for a pharmaceutical composition comprising the polypeptide and methods of medical treatment or use of the polypeptide.

title: Cerebral stroke warning genetic circuit model
text: Cerebral stroke warning genetic circuit model. The invention discloses a cerebral stroke warning genetic circuit model which comprises a joint detection circuit module and a feedback circuit module. Output of the joint detection circuit module is input of the feedback circuit module, output of the feedback circuit module acts on the output of the joint detection circuit module via internal feedback branch circuits, and in other words, the feedback circuit module is a next-level circuit of the joint detection circuit module. The cerebral stroke warning genetic circuit model has the advantages that the cerebral stroke warning genetic circuit model is high in structural universality, cerebral stroke can be quickly monitored by the simple and effective joint detection circuit module, interference due to uncertain factors such as environments can be reduced by the introduced feedback circuit module, and accordingly the cerebral stroke can be accurately and reliably warned by systems.

title: MICROORGANISM PROTOTYPING GENETIC CIRCUIT-BASED SYSTEM FOR SEARCHING FOR NOVEL MICROORGANISM RESOURCES
text: MICROORGANISM PROTOTYPING GENETIC CIRCUIT-BASED SYSTEM FOR SEARCHING FOR NOVEL MICROORGANISM RESOURCES. The present invention relates to: a genetic circuit comprising a gene encoding a transcriptional regulatory factor expressed by sensing phenol, and a downstream reporter gene operated by the transcriptional regulatory factor; a sensor cell comprising the genetic circuit; and a method for sensing and searching for, directly from natural specimens, novel microorganism strains or genetic resources having target enzyme activity, by using the sensor cell and a phenol-tag substrate. The genetic circuit and the sensor cell comprising the same, of the present invention, comprise, as reporter genes, a fluorescent protein together with an auxotrophy-related enzyme for controlling the survival of the sensor cell, thereby enabling easy identification, by a simple method of co-culturing the sensor cell and an unknown microorganism strain having a desired target enzyme activity and measuring the fluorescence thereof, of novel microorganism strains or genetic resources having the corresponding target enzyme activity. Additionally, by introducing the dual reporter system, visible and clearer confirmation, of whether the target enzyme is activated, is enabled. Moreover, when searching for useful resources directly from nature, the possibility of target enzyme gene expression and the simplicity of experimentation can be maximized since the process of DNA separation and library construction is not performed and the use of antibiotics is limited, and the GMO/LMO problems caused by recombinant microorganisms can be minimized by directly using natural microorganisms having high activity.

title: NFSAI gene from lactobacillus acidophilus and preparation method and application thereof
text: NFSAI gene from lactobacillus acidophilus and preparation method and application thereof. The invention discloses an NFSAI gene from lactobacillus acidophilus and a preparation method and application thereof. The nucleotide sequence of the NFSAI gene is shown by SEQ ID No.1, and the coded amino acid sequence is shown by SEQ ID No.2; the NFSAI gene is prepared by a gene synthesis method; by adopting the NFSAI gene to transform a plant, the TNT degrading capacity of the transgenic plant can be improved. Therefore, the NFSAI gene disclosed by the invention can promote the plant-involved degradation of TNT to provide beneficial help to the phytoremediation of TNT pollution.

title: Biological synthesis gene cluster of siomycin
text: Biological synthesis gene cluster of siomycin. The invention relates to clone, sequencing, analysis and functional study for a biosynthetic gene cluster generated by streptomycete and having antibiotic-siomycin against Gram-positive bacteria, and application thereof. The integral gene cluster totally contains 21 genes, wherein 10 genes and macrocycle of the siomycin biologically synthesize a related gene, and 8 genes and side chain biologically synthesize a related gene. Synthesis of the siomycin can be blocked through genetic operation for the biosynthetic genes. The genes and proteins can be used for looking for and discovering compounds or genes and proteins for medicine, industry or agriculture.

title: MULTIPLEX ASSEMBLY OF NUCLEIC ACID MOLECULES
text: MULTIPLEX ASSEMBLY OF NUCLEIC ACID MOLECULES. Compositions and methods are provided herein for high throughput synthesis and assembly of nucleic acid molecules. Specific aspects include methods and rules for designing, grouping and pooling of nucleic acid molecules for efficient multiplex assembly, amplification, processing and analysis to obtain error-free assembly products. Provided compositions and methods allow for miniaturization, parallelization, high throughput production and scaling, and cost reduction in gene synthesis workflows.

title: SYSTEMS FOR CELL PROGRAMMING AND METHODS THEREOF
text: SYSTEMS FOR CELL PROGRAMMING AND METHODS THEREOF. Provided herein are systems of modulating gene expression, methods of use thereof, and cells engineered thereof for the purpose of differentiating cells, for example immune cells.

title: Cascade genetic circuit useful for regulated expression of cloned genes, comprises several nucleic acid constructs encoding regulators arranged in order and final target promoter responsive in dose-dependent fashion
text: Cascade genetic circuit useful for regulated expression of cloned genes, comprises several nucleic acid constructs encoding regulators arranged in order and final target promoter responsive in dose-dependent fashion. A cascade genetic circuit (I), comprising nucleic acid constructs (II) encoding several transcriptional regulators arranged in a hierarchical order so that expression of an upstream regulator stimulates expression of a downstream regulator, and a final target promoter (III) responsive in a dose-dependent fashion to a terminal downstream regulator, is new. Independent claims are also included for the following: (1) a cell comprising (I) and (2) regulating the expression of a nucleic acid, comprising: (a) establishing a genetic circuit according to the novel method (b) placing the sequence under the control of (III) and (c) inducing the cascade genetic circuit to stimulate expression of the sequence.

title: METHODS OF USE FOR THERMOSTABLE RNA LIGASES
text: METHODS OF USE FOR THERMOSTABLE RNA LIGASES. The activity and functional properties of novel thermostable RNA ligases isolated from thermophilic bacteriophages are described. Also described are methods of using these thermostable RNA ligases for various applications including nucleotide labeling, oligonucleotide synthesis, gene synthesis, gene amplification and amplification of mRNA and synthesis of cDNA.

title: GENE SYNTHESIS SOFTWARE
text: GENE SYNTHESIS SOFTWARE. Pieces of software and databases are used to facilitate the design and synthesis of genes. The synthesis techniques allow identification, quantification, transcription, translation, and manipulation of portions of the gene sequence represented as O's and l's in a computing system. These pieces of software and databases compile constraint information provided by a user to create synthetic genes to express proteins lacking disordered or variable regions and containing surface mutations that promote solubility and/or crystallization. Regions of the gene sequence that do not form stable structures can be removed and replaced by a short biological linker to improve crystallization of proteins.

title: MOLECULAR CLONING METHOD BASED ON SYNTHETIC GENE AND SACCHAROMYCES CEREVISIAE HOMOLOGOUS RECOMBINATION MECHANISM
text: MOLECULAR CLONING METHOD BASED ON SYNTHETIC GENE AND SACCHAROMYCES CEREVISIAE HOMOLOGOUS RECOMBINATION MECHANISM. A molecular cloning method combining gene synthesis and an endogenous homologous recombination system of Saccharomyces cerevisiae is not limited to the type (deletion, mutation or insertion) and complexity of sequence changes, and only involves simple molecular cloning operations in vitro. By using a synthetic sequence having homology arms at both ends and yeast homologous recombination, seamless modification of an original vector is achieved, and synchronous transformation of multiple discontinuous DNA segments on a 4-150kbp shuttle plasmid and batch construction of multiple versions can be achieved.

title: PROKARYOTIC 2-COMPONENT SIGNALING PATHWAYS FOR USE AS LOGIC GATES IN MAMMALIAN CELLS
text: PROKARYOTIC 2-COMPONENT SIGNALING PATHWAYS FOR USE AS LOGIC GATES IN MAMMALIAN CELLS

title: GENE SYNTHESIS SOFTWARE
text: GENE SYNTHESIS SOFTWARE. Pieces of software and databases are used to facilitate the design and synthesis of genes. The synthesis techniques allow identification, quantification, transcription, translation, and manipulation of portions of the gene sequence represented as O's and l's in a computing system. These pieces of software and databases compile constraint information provided by a user to create synthetic genes to express proteins lacking disordered or variable regions and containing surface mutations that promote solubility and/or crystallization. Regions of the gene sequence that do not form stable structures can be removed and replaced by a short biological linker to improve crystallization of proteins.

title: GENE SYNTHESIS BY CONVERGENT ASSEMBLY OF OLIGONUCLEOTIDE SUBSETS
text: GENE SYNTHESIS BY CONVERGENT ASSEMBLY OF OLIGONUCLEOTIDE SUBSETS. The invention provides a system and method for synthesizing polynucleotides by solid phase assembly oligonucleotide precursors, in accordance with the method, a polynucleotide is partitioned into an ordered set of subunits, wherein each subunit is assembled in a single reaction from a subset of oligonucleotide precursors that uniquely anneal together to produce the subunit. The subunits are then assembled to form the desired polynucleotide. An important feature of the invention is the selection of subunits that are free of undesired sequence elements, such as palindromes, repetitive sequences, and the like, which would result in more than one subunit product alter ligating a pool of oligonucleotide precursors.

title: A method for synthesizing single-stranded DNA
text: A method for synthesizing single-stranded DNA. Provided is a method for synthesizing single-stranded DNA, which relates to the field of gene synthesis, in particular to a process for preparing single-stranded DNA without base mutation. Specifically, the present invention provides a method for preparing single-stranded DNA by uracil-specific excision reagent (USER)-mediated double-stranded DNA spontaneous cyclization and rolling circle replication.

title: SYNTHESIS AND ERROR CORRECTION METHOD FOR SINGLE-STRANDED DNA
text: SYNTHESIS AND ERROR CORRECTION METHOD FOR SINGLE-STRANDED DNA. Provided is a synthesis and error correction method for single-stranded DNA, relating to the technical field of gene synthesis and in particular to synthesis of single-stranded DNA and error correction method in synthesis. The method can eliminate erroneous fragments in DNA synthesis and improve the accuracy of single-stranded DNA synthesis fragments. Through designing enzyme-cutting sites at both ends of a target sequence and forming a hairpin structure, the method can obtain correct DNA fragments by correcting errors with restrictive endonucleases and mismatch-specific enzymes.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR ACQUIRING HIGH-PURITY NUCLEOTIDES
text: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR ACQUIRING HIGH-PURITY NUCLEOTIDES. The present invention relates to a method and apparatus for acquiring high-purity nucleotides that can be favorably used in a genome/gene synthesis field, as DNA synthesis techniques capable of synthesizing nucleotide sequences with high purity at a low cost.

title: NOVEL NUCLEIC ACID CONSTRUCTS CONTAINING ORTHOGONAL SITE SELECTIVE RECOMBINASES (OSSRs)
text: NOVEL NUCLEIC ACID CONSTRUCTS CONTAINING ORTHOGONAL SITE SELECTIVE RECOMBINASES (OSSRs). The present invention provides for a recombinant nucleic acid comprising a nucleotide sequence comprising a plurality of constructs, wherein each construct independently comprises a nucleotide sequence of interest flanked by a pair of recombinase recognition sequences. Each pair of recombinase recognition sequences is recognized by a distinct recombinase. Optionally, each construct can, independently, further comprise one or more genes encoding a recombinase capable of recognizing the pair of recombinase recognition sequences of the construct. The recombinase can be an orthogonal (non-cross reacting), site-selective recombinase (OSSR).

title: Method for the complete chemical synthesis and assembly of genes and genomes
text: Method for the complete chemical synthesis and assembly of genes and genomes. The present invention relates generally to the fields of oligonucleotide synthesis. More particularly, it concerns the assembly of genes and genomes of completely synthetic artificial organisms. Thus, the present invention outlines a novel approach to utilizing the results of genomic sequence information by computer directed gene synthesis based on computing on the human genome database. Specifically, the present invention contemplates and describes the chemical synthesis and resynthesis of genes defined by the genome sequence in a host vector and transfer and expression of these sequences into suitable hosts.

title: Transcriptional control in prokaryotic cells using DNA-binding repressors
text: Transcriptional control in prokaryotic cells using DNA-binding repressors. The present disclosure relates generally to methods and compositions for transferring a genetic circuit from one prokaryotic cell (“donor cell”) to another prokaryotic cell (“recipient cell” or “target cell” which are used interchangeably herein). More specifically, the present disclosure relates to prokaryotic donor cells comprising (i) a genetic circuit of interest and (ii) one or more expressed transcriptional repressor proteins and the use of said donor cells in the efficient transfer of the genetic circuit into a prokaryotic recipient cell. The genetic circuit includes nucleic acid sequences encoding a RNA molecule or protein of interest.

title: Regulated expression of cloned genes using a cascade genetic circuit
text: Regulated expression of cloned genes using a cascade genetic circuit. The present invention relates to the design, preparation and use of cascade regulatory circuits for amplification of gene expression. The genetic circuit is based on a plurality (e.g., two or more) of regulatory genes organized in a hierarchical order of expression in a genetic construct or constructs, which can be established in a cell, e.g., a gram-negative bacteria, by means of autoreplicative vectors or by chromosomal insertion. In one embodiment, the genetic construct(s) can be stabily maintained in the chromosome without selective pressure, and gene expression induced three orders of magnitude therefrom using economical biodegradable benzoate derivatives.

title: GENE SYNTHESIS BY CIRCULAR ASSEMBLY AMPLIFICATION
text: GENE SYNTHESIS BY CIRCULAR ASSEMBLY AMPLIFICATION. Compositions and methods for synthesizing polynucleotides, for reducing errors during polynucleotide synthesis, for correcting mismatches in synthetic polynucleotide sequences, and for selecting correctly assembled polynucleotide sequences are provided.

title: ENGINEERED DCAS9 WITH REDUCED TOXICITY AND ITS USE IN GENETIC CIRCUITS
text: ENGINEERED DCAS9 WITH REDUCED TOXICITY AND ITS USE IN GENETIC CIRCUITS. Disclosed herein are novel CRISPR/dCas9-based fusion proteins that facilitate the scaling up of genetic circuits, including those with non-linear response curves. The dCas9 based fusion proteins produce significantly less toxicity in comparison to previously described CRISPR/Cas9-based proteins used with logic gates. These improvements enable the generation of complex genetic circuits when both digital response curves and large amounts of dCas9 protein are needed. Also disclosed herein are methods of regulating expression of an output sequence through the introduction of novel CRISPR/dCas-9-based fusion proteins and genetic circuits into a cell.

title: PEG-MEDIATED ASSEMBLY OF NUCLEIC ACID MOLECULES
text: PEG-MEDIATED ASSEMBLY OF NUCLEIC ACID MOLECULES. The present invention discloses methods for assembling a nucleic acid molecule from a set of overlapping oligonucleotides. The method involves contacting a set of overlapping oligonucleotides with a DNA polymerase, a mixture of dNTPs, and a crowding agent to form an assembly mixture. In one embodiment the crowding agent is polyethylene glycol (PEG). The presence of the crowding agent facilitates the nucleic acid assembly process of the invention. The assembly mixture is then subjected to multiple cycles, each cycle comprising an annealing phase, an extension phase, and a denaturation phase, and the desired nucleic acid molecule is thereby assembled. In some embodiments one or more of the phases are time varied.

title: SYSTEMS FOR CELL PROGRAMMING AND METHODS THEREOF
text: SYSTEMS FOR CELL PROGRAMMING AND METHODS THEREOF. Provided herein are systems of modulating gene expression and methods of use thereof for converting cells of one type to another type (e.g. reprogramming of stem cells into tissue-specific progenitor cells).

title: Herpes simplex virus type II mRNA vaccine and application thereof
text: Herpes simplex virus type II mRNA vaccine and application thereof. The invention discloses a herpes simplex virus type II mRNA (messenger ribonucleic acid) vaccine and application thereof, and relates to the technical field of biological pharmacy, and the nucleotide sequence of the herpes simplex virus type II mRNA of the herpes simplex virus type II mRNA vaccine is as shown in SEQ ID NO. 1. The mRNA vaccine is obtained through gene synthesis, in-vitro transcription and lipid nanoparticle wrapping, the current epidemic HSV-2 can be rapidly dealt with, and the research, development and production cycle of new vaccine development is shortened.

title: DNA POLYMERASE AND DNA POLYMERASE DERIVED 3'-5'EXONUCLEASE
text: DNA POLYMERASE AND DNA POLYMERASE DERIVED 3'-5'EXONUCLEASE. The present invention relates to enzymes having DNA polymerase and 3′-5′ exonuclease activities. In particular, the present invention relates to a heat labile enzyme possessing a DNA polymerase II activity and a 3′-5′ exonuclease activity of marine origin. Furthermore, the present invention relates to a DNA polymerase primarily exerting a 3′-5′ activity, i.e. where the polymerase activity is absent. The present invention furthermore relates to the use of the exonuclease activity to degrade the 3′-5′ strand of double stranded DNA to perform single stranded overhang, e.g. in recombinant cloning processes, or in processes for removal of contaminating nucleic acid molecules.

title: SYSTEMS FOR CELL PROGRAMMING TOWARDS HEMATOPOIETIC LINEAGE AND METHODS THEREOF
text: SYSTEMS FOR CELL PROGRAMMING TOWARDS HEMATOPOIETIC LINEAGE AND METHODS THEREOF. Provided herein are systems of modulating gene expression, methods of use thereof, and cells engineered thereof for the purpose of differentiating cells, for example hematopoietic cells.

title: ENGINEERED DCAS9 WITH REDUCED TOXICITY AND ITS USE IN GENETIC CIRCUITS
text: ENGINEERED DCAS9 WITH REDUCED TOXICITY AND ITS USE IN GENETIC CIRCUITS. Disclosed herein are novel CRISPR/dCas9-based fusion proteins that facilitate the scaling up of genetic circuits, including those with non-linear response curves. The dCas9 based fusion proteins produce significantly less toxicity in comparison to previously described CRISPR/Cas9-based proteins used with logic gates. These improvements enable the generation of complex genetic circuits when both digital response curves and large amounts of dCas9 protein are needed. Also disclosed herein are methods of regulating expression of an output sequence through the introduction of novel CRISPR/dCas-9-based fusion proteins and genetic circuits into a cell.

title: LONG NUCLEIC ACID SEQUENCES CONTAINING VARIABLE REGIONS
text: LONG NUCLEIC ACID SEQUENCES CONTAINING VARIABLE REGIONS. This invention pertains to improved methods for the synthesis of long, double stranded nucleic acid sequences containing difficult to clone or variable regions.

title: Gene synthesis software
text: Gene synthesis software. Pieces of software and databases are used to facilitate the design and synthesis of genes. The synthesis techniques allow identification, quantification, transcription, translation, and manipulation of portions of the gene sequence represented as 0's and 1's in a computing system. These pieces of software and databases compile constraint information provided by a user to create synthetic genes to express proteins lacking disordered or variable regions and containing surface mutations that promote solubility and/or crystallization. Regions of the gene sequence that do not form stable structures can be removed and replaced by a short biological linker to improve crystallization of proteins.

title: HUMAN PAPILLOMAVIRUS TYPE 18 CHIMERIC PROTEIN AND USE THEREOF
text: HUMAN PAPILLOMAVIRUS TYPE 18 CHIMERIC PROTEIN AND USE THEREOF. The present invention relates to a human papillomavirus type 18 chimeric protein and the use thereof. Specifically, the present invention relates to a papillomavirus chimeric protein, which comprises or consists of an HPV type 18 L1 protein or a mutant of the HPV type 18 L1 protein, and a polypeptide from a HPV type 59 L2 protein inserted into the surface region of the HPV type 18 L1 protein or the mutant of the HPV type 18 L1 protein, wherein the amino acid sequence of the HPV type 18 L1 protein is as shown in SEQ ID No. 1, and the amino acid sequence of the HPV type 59 L2 protein is as shown in SEQ ID No. 2.

title: GENETIC CIRCUIT SIMULATING ARTIFICIAL NEURAL NETWORK AND CONSTRUCTION METHOD THEREFOR
text: GENETIC CIRCUIT SIMULATING ARTIFICIAL NEURAL NETWORK AND CONSTRUCTION METHOD THEREFOR. A genetic circuit simulating an artificial neural network, comprising at least one input layer (100), at least two hidden layers (210, 220), and at least one output layer (300). The input layer (100) comprises several input layer nodes, each of the hidden layers (210, 220) comprises several hidden layer nodes, the output layer (300) comprises several output layer nodes, input vectors of each input layer (100) node comprise a promoter and an input gene, each hidden layer (210, 220) node comprises a hidden layer gene, and each hidden layer gene is regulated and controlled by the promoter of the gene of the layer or a gene product of the gene of the previous layer; the output node outputs a genetic circuit result; the regulation and control to the hidden layer (210, 220) gene and the output layer (300) node by the product of the gene of the previous layer conforms with an excitation function; the regulation and control to the input layer (100) node by the input promoter conforms with an excitation function. A genetic circuit structure is used to simulate an artificial neural network, so that complex operation is simulated at the molecular level, and complex application in the fields of biology, medicine, chemistry, electronics and the like is achieved.

title: METHOD OF ON-CHIP NUCLEIC ACID MOLECULE SYNTHESIS
text: METHOD OF ON-CHIP NUCLEIC ACID MOLECULE SYNTHESIS. A method of synthesizing a nucleic acid molecule, such as a gene, on a substrate or microchip is described. In particular, a method for synthesizing, amplifying, and assembling DNA oligonucleotides into a nucleic acid molecule or gene product, on a single substrate or microchip is described. Also described are a method of correcting a sequence error in a synthesized nucleic acid molecule, as well as a method for synthesizing and screening a library of codon variants to identify a nucleic acid molecule with an optimized level of protein expression.

title: Streptavidin and purification method thereof
text: Streptavidin and purification method thereof. The invention belongs to the technical field of molecular biology, and discloses streptavidin and a purification method thereof. The amino acid sequence of the streptavidin is shown as SEQ ID NO.1. The preparation method comprises the following steps: artificial gene synthesis and site-specific mutagenesis introduction, initiation codon addition, codon optimization, termination codon addition, synthesis, sequence introduction and expression, culture and purification. The streptavidin JC2 is analyzed and determined, and the JC2 is subjected to directional mutation modification to improve the biotin binding capacity of the JC2.

title: Method for synthesizing gene by using high depth oligonucleotide tiling
text: Method for synthesizing gene by using high depth oligonucleotide tiling. The present invention relates to a method for synthesizing genes through high density oligonucleotide tiling. Provided is a stable gene synthesis method without an error by tiling oligonucleotides through over-overlapping, recovering the oligonucleotides synthesized without an error through next generation sequencing of oligonucleotides synthesized through DNA microarray, and assembling the oligonucleotides to synthesize genes.

title: METHODS OF USING MICROFLUIDIC POSITIONAL ENCODING DEVICES
text: METHODS OF USING MICROFLUIDIC POSITIONAL ENCODING DEVICES. Embodiments relate to methods and compositions useful for routing and tracking multiple mobile units within a microfluidic device. Mobile units may be routed through a plurality of chemical environments, and the mobile units may be tracked to determine the path and/or environments that the mobile units have routed through. Mobile units may be routed in accordance with a predetermined algorithm. Mobile units may be routed through microfluidic devices in ordered flow. Mobile units routed through the microfluidic device can be used to perform various chemical reactions uniquely associated to the units, including without limitation peptide synthesis, enzymatic gene synthesis and gene assembly.

title: VHH antibody capable of being combined with bacillus anthracis
text: VHH antibody capable of being combined with bacillus anthracis. The invention discloses a VHH antibody capable of being combined with bacillus anthracis, and relates to the technical field of VHH antibodies, a target gene is inserted into a pPICZalpha A vector through gene synthesis, a linearized recombinant plasmid is obtained by using enzyme digestion and purification technologies, the plasmid is converted into X33 yeast competent, and the VHH antibody capable of being combined with bacillus anthracis is obtained. Performing positive clone screening, inducible expression and protein identification to obtain a stable VHH antibody of bacillus anthracis; therefore, the stability of the bacillus anthracis antibody is improved, the production process of the bacillus anthracis antibody is optimized, and the more stable VHH antibody of the bacillus anthracis can be obtained.

title: T CELL ACTIVATION RESPONSIVE CONSTRUCTS FOR ENHANCED CAR-T CELL THERAPY
text: T CELL ACTIVATION RESPONSIVE CONSTRUCTS FOR ENHANCED CAR-T CELL THERAPY. The present disclosure generally relates to genetic circuits including response elements engineered from the NR4A1 that augment chimeric antigen receptor activation with the delivery of a bioactive molecule. Particularly, the disclosure provides nucleic acid constructs including response elements operably linked to a nucleic acid sequence of interest such that are responsive to activation of CAR-T cells. The disclosure also relates to vectors and cells including the genetic circuits and the nucleic acid constructs. Also provided are methods of inducing an immune response, methods of treatment of subjects, and methods for delivering a bioactive molecule by an activated T cell having a chimeric antigen receptor and the response element constructs of the disclosure.

title: Preparation method of erythrocyte membrane coated framework nucleic acid nanogel
text: Preparation method of erythrocyte membrane coated framework nucleic acid nanogel. The invention belongs to but not limited to the technical field of gene synthesis, and discloses a preparation method of framework nucleic acid nanogel coated with an erythrocyte membrane, NG is formed through complementary pairing of bases, and compounding of RBCm and NG is realized by using an Avanti small extruder. SiRNA and TDN are complementarily combined through bases and cross-linked to form NG, TDN provides a first layer of protection for siRNA, and the stability and efficacy of siRNA are enhanced; and the erythrocyte membrane is a second layer of protection to prevent enzymatic degradation and protein adsorption. The M (at) NG combines the bionic property and biocompatibility of RBCm and the efficient drug loading and stability of NG, and has the characteristics of targeted drug delivery, immune escape and the like. The system is suitable for treating various diseases, and the application field of the RNA interference technology is expanded.

title: THERMOSTABLE LUCIFERASE AND METHOD OF PRODUCTION
text:
THERMOSTABLE LUCIFERASE AND METHOD OF PRODUCTION.

PROBLEM TO BE SOLVED: To provide mutant luciferase enzymes with greatly enhanced thermostability in comparison with natural luciferases or mutant luciferases.

SOLUTION: A modified luciferase conjugate is prepared by using a beetle luciferase gene, applying it to various means for inducing random mutation, particularly to gene synthesis using a polymerase easily happening to be in errors, preparing a modified luciferase gene library, expressing the same with E. coli, and combining selected mutations. The method includes recursive mutagenesis in which a novel library is prepared from modified luciferases through random mutation induction and the process is repeatedly selected.

title: Method and apparatus for obtaining high purity nucleotides
text: Method and apparatus for obtaining high purity nucleotides. The present invention relates to a method and an apparatus for obtaining high-purity nucleotides that can be usefully used in the field of genome/gene synthesis as a DNA synthesis technology capable of synthesizing a nucleotide sequence at a low cost and with high purity. The method of the present invention comprises the following steps: (a) preparing nucleic acid fragments; (b) tagging the nucleic acid fragments with barcode sequences; (c) identifying position coordinates for fragments having verified sequences among the nucleic acid fragments tagged with the barcode sequences on a support; and (d) recovering fragments having the verified sequence using the position coordinates.

title: Mutated Artificial Genetic Circuit and Method for Detection and Quantification of Phenolic Compounds Based on Communities of the Circuit and Machine Learning Algorithms
text: Mutated Artificial Genetic Circuit and Method for Detection and Quantification of Phenolic Compounds Based on Communities of the Circuit and Machine Learning Algorithms. The present invention relates to a method of detection and quantification of phenolic compounds using a mutated artificial genetic circuit. More specifically, the mutated artificial genetic circuit is configured using a mutated dmpR protein which is obtained by substituting methionine (M) at position 52 in wild type dmpR with isoleucine (I) or cysteine (C), or additionally substituting glutamine (Q) at position 71 in wild type dmpR with alanine (A), glutamic acid (E) at position 135 with lysine (K), or lysine at position 188 with arginine (R). Accordingly, the present invention provides the effect of being able to accurately detect and quantify even a small amount of various phenol compounds.

title: Programmable DNA-guided artificial restriction enzymes
text: Programmable DNA-guided artificial restriction enzymes. Methods and compositions for specific cleavage of DNA molecules using single-stranded guides and Argonaute (Ago) proteins are described.

title: Gene descended from antifreeze peptide insect and preparation method and application thereof
text: Gene descended from antifreeze peptide insect and preparation method and application thereof. The invention relates to a gene descended from insect antifreeze peptide and a preparation method and application thereof. The nucleotide sequence of the gene is shown in SEQ ID NO 1, the amino acid sequence of the protein encoded by the gene is shown in SEQ ID NO 2; and the gene is the CFAFP3I gene which is prepared by modifying the CFAFP3I gene of insect antifreeze peptide according to the preferred codons of plant through the gene synthesis method. When the gene is used to transform Arabidopsis thaliana, the anti-freezing capability of the transgenic Arabidopsis thaliana can be increased. The gene of the invention plays an extremely important role in the cultivation of the anti-freezing plant variety and has important theoretical significance and practical significance.

title: NOVEL METHOD FOR DETECTING AND QUANTITATING TARGET ENZYME ACTIVITY USING ARTIFICIAL GENETIC CIRCUITRY
text: NOVEL METHOD FOR DETECTING AND QUANTITATING TARGET ENZYME ACTIVITY USING ARTIFICIAL GENETIC CIRCUITRY. PURPOSE: A novel method for searching and quantitating target enzyme activation is provided to quickly sense and quantitate useful genes with high sensitivity. CONSTITUTION: A method for searching target enzyme activity using a genetic circuit comprises: a step of preparing a phenol compound sensing artificial genetic circuit containing a gene encoding phenol compound lyase control protein, reporter gene and gene expression control region; a step of preparing clone or gene library having a gene encoding detection enzyme; a step of introducing the artificial genetic circuit to a host microorganism; a step of treating the recombinant microorganism with phenol releasing-compound; and a step of sensing activation of induced reporter protein.

title: Swine alpha-interferon gene synthesis, expression vector establishment and product preparing method
text: Swine alpha-interferon gene synthesis, expression vector establishment and product preparing method. One producing method of gene composition of pig a-interferon, expression vector structuring and its offspring includes synthesing pig a-interferon consisted of bacilluscoli preference codon and constructs highly active expression vector with this gene, and realizes the highly active expression in bacilluscoli of pig-a interferon, with 26% expression of the whole mycoprotein. At the same time, it provides a purely chemical engineering system of technique of induced expression, inclusion bodies separating and cracking, and complexity of albumen. Pig a-interferon has the functions of highly active antivirus and immunoregulation, and can be applied in preventing and treatment of pig contagion. This invention is able to produce efficient, safe, and cheap pig a-interferon which has no potential virus pollution. This lays a solid foundation for mass-production of pig a-interferon in our country.

title: INNOVATIVE PREPARATION PROCESS FOR BMP-2 STEM CELLS
text: INNOVATIVE PREPARATION PROCESS FOR BMP-2 STEM CELLS. The present invention discloses an innovative preparation process for recombinant human fat cell peptide (BMP-2 stem cells for short), comprising the following steps: gene synthesis of BMP-2 stem cells, specific expression system construction of mature peptide, expression induction, separation, purification and amplification.
