The method disclosed by the present invention is simple and flexible; has simple product separation and purification process, high purity, high activity and low production cost; has the functions of preventing clinical virus, resisting cell aging, and promoting wound healing and repair in epidermis, dermis and mucosa; and has broad application prospects in the fields such as medical care, cosmetology and health care.

title: High-efficiency expression of recombination human basic fibroblast growth factor
text: High-efficiency expression of recombination human basic fibroblast growth factor. By means of all-gene synthesis technology and without altering amino acid sequence, the codon encoding human whole-length bFGF is changed into colibacillus encoding codon before being constituted into prokaryotic expression vector pET23b with T7 promoter and prokaryotic expression vector pKK223-3 with Tac promoter to constitute high-efficiency expressing recombinant plamid pET-hbFGF and pKK223-hbFGF. Through subsequent converting colibacillus and IPTG induction, hbFGF expression amount will account for over 25 % of total thallus protein, obviously higher than available expression level.

title: Genetic Circuit for Detecting Terephthalic Acid and Use thereof
text: Genetic Circuit for Detecting Terephthalic Acid and Use thereof. An object of the present invention is to detect terephthalic acid (TPA), which is a decomposition product of PET, with high sensitivity. A genetic circuit for detecting terephthalic acid includes: a first gene construct comprising a first promoter and a gene of TphR or a variant thereof, the expression of which is controlled by the first promoter; and a second gene construct comprising a second promoter activated by TphR recognizing terephthalic acid, and a gene of a reporter, the expression of which is controlled by the activation of the second promoter. The genetic circuit of the present invention has excellent sensitivity for terephthalic acid, thereby exhibiting an effect of detecting terephthalic acid with high sensitivity by sensitively responding even to a trace amount of terephthalic acid.

title: PCR (polymerase chain reaction) detection method for locustacarus buchneri
text: PCR (polymerase chain reaction) detection method for locustacarus buchneri. The invention relates to a PCR (polymerase chain reaction) detection method for locustacarus buchneri, and belongs to the field of biotechnologies. A PCR reaction system is provided with a pair of specific primers Loc-F and Loc-R, a 220-bp fragment in DNA (deoxyribonucleic acid) extracted from bumblebee breast tissues infected by the locustacarus buchneri can be specifically amplified by the primers, and accordingly PCR detection can be carried out on the locustacarus buchneri. The PCR detection method for the locustacarus buchneri has the advantages that the detection sensitivity for the whole-gene synthesis locustacarus buchneri DNA is 0.88 fg/micro-L, the PCR detection method is high in specificity and good in stability, is speedy and accurate and can be used for detecting the locustacarus buchneri in imported bumblebees and domestic cultivated bumblebees, and accordingly healthful development of domestic bees can be guaranteed.

title: Acylsulfonic acid transferase and application thereof
text: Acylsulfonic acid transferase and application thereof. The invention discloses acyl sulfonic acid transferase and application thereof, and belongs to the field of enzyme engineering. On the basis of an NCBI (National Center of Biotechnology Information) database, a new enzyme sequence is mined through a bioinformatics means to obtain the acyl sulfonate transferase derived from Podarcismuris, a new enzyme is obtained through codon optimization, gene synthesis and cell expression, and the activity of the acyl sulfonate transferase is measured. Compared with reported mouse acyl sulfonate transferase, the acyl sulfonate transferase provided by the invention has better catalytic activity in a regeneration system of PAPS, and a new choice is provided for enzymatic synthesis of PAPS.

title: Gene detection tool and gene detection kit
text: Gene detection tool and gene detection kit. The present invention provides a gene detection tool capable of rapidly sampling and adding a test sample by a simple operation, and further capable of reducing the amount of waste. A gene detection tool (2) is provided with: a container main body (21) for accommodating a gene synthesis reaction liquid (10); a container lid (22) that seals the container main body (21); and an electrode part (23) and a sample collecting part (24) which are provided on the container lid (22) and are inserted into the container main body (21). The sample collection unit (24) is configured so as to be able to suck a test sample that may contain a gene to be detected.

title: Vomitoxin detoxifying enzyme and application thereof
text: Vomitoxin detoxifying enzyme and application thereof. The invention belongs to the technical field of biology, particularly relates to a vomitoxin detoxification enzyme and application thereof, and discloses a DON detoxification enzyme gene aadh sequence derived from acidobacteria, a DON detoxification enzyme coded by the DON detoxification enzyme gene aadh, a preparation method of the DON detoxification enzyme and application of the DON detoxification enzyme in degradation of vomitoxin. The preparation method comprises the following steps: performing codon optimization and gene synthesis on a DON detoxifying enzyme gene aadh; constructing a prokaryotic expression vector of the DON detoxifying enzyme gene aadh; expressing the prokaryotic expression vector in a prokaryotic host cell; the DON detoxifying enzyme provided by the invention can degrade vomitoxin, and can remove toxins in mildewed cereal raw materials and feeds.

title: RNA vaccine for porcine epidemic diarrhea and preparation method thereof
text: RNA vaccine for porcine epidemic diarrhea and preparation method thereof. According to the RNA vaccine for porcine epidemic diarrhea and the preparation method of the RNA vaccine, the RNA vaccine is obtained through gene synthesis, in-vitro transcription and lipid nanoparticle wrapping, the current epidemic new PEDV strain can be rapidly dealt with, the research and development and production cycle of new vaccine development is shortened, and an effective technical means is provided for prevention and control of new variant virulent strains. The RNA vaccine for porcine epidemic diarrhea prepared by the invention is high in production efficiency, can generate a high-level porcine epidemic diarrhea virus neutralizing antibody and binding antibody in vivo, can effectively reduce the occurrence of piglet diarrhea, and has great popularization value for prevention and control of porcine epidemic diarrhea.

title: Method to obtain synthetic L-alanine dehydrogenase genes and application of method
text: Method to obtain synthetic L-alanine dehydrogenase genes and application of method. The invention discloses a method to obtain synthetic L-alanine dehydrogenase genes. The method includes: optimizing alanine dehydrogenase genes (alaD) of geobacillus stearothermophilus according to codon usage bias of Escherichia coli; respectively adding a promoter and a transcription terminator to 5' and 3' ends of the optimized L-alanine dehydrogenase genes of the geobacillus stearothermophilus; subjecting the optimized genes to complete gene synthesis to obtain the synthetic L-alanine dehydrogenase genes. With the method, gene expression efficiency of alaD in the Escherichia coli can be improved, accumulation of L-alanine can be increased, and the yield of the L-alanine is increased.

title: Efficient codon optimization method
text: Efficient codon optimization method. The invention discloses an efficient codon optimization method, belongs to the field of gene synthesis, and relates to a codon optimization technology. During codon optimization, various optimization hosts can be combined at the same time, optimization sequences are distinguished in the optimization process, the sequences do not need to be optimized, and input of different positions is carried out; the workload of codon optimization is reduced, so that the codon optimization efficiency is improved, and the requirement of efficient codon optimization is met; through setting of the content limiting unit, the length limiting unit, the sequence shielding unit and the restriction enzyme cutting avoiding site, a repetitive sequence, an RNA ring structure and an RNA hairpin structure which commonly influence protein expression are controlled, formation of a spatial topological structure which influences RNA transcription efficiency is avoided, and the downstream protein yield is greatly improved.

title: 5-enolpyruvylshikimate-3-phosphate synthase gene derived from thermotoga maritima and application of gene
text: 5-enolpyruvylshikimate-3-phosphate synthase gene derived from thermotoga maritima and application of gene. The invention relates to a 5-enolpyruvylshikimate-3-phosphate synthase gene derived from thermotoga maritima and application of the gene. The nucleotide sequence of the 5-enolpyruvylshikimate-3-phosphate synthase gene derived from thermotoga maritima (aroA-Tm) is as shown in SEQ ID NO. 1 and is 1266bp in total length, and the coded protein sequence is as shown in SEQ ID NO. 2. The aroA-Tm gene is prepared by virtue of a gene synthesis method. Analysis on enzymatic kinetic characteristics and a function test verify that the synthesized 5-enolpyruvylshikimate-3-phosphate synthase gene is relatively high in glyphosate tolerance, and is applicable to the breeding of transgenic crops.

title: METHOD FOR THE FERMENTATIVE PRODUCTION OF GUANIDINOACETIC ACID
text: METHOD FOR THE FERMENTATIVE PRODUCTION OF GUANIDINOACETIC ACID. A microorganism is transformed to be capable of producing guanidinoacetic acid (GAA). A method can be used for the fermentative production of GAA using such microorganism. A corresponding method can also be used for the fermentative production of creatine.

title: Rapid synthesis method of long fragment gene
text: Rapid synthesis method of long fragment gene. The invention discloses a rapid synthesis method of a long-fragment gene. The method comprises the following steps: designing and synthesizing primers in groups, wherein each group comprises two pairs (4) of primers; the synthesized primers are grouped and assembled in a segmented mode through an Overlap PCR method, and multiple reactions are carried out at the same time; and carrying out purification and sequencing verification on the final PCR product. Gene amplification is carried out in a grouping and segmenting mode, the problem that the success rate of traditional Overlap PCR amplification growth fragments is low is solved, synthesis time is saved, additional reagents are not needed, and the method has the advantages of being low in cost and easy to implement and popularize and has good application prospects in the technical field of gene synthesis.

title: Recombinant plasmid as well as preparation method and application thereof
text: Recombinant plasmid as well as preparation method and application thereof. The invention provides a recombinant plasmid as well as a preparation method and application thereof, and the recombinant plasmid disclosed by the invention is used for simulating patient or carrier positive genome DNA which is difficult to obtain in a real scene through gene synthesis and chromosome copy number quantification by utilizing wild type gDNA and artificially synthesized DNA. The recombinant plasmid provided by the invention can be used for effectively replacing the culture of a patient-derived or gene editing cell line, and a required gene copy number variation positive standard substance can be conveniently and quickly obtained.

title: Perforin HolSGF2 as well as preparation method and application thereof
text: Perforin HolSGF2 as well as preparation method and application thereof. The invention discloses perforin HolSGF2 as well as a preparation method and application thereof, and belongs to the technical field of gene engineering. According to the perforin HolSGF2 as well as the preparation method and the application thereof disclosed by the invention, a prokaryotic expression vector plasmid HolSGF2-pET-32a containing a gene for coding the perforin HolSGF2 is obtained through gene synthesis, a recombinant plasmid is obtained by converting DH5alpha competent cells, and the recombinant plasmid is introduced into an escherichia coli BL21 expression system for mass expression of protein. And purifying by virtue of an affinity medium, so as to realize in-vitro high-efficiency expression of the perforin HolSGF2. The HOLSGF2 has good bactericidal activity, has a good inhibition effect on gram-negative bacteria and gram-positive bacteria, and has a good application prospect in the development of novel bacteriophage drugs.

title: NANOFLUIDIC SORTING SYSTEM FOR GENE SYNTHESIS AND PCR REACTION PRODUCTS
text: NANOFLUIDIC SORTING SYSTEM FOR GENE SYNTHESIS AND PCR REACTION PRODUCTS. Devices and methods integrate nanopore and microfluidic technologies for recording molecular characteristics of individual molecules such as, for example, biomolecules. Devices comprise a first substrate comprising a microchannel, a second substrate comprising a microchannel, the second substrate positioned below the first substrate, and a membrane having a thickness of about 0.3 nm to about 1 nm and comprising at least one nanopore, the membrane positioned between the first substrate and the second substrate, wherein a single nanopore of the membrane is constructed and arranged for electrical and fluid communication between the microchannel of the first substrate and the microchannel of the second substrate. To mitigate the effect of errors that occur during de novo DNA synthesis, longer DNA molecules are typically synthesized from shorter oligonucleotides by polymerase construction and amplification (PCA), or by other methods.

title: T CELL ACTIVATION RESPONSIVE CONSTRUCTS FOR ENHANCED CAR-T CELL THERAPY
text: T CELL ACTIVATION RESPONSIVE CONSTRUCTS FOR ENHANCED CAR-T CELL THERAPY. The present disclosure generally relates to genetic circuits including response elements engineered from the NR4A1 that augment chimeric antigen receptor activation with the delivery of a bioactive molecule. Particularly, the disclosure provides nucleic acid constructs including response elements operably linked to a nucleic acid sequence of interest such that are responsive to activation of CAR-T cells. The disclosure also relates to vectors and cells including the genetic circuits and the nucleic acid constructs. Also provided are methods of inducing an immune response, methods of treatment of subjects, and methods for delivering a bioactive molecule by an activated T cell having a chimeric antigen receptor and the response element constructs of the disclosure.

title: Construction method and application of sequence synthesis period prediction model
text: Construction method and application of sequence synthesis period prediction model. The invention discloses a construction method and application of a sequence synthesis period prediction model, and the method comprises the steps: selecting a plurality of gene sequences with different known lengths and different synthesis periods, carrying out the sequence feature extraction of the known sequences, taking the extracted sequence features and the known sequences as the training data of a database, and constructing a sequence synthesis period prediction model. And then, establishing a sequence synthesis period prediction model by utilizing an Embedding technology, a Transform model and two neural networks in deep learning. The method provided by the invention can be used for predicting the synthesis period of gene sequences with different complexities, is simple to operate and high in accuracy, is beneficial to overall arrangement of gene synthesis, and improves the synthesis efficiency.

title: Kit for detecting copy number of lipoprotein A subtype KIV-2 structural domain
text: Kit for detecting copy number of lipoprotein A subtype KIV-2 structural domain. The invention relates to the technical field of biology, in particular to a kit and method for detecting the repetition number of an Apo A molecule KIV-2 subtype structural domain from the gene level.According to the kit, a gene synthesis method is adopted to synthesize a KIV-2 standard substance with the known repetition number, so that the problem in determination of no positive sample or limited sample quantity is solved; and an SYBRGreen qPCR detection method is adopted, so that genome DNA with the total amount larger than or equal to 1 ng can be detected in a high-sensitivity and high-specificity mode, and a result can be rapidly obtained within 1-2 days.

title: Clostridium difficile exotoxin B carboxyl-terminal protein gene highly expressing in Escherichia coli
text: Clostridium difficile exotoxin B carboxyl-terminal protein gene highly expressing in Escherichia coli. The invention discloses a Clostridium difficile exotoxin B carboxyl-terminal protein gene highly expressing in Escherichia coli, and its high expression method in Escherichia coli. The optimization of the Clostridium difficile exotoxin B carboxyl-terminal protein gene in the invention improves the influences of a plurality of duplicate blocks of an original sequence to the chemical gene synthesis and reduces the synthesis cost, the gene can highly express in Escherichia coli, and the expression level can reach 70% of total mycoproteins, and is far higher than that of present Clostridium difficile exotoxin B carboxyl-terminal protein genes.

title: METHOD FOR FERMENTATIVE PRODUCTION OF D-PANTOTHEMIC ACID USING CORYNEFORM BACTERIA
text: METHOD FOR FERMENTATIVE PRODUCTION OF D-PANTOTHEMIC ACID USING CORYNEFORM BACTERIA. The invention refers to the recombinated DNK originating from corynebacterium which can be replicated in microorganisms of specimens of corynebacterium, and which contains at least one of the following sequences of nucleotides, chosen from the group: (a) which codes pan B-gene (transferase of ketopantoathidroxy methile) displayed with SEQ ID No 1, (b) which codes pan C-gene (synthesis of pantothenate), shown with SEQ ID No 2, especially pan BC-operone, and, in the given case, (c) which codes ilv D-gene (dehydratase of dihydroxy acid), shown with SEQ ID No 4, and on one procedure for the fermentativen production of D-pantothenic acid with the application of microorganisms of specimens corynebacterium, where the mentioned genes have been amplified.

title: Eukaryotic expression method of fish viral hemorrhagic septicemia virus G gene
text: Eukaryotic expression method of fish viral hemorrhagic septicemia virus G gene. The invention discloses a eukaryotic expression method of a fish viral hemorrhagic septicemia virus G gene. The method includes the following steps of (1) gene synthesis of a main antigenic domain optimized by codons, (2) construction of a reconstructed baculovirus transfer vector, (3) construction of shuttle plasmids and (4) analysis and identification of reconstructed Bacmid transfection and expression products. By means of the method, efficient expression of the G protein antigenic domain in a Bac-to-Bac baculovirus system; the activity of the expression protein is primarily identified and analyzed through Western blotting, it is proved that the expression protein has immunogenicity, and a foundation is provided for further epitope vaccine development and VHSV molecular diagnosis technology establishment.

title: LysSGF2 lyase as well as preparation method and application thereof
text: LysSGF2 lyase as well as preparation method and application thereof. The invention discloses lyase LysSGF2 as well as a preparation method and application thereof, and belongs to the technical field of gene engineering. The invention discloses a lyase LysSGF2 as well as a preparation method and application thereof. A prokaryotic expression vector plasmid LysSGF2-pET-32a containing a gene for coding the lyase LysSGF2 is obtained through gene synthesis. Recombinant plasmids are obtained by transforming DH5alpha competent cells, and are introduced into an escherichia coli BL21 expression system for mass expression of proteins. And purifying by virtue of an affinity medium, so as to realize in-vitro high-efficiency expression of the lyase LysSGF2. The LysSGF2 has good bactericidal activity, has a good splitting effect on gram-negative bacteria and gram-positive bacteria, and has a good application prospect in the development of novel bacteriophage drugs.

title: GAS VESICLE EXPRESSION SYSTEMS, GAS VESICLE CONSTRUCTS AND RELATED GENETIC CIRCUITS, VECTORS, MAMMALIAN CELLS, HOSTS, COMPOSITIONS, METHODS AND SYSTEMS
text: GAS VESICLE EXPRESSION SYSTEMS, GAS VESICLE CONSTRUCTS AND RELATED GENETIC CIRCUITS, VECTORS, MAMMALIAN CELLS, HOSTS, COMPOSITIONS, METHODS AND SYSTEMS. Provided herein are genetically engineered gas vesicle expression systems (GVES) that are configured to express gas vesicles (GVs) in a mammalian cell, related gas vesicle polynucleotide constructs, gas vesicle reporting genetic circuits, vectors, genetically engineered mammalian cells, non-human mammalian hosts, compositions, methods and systems, which in several embodiments can be used together with contrast-enhanced imaging techniques to detect and report biological events in an imaging target site comprising a mammalian cell and/or organism.

title: Gene synthesis, expression plasmid construction and product production of human alpha 26 interferon
text: Gene synthesis, expression plasmid construction and product production of human alpha 26 interferon. The human alpha 2b type interferon in a gene engineering new medicine for curing viral dysentery and malignant tumor. SAid invention designs and synthesizes the human alpha 26 type interferon gene formed from codon being font of use by colibacillus, and constitutes the expression plasmid with said gene to obtain high-effective expression in the colibacillus, and its expression quantity can be up to 30% of total protein of somatic, so that it provides the favourable condition for producing lots of human alpha 26 type interferon.

title: Novel natural pigment compound as well as preparation method and application thereof
text: Novel natural pigment compound as well as preparation method and application thereof. The invention discloses a novel natural pigment compound and a preparation method and application thereof. The molecular weight of a novel natural pigment compound is 257; the molecular formula of the compound is C14H11NO4; and the compound is obtained after synthetase DeBI prepared through whole-gene synthesis and shown in the nucleotide sequence SEQ ID No. 1 is expressed in an engineered saccharomyces cerevisiae strain. The compound has the characteristics of hydrophobicity and oleophobicity, shows good color rendering property, leveling property and color fastness, can be widely applied to the fields of synthetic fibers, plastics, rubber, leather, coatings, printing ink, paint, cosmetics, food additives and the like, is synthesized by utilizing engineering bacteria, is high in yield, not limited by raw material sources, free of toxicity and harm and easy to degrade, and has no pollution to the ecological environment.

title: Biosynthesis gene cluster of arylomycins A
text: Biosynthesis gene cluster of arylomycins A. The invention discloses a biosynthesis gene cluster of arylomycins A which is an antibiotic with an antibacterial activity and generated by a Streptomyces parvus CGMCC No. 4027 strain. The whole gene cluster comprises 8 genes: 3 NRPS genes (aryA, aryB, aryD); one post-modification gene (aryC); 3 precursor biosynthesis genes (aryF, aryG, aryH) and an MbtH gene (aryE). Through genetic manipulation for the above biosynthesis genes, synthesis of arylomycin A2 and arylomycinA4 is blocked. The genes provided by the invention can be used for searching and discovering compounds or genes and proteins capable of being used for medicine, industry or agriculture.

title: Functional magnetosome constructed by connecting antibody affinity protein in series and preparation method of functional magnetosome
text: Functional magnetosome constructed by connecting antibody affinity protein in series and preparation method of functional magnetosome. The invention provides another preparation method of the magnetosome. The target genes of at least two antibody affinity proteins are fused with the MamC gene of the membrane protein of the magnetosome through a surface display technology (RSM), so that the antibody affinity proteins are simultaneously expressed on the membrane of the magnetosome. The invention also provides a preparation method of the recombinant magnetospirillum. The method comprises the following steps: a target gene synthesis step, a loading step, a jointing step, a screening and amplification step and a verification step. The magnetosome prepared by the invention has higher efficiency when being combined with immune protein IgG, and can continuously have good activity at low temperature.

title: Production method of nano-porous glass for oligonucleotide synthesis
text: Production method of nano-porous glass for oligonucleotide synthesis. The invention belongs to the technical field of material processing, and particularly relates to a method for producing aperture-controllable nano-porous glass by using a circulating integrated device. The integrated device is adopted for CPG production, different treatment conditions are set for borosilicate glass with different mesh numbers, the waste liquid output, the reagent dosage and the treatment time can be reduced to the minimum, the treatment cost is greatly reduced, the production efficiency is improved, the energy consumption is reduced, the obtained product pore channels are moreuniform, the blockage phenomenon is avoided. The method plays a powerful role in promoting the fields of domestic gene synthesis industry, oligonucleotide drug research and the like.

title: Separation and purification method of recombinant human keratin
text: Separation and purification method of recombinant human keratin. The invention belongs to the technical field of keratin separation and purification, and particularly relates to a separation and purification method of a recombinant human keratin K31 full-length sequence. The separation and purification method of the recombinant human keratin is a method for obtaining high-purity full-length recombinant human keratin through escherichia coli expression and purification. The method comprises the steps of gene synthesis, vector construction, IPTG induced expression, inclusion body enrichment, inclusion body cleaning, affinity chromatography, on-column renaturation, protein elution and the like. The separation and purification method of the recombinant human keratin can obtain the recombinant human keratin with a complete structure, also has the advantages of high purity and high recovery rate, and is an ideal separation and purification method of the recombinant human keratin.

title: Method for producing phloretin by using saccharomyces cerevisiae
text: Method for producing phloretin by using saccharomyces cerevisiae. The invention discloses a method for producing phloretin by using saccharomyces cerevisiae, and belongs to the technical field of microbial genetic engineering. A natural metabolic phloretin path in a plant is successfully constructed in the saccharomyces cerevisiae BY4741, synthesis of phloretin in microorganisms is achieved, synthesis of the phloretin in the microorganisms is enhanced by enhancing synthesis path genes of tyrosine and phenylalanine in the saccharomyces cerevisiae, including elimination of tyrosine feedback inhibition and introduction of exogenous genes, synthesis of prerequisite tyrosine and phenylalanine is enhanced, and the yield of the phloretin is increased. And the yield of the phloretin is further improved by enhancing the expression of a key gene in a phloretin synthesis pathway. The yield of phloretin synthesized from the beginning by the engineering yeast can reach 246.21 mg/L, the synthesis problem of phloretin serving as a carbohydrase inhibitor is effectively solved, and a foundation is laid for synthesizing phloretin through efficient fermentation of microorganisms.

title: Zika Virus NS1 antigenic mutant and application thereof
text: Zika Virus NS1 antigenic mutant and application thereof. The invention provides a Zika Virus NS1 antigenic mutant and application thereof. By conducting comparative analysis on four types of NS1 antigen amino acid sequences of Zika Virus NS1 antigens and dengue virus, the parts with extremely high homology are found out to conduct knockout mutation, gene synthesis, vector construction, prokaryotic expression, antigen purification and immunology functional analysis in a genetic level, the mutant which does not influence the detected sensitivity of Zika and can obviously lower the detectable rate of a dengue positive specimen is screened out, and thus the cross in detection of Zika Virus and dengue virus antibodies is lowered.

title: Expression method of recombination human bone formation protein-2
text: Expression method of recombination human bone formation protein-2. This invention discloses a expressing method of human bone recombination to form albumen-2. It includes the synthesis of aiming gene hBMP-2 gene synthesis, hBMP-2 gene and carrier are cut by Nde I and BamH I, the carrier is recombined, host germ is transferred to construct hBMP-2 gene engineering germ, then hBMP-2 gene engineering germ is cultured, then occlusion body salvation is got after the occlusion body is separated, washed and dissolved. Then it is purified, ultrafiltrated, dialyzed and frozen dry to get albumen-2. The technique is simple, cost is low, output is high, activity is good, its renaturation can reach 60 percent, so it has great superiority in industrialization generating, so it is propitious to large scale generation.

title: Construction of immune tolerance inducing fusion peptide
text: Construction of immune tolerance inducing fusion peptide. The present invention belongs to the technical fields of autoimmune diseases and organ transplantation immune tolerance induction treatment, and particularly relates to an antigen peptide, proteolytic cleavage function gene selection, gene recombination, and a fusion protein construction method. According to the present invention, the gene coding sequence of a TAT protein core amino acid sequence, a CLPP1 domain coding gene sequence and a specific antigen peptide coding gene sequence are connected by adopting a gene synthesis method, wherein the three substances are connected through specific restriction site coding gene sequences. The immune tolerance inducing fusion peptide can be used for individualized treatments of autoimmune diseases and organ transplantation immunologic rejection.

title: GAS VESICLE EXPRESSION SYSTEMS, GAS VESICLE CONSTRUCTS AND RELATED GENETIC CIRCUITS, VECTORS, MAMMALIAN CELLS, HOSTS, COMPOSITIONS, METHODS AND SYSTEMS
text: GAS VESICLE EXPRESSION SYSTEMS, GAS VESICLE CONSTRUCTS AND RELATED GENETIC CIRCUITS, VECTORS, MAMMALIAN CELLS, HOSTS, COMPOSITIONS, METHODS AND SYSTEMS. Provided herein are genetically engineered gas vesicle expression systems (GVES) that are configured to express gas vesicles (GVs) in a mammalian cell, related gas vesicle polynucleotide constructs, gas vesicle reporting genetic circuits, vectors, genetically engineered mammalian cells, non-human mammalian hosts, compositions, methods and systems, which in several embodiments can be used together with contrast-enhanced imaging techniques to detect and report biological events in an imaging target site comprising a mammalian cell and/or organism.

title: Acyl sulfonic acid transferase mutant and application thereof
text: Acyl sulfonic acid transferase mutant and application thereof. The invention discloses a new enzyme with acylsulfonate transferase activity or a mutant thereof and application thereof, and belongs to the field of enzyme engineering. On the basis of an NCBI (National Center of Biotechnology Information) database, a new enzyme sequence is mined through a bioinformatics means, a new enzyme is obtained through codon optimization, gene synthesis and cell expression, the activity of acylsulfonate transferase is measured, and a corresponding mutant is obtained by using a directed evolution method. Compared with reported mouse acyl sulfonate transferase (ASTIV), the enzyme sequence obtained in the invention has better catalytic activity in a regeneration system of PAPS, and provides a new choice for enzymatic synthesis of PAPS.

title: Method for Detection and Quantification of Nitrile Lactone or Lactam Compounds Using Mutated Artificial Genetic Circuits
text: Method for Detection and Quantification of Nitrile Lactone or Lactam Compounds Using Mutated Artificial Genetic Circuits. The present invention relates to a method for detecting and quantifying nitrile, lactone or lactam compounds using artificial genetic circuits. More specifically, mutated artificial genetic circuits are constructed with a 117th leucine (L) of wild type nitR protein being substituted with any one selected from a group consisting of phenylalanine (F), tyrosine (Y) and tryptophan (W), or by using a modified nitR protein in which a 133th serine (S) of wild type nitR protein is replaced with proline (P), thereby having an effect of accurately detecting and quantifying even very small amounts of nitrile, lactone or lactam compounds.

title: Improved Methods for Rapid Gene Synthesis
text: Improved Methods for Rapid Gene Synthesis. Disclosed are methods and materials for assembling long polynucleotides from synthetic oligonucleotides. The use of synthetic oligonucleotides permits non-natural design of sequences. The oligonucleotides used for construction may be relatively short, according to practicalities of nucleotide synthesis. They are assembled using a ligase which is operative over a range of temperatures, i.e., is thermostable. The method and oligonucleotides are designed such that the melting temperature of the strands to be hybridized is set at a number of selected specific temperatures for each group of oligonucleotides to be hybridized and ligated. Hybridization and ligation take place at or near the melting temperature, so that each succeeding ligation is governed by a temperature that will prevent hybridization if any mismatches are present.

title: Genetically engineered bacterium for over-expression of NADH dehydrogenase subunit III of procambarus clarkia
text: Genetically engineered bacterium for over-expression of NADH dehydrogenase subunit III of procambarus clarkia. The invention relates to a method for intracellular production of NADH dehydrogenase subunit I III of procambarus clarkia by virtue of an escherichia coli expression system, and belongs to the field of bioengineering technology. According to the method disclosed by the invention, an nad3 gene is obtained through total-gene synthesis, a recombinant expression vector pColdII-nad3 is constructed and an escherichia coli expression strain BL21 (DE3) is transformed so as to achieve efficient expression; therefore, the method disclosed by the invention lays an important foundation for subsequent advanced studies on an ovary 'eyestalk-ablation effect' molecular mechanism of the procambarus clarkia.

title: Gene synthesis, expression plasmid construction and product production of human 16 interferon
text: Gene synthesis, expression plasmid construction and product production of human 16 interferon. The human alpha Ib type interferon is a gene engineering new medicine for curing viral diseases and malignant tumor. Said invention disigns and synthesizes the human lapha 1b type interferon gene formed from codeon being font of use by colibacillus, and constitutes the expression plasmid with said gene to obtain high-effective ecpression in colibacillus, and its expression quantity can be up to 30% of total protein of somatic, so that it provides the favourable condition for producing large amount of human alpha 1b type interferon.

title: Method for intracellular production of NADH dehydrogenase subunit I protein of procambarus clarkia
text: Method for intracellular production of NADH dehydrogenase subunit I protein of procambarus clarkia. The invention relates to a method for intracellular production of NADH dehydrogenase subunit I protein of procambarus clarkia by virtue of an escherichia coli expression system, and belongs to the field of bioengineering technology. According to the method disclosed by the invention, an nad1 gene is obtained through total-gene synthesis, a recombinant expression vector pColdII-nad1 is constructed and an escherichia coli expression strain BL21 (DE3) is transformed so as to achieve efficient expression; therefore, the method disclosed by the invention lays an important foundation for subsequent advanced studies on an ovary 'eyestalk-ablation effect' molecular mechanism of the procambarus clarkia.

title: Recombinant Escherichia coli for biosynthesis of dextro-bicyclic gemmarene, construction method and application
text: Recombinant Escherichia coli for biosynthesis of dextro-bicyclic gemmarene, construction method and application. The invention relates to recombinant Escherichia coli for biosynthesis of dextro-bicyclic gemmarene, a construction method and application. The recombinant escherichia coli strain disclosed by the invention is obtained by modifying an endogenous MEP (Methyl Ester Protein) path of escherichia coli by adopting a genetic engineering means, and then assembling a dextro-bicyclic gemmarene heterologous gene synthesis module and a modified chassis strain, so as to obtain the recombinant escherichia coli for producing dextro-bicyclic gemmarene. When the recombinant bacterium is fermented in a 5L fermentation tank for 48h, the dextro-bicyclic gemmacene can be accumulated to 210.7 mg/L, and the specific yield is 12.13 mg/gdcw.

title: Application of GmMYB85 gene in regulation and control of synthesis of vegetable fat
text: Application of GmMYB85 gene in regulation and control of synthesis of vegetable fat. The invention relates to the technical field of plant breeding, in particular to application of a GmMYB85 gene in regulation and control of vegetable fat synthesis. The sequence of the GmMYB85 gene is as shown in SEQ ID NO.1, and the GmMYB85 gene achieves the purpose of regulating and controlling grease synthesis by regulating and controlling synthesis of triglyceride and fatty acid in plants. The fatty acid comprises oleic acid, linoleic acid and linolenic acid. Through overexpression of the GmMYB85 gene, synthesis of triglyceride and fatty acid in a plant is promoted, and the plant comprises arabidopsis thaliana. According to the invention, the biological function of the GmMYB85 gene in soybean oil synthesis regulation is defined, and the GmMYB85 gene has important significance in further improvement of soybean seed oil traits.

title: SYSTEMS FOR CELL PROGRAMMING TOWARDS CHONDROGENIC LINEAGE AND METHODS THEREOF
text: SYSTEMS FOR CELL PROGRAMMING TOWARDS CHONDROGENIC LINEAGE AND METHODS THEREOF. Provided herein are systems of modulating gene expression, methods of use thereof, and cells engineered thereof for the purpose of differentiating cells, for example chondroprogenitor cells.

title: A MEDICAL DIAGNOSTIC DEVICE
text: A MEDICAL DIAGNOSTIC DEVICE. The present invention relates to a medical diagnostic device with a cellular biosensor which detects urea and uric acid by means of a synthetic genetic circuit essentially consisting of transcriptional regulator and bio-sensing module.

title: Red natural pigment compound as well as preparation method and application thereof
text: Red natural pigment compound as well as preparation method and application thereof. The invention discloses a red natural pigment and a preparation method and application thereof.The molecular weight of a red natural pigment compound is 513, the molecular formula is C28H19NO9, the compound is obtained by expressing synthase PskI obtained through whole-gene synthesis and shown in the nucleotide sequence SEQ ID No.1 in saccharomyces cerevisiae engineering bacteria,and the compound has the hydrophobic and oleophobic characteristics. The compound has good color rendering property, leveling property and color fastness, can be widely applied to the fields of synthetic fibers, plastics, rubber, leather, coatings, printing ink, paint, cosmetics, food additives and the like, is synthesized by utilizing engineering bacteria, is high in yield, is not limited by raw material sources, is non-toxic and harmless, is easy to degrade, and does not cause pollution to the ecological environment.

title: RNA VACCINE FOR PORCINE EPIDEMIC DIARRHEA AND PREPARATION METHOD THEREFOR
text: RNA VACCINE FOR PORCINE EPIDEMIC DIARRHEA AND PREPARATION METHOD THEREFOR. Provided are an RNA vaccine for porcine epidemic diarrhea and a preparation method therefor. The RNA vaccine is obtained by means of gene synthesis, in-vitro transcription, and lipid nanoparticle encapsulation, which can rapidly respond to the currently prevalent new PEDV strains, reduces the research and production cycle of new vaccine development, and provides an effective technical approach for the prevention and control of newly mutated highly virulent strains. The prepared RNA vaccine for porcine epidemic diarrhea has high production efficiency, can induce relatively high levels of porcine epidemic diarrhea virus neutralizing antibodies and binding antibodies in vivo, effectively reducing the occurrence of diarrhea in piglets, and thus holds great promotional value for the prevention and control of porcine epidemic diarrhea.

title: NAD (H) -dependent 3 alpha-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase and coding gene thereof
text: NAD (H) -dependent 3 alpha-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase and coding gene thereof. The invention provides a novel NAD (H) -dependent 3 alpha-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase. The amino acid sequence of the novel NAD (H) -dependent 3 alpha-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase is shown as SEQ ID NO. 1. A coding gene (the nucleotide sequence is shown as SEQ ID NO. 2) of the novel 3 alpha-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase is obtained in a whole-gene synthesis manner. The 3 alpha-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase with high detection efficiency is provided for detection of the total content of bile acid, and has huge application value in clinical examination.

title: Gene cluster for biological synthesis of Nosiheptide
text: Gene cluster for biological synthesis of Nosiheptide. The invention relates to gene cluster for biological synthesis of Nosiheptide, particularly relates to cloning, sequencing, analyzing, researching function and use thereof of gene cluster for biological synthesis of streptomycete produced Nosiheptide with anti-Gram positive antibiotics. The entire gene cluster contain 16 genes: six genes related to macrocyclic biosynthesis of Nosiheptide, five genes related to biological synthesis of lateral chain, four genes related to modification after translation, and one regulatory gene. Synthesis of Nosiheptide can be blocked by genetic operations of thegenes for biological synthesis. The gene and protein thereof provided by the invention can be used for looking for and discovering compound or gene, protein for medicine, industry or agricultural.

title: SYSTEMS FOR CELL PROGRAMMING AND METHODS THEREOF
text: SYSTEMS FOR CELL PROGRAMMING AND METHODS THEREOF. Provided herein are systems of modulating gene expression, methods of use thereof, and cells engineered thereof for the purpose of differentiating cells, for example immune cells.

title: Exonuclease-based oligonucleotide purification method
text: Exonuclease-based oligonucleotide purification method. The invention relates to a purification method of oligonucleotides. According to the method, the secondary structure of target oligonucleotides and the principle that DNA single-stranded Exonuclease specifically degrades the oligonucleotides of different structures are utilized, and the purpose of separating and purifying the oligonucleotides of different structures is achieved. Particularly, whenthe structure of the target oligonucleotide is a hairpin structure and the structure of the non-target oligonucleotide is a linear single-stranded structure, the DNA single-stranded exonuclease can be used for realizing specific degradation of the non-target oligonucleotide and reserving the target oligonucleotide. The oligonucleotides purified by the method can be directly used for downstream operations, especially for gene synthesis. The operations of gel cutting, leaching or gel melting, centrifuging, cleaning, eluting and the like involved in other methods are reduced, and the purpose ofpurifying oligonucleotides with high flux and low cost can be achieved.

title: Genetic circuit inverting amplifier
text: Genetic circuit inverting amplifier. We describe methods and compositions for setting the level of gene expression in a cell. The instant invention is of an amplifier, an analog circuit. It is directly analogous to the inverting amplifier commonly used in electronics. This circuit produces an output wherein an increase in the input signal leads to a proportional decrease in the output. Similarly, a decrease in the input leads to an increase in the output signal. Here the inputs and outputs may be concentrations of intracellular molecular species. The circuit is implemented as nucleic acid constructs. By analogy with the similar circuit found in analog electronics, such a circuit is expected to have improved signal to noise ratio, improved stability, and increased accuracy. It should aid in the development of intracellular instrumentation and hence the diagnosis and treatment of disease.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS USING MACHINE LEARNING FOR EVOLUTIONARY DATA-DRIVEN DESIGN OF PROTEINS AND OTHER SEQUENCE DEFINED BIOMOLECULES
text: METHOD AND APPARATUS USING MACHINE LEARNING FOR EVOLUTIONARY DATA-DRIVEN DESIGN OF PROTEINS AND OTHER SEQUENCE DEFINED BIOMOLECULES. A method and apparatus are provided for designing sequence-defined biomolecules, such as proteins using a data-driven, evolution-based process. To design proteins, an iterative method founded on a combination of an unsupervised sequence-based model with a supervised functionality-based model can select candidate amino acid sequences that are likely to have a desired functionality. Feedback from measuring the candidate proteins using a high-throughput gene-synthesis and a protein screening process is used to refine and improve the models guiding the candidate selection to the most promising regions of the very large amino acid sequence search space.

title: SYSTEMS FOR STEM CELL PROGRAMMING AND METHODS THEREOF
text: SYSTEMS FOR STEM CELL PROGRAMMING AND METHODS THEREOF. Provided herein are systems of modulating gene expression and methods of use thereof for converting cells of one type to another type (e.g., reprogramming of differentiated cells into stem cells).

title: Method and apparatus for obtaining high purity nucleotides
text: Method and apparatus for obtaining high purity nucleotides. The present invention relates to a method and apparatus for acquiring high-purity nucleotides that can be favorably used in a genome/gene synthesis field, as a DNA synthesis technique capable of synthesizing base sequences with high purity at a low cost. To this end, the method comprises the following steps: (a) preparing nucleic acid fragments; (b) tagging the nucleic acid fragments with bar code sequences; (c) confirming positional coordinates of the fragments having verified sequences among the nucleic acid fragments tagged with the bar code sequences; and (d) recovering fragments having the verified sequences using the positional coordinates.

title: VHH antibody capable of being combined with helicobacter pylori
text: VHH antibody capable of being combined with helicobacter pylori. The invention discloses a VHH antibody capable of being combined with helicobacter pylori, and relates to the technical field of VHH.A target gene is inserted into a pPICZalpha A carrier through gene synthesis, a linearized recombinant plasmid is obtained through enzyme digestion and purification technologies, the plasmid is converted into an X33 yeast competent state, and the VHH antibody capable of being combined with helicobacter pylori is obtained. Then obtaining a stable helicobacter pylori VHH antibody through positive clone screening, inducible expression and protein identification; according to the invention, the stability of the helicobacter pylori antibody can be improved, the production process of the helicobacter pylori antibody is optimized, and the more stable helicobacter pylori VHH antibody can be obtained; the method is simple, effective, easy and practical.

title: RNA-BASED REGULATORY TECHNOLOGIES FOR MIRNA SENSORS
text: RNA-BASED REGULATORY TECHNOLOGIES FOR MIRNA SENSORS. Provided herein are genetic circuits and cell state classifiers for detecting the microRNA profile of a cell. In some embodiments, the cell state classifiers described herein utilize an endoribonuclease and a self-amplifying RNA molecule for controlling the expression of an output molecule. In some embodiments, the cell state classifiers described herein are encoded on a single RNA transcript, which is then processed to produce individual genetic circuits that function independently. The genetic circuits and cell state classifiers described herein may be used in various applications (e.g., therapeutic or diagnostic applications).

title: An EPSP synthase gene AroA-R1 derived from rhizobium leguminosarum and a preparing method and applications thereof
text: An EPSP synthase gene AroA-R1 derived from rhizobium leguminosarum and a preparing method and applications thereof. An EPSP synthase gene AroA-R1 derived from rhizobium leguminosarum and a preparing method and applications thereof are disclosed. The nucleotide sequence of the EPSP synthase gene AroA-R1 is shown as SEQ ID NO1, and an encoded protein sequence is shown as SEQ ID NO2. The EPSP synthase gene AroA-R1 is prepared by utilization of a gene synthesis method. The EPSP synthase encoded by the AroA-R1 gene has resistance to glyphosate, can improve resistance of plants to glyphosate herbicides after the EPSP synthase is transformed into the plants, and can be used for cultivation of glyphosate-resistant transgenic crops.

title: Construction method of luciferase transgenic zebrafish driven by glucocorticoid response element
text: Construction method of luciferase transgenic zebrafish driven by glucocorticoid response element. The invention provides a construction method of a luciferase transgenic zebrafish driven by a glucocorticoid response element. The construction method comprises the following steps that firstly, a carrier Tol2-9*GRE-HSV.U123-Luciferase-SV40pA-Tol2 is constructed through methods of gene synthesis and enzyme-digestion connection; secondly, the carrier obtained in the first step and Tol2 mRNA are jointly injected into a single-celled fertilized egg of the zebrafish; and thirdly, the fertilized egg is cultured, and thus the luciferase transgenic zebrafish driven by the glucocorticoid response element is obtained. Compared with other methods for detecting the activity of glucocorticoid, the qualitative and quantitative effects are easier through the construction method.

title: Nucleic acid linkers and their use in gene synthesis
text: Nucleic acid linkers and their use in gene synthesis. Single-stranded nucleic acid (I), for use in preparation of nucleic acid (NA) comprising a part (A) comprising the sequence of a recognition site for a type IIS restriction enzyme, or its fragments or complement, and a part (B) that is a defined sequence of nucleotides, is new. <??>Independent claims are also included for: <??>(1) a library of many (I); <??>(2) method for preparing nucleic acid (NA) using (I); <??>(3) kit for preparation of NA containing the library of (1); <??>(4) methods for enzymatic preparation of a partly double-stranded oligonucleotide (ON) with a 3 nucleotide overhang; <??>(5) several methods for preparing NA, or a group of NA molecules, using ON; and <??>(6) method for amplifying a ligation product, produced during a sloning process.

title: Anti-eperythrozoon ovine HSP70 protein polyclonal antibody as well as preparation method and application thereof
text: Anti-eperythrozoon ovine HSP70 protein polyclonal antibody as well as preparation method and application thereof. The invention provides an anti-eperythrozoon ovine HSP70 protein polyclonal antibody as well as a preparation method and application thereof, and belongs to the technical field of bioengineering. The polyclonal antibody disclosed by the invention is prepared by immunizing an animal with an immunogen containing 109th-266th amino acids of the eperythrozoon ovine HSP70 protein, and has the characteristics of high titer and strong specificity. The preparation method of the polyclonal antibody comprises the steps of gene synthesis, protein expression and purification, animal immunization, antibody purification and the like. The invention also provides an application of the polyclonal antibody in immunological detection such as Western Blot, ELISA (Enzyme-Linked Immunosorbent Assay) and the like, and a detection kit containing the polyclonal antibody. The polyclonal antibody provides a core reagent for serological diagnosis of sheep eperythrozoonosis, and has important application value.

title: Recombination variant of human nerve growth factors and preparation method thereof
text: Recombination variant of human nerve growth factors and preparation method thereof. The invention discloses a recombination variant (rhNGF) of human nerve growth factors and a preparation method thereof. According to the recombination variant of the human nerve growth factors designed by a molecular structure research, ten SSSHPIFHRG amino acids are missed and two GA amino acids are excess at the N end of a peptide fragment, after that a sequence starting from EFSV is same as the normal sequence of the human nerve growth factors. CDNA of rhNGF is obtained with a gene synthesis method, engineering bacteria are built to express, an rhNGF fusion protein is obtained, the rhNGF fusion protein is cut by hydroxylamine and then is purified to obtain the purified rhNGF, and the purified rhNGF has natural biological activity.

title: Preparation method and application of waterproof bonding material
text: Preparation method and application of waterproof bonding material. The invention discloses a preparation method and application of a waterproof adhesive, the waterproof adhesive is a red giant barnacle TRX-Mrcp-20K protein solution prepared by recombination, and the nucleic acid sequence and the protein sequence of the TRX-Mrcp20K protein are respectively shown as SEQ ID No. 1 and SEQ ID No. 2. The preparation method comprises the steps of gene synthesis, recombinant plasmid construction, transformation, induction, affinity chromatography and the like, and the biological mucilage glue is high in adhesion performance, relatively high in yield, low in preparation cost and capable of being used for adhesion of various materials in a humid environment.

title: Method for DNA sequence synthesis
text: Method for DNA sequence synthesis. The invention belongs to the technical field of gene synthesis, and particularly relates to a method for DNA sequence synthesis. The method comprises the following steps: S1, adding a universal sequence at two ends of a DNA sequence to be synthesized as a target DNA sequence, and designing a short DNA single strand for assembly according to the sequence; s2, performing dissolution, mixing, PCR (polymerase chain reaction) and other treatments on the DNA single chain; s3, carrying out PCR (Polymerase Chain Reaction) amplification by utilizing a stationary phase; and S4, carrying out PCR (Polymerase Chain Reaction) amplification by utilizing the universal primer. The method is simple to operate, low in cost and high in synthesis efficiency, has specificity, and is suitable for operation based on an automatic pipetting workstation and a micro-fluidic chip.

title: Construction method and application of engineering shewanella for degrading nicotine wastewater
text: Construction method and application of engineering shewanella for degrading nicotine wastewater. The invention discloses a construction method and application of engineering shewanella for degrading nicotine wastewater, and belongs to the technical field of wastewater treatment. The method comprises the following steps: acquiring a flavinase NicA2 gene, a cytochrome c protein gene and an arabinose inducible promoter gene of pseudomonas by using a gene synthesis technology, and connecting the three gene segments to a vector plasmid pYYDT skeleton by using a Gibson connection technology to construct a pYYDT-NC plasmid; the engineering shewanella MR-1/pYYDT-NC is obtained by electrically transferring the pYYDT-NC plasmid into shewanella, the strain is used for treating nicotine in tobacco wastewater, the performance of efficient oxidative degradation of nicotine is shown, the strain has good adaptability to wastewater with different concentrations of nicotine, and the practical application effect of degrading nicotine in tobacco wastewater is remarkable.

title: Novel ribonuclease A and purification production process thereof
text: Novel ribonuclease A and purification production process thereof. The invention provides novel ribonuclease A which is formed by carrying out mutation on a plurality of specific sites on the basis of cattle pancreatic gland ribonuclease A. A purification production process of the novel ribonuclease A comprises the following steps: constructing a pichia pastoris recombinant plasmid after carrying out gene synthesis on a gene sequence; and then, carrying out seed culture, multiplication culture of a fermentation medium, batch culture of glycerol feed supplement and inducible expression of methanol feed supplement, and carrying out protein purification to obtain target novel ribonuclease A fusion protein. The target protein obtained by the method is high in expression quantity, easy to purify, high in stability, long in acting time of active ingredients and long in retention period.

title: SYSTEMS FOR CELL PROGRAMMING TOWARDS PANCREATIC LINEAGE AND METHODS THEREOF
text: SYSTEMS FOR CELL PROGRAMMING TOWARDS PANCREATIC LINEAGE AND METHODS THEREOF. Provided herein are systems of modulating gene expression, methods of use thereof, and cells engineered thereof for the purpose of differentiating cells, for example beta cells.

title: Method for massive directed mutagenesis
text: Method for massive directed mutagenesis. Method for producing a library (A) of mutated genes, useful for generating proteins with improved properties, by synthesizing many mutated oligonucleotides on a support, solubilizing and using them for gene synthesis, is new. Method for producing a library (A) of mutated genes comprises: (a) synthesizing, on a solid support, a library of oligonucleotides (ON) complementary to one or more regions of one or more target genes (TG), each including, preferably at its center, one or more mutations relative to TG; (b) forming a solution of the library of ON; and (c) generating (A) from the solution of step (b) and one or more matrices that contain TG. An independent claim is also included for a method for mutagenesis of one or more target proteins (TP) comprising generating (A) by the new method then expressing it.

title: Pair of TALENs for efficiently editing rice WAXY gene, and identification targeting site and application thereof
text: Pair of TALENs for efficiently editing rice WAXY gene, and identification targeting site and application thereof. The invention provides a pair of TALENs for efficiently editing a rice WAXY gene, and an identification targeting site and an application thereof, and concretely provides a transcription activator-like effect nuclease (TALEN) gene for efficiently targeting the rice endogenous WAXY gene, and a method using a recombinant plasmid containing the gene to carry out oriented targeting. The TALENs contain a pair of TALE proteins and DNA endonuclease catalytic subunits respectively fused with the TALE proteins, and can respectively identify and cut two adjacent sites of the rice endogenous WAXY gene. Synthesis of a nucleotide sequence for encoding the TALENs and construction of a vector including the nucleotide sequence are realized on the basis of designing the amino acid sequence of the TALEs, and the cell targeting efficiency is greatly improved through plasmid transfection of cells by the TALENs.

title: Bifunctional expression vector and chimeric antigen receptor modified macrophage
text: Bifunctional expression vector and chimeric antigen receptor modified macrophage. The invention relates to a bifunctional expression vector and a chimeric antigen receptor modified macrophage. According to the invention, gene segments of whole gene synthesis shKLF4 and a p85 subunit of chimeric antigen receptor CD8SP-anti-MUC1sc Fv-IghG1 hinge region-CD8TM-FcR gamma1-PI3kappa are inserted into a PiggyBac vector, and the bifunctional expression vector for targeting MUC1 and silencing KLF4 is provided. The expression vector is packaged in an MCA high polymer material to transfect macrophages, so that the macrophages express the chimeric antigen receptor. The transformation method of the high polymer material provided by the invention has the advantages of high transformation efficiency, stable expression of exogenous genes and the like when being applied to transfection of macrophages, and the prepared macrophages have ideal clinical application prospects.

title: T CELL ACTIVATION RESPONSIVE CONSTRUCTS FOR ENHANCED CAR-T CELL THERAPY
text: T CELL ACTIVATION RESPONSIVE CONSTRUCTS FOR ENHANCED CAR-T CELL THERAPY. The present disclosure generally relates to genetic circuits including response elements engineered from the NR4A1 that augment chimeric antigen receptor activation with the delivery of a bioactive molecule. Particularly, the disclosure provides nucleic acid constructs including response elements operably linked to a nucleic acid sequence of interest such that are responsive to activation of CAR-T cells. The disclosure also relates to vectors and cells including the genetic circuits and the nucleic acid constructs. Also provided are methods of inducing an immune response, methods of treatment of subjects, and methods for delivering a bioactive molecule by an activated T cell having a chimeric antigen receptor and the response element constructs of the disclosure.

title: GENE DETECTION TOOL AND GENE DETECTION KIT
text: GENE DETECTION TOOL AND GENE DETECTION KIT. Provided is a gene detection tool with which it is possible to quickly collect and add a test sample through a simple operation and also to reduce the amount of waste. This gene detection tool 2 comprises a container body 21 for accommodating a gene synthesis reaction solution 10, a container lid 22 for sealing the container body 21, and an electrode part 23 and a sample collection part 24 that are provided to the container lid 22 and inserted into the container body 21. The sample collection part 24 is configured to be capable of suctioning a test sample that may include a gene to be detected.

title: Gene cluster for biological synthesis of Nosiheptide
text: Gene cluster for biological synthesis of Nosiheptide. The invention relates to gene cluster for biological synthesis of Nosiheptide, particularly relates to cloning, sequencing, analyzing, researching function and use thereof of gene cluster for biological synthesis of streptomycete produced Nosiheptide with anti-Gram positive antibiotics. The entire gene cluster contain 16 genes: six genes related to macrocyclic biosynthesis of Nosiheptide, five genes related to biological synthesis of lateral chain, four genes related to modification after translation, and one regulatory gene. Synthesis of Nosiheptide can be blocked by genetic operations of the genes for biological synthesis. The gene and protein thereof provided by the invention can be used for looking for and discovering compound or gene, protein for medicine, industry or agricultural.

title: Synthesis of single-stranded DNA and error correction method
text: Synthesis of single-stranded DNA and error correction method. The invention provides the synthesis of single-stranded DNA and an error correction method, relates to the field of gene synthesis, and specifically relates to the synthesis of the single-stranded DNAand the error correction method during synthesis. Error fragments during the synthesis of the DNA can be eliminated, so that the accuracy of single-stranded DNA synthetic fragments can be enhanced. Amethod for synthesizing the single-stranded DNA is provided; restriction sites are designed on the two ends of a target sequence, and a hairpin structure is formed; and error correction is performedthrough restriction endonuclease and mispairing specific endonuclease, so that correct DNA fragments can be obtained.

title: Acyl-coenzyme A synthetase mutant as well as coding gene, expression strain and application thereof
text: Acyl-coenzyme A synthetase mutant as well as coding gene, expression strain and application thereof. The invention discloses an acyl coenzyme A synthetase mutant, and a coding gene, an expression strain and application thereof, and the acyl coenzyme A synthetase mutant is based on acyl coenzyme A synthetase from marine uncultured microorganisms, and a mutant gene is obtained through complete gene synthesis after a mutation site is designed through Funclib. After the engineering bacteria containing the mutant plasmids are subjected to induced expression, the acyl-coenzyme A synthetase with improved specific enzyme activity and improved stability is obtained. When palmitic acid is used as a substrate, the specific enzyme activity of the mutant is improved by 7.0 times, and the mutant has potential application value in an in-vitro diagnostic kit for detecting free fatty acid by an enzyme method.

title: METHOD FOR CONSTRUCTING MODEL FOR GENE SEQUENCE SYNTHESIS DIFFICULTY ANALYSIS AND USE THEREOF
text: METHOD FOR CONSTRUCTING MODEL FOR GENE SEQUENCE SYNTHESIS DIFFICULTY ANALYSIS AND USE THEREOF. A method for constructing a model for gene sequence synthesis difficulty analysis and a use thereof. The method comprises: acquiring a plurality of different gene sequences having known sequence synthesis difficulties to form a database for modeling; extracting sequence features from the gene sequences in the database; and using a regression algorithm to establish a quantitative prediction model on the basis of the sequence features and the known sequence synthesis difficulties. The constructed quantitative prediction model can provide a client with a precise period required for gene sequence synthesis as specified in an order form, allow a gene synthesis company to carry out comprehensive management, and improve production efficiency.

title: Fusion protein with DNA (Deoxyribose Nucleic Acid) error correction enzyme activity and preparation method and application thereof
text: Fusion protein with DNA (Deoxyribose Nucleic Acid) error correction enzyme activity and preparation method and application thereof. The invention discloses a fusion protein with DNA (Deoxyribose Nucleic Acid) error correction enzyme activity as well as a preparation method and application thereof. The present invention provides a fusion protein comprising a base mismatch specific endonuclease and a domain that contributes to affinity adsorption of a zymoprotein to a matrix carrier or promotes expression of the zymoprotein. The base mismatched specific endonuclease is escherichia coli T7 bacteriophage endonuclease I, and the structural domain facilitating affinity adsorption of zymoprotein to a specific matrix carrier or promoting zymoprotein expression is a cellulose specific binding structural domain CBM3. The method can be used for DNA base error or mutation detection, base error correction in the gene synthesis process and the like.

title: Gas vesicle expression systems, gas vesicle constructs and related genetic circuits, vectors, mammalian cells, hosts, compositions, methods and systems
text: Gas vesicle expression systems, gas vesicle constructs and related genetic circuits, vectors, mammalian cells, hosts, compositions, methods and systems. Provided herein are genetically engineered gas vesicle expression systems (GVES) that are configured to express gas vesicles (GVs) in a mammalian cell, related gas vesicle polynucleotide constructs, gas vesicle reporting genetic circuits, vectors, genetically engineered mammalian cells, non-human mammalian hosts, compositions, methods and systems, which in several embodiments can be used together with contrast-enhanced imaging techniques to detect and report biological events in an imaging target site comprising a mammalian cell and/or organism.

title: Rapid bidirectional multilocus gene mutation method
text: Rapid bidirectional multilocus gene mutation method. The invention relates to a rapid bidirectional multilocus gene mutation method. According to the method, by taking an improved overlap extension PCR method as a basis and adopting sequence specific primers with mutation sites, DNA bidirectional multilocus gene mutation is realized by carrying out two PCR amplifications and joining DNA segments. The method can be widely used in target gene site-directed mutagenesis, gene synthesis, protein structure-function research, protein expression optimization and target gene packaging. According to the method, three DNA segments and three corresponding pairs of specific amplification primers are designed based on sequence characteristics of DNA segments needing mutation. Target gene segments for introducing needed mutation sites are formed by carrying out two PCR amplifications and setting specific amplification programs.

title: COLLECTIONS AND USES THEREOF
text: COLLECTIONS AND USES THEREOF. The present disclosure relates to synthetic collections of candidate proteinaceous binding molecules, for example, antibodies, wherein one or more candidate proteinaceous binding molecules comprise an optimized property as compared to a parental proteinaceous binding molecule. In particular, the collections of candidate proteinaceous binding molecules are made by gene synthesis, and are based upon the sequence of the parental proteinaceous binding molecule, for example, antibody, therefore a custom collection of candidate proteinaceous binding molecules can be made specifically for one parental proteinaceous binding molecules. The present disclosure also relates to methods of making and using such collections for optimizing parental proteinaceous binding molecules, for example, antibodies. The present disclosure also relates to the candidate proteinaceous binding molecules of the synthetic collections comprising optimized properties.

title: Targeted tyrosinase gene loaded small nucleic acid molecule exosome and preparation method and application thereof
text: Targeted tyrosinase gene loaded small nucleic acid molecule exosome and preparation method and application thereof. The invention discloses an exosome of a small nucleic acid molecule loaded with a targeted tyrosinase gene as well as a preparation method and application of the exosome. The exosome contains siRNA (small interfering Ribonucleic Acid) or shRNA (short hairpin Ribonucleic Acid) of a targeted The method is simple and easy to operate, stable in process and good in applicability. The obtained exosome is loaded with a large number of siRNA or shRNA of targeted tyrosinase genes, synthesis of tyrosine kinase can be efficiently inhibited, formation of melanin is reduced, and the exosome can serve as an efficient drug delivery system to be applied to the field of medical cosmetology.

title: ABC transporter gene MRP1S as well as preparation method and application thereof
text: ABC transporter gene MRP1S as well as preparation method and application thereof. The invention discloses an ABC transport protein gene mrp1S from grapes as well as a preparation method and application of the ABC transport protein gene mrp1S. The nucleotide sequence of the ABC transport protein gene mrp1S is shown as SEQ ID No 1, and the coded amino acid sequence of the ABC transport protein gene mrp1S is shown as SEQ ID No 2. The mrp1S gene is prepared from an ABC transportergene MRP1 derived from grapes by using a gene synthesis method. The mrp1S gene is transformed into arabidopsis thaliana to obtain transgenic arabidopsis thaliana, and the cadmium resistance of the transgenic arabidopsis thaliana is obviously superior to that of wild arabidopsis thaliana; and therefore, the mrp1S gene can be applied to cadmium-tolerant plant breeding.

title: Method and apparatus using machine learning for evolutionary data-driven design of proteins and other sequence defined biomolecules
text: Method and apparatus using machine learning for evolutionary data-driven design of proteins and other sequence defined biomolecules. A method and apparatus are provided for designing sequence-defined biomolecules, such as proteins using a data-driven, evolution-based process. To design proteins, an iterative method founded on a combination of an unsupervised sequence-based model with a supervised functionality-based model can select candidate amino acid sequences that are likely to have a desired functionality. Feedback from measuring the candidate proteins using a high-throughput gene-synthesis and a protein screening process is used to refine and improve the models guiding the candidate selection to the most promising regions of the very large amino acid sequence search space.

title: Method and apparatus for obtaining high purity nucleotides
text: Method and apparatus for obtaining high purity nucleotides. The present invention relates to a DNA synthesis technique capable of synthesizing a base sequence at a low cost and high purity. More specifically, the present invention relates to a method for obtaining high purity nucleotide and an apparatus, useful in a field of genome/gene synthesis. To this end, the method comprises the following steps: (a) preparing nucleic acid fragments; (b) tagging the nucleic acid fragments with bar code sequences; (c) confirming positional coordinates of the fragments having verified sequences among the nucleic acid fragments tagged with the bar code sequences; and (d) recovering fragments having the verified sequence using the positional coordinates.

title: METHODS FOR CIRCULARIZING LINEAR DOUBLE STRANDED NUCLEIC ACIDS
text: METHODS FOR CIRCULARIZING LINEAR DOUBLE STRANDED NUCLEIC ACIDS. The present invention relates to a method, in particular an in vitro method, for the circularization of a double stranded DNA nucleic acid. The invention also relates to a circularized double stranded DNA nucleic acid obtainable by a method according to the instant invention. Another aspect of the invention pertains to a host cell comprising a circularized double stranded DNA nucleic acid obtainable by a method according to the instant invention. The invention further concerns therapeutic and non-therapeutic uses of a circularized double stranded DNA nucleic acid obtainable by a method according to the instant invention. Finally, the invention relates to a kit for the circularization of a double stranded DNA nucleic acid.

title: Echinococcus multilocularis leucine aminopeptidase subunit vaccine LAP, preparation method and application thereof
text: Echinococcus multilocularis leucine aminopeptidase subunit vaccine LAP, preparation method and application thereof. The invention discloses an echinococcus multilocularis leucine aminopeptidase subunit vaccine LAP, a preparation method and application thereof, and the active ingredient of the subunit vaccine LAP isa polypeptide and is mainly composed of an amino acid sequence of echinococcus multilocularis antigen protein LAP. An echinococcus multilocularis subunit vaccine LAP genetic sequence is synthesized mainly through a gene synthesis technology and is connected to an expression vector through double digestion, and then the expression vector is transformed into Arctic Express for fusion protein expression. After the protein is purified, the echinococcus multilocularis subunit vaccine LAP is obtained. The echinococcus multilocularis subunit vaccine can induce an organism to generate T cell and B cell immune responses and high-titer specific antibody humoral immune responses aiming at echinococcus multilocularis, can effectively prevent mice from infecting echinococcus multilocularis, and can beused for preventing and treating echinococcus multilocularis infection related diseases.

title: IMPROVED LENTIVIRAL EXPRESSION VECTOR, CONSTRUCTION METHOD FOR SAME, AND APPLICATIONS THEREOF
text: IMPROVED LENTIVIRAL EXPRESSION VECTOR, CONSTRUCTION METHOD FOR SAME, AND APPLICATIONS THEREOF. Provided are an improved lentiviral expression vector, a construction method for same, and applications thereof. The plasmid backbone of the lentiviral expression vector is based on pLVX-Puro and is improved via the following steps: a. adding a CMV enhancer and promoter to the upstream of 5'LTR by means of gene synthesis and subcloning; b. replacing the original 5'LTR with a new 5'LTR (SEQ ID NO: 3) of which the length is 181 bps; c. replacing the CMV promotor on an original vector with an EFS promotor; d. deleting a PGK promotor after multiple cloning sites of the original vector and, at the same time, introducing a P2A connection subsequence; e. replacing original resistance against Puro with resistance against a shorter Blasticidin; and f. replacing original 3'LTR with an LTR (SEQ ID NO: 7) with a partially deleted U3. The lentiviral expression vector acquired by the improvement provides a greater packaging capacity in comparison with pLVX-Puro.

title: Fusion enzyme as well as preparation method and application thereof
text: Fusion enzyme as well as preparation method and application thereof. The invention discloses a fusion enzyme as well as a preparation method and application thereof, and belongs to the technical field of biological medicines. A linker protein Linker is utilized, nucleoside 2 '-deoxyribosyltransferase LlNDT and adenosine deaminase LlADA are used as original enzymes, a fusion enzyme NLA connected with three amino acid sequences is designed, a recombinant plasmid containing a fusion enzyme NLA gene sequence is constructed through an artificial gene synthesis technology, enzyme clear liquid containing the fusion enzyme NLA is obtained through escherichia coli transformation, amplification and inducible expression, and the recombinant plasmid containing the fusion enzyme NLA is obtained.
