The method is used for synthesizing the nucleoside analogues, two-step enzymatic conversion reaction for synthesizing the nucleoside analogues in the prior art is unified into one-step continuous enzyme reaction, the synthesis process of the nucleoside analogues is simplified, and the production efficiency is improved.

title: Lipase mutant and application thereof in synthesis of vitamin A palmitate
text: Lipase mutant and application thereof in synthesis of vitamin A palmitate. The invention belongs to the technical field of gene engineering of enzymes, and particularly relates to lipase as well as preparation and application thereof. The wild type lipase TWG5L coding gene from Tilletia walkeri is obtained through whole gene synthesis, and the wild type lipase and the coding gene twg5l of the wild type lipase are obtained. A wild type lipase gene is modified by using a key site amino acid residue site-directed mutagenesis technology to obtain a lipase mutant TWG5LN87Y and an encoding gene twg5l2 thereof, recombinant plasmids are constructed, high-efficiency expression of the lipase mutant TWG5LN87Y in pichia pastoris is realized, and the relative enzyme activity of the mutant is 299% of that of the wild type. The lipase mutant catalyzes vitamin A acetate and palmitic acid to generate vitamin A palmitate, the yield of the lipase mutant is 190% of that of a wild type, and the lipase mutant has important industrial value.

title: RhCol-CTGF fusion protein as well as preparation method and application thereof
text: RhCol-CTGF fusion protein as well as preparation method and application thereof. The preparation method of the HLC-CTGF fusion protein disclosed by the invention comprises the following steps: designing a target gene synthesis and enzyme cutting site, reactivating a strain, and then sequentially carrying out expression vector construction of the fusion protein HLC-CTGF, expression of the fusion protein HLC-CTGF, purification of the fusion protein HLC-CTGF and immunoblotting of the fusion protein HLC-CTGF. The rhCol-CTGF fusion protein prepared by the preparation method disclosed by the invention is non-toxic and has good cell compatibility. The collagen can be cross-linked to prepare a hydrogel scaffold, and the collagen creates a good environment for the growth of osteoblasts by providing necessary nutrition and structural support; the CTGF is slowly released by controlling the degradation of the hydrogel, so that the defect that the expression of the CTGF in vivo has time dependence is overcome, and bone tissue repair is accelerated.

title: Novel carbohydrate enzyme in horse intestinal metagenome and application of novel carbohydrate enzyme
text: Novel carbohydrate enzyme in horse intestinal metagenome and application of novel carbohydrate enzyme. The invention provides novel carbohydrase in a horse intestinal metagenome and application of the novel carbohydrase, and belongs to the technical field of novel enzymes. The invention discloses a novel carbohydrase-BfLac2275 which exists in bacteroides fragilis MAG117.bin13 in intestinal tracts of horses, and has the capability of degrading alpha-lactose (154.94 U/mg), D-(+)-maltose (92.59 U/mg), raffinose (86.81 U/mg) and hyaluronic acid (5.71 U/mg). The enzyme is found through the steps of genome data set candidate gene screening, candidate gene bioinformatics analysis, BfLac2275 gene synthesis, expression, purification and the like. The BfLac2275 gene is a potential new member of the GH family with high lactose degradation capacity. Discovery of the enzyme has very important guiding significance for development of all applications related to polysaccharide and glycan degradation.

title: Yeast recombinant collagen
text: Yeast recombinant collagen. The invention belongs to the technical field of bioengineering, and particularly relates to a production method for a yeast recombinant collagen, which includes construction of recombinant pichia pastoris engineered strain and production of constitutively and secretively expressed recombinant collagen without methanol induction. The invention first utilizes conventional codons of pichia pastoris at the genetic level to optimize a collagen gene sequence and performs artificial total gene synthesis, the optimized collagen gene sequence is then integrated into yeast chromosome, and thereby a constitutively and secretively expressed recombinant pichia pastoris engineered strain is constructed. The fermentation process is easy to operate, yield is high, and the separation and purification of the product are simple; methanol induction is not needed, the fire-proof and explosion-proof indexes of production equipment and factory buildings are low, and the production environment is environmentally friendly and pollution-free; and no toxic and harmful substances are use, so the product is safer. A specific affinity purification label is carried, the product with higher purity can be easily obtained, and the product can be applied to biomedical materials.

title: Codon-optimized N-acetylglucosamine transferase gene and application thereof
text: Codon-optimized N-acetylglucosamine transferase gene and application thereof. The invention discloses a codon-optimized N-acetylglucosamine transferase gene and an application thereof, and belongs to the technical field of bioengineering. According to the invention, a Nodc gene from Sinorhizobium meliloti is taken as a starting sequence, the N-acetylglucosamine transferase is subjected to codon optimization according to codon preference of bacillus amyloliquefaciens and then is subjected to whole-gene synthesis, and a nucleotide sequence of the N-acetylglucosamine transferase is shown as SEQ ID NO.2. A recombinant vector is constructed aby using the codon-optimized N-acetylglucosamine transferase, and the recombinant vector is transferred into host bacillus amyloliquefaciens to obtain the genetically engineered bacterium capable of producing the chitosan oligosaccharide by fermentation. The concentration of the chitooligosaccharide in extracellular fermentation liquor of the genetically engineered bacterium reaches 676mg/L, and the contents of the chitotetraose and the chitopentaose are 246mg/L and 430mg/L respectively, so that a new fermentation process for extracellular synthesis of the chitooligosaccharide such as the chitotetraose and the chitopentaose can be realized by utilizing the recombinant strain.

title: Method of researching gene function of Bradyrhizobium japonicum USDA110
text: Method of researching gene function of Bradyrhizobium japonicum USDA110. The invention discloses a method of researching functions of genes of Bradyrhizobium japonicum USDA110, and provides a prokaryotic expression system which utilizes pseudomonas aeruginosa as host bacteria; the pseudomonas aeruginosa is Pseudomonas aeruginosa PAO1. In the invention, phenotype research related to gene synthesis and degradation functions of intracellular messenger cyclic di-guanosinemonophosphate (c-di-GMP) in the Bradyrhizobium japonicum is directly carried out in the pseudomonas aeruginosa of which the background mechanism research is definite; by observing the character changes of the pseudomonas aeruginosa, functions of the target gene is initially obtained. The invention provides the novel prokaryotic expression system in which the gene of the Bradyrhizobium japonicum isexpressed and colonial morphology, moveability, generation of exopolysaccharide and generation quantity of biomembrane are inspected, thereby initially obtaining the function of the gene of the Bradyrhizobium japonicum. The method saves time and consumable costs, accelerates researching process and is beneficial to research on the gene function of Bradyrhizobium japonicum.

title: RSV F protein mutant and application thereof
text: RSV F protein mutant and application thereof. The invention relates to an RSV F protein mutant and application thereof, and belongs to the field of vaccines. On the basis of the amino acid sequence of wild RSV F protein, multiple aspects and multiple modes of modification are carried out, including disulfide bond mutation, proline mutation, cavity filling mutation, electrostatic mutation, p27 sequence modification and/or trimerization structural domain modification, and multiple RSV F protein mutants are designed. And then, through gene synthesis, expression and ELISA detection, the pre-conformation (pre-F) proportion of the RSV F protein mutants and the stability under high-temperature and low-temperature conditions are tested, and a plurality of RSV F protein mutants with relatively high pre-conformation proportion and stable structures are screened out. Finally, candidate antigen representatives are selected for immunogenicity detection, and it is proved that the candidate antigen representatives have good immunogenicity and can be used for developing RSV vaccines and preparing RSV specific antibodies or RSV detection reagents.

title: Terminal deoxynucleotidyl transferase mutant, coding gene thereof, recombinant expression plasmid and genetically engineered bacterium
text: Terminal deoxynucleotidyl transferase mutant, coding gene thereof, recombinant expression plasmid and genetically engineered bacterium. The invention discloses a terminal deoxynucleotidyl transferase mutant with improved thermal stability, which is characterized in that the site of the amino acid sequence of wild terminal deoxynucleotidyl transferase as shown in SEQ ID NO.1 is mutated to obtain the mutant with six mutated sites of N135P, S138H, Q229D, K232D, L234R and V366M. The invention also discloses a coding gene, a recombinant expression plasmid and a genetically engineered bacterium. According to the terminal deoxynucleotidyl transferase mutant, the thermal stability of the terminal deoxynucleotidyl transferase can be remarkably improved, the binding efficiency of the terminal deoxynucleotidyl transferase and 3 '-OH end modified nucleotide is improved, and the mutant can replace wild TdT to play a role and can be applied to the fields of gene synthesis and information storage.

title: RSV F protein mutant and application thereof
text: RSV F protein mutant and application thereof. The invention relates to an RSV F protein mutant and application thereof, and belongs to the field of vaccines. On the basis of the amino acid sequence of wild RSV F protein, multiple aspects and multiple modes of modification are carried out, including disulfide bond mutation, proline mutation, cavity filling mutation, electrostatic mutation, p27 sequence modification and/or trimerization structural domain modification, and multiple RSV F protein mutants are designed. And then, through gene synthesis, expression and ELISA detection, the pre-conformation (pre-F) proportion of the RSV F protein mutants and the stability under high-temperature and low-temperature conditions are tested, and a plurality of RSV F protein mutants with relatively high pre-conformation proportion and stable structures are screened out. Finally, candidate antigen representatives are selected for immunogenicity detection, and it is proved that the candidate antigen representatives have good immunogenicity and can be used for developing RSV vaccines and preparing RSV specific antibodies or RSV detection reagents.

title: Single-chain antibody TRsAb2 against ToRSV and preparation method thereof
text: Single-chain antibody TRsAb2 against ToRSV and preparation method thereof. Provided is a single-chain antibody TRsAb2 against ToRSV, having an amino acid sequence shown in SEQ ID NO.l, and consisting of a 15 aa histidine tag region, a 184 aa ToRSV heavy chain variable region, a 15 aa linker peptide, and a reverse-linked 102 aa ToRSV light chain variable region, connected in sequence, with a length of 316 aa residues. The sequences of the 184 aa heavy chain variable region and the 102 aa light chain variable region are derived from the sequence of a monoclonal antibody produced by the hybridoma cell line CGMCCl 8320. The TRsAb2 gene is obtained by a whole gene synthesis method, and the codon composition is optimized. The single-chain antibody TRsAb2 of the invention has the characteristics of selectively identifying the peptide AFGKGVEEIEQTST in the ToRSV coat protein, and has a positive response to natural ToRSV particles.

title: SYSTEMS FOR STEM CELL PROGRAMMING AND METHODS THEREOF
text: SYSTEMS FOR STEM CELL PROGRAMMING AND METHODS THEREOF. Provided herein are systems of modulating gene expression and methods of use thereof for converting cells of one type to another type (e.g., reprogramming of differentiated cells into stem cells).

title: REGULATED EXPRESSION OF CLONED GENES USING A CASCADE GENETIC CIRCUIT
text: REGULATED EXPRESSION OF CLONED GENES USING A CASCADE GENETIC CIRCUIT. The present invention relates to the design, preparation and use of cascade regulatory circuits for amplification of gene expression. The genetic cirucit is based on a plurality (e.g., two or more) of regulatory genes organized in a hierarchical order of expression in a genetic construct or constructs, which can be established in a cell, e.g., a gram-negative bacteria, by means of autoreplicative vectors or by chromosomal insertion. In one embodiment, the genetic construct(s) can be stabiliy maintained in the chromosome without selective pressure, and gene expression induced three orders of magnitude therefrom using economical biodegradable benzoate derivatives.

title: Recombinant sea cucumber peptide with soothing effect as well as preparation method and application of recombinant sea cucumber peptide
text: Recombinant sea cucumber peptide with soothing effect as well as preparation method and application of recombinant sea cucumber peptide. The invention discloses a recombinant sea cucumber peptide with a relieving effect as well as a preparation method and application thereof. The amino acid sequence with a repairing effect is shown as SEQ ID NO.1. The invention further discloses a preparation method of the recombinant sea cucumber peptide with the relieving effect. The invention also provides a preparation method of the recombinant sea cucumber peptide. The preparation method mainly comprises the following steps: gene synthesis; constructing a carrier plasmid: integrating the target gene sequence into the carrier plasmid, and introducing the plasmid into a competent gene engineering strain; fermenting and expressing a target product; the high-purity sea cucumber peptide is obtained through purification means (solid-liquid separation, salting-out, isoelectric precipitation, chromatography technology and ultrafiltration), and the sea cucumber peptide has excellent water solubility and high purity and can be widely applied to cosmetics.

title: Scorpion toxin polypeptide and method for preparing scorpion toxin polypeptide with similar natural physiological functions
text: Scorpion toxin polypeptide and method for preparing scorpion toxin polypeptide with similar natural physiological functions. The invention provides a scorpion toxin polypeptide and a method for preparing a scorpion toxin polypeptide with similar natural physiological functions. The method comprises the following steps: preparing a gene of the scorpion toxin polypeptide by using a gene synthesis method, establishing the gene into recombinant plasmid with a His6 purification tag and an SUMO hydrotropy tag, and performing high-efficiency expression by using an escherichia coli expression system; after cell crushing, performing nickel column purification, performing enzyme digestion to remove the hydrotropy tag, and performing HPLC (High Performance Liquid Chromatography) purification, thereby finally obtaining the scorpion toxin polypeptide with similar natural physiological functions. According to the method, the preparation process is simple, the cost is low and the yield is high, and the prepared scorpion toxin polypeptide has physiological activities same as those of natural scorpion toxin polypeptides.

title: Puccinia graminis chitin deacetylase as well as preparation method and application thereof
text: Puccinia graminis chitin deacetylase as well as preparation method and application thereof. The invention discloses a puccinia graminis chitin deacetylase as well as a preparation method and application thereof. According to codon preference of pichia pastoris, an optimized nucleic acid sequence is shown as SEQ ID NO.2, and a chitin deacetylase encoding gene sequence of the puccinia graminis is obtained by utilizing a whole-gene synthesis method. Furthermore, a pichia pastoris expressionsystem is used for outperforming secretory expression on the optimized chitin deacetylase encoding gene to obtain chitin deacetylase, and the amino acid sequence of the chitin deacetylase is shown asSEQ ID NO.1. The obtained chitin deacetylase disclosed by the invention can be used for removing acetyl in chitin, chitosan and chitosan oligosaccharide, so that the chitosan or the chitosan oligosaccharide adopting a specific structure is obtained. The chitosan or the chitosan oligosaccharides adopting specific structures can have new or higher biological activity. Therefore, the puccinia graminis chitin deacetylase and a deacetylation product thereof have good industrial application prospects.

title: Construction method of recombinant HCV (hepatitis c virus) multi-epitope toxicity attenuation Listeria bacteria vaccine vector
text: Construction method of recombinant HCV (hepatitis c virus) multi-epitope toxicity attenuation Listeria bacteria vaccine vector. The invention discloses a construction method of a recombinant HCV (hepatitis c virus) multi-epitope toxicity attenuation Listeria bacteria vaccine vector. The method comprises the following steps: 1, constructing LM 10403s-deltaactA & deltainlB dual-gene deleted Listeria monocytogenes (LM) 10403s; 2, carrying out gene synthesis to obtain an HCV multi-epitope expression framework which comprises a promoter and secreting signal peptides from a gene hly of the LM 10403s, a green fluorescent protein EGFP and an HCV multi-epitope antigen; 3, connecting the HCV multi-epitope expression framework in the step 2 with the vector to obtain the LM 10403s-deltaactA & deltainlB-recombinant HCV multi-epitope toxicity attenuation Listeria bacteria vaccine vector.

title: Preparation method of circular RNA (Ribonucleic Acid) hsa _ circ-0008602 dual luciferase reporter gene vector
text: Preparation method of circular RNA (Ribonucleic Acid) hsa _ circ-0008602 dual luciferase reporter gene vector. The invention relates to a preparation method of a circular RNA hsa _ circ_0008602 dual luciferase reporter gene vector, which comprises the following steps: (1) acquiring a full-length sequence of hsa _ circ_0008602 through a circbase database, and constructing a pUC57-Amp vector through full-gene synthesis; (2) designing a pair of homologous recombination primers, and amplifying a target sequence from the pUC57-Amp vector; meanwhile, carrying out XhoI and NotI enzyme digestion linearization on the psiCHECK2 skeleton vector, purifying and recycling an amplification product and an enzyme digestion product through agarose gel, and carrying out homologous recombination reaction on the amplification product and the enzyme digestion product to obtain a recombinant product; and (3) transferringthe recombinant product into competent cells, and selecting monoclone to carry out electrophoresis verification and sequencing identification, so as to obtain the vector psiCHECK2-8602.

title: Expression and purification of citrus allergen Cit s 1.01 antigen, preparation of polyclonal antibody and application
text: Expression and purification of citrus allergen Cit s 1.01 antigen, preparation of polyclonal antibody and application. The invention discloses expression and purification of a citrus allergen Cit s 1.01 antigen, preparation of a polyclonal antibody and an application, specifically discloses a codon-optimized rutaceaeplant allergen Cit s 1.01 gene, and is characterized in that the gene includes the sequence shown in SEQ ID NO:2. By optimization of a Cit s 1.01 gene prokaryotic expression sequence and gene synthesis, a prokaryotic expression system of the allergen Cit s 1.01 is constructed. The rabbit polyclonal antibody Anti-Cit s 1.01 is prepared by the expressed and purified rCit s 1.01 protein, the contentof the allergen Cit s 1.01 in citrus peel and pulp is detected, and the potential allergenicity of the allergen Cit s 1.01 in edible parts of citrus fruits can be evaluated by a method of measuring the Western blot imprinting strength with optical density value.

title: GAS VESICLE EXPRESSION SYSTEMS, GAS VESICLE CONSTRUCTS AND RELATED GENETIC CIRCUITS, VECTORS, MAMMALIAN CELLS, HOSTS, COMPOSITIONS, METHODS AND SYSTEMS
text: GAS VESICLE EXPRESSION SYSTEMS, GAS VESICLE CONSTRUCTS AND RELATED GENETIC CIRCUITS, VECTORS, MAMMALIAN CELLS, HOSTS, COMPOSITIONS, METHODS AND SYSTEMS. Provided herein are genetically engineered gas vesicle expression systems (GVES) that are configured to express gas vesicles (GVs) in a mammalian cell, related gas vesicle polynucleotide constructs, gas vesicle reporting genetic circuits, vectors, genetically engineered mammalian cells, non-human mammalian hosts, compositions, methods and systems, which in several embodiments can be used together with contrast-enhanced imaging techniques to detect and report biological events in an imaging target site comprising a mammalian cell and/or organism.

title: SYSTEMS FOR CELL PROGRAMMING TOWARDS HEMATOPOIETIC LINEAGE AND METHODS THEREOF
text: SYSTEMS FOR CELL PROGRAMMING TOWARDS HEMATOPOIETIC LINEAGE AND METHODS THEREOF. Provided herein are systems of modulating gene expression, methods of use thereof, and cells engineered thereof for the purpose of differentiating cells, for example hematopoietic cells.

title: GAS VESICLE EXPRESSION SYSTEMS, GAS VESICLE CONSTRUCTS AND RELATED GENETIC CIRCUITS, VECTORS, MAMMALIAN CELLS, HOSTS, COMPOSITIONS, METHODS AND SYSTEMS
text: GAS VESICLE EXPRESSION SYSTEMS, GAS VESICLE CONSTRUCTS AND RELATED GENETIC CIRCUITS, VECTORS, MAMMALIAN CELLS, HOSTS, COMPOSITIONS, METHODS AND SYSTEMS. Provided herein are genetically engineered gas vesicle expression systems (GVES) that are configured to express gas vesicles (GVs) in a mammalian cell, related gas vesicle polynucleotide constructs, gas vesicle reporting genetic circuits, vectors, genetically engineered mammalian cells, non-human mammalian hosts, compositions, methods and systems, which in several embodiments can be used together with contrast-enhanced imaging techniques to detect and report biological events in an imaging target site comprising a mammalian cell and/or organism.

title: Tomato yellow leaf curl virus resistant polygene chimera AVCTC as well as preparation method and application thereof
text: Tomato yellow leaf curl virus resistant polygene chimera AVCTC as well as preparation method and application thereof. The invention discloses tomato yellow leaf curl virus resistant polygene chimera AVCTC as well as a preparation method and application thereof. The nucleotide sequence of the polygene chimera AVCTC is shown as a SEQ ID No.1, the polygene chimera AVCTC is the chimeric gene of tomato TMV replication enzyme gene segment ORF400-700, tomato CMV replication enzyme gene segment ORF45-345, tomato TYLCV replication enzyme gene segment ORF420-720, tobacco TMV replication enzyme gene segment ORF2964-3264 and cucumber CMV replication enzyme gene segment ORF2057-2357, and is prepared by using a gene synthesis method. The polygene chimera AVCTC is transferred into tomatoes, so that tomato yellow leaf curl virus resisting capability of transgenic tomatoes is improved, and beneficial aid is provided for plant to resist viruses.

title: Myrothecium sp transformation and expression system
text: Myrothecium sp transformation and expression system. The present invention is related to a transformation and an expression system in which Myrothecium sp. host cells are used to express homologous or heterologous proteins or are used to genetically engineer metabolic pathways.

title: METHODS AND SYSTEMS FOR CHEMOAUTOTROPHIC PRODUCTION OF ORGANIC COMPOUNDS
text: METHODS AND SYSTEMS FOR CHEMOAUTOTROPHIC PRODUCTION OF ORGANIC COMPOUNDS. The present disclosure identifies pathways, mechanisms, systems and methods to confer chemoautotrophic production of carbon-based products of interest, such as sugars, alcohols, chemicals, amino acids, polymers, fatty acids and their derivatives, hydrocarbons, isoprenoids, and intermediates thereof, in organisms such that these organisms efficiently convert inorganic carbon to organic carbon-based products of interest using inorganic energy, such as formate, and in particular the use of organisms for the commercial production of various carbon-based products of interest.

title: Novel, efficient and economical eccDNA synthesis method and application of eccDNA in overexpression of microRNA
text: Novel, efficient and economical eccDNA synthesis method and application of eccDNA in overexpression of microRNA. The invention provides a novel, efficient and economical eccDNA synthesis method and application thereof in overexpression of microRNA, and belongs to the field of gene synthesis based on biological DNA sequences. The method comprises the following steps: extracting total DNA of a sample, and purifying to obtain endogenous eccDNA; mixing the endogenous eccDNA with a random primer, carrying out denaturation annealing, and carrying out RCA reaction on an annealing product; by taking the RCA reaction product as a template, amplifying to obtain a target linear fragment A and a target linear fragment B which are semi-complementary; and mixing the linear fragment A and the linear fragment B, and carrying out denaturation annealing and ligation reaction in a reaction system containing TaqDNA ligase to obtain the target eccDNA. According to the method disclosed by the invention, the DNA synthesis cost is saved, the time period is shortened, the endogenous eccDNA can be obtained with high fidelity, and the function of the endogenous eccDNA can be better simulated.

title: Inositol methyl transferase gene derived from ice-leaf-midnight flower arista as well as preparation method and application of inositol methyl transferase gene
text: Inositol methyl transferase gene derived from ice-leaf-midnight flower arista as well as preparation method and application of inositol methyl transferase gene. The invention relates to an inositol methyl transferase gene derived from ice-leaf-midnight flower arista as well as a preparation method and application of the inositol methyl transferase gene. The nucleotide sequence of the inositol methyl transferase (IMTI) is shown as SEQ ID NO 1, the protein sequence coded by the IMTI is shown as SEQ ID NO 2. The IMTI gene is prepared by utilizing a gene synthesis method, and is transferred into arabidopsis by utilizing a flower-dipping method. Through the frost resisting test, the survival rate of the transgenosis arabidopsis transferred by the IMTI gene is more than 70%, thus stating that the low temperature resistant capacity of arabidopsis plants is greatly improved by the transgenosis of the IMTI gene. The IMTI gene has important significance on fostering plant species with low temperature resistance.

title: OYE 3 gene deriving from saccharomyces cerevisiae and preparation method and application thereof
text: OYE 3 gene deriving from saccharomyces cerevisiae and preparation method and application thereof. The invention discloses an OYE 3 gene deriving from saccharomyces cerevisiae and a preparation method and application thereof. A nucleotide sequence of the OYE 3 gene is SEQ ID NO 1, and a coded protein sequence of the OYE 3 gene is SEQ ID NO 2. The OYE 3 gene is prepared through a gene synthesis method and transformed into arabidopsis, tobacco, tomatoes and rice, germination percentage of seeds of obtained transgenic plants is improved by more than 2 times compared with non-transgenic plants, and root length of the transgenic plants is increased by more than 45% compared with the non-transgenic plants if the obtained transgenic plants are on a flat plate containing trinitrotoluene (TNT). The gene can promote plants to take part in degrading of the TNT, capacity of the transgenic plants for degrading the TNT is improved, and beneficial help for plants to recover TNT pollution is provided.

title: Expression and purification of citrus allergen Cit s 2.01 antigen, preparation of polyclonal antibody and application
text: Expression and purification of citrus allergen Cit s 2.01 antigen, preparation of polyclonal antibody and application. The invention discloses expression and purification of a citrus allergen Cit s 2.01 antigen, preparation of a polyclonal antibody and an application, specifically discloses a codon optimized rutaceaeplant allergen Cit s 2.01 gene, and is characterized in that the gene includes the sequence shown in SEQ ID NO:2. By optimization of a Cit s 2.01 gene prokaryotic expression sequence and gene synthesis, the prokaryotic expression system of the allergen Cit s 2.01 is constructed. The rabbit polyclonal antibody Anti-Cit s 2.01 is prepared by the expressed and purified rCit s 2.01 protein, the content of the allergen Cit s 2.01 in citrus peel and pulp is detected, and the potential allergenicity of the allergen Cit s 2.01 in edible parts of citrus fruits can be evaluated by a method of measuringthe Western blot imprinting strength with optical density value.

title: High thousand seed weight gene of common wheat as well as construction method and application of high thousand seed weight gene
text: High thousand seed weight gene of common wheat as well as construction method and application of high thousand seed weight gene. The invention relates to the technical field of gene synthesis and application, in particular to a common wheat high thousand seed weight gene as well as a construction method and application of the gene. The open reading frame sequence of the gene is as shown in SEQ ID NO.1, the genome sequence of the gene is as shown in SEQ ID NO.2, and the gene is named as TaCHLI-7A. The TaCHLI-7A is used for supplementing the wheat variety stone 4185, so that the leaf width and the chlorophyll content can be obviously increased; the TaCHLI-7A is used for supplementing the wheat variety stone 4185, so that the size and thousand seed weight of grains can be obviously increased; the TaCHLI-7A is used for supplementing the wheat variety stone 4185, so that the single-plant yield of wheat can be obviously improved.

title: OPTOGENETIC CONTROL OF MICROBIAL CO-CULTURE POPULATIONS
text: OPTOGENETIC CONTROL OF MICROBIAL CO-CULTURE POPULATIONS. Microbial consortia exert great influence over the physiology of humans, animals, plants, and ecosystems. However, difficulty in controlling their composition and population dynamics have limited their application in medicine, agriculture, biotechnology, and the environment. The approach disclosed herein provides an effective method to dynamically control population compositions in microbial consortia, which we demonstrate in the context of co-culture fermentations for chemical production. Co-culture fermentations can improve chemical production from complex biosynthetic pathways over monocultures by distributing enzymes across multiple strains, thereby reducing metabolic burden, overcoming endogenous regulatory mechanisms, or exploiting natural traits of different microbial species. However, stabilizing and optimizing microbial sub-populations for maximal chemical production remains a major obstacle in the field. An optogenetic circuit, called OptoTA, is disclosed for regulating a toxin-antitoxin system, which enables tunability of, e.g., Escherichia coli growth using only blue light. With the disclosed system, one can control population ratios of co-cultures of, e.g., E. coli and Saccharomyces cerevisiae containing different metabolic modules of biosynthetic pathways. Results reveal that intermediate light duty cycles improve chemical production by establishing optimal co-culture populations.

title: Red hydrophobic pigment as well as preparation method and application thereof
text: Red hydrophobic pigment as well as preparation method and application thereof. The invention discloses a red hydrophobic pigment compound and a preparation method and application thereof. The molecular weight of the red hydrophobic pigment compound is 512; the molecular formula of the red hydrophobic pigment compound is C28H20N2O8; and the red hydrophobic pigment compound is obtained after synthetase DeBI prepared through whole-gene synthesis and shown in the nucleotide sequence SEQ ID No. 1 is expressed in an engineered saccharomyces cerevisiae strain. The compound has the characteristics of hydrophobicity and oleophobicity, shows good color rendering property, leveling property and color fastness, can be widely applied to the fields of synthetic fibers, plastics, rubber, leather, coatings, printing ink, paint, cosmetics, food additives and the like, is synthesized by utilizing engineering bacteria, is high in yield, not limited by raw material sources, free of toxicity and harm and easy to degrade, and does not cause pollution to the ecological environment.

title: Regulated expression of cloned genes using a cascade genetic circuit
text: Regulated expression of cloned genes using a cascade genetic circuit. The present invention relates to the design, preparation and use of cascade regulatory circuits for amplification of gene expression. The genetic circuit is based on a plurality (e.g., two or more) of regulatory genes organized in a hierarchical order of expression in a genetic construct or constructs, which can be established in a cell, e.g., a gram-negative bacteria, by means of autoreplicative vectors or by chromosomal insertion. In one embodiment, the genetic construct(s) can be stabily maintained in the chromosome without selective pressure, and gene expression induced three orders of magnitude therefrom using economical biodegradable benzoate derivatives.

title: Antifreeze ferritin accurately conjugated with polypeptide and preparation method of antifreeze ferritin
text: Antifreeze ferritin accurately conjugated with polypeptide and preparation method of antifreeze ferritin. The invention discloses anti-freezing ferritin accurately conjugated with polypeptide and a preparation method of the anti-freezing ferritin, and belongs to the technical field of biological material cryopreservation. The ferritin complex is prepared by creatively utilizing a gene synthesis mode, the growth speed of ice crystals can be effectively slowed down, the high-strength ice recrystallization inhibition capability is shown, and the ice crystals formed by applying the ferritin complex provided by the invention are uniform in size, round and full in periphery and free of sharpness, and are suitable for preservation of biological materials; the polypeptide is firmly combined with the inner core of the ferritin, a chemical grafting reagent is not needed, and the ferritin is a component of an organism, has no potential safety hazard, has extremely high biocompatibility and can be self-metabolized in the organism; large-scale preparation can be performed in a fermentation manner, the dosage is smaller, and the use cost is lower.

title: Molecular cloning method based on synthetic gene and saccharomyces cerevisiae homologous recombination mechanism
text: Molecular cloning method based on synthetic gene and saccharomyces cerevisiae homologous recombination mechanism. The invention provides a molecular cloning method combining gene synthesis and a saccharomyces cerevisiae endogenous homologous recombination system. The molecular cloning method is not limited by thetype (deletion, mutation or insertion) and complexity of sequence change, and only relates to simple in-vitro molecular cloning operation, seamless modification of an original vector is realized by utilizing a synthetic sequence with homologous arms at two ends and yeast homologous recombination, synchronous transformation of multiple discontinuous DNA sections on 4-150 kbp shuttle plasmids and batch construction of multiple versions can be realized, the operation is simple, the construction efficiency is higher, and the construction success rate is higher, so that the molecular cloning method is suitable for multi-version and high-flux seamless cloning operation.

title: Expression method and anti-tumor application of recombinant bombyxins
text: Expression method and anti-tumor application of recombinant bombyxins. The present invention discloses an expression method and an anti-tumor application of recombinant bombyxins, and belongs to the technical field of gene engineering. The method comprises: according to an expression system, carrying out codon preference optimization on a target gene; adopting a complete gene synthesis technology to synthesize optimized bombyxin gene; linking the gene to an expression vector to construct a recombinant expression vector; transforming the recombinant expression vector into a corresponding expression system; and carrying out further culture, screening and identification to obtain the recombinant expression vector-containing strain, and carrying out induced expression to obtain the target protein. According to the present invention, the recombinant bombyxin II, the recombinant bombyxin IV and the mixture of the recombinant bombyxin II and the recombinant bombyxin IV have characteristics of broad antitumor activity and no biological toxicity, and can be used for preparation of anti-tumor drugs.

title: Engineered terminal deoxynucleotidyl transferase mutant and application thereof
text: Engineered terminal deoxynucleotidyl transferase mutant and application thereof. The invention discloses an engineered terminal deoxynucleotidyl transferase mutant and application thereof. The engineering TdT enzyme variant containing single mutation and multi-site mutation is obtained by screening through a directed evolution technology on the basis of a DNA (Deoxyribonucleic Acid) sequence of wild type ZaTdT from a diphtheria sparrow. The engineered TdT enzyme variant designed by the invention has a unique function of specifically catalyzing an aminooxy modified nucleotide monomer to carry out single base extension, and high-precision controllable synthesis of a single-stranded DNA molecule can be realized under the condition of no template dependence. According to the method, the dependency of traditional enzymatic synthesis on a template chain is broken through, the single-stranded oligonucleotide synthesis efficiency and the sequence control precision are remarkably improved, and a novel tool platform is provided for innovative development in the fields of gene synthesis, molecular diagnosis, biological storage and the like.

title: Engineering bacterium for efficiently expressing cat hair allergen component protein, construction method and application thereof
text: Engineering bacterium for efficiently expressing cat hair allergen component protein, construction method and application thereof. The invention discloses an engineering bacterium for efficiently expressing cat hair allergen component protein as well as a construction method and application of the engineering bacterium. The nucleotide sequences of Fel d 2 and Fel d 4 after codon optimization are synthesized by a gene synthesis method, are recombined with a pPIC9K vector, and are transformed into pichia pastoris GS115 for fusion. Carrying out fermentation culture on the recombinant pichia pastoris for expressing the cat hair allergen protein to obtain a target allergen component protein; compared with the original gene, the expression yield of the gene sequence after codon optimization is remarkably increased, so that high-efficiency expression is realized, and the method can be used for industrial production of the cat hair allergen protein and meets the technical requirements of high-efficiency production of the cat hair allergen protein in China.

title: Annular RNA (Ribonucleic Acid) vaccine for resisting porcine epidemic diarrhea virus as well as construction method and application thereof
text: Annular RNA (Ribonucleic Acid) vaccine for resisting porcine epidemic diarrhea virus as well as construction method and application thereof. The invention belongs to the technical field of biological medicines, and particularly relates to a circular RNA (Ribonucleic Acid) vaccine for resisting porcine epidemic diarrhea virus as well as a construction method and application of the circular RNA vaccine. According to the invention, the annular PEDV-COE1 RNA vaccine is obtained through gene synthesis, in-vitro transcription, cyclization and liposome nanoparticle wrapping. The DNA molecule, the circular RNA molecule, the protein sequence and the gene sequence can be used for preparing various porcine diarrhea virus vaccines. When the porcine epidemic diarrhea virus circular RNA is injected into the porcine muscle, a high-level porcine epidemic diarrhea virus neutralizing antibody can be generated in the porcine body, the occurrence of piglet diarrhea can be effectively reduced, and the porcine epidemic diarrhea virus circular RNA has great popularization value in prevention and control of porcine epidemic diarrhea.

title: Method for synthesizing gene growing at constant temperature in double directions
text: Method for synthesizing gene growing at constant temperature in double directions. The invention relates to a method for synthesizing gene growing at constant temperature in double directions. The method is characterized by comprising the following steps: (1) designing and synthesizing an oligonucleotide chain for extending or sublimated and synthesized oligonucleotide according to a target DNA sequence; (2) mixing the oligonucleotide chain obtained in the step (1) in a reaction system and adding mixed enzymes with three activities of polymerase, joining enzyme and restriction enzyme into the reaction system, growing at constant temperature in double directions under the synergic action of a plurality of enzymes, making an extending reaction and synthesizing the target DNA long chain. The invention has the advantages of high success ratio, simple design, simple operation, high degree of automation, and the like, thereby having potential low cost advantage and having potential application values to the development in the fields of gene synthesis popularization, biological engineering, biomedicine, information biology, and the like.

title: Electrode array device for gene sequence synthesis and control method thereof
text: Electrode array device for gene sequence synthesis and control method thereof. The invention belongs to the technical field of electronic information and biotechnology crossing, and particularly relates to an electrode array device for gene sequence synthesis and a control method of the electrode array device. The electrode array device of the present invention comprises a substrate and an electrode array formed on the substrate, an electrode unit comprises an anode and a cathode, and the substrate is covered with a microfluidic cover; and the device further comprises a microfluidic module and a control module, and the control module accurately controls the potential difference between the anode and the cathode on an array unit by controlling the on-off of high and low levels of the output voltage or the magnitude of the voltage; and the microfluidic module is controlled to accurately control the transportation of various reagents required by gene synthesis. The device disclosed by the invention is simple in structure and convenient to prepare, and does not need a complex micromachining process; and when the device is used for gene sequence synthesis, control is simple, and industrialization and large-scale application are facilitated.

title: Amonolysis auxiliary device and ammonolysis system
text: Amonolysis auxiliary device and ammonolysis system. The utility model relates to the technical field of gene synthesis, in particular to an ammonolysis auxiliary device and an ammonolysis system. The ammonolysis auxiliary device comprises a first container, a second container, a first fluid channel, a driving mechanism and a second fluid channel. When a solution in the ammonolysis instrument needs to be replaced, as the height of the second container is lower than that of the ammonolysis instrument, waste liquid in the ammonolysis instrument can be spontaneously discharged to the second container through the second fluid channel under the action of gravity; and the ammonia water in the first container is automatically pressed into the first fluid channel through the driving mechanism and flows into the ammonolysis instrument, so that a fresh solution is injected into the ammonolysis instrument, and automatic replacement of the solution is realized. The waste liquid in the ammonolysis instrument does not need to be poured out or the solution does not need to be poured into the ammonolysis instrument manually, so that the replacement operation is more convenient while the replacement efficiency is improved, and the working intensity of operators is reduced.

title: Zero-background multi-fragment gene efficient assembling method
text: Zero-background multi-fragment gene efficient assembling method. The invention discloses a zero-background multi-fragment gene efficient assembling method. The method includes the following steps that a puc57-CATP carrier is established; plasmid in which CATP genefragments are cloned is established; a target gene is selected, the target gene is divided into small fragments according to the length of the gene for gene synthesis, second-class enzyme loci are added to the two ends of the gene, and the gene is connected to a cloning carrier; the small fragments comprise the established CATP gene fragments and are assembled through a Golden gate cloning reaction, T7DNA polymerase is used as ligase, an assembled product converts escherichia coli, and an LB panel with ampicillin and chloromycetin resistance is coated with the assembled product for screening.Under the condition that the carrier plasmid does not need to be treated in advance, by means of special carrier and fragment design, multiple gene fragments are efficiently assembled simultaneously at a time. The T7DNA ligase with higher fidelity is used, and the probability of correct assembly of the fragments is increased.

title: REGULATED EXPRESSION OF CLONED GENES USING A CASCADE GENETIC CIRCUIT
text: REGULATED EXPRESSION OF CLONED GENES USING A CASCADE GENETIC CIRCUIT

title: Method for constructing carrier containing klenow-fragment reverse complementary sequences
text: Method for constructing carrier containing klenow-fragment reverse complementary sequences. The invention relates to the technical field of molecular biology, in particular to a method for constructing a carrier containing klenow-fragment reverse complementary sequences. The method comprisesthe steps of diving an objective sequence containing the klenow-fragment reverse complementary sequences into two fragments (a fragment 1 and a fragment 2) without reverse complementary sequences; performing gene synthesis on the two fragments, and adding TypeIIS restriction enzyme cutting sites to two ends of each fragment; and enabling complementary sticky terminals to be produced among the fragment 1, the fragment 2 and an objective carrier after the enzyme cutting, and performing connection and transformation so as to obtain positive clone containing the klenow-fragment reverse complementary sequences. The method can effectively solve the difficult problem of constructing the carrier containing the klenow-fragment reverse complementary sequences, and has important significance.

title: METHOD AND DEVICE
text: METHOD AND DEVICE. The present invention provides a method for the assembly of a polynucleic acid sequence from a plurality of nucleic acid sequences in which the polynucleic acid sequence is of a formula Nn+1, in which N represents a nucleic acid sequence and where n is 1 or greater than 1 and each N may be the same or a different nucleic acid sequence, in which the method comprises: (i) providing a first nucleic acid sequence N1 which has an oligonucleotide linker sequence L13' at the 3'-end of the nucleic acid sequence; (ii) providing a second nucleic acid sequence N2 which optionally has an oligonucleotide linker sequence L23 at the 3'-end of the nucleic acid sequence and which has an oligonucleotide linker sequence L25 at the 5'-end of the nucleic acid sequence, wherein the 5'-end linker sequence L25 of nucleic acid sequence N2 is complementary to the 3'-end linker sequence L13 of nucleic acid sequence N1; (iii) optionally providing one or more additional nucleic acid sequences N, wherein nucleic acid sequence N2 has an oligonucleotide linker sequence L23at the 3'-end of the nucleic acid sequence, and wherein said one or more additional nucleic acid sequences N comprises a terminal additional nucleic acid sequence NZ, and wherein each additional nucleic acid sequence N has an oligonucleotide linker sequence at its 3'-end, wherein said terminal additional nucleic acid sequence NZ optionally lacks an oligonucleotide linker sequence at its 3'-end and wherein each additional nucleic acid sequence N has an oligonucleotide linker sequence at its 5'-end, wherein for the first additional nucleic acid sequence N3 the 5'-end linker sequence L35 is complementary to the 3'-end linker sequence L23' of nucleic acid sequence N2 and for each second and subsequent additional nucleic acid sequence N the 5'-end linker sequence is complementary to the 3'-end linker sequence of the respective preceding additional nucleic acid sequence; and (iv) ligating said nucleic acid sequences to form said polynucleic acid sequence; wherein at least step (iv) is carried out on a microfluidic device.

title: Preparation method and application of sarcosine oxidase
text: Preparation method and application of sarcosine oxidase. The invention discloses a preparation method of sarcosine oxidase. The method comprises the following steps: transforming a whole-gene synthesis humanization sarcosine oxidase coding deoxyribonucleic acid sequence, and removing escherichia coli rare codon; inserting the transformed sarcosine oxidase coding deoxyribonucleic acid sequence into an expression carrier, and carrying out transformation and expression by using an escherichia coli strain; inoculating the strain subjected to transformation and expression into a luria-bertani (LB) culture medium, and carrying out isopropyl-beta-d-thiogalactoside (IPTG) induction; and breaking the obtained thallus, centrifugally separating and purifying through an Ni-nitrilotriacetic acid (NTA) column. The method is simple in sarcosine oxidase preparation operation, low in preparation cost, high in finished product yield, high in purity and high in biological activity, and can be used as a diagnostic reagent for sarcosine assay kit research.

title: RSV F protein mutant and application thereof
text: RSV F protein mutant and application thereof. The invention relates to an RSV F protein mutant and application thereof, and belongs to the field of vaccines. On the basis of the amino acid sequence of wild RSV F protein, multiple aspects and multiple modes of modification are carried out, including disulfide bond mutation, proline mutation, cavity filling mutation, electrostatic mutation, p27 sequence modification and/or trimerization structural domain modification, and multiple RSV F protein mutants are designed. And then, through gene synthesis, expression and ELISA detection, the pre-conformation (pre-F) proportion of the RSV F protein mutants and the stability under high-temperature and low-temperature conditions are tested, and a plurality of RSV F protein mutants with relatively high pre-conformation proportion and stable structures are screened out. Finally, candidate antigen representatives are selected for immunogenicity detection, and it is proved that the candidate antigen representatives have good immunogenicity and can be used for developing RSV vaccines and preparing RSV specific antibodies or RSV detection reagents.

title: GENE SYNTHESIS USING POOLED DNA
text: GENE SYNTHESIS USING POOLED DNA. A method and system for synthesizing one or more pieces of DNA with desired sequences using pooled DNA, the method comprising a hierarchical division phase and a hierarchical assembly phase. In the division phase, the sequences of one or more pieces of DNA with desired nucleic acid sequences are recursively: divided into partially overlapping resulting pieces of DNA, and the resulting pieces of DNA assigned to a plurality of pools except after the after the final division step, wherein overlapping, adjacent resulting pieces of DNA are assigned to different pools. In the assembly phase, pools of oligonucleotides are obtained corresponding to the pools of the resulting pieces of DNA, and one or more pieces of DNA with desired sequences are assembled by overlap extension in the reverse order of the hierarchical division. Embodiments of the method combine the advantages of hierarchical assembly with the advantages of pooled oligonucleotides.

title: Carrier fragment for positioning target protein on surface of cell membrane and application of carrier fragment
text: Carrier fragment for positioning target protein on surface of cell membrane and application of carrier fragment. The invention discloses a carrier for localizing a target protein on the surface of a cell membrane, the carrier comprises a Claudin18.2 1-27aa sequence, a PDGFR membrane localization sequence, multiple cloning sites and a gene for coding the target protein, the multiple cloning sites are located between the Claudin18.2 1-27aa sequence and the PDGFR membrane localization sequence, the gene for coding the target protein is inserted into the multiple cloning sites, and the nucleotide sequence of the Claudin18.2 1-27aa sequence is shown as SEQ ID No.6. The invention further discloses a preparation method of the carrier for localizing the target protein on the surface of the cell membrane. The molecular design of the vector fragment can efficiently and quickly locate the protein/polypeptide on the cell membrane surface, does not need to synthesize the full length of the protein, reduces the gene synthesis cost, and can be applied to production of liquid cell quality control products.

title: Chassis cell for DNA assembly and construction method and application thereof
text: Chassis cell for DNA assembly and construction method and application thereof. The invention belongs to the technical field of biology, and provides a chassis cell for DNA assembly and a construction method and application thereof. The chassis cell is saccharomyces cerevisiae overexpressing the Shu compound. The construction method of the chassis cells comprises the following steps: constructing expression plasmids of a Shu compound; and introducing the expression plasmid into saccharomyces cerevisiae. The invention also discloses a method for carrying out DNA assembly by using the chassis cell. The method comprises the following steps: introducing a carrier to be assembled and a DNA fragment into the chassis cell together, and completing assembly by using the chassis cell. The saccharomyces cerevisiae with a DNA homologous recombination function is combined with the Shu compound, so that the intracellular recombination capacity of DNA is further improved, especially, the intracellular efficient assembly of large-fragment DNA can be met, and a foundation is laid for the feasibility and practicability of a synthetic biological system.

title: Nucleic acid synthesis method based on bidirectional isothermal extension
text: Nucleic acid synthesis method based on bidirectional isothermal extension. The present invention relates to a nucleic acid splicing method based on bidirectional isothermal extension. The method is as follows: an extension system is set up, the extension system comprises a starting double strand, a group of oligonucleotides which are mutually different and orderly spliced, mixed enzyme with connection, polymerization and restriction endonuclease activity, and a reaction buffer matching with the mixed enzyme; in the cooperation of multiple kinds of enzymes, and starting from the starting double strand, the oligonucleotides can be isothermally spliced for synthesis of a target DNA long chain. The nucleic acid splicing method has the characteristics of high success rate, simple design and simple operation, high automation, and the like, so that the nucleic acid splicing method has a potential low cost advantage, and has potential values of application in promotion of gene synthesis, and development of biological engineering, biomedical and bioinformatics fields.

title: Method for production of L-4-hydroxyproline by using recombinant escherichia coli fermentation
text: Method for production of L-4-hydroxyproline by using recombinant escherichia coli fermentation. The invention belongs to the field of gene engineering, and discloses a method for production of L-4-hydroxyproline by using recombinant escherichia coli fermentation. Recombinant escherichia coli is constructed by the following method: according to a published proline-4-hydroxylase gene and tryptophan promoter sequence, first optimally designing a tryptophan tandem promoter and a proline-4-hydroxylase structure gene, then after the total gene synthesis of the tryptophan tandem gene promoter and the proline-4-hydroxylase structure gene, connecting the promoter and the structure gene to pAMP plasmid to construct recombinant plasmid pAMP-P2trp-Hyp for overexpression of proline-4-hydroxylase. The invention also discloses the application of the escherichia coli in the production of the 4-hydroxyproline, the shake flask fermentation results show that the 4-hydroxyproline prepared by the recombinant escherichia coli has a yield reached 0.31g / L, and suggest that the recombinant escherichia coli has good industrial development prospects.

title: HIERARCHIAL GENE SYNTHESIS METHODS OF A TARGET NUCLEIC ACID SEQUENCE
text: HIERARCHIAL GENE SYNTHESIS METHODS OF A TARGET NUCLEIC ACID SEQUENCE. PURPOSE: A method for synthesizing a target nucleic acid sequence is provided to effectively synthesize 1kb or more of target nucleic acid sequence of using an oligonucleotide synthesized on a DNA microchip. CONSTITUTION: A hierarchical method for synthesizing a target nucleic acid sequence comprises: a step of preparing an oligonucleotide pool containing 5'-terminal first common priming sequence, primary 5'-restriction enzyme cleavage sequence, an overlapping target sequence, primary 3'-restriction enzyme cleavage sequence, and 3'-terminal first common priming sequence; a step of performing PCR of the oligonucleotide pool using a primary primer set; a step of cleaving the PCR product with a restriction enzyme; a step of performing a primary assembly PCR to prepare first and second intermediate products; a step of cleaving the first and second intermediate products with restriction enzymes; and a step of assembling the resultant and acquiring the target nucleic acid sequence.

title: Application of gma-miR169c gene in regulation and control of synthesis of vegetable fatty acid
text: Application of gma-miR169c gene in regulation and control of synthesis of vegetable fatty acid. The invention relates to the technical field of plant breeding, in particular to application of a gma-miR169c gene in regulation and control of synthesis of vegetable fatty acid. The sequence of the gma-miR169c gene is as shown in SEQ ID NO.1, and the fatty acid comprises oleic acid, behenic acid, linolenic acid and eicosamonoenoic acid. Through overexpression of the gma-miR169c gene, synthesis of oleic acid and behenic acid in plants is inhibited, and synthesis of linolenic acid and eicosamonoenoic acid is promoted. The plants comprise arabidopsis thaliana and soybeans. By exploring the influence of the gma-miR169c gene on fatty acid anabolism related pathways, the report of miRNA related to soybean fatty acid synthesis is expanded, and a new thought is provided for plant molecular breeding improvement and fatty acid content increase.

title: A design, preparing method and applications of an echinococcus multilocularis multi-epitope vaccine LTB-AE
text: A design, preparing method and applications of an echinococcus multilocularis multi-epitope vaccine LTB-AE. The invention relates to a design, preparing method and applications of an echinococcus multilocularis multi-epitope vaccine LTB-AE. An active component of the multi-epitope vaccine LTB-AE is a polypeptide. The polypeptide mainly comprises an echinococcus multilocularis multi-epitope peptide AE, and a mucosal immune adjuvant that is an escherichia coli heat-labile enterotoxin B subunit (LTB). A gene sequence of the multi-epitope vaccine LTB-AE is synthesized mainly by a gene synthesis technique, and is connected to an expression vector through double digestion, then the expression vector is converted into arctic express to perform expression of a fusion protein, and after the protein is purified, the multi-epitope vaccine LTB-AE is obtained. The multi-epitope vaccine can induce a body to generate immune responses and high-titer specific antibody humoral immune responses to echinococcus multilocularis T cells and B cells, and can be used for preventing and treating echinococcus multilocularis infection related disease.

title: Improved lentiviral expression vector as well as construction method and application thereof
text: Improved lentiviral expression vector as well as construction method and application thereof. The invention provides a lentiviral expression vector and a construction method and application thereof, a plasmid skeleton of the lentiviral expression vector is based on PLVX-Puro, and the lentiviral expression vector is modified by the following steps: a, adding a CMV enhancer and a promoter at the upstream of 5 'LTR by means of gene synthesis and subcloning; b, replacing the original 5 'LTR with a new 5' LTR (SEQ ID NO: 3) with the length of 181bps; c, replacing a CMV promoter on the original carrier with an EFS promoter; d, deleting a PGK promoter behind multiple cloning sites of the original vector, and introducing a P2A linker sequence at the same time; e, the original Puro resistance is replaced with the shorter Blasticidin resistance, and the Blasticidin resistance is replaced with the Blasticidin resistance; and f, replacing the original 3 'LTR with the LTR (SEQ ID NO: 7) of which the U3 is partially deleted. Compared with PLVX-Puro, the lentiviral expression vector obtained by transformation has higher packaging capacity.

title: Rabbit polyclonal antibody for specific detection of areca yellow phytoplasma as well as preparation method and application of rabbit polyclonal antibody
text: Rabbit polyclonal antibody for specific detection of areca yellow phytoplasma as well as preparation method and application of rabbit polyclonal antibody. The invention relates to a rabbit anti-polyclonal antibody for specifically recognizing areca yellowed phytoplasma as well as a preparation method and a use method of the rabbit anti-polyclonal antibody. According to the present invention, the gene sequence of the IMP protein is found from the genome of the betel nut yellow phytoplasma previously detected by the inventor, the codon optimization of the prokaryotic expression is performed to obtain the (40-154aa) nucleotide region of the gene synthesis IMP protein, and the rabbit anti-polyclonal antibody is further prepared; the antibody can be used for well detecting and distinguishing areca catechu yellowing materials infected with the phytoplasma at the protein level, overcomes the technical problems of tedious detection process, high detection cost, high detection technical difficulty, long time consumption and the like of the current infected phytoplasma, and has a wide application prospect.

title: Method for preparing P2X7R (purinergic ligand-gated ion channel 7 receptor) immunogen
text: Method for preparing P2X7R (purinergic ligand-gated ion channel 7 receptor) immunogen. The invention discloses a method for preparing a P2X7R (purinergic ligand-gated ion channel 7 receptor) immunogen. The P2X7R protein extracellular section amino acid sequence is optimized; the whole gene synthesis is used; the P2X7R extracellular section gene coding sequence is inserted into an expression carrier pET30a through restrictive enzyme cutting sites NdeI and HindIII; by using a enzyme digestion method and sequencing, the accuracy of the final expression carrier is ascertained; the built protein expression recombinant plasmid containing P2X7R extracellular section gene coding sequence is converted into a BL21 expression strain for IPTG induced expression; P2X7R protein is subjected to affinity chromatography purification in liquid supernatant; detection is performed. The method has the beneficial effects that the immunogenic P2X7R extracellular section can be obtained; the method can be used for P2X7R antibody preparation.

title: Aspergillus nidulans chitin deacetylase, and preparation method and application thereof
text: Aspergillus nidulans chitin deacetylase, and preparation method and application thereof. The invention discloses an Aspergillus nidulans chitin deacetylase, and a preparation method and an application thereof. The sequence of a chitin deacetylase coding gene in Aspergillus nidulans is obtained by a whole gene synthesis technology according to the codon preference of Pichia yeast, and the optimized nucleic acid sequence is represented by SEQ ID NO.2. The optimized chitin deacetylase encoding gene is secreted and expressed by a Pichia pastoris expression system to obtain the Aspergillus nidulans chitin deacetylase, and the amino acid sequence of the Aspergillus nidulans chitin deacetylase is represented by SEQ ID NO.1. The Aspergillus nidulans chitin deacetylase obtained in the invention can remove acetyl groups in chitosan and chitosan oligosaccharides in order to obtain the chitosan or chitosan oligosaccharides with a specific structure. The modified chitosan or chitosan oligosaccharides have new or higher biological activities than unmodified chitosan or chitosan oligosaccharides. So the above enzyme and its deacelation product have good industrial application prospects.

title: Method for inhibiting target gene expression by soaking dsRNA (double-stranded ribonucleic acid) in pre-pupal stage of liriomyza trifoliata
text: Method for inhibiting target gene expression by soaking dsRNA (double-stranded ribonucleic acid) in pre-pupal stage of liriomyza trifoliata. The invention discloses a method for inhibiting expression of a target gene by soaking dsRNA in a liriomyza trifoliate in a pre-pupal stage, which comprises two parts of soaking delivery of the dsRNA and detection of the inhibition efficiency of the target gene, and specifically comprises the following steps: screening verification of the target gene, synthesis of the dsRNA, pre-pupa soaking of the dsRNA under different concentrations and detection of the inhibition efficiency of the target gene. According to the invention, not only is the optimal concentration of the pre-pupa soaking dsRNA in the liriomyza trifoliate found out, but also the RNA interference (RNAi) system of the liriomyza trifoliate pre-pupa soaking dsRNA is systematically and comprehensively established, which is of great significance in liriomyza trifoliate gene function research and RNAi-based liriomyza trifoliate prevention and control technology research.

title: Single-chain antibody TRSAB1 against TORSV and preparation method thereof
text: Single-chain antibody TRSAB1 against TORSV and preparation method thereof. Provided is a single-chain antibody TRsAbl against ToRSV, having an amino acid sequence shown in SEQ ID NO.1, and consisting of a 15aa histidine tag region, a 180aa ToRSV heavy chain variable region, a 15aa linker peptide, and a reverse-linked 117aa ToRSV light chain variable region, connected in sequence, with a length of 327 amino acid residues. The sequences of the 180aa heavy chain variable region and the 117aa light chain variable region are derived from the sequence of a monoclonal antibody produced by the hybridoma cell line CGMCC18321. The yield of TRsAbl, obtained by a whole gene synthesis method, is 200-2000mg/L, with a purity of 82-95%. The single-chain antibody TRsAbl has the characteristics of selectively identifying the peptide QAAQQAGKNPFGRG in the ToRSV coat protein, and has a positive response to natural ToRSV particles. The invention has broad application prospects in the Entry-Exit inspection and quarantine of the ToRSV.

title: Synthetic collagen mask liquid taking seaweed as base plate and preparation method
text: Synthetic collagen mask liquid taking seaweed as base plate and preparation method. The invention discloses a synthetic collagen mask liquid taking seaweed as a chassis and a preparation method, and particularly relates to the technical field of mask liquid, the preparation method comprises the following steps: step 1, determining an amino acid sequence of recombinant human collagen, the amino acid sequence is RHIIIC8. GERGGPGGPGPQGPPGKNGETGPQGPPGPT, and selecting the sequence for codon optimization and whole gene synthesis; step 2, selecting the seaweed as Laminia japonica, changing the gene by a gene gun method, and selecting the promoter as GUS (CaMV35S promoter); under the plant synthesis background with seaweed as a base, the recombinant collagen can be applied to the mask without separation and purification steps, the use cost of the recombinant collagen is remarkably reduced, and the mask liquid has the functions of high moisture retention and skin elasticity improvement based on high hydrophilicity and high activity of the recombinant collagen.

title: Expression and purification of citrus allergen Cit s 3.01 antigen, preparation of polyclonal antibody and application
text: Expression and purification of citrus allergen Cit s 3.01 antigen, preparation of polyclonal antibody and application. The invention discloses expression and purification of a citrus allergen Cit s 3.01 antigen, preparation of a polyclonal antibody and an application, specifically discloses a codon optimized rutaceaeplant allergen Cit s 3.01 gene, and is characterized in that the gene includes the sequence shown in SEQ ID NO:2. By optimization of a Cit s 3.01 gene prokaryotic expression sequence and gene synthesis, the prokaryotic expression system of the allergen Cit s 3.01 is constructed. The rabbit polyclonal antibody Anti-Cit s 3.01 is prepared by the expressed and purified rCit s 3.01 protein, the content of the allergen Cit s 3.01 in citrus peel and pulp is detected, and the potential allergenicity of the allergen Cit s 3.01 in edible parts of citrus fruits can be evaluated by a method of measuringthe Western blot imprinting intensity with optical density value.

title: Preparation method and application of GPRC5D protein
text: Preparation method and application of GPRC5D protein. The invention relates to a preparation method and application of GPRC5D protein. The preparation method of the GPRC5D protein comprises the following steps: optimizing a GPRC5D original nucleotide sequence, carrying out gene synthesis, and carrying out PCR reaction by taking a synthetic gene as a template to obtain a target fragment; connecting the target fragment to a plasmid gene to form a recombinant plasmid; placing the recombinant plasmid directly in a culture system for culture to obtain a reaction product liquid; and purifying the reaction product liquid to obtain the GPRC5D full-lengthprotein. Compared with the prior art, the preparation method of the GPRC5D protein has the advantages that the GPRC5D full-length protein is prepared by adopting a cell-free expression system, so that the GPRC5D full-length protein can be conveniently used as a new target of myeloma for subsequent research and test.

title: Method for preparing DNA assembly products with viscous terminals based on PCR
text: Method for preparing DNA assembly products with viscous terminals based on PCR. The invention discloses a method for preparing DNA assembly products with viscous terminals based on PCR. Amplification is performed by two pairs of PCR amplification primers, then the two products are mixed together, the PCR products are degenerated into single-stranded DNAs at a high temperature, and the slow renaturation is performed, so that DNA assembly products with viscous terminals are formed, other assembly products connected with the viscous terminals can be prepared according to the method; under the action of ligase, the assembly of the two products can be performed easily. A new method is provided for assembly of DNA molecules.

title: Genetically engineered bacterium for preparing sarcosine oxidase, and preparation method and application thereof
text: Genetically engineered bacterium for preparing sarcosine oxidase, and preparation method and application thereof. The invention belongs to the field of genetic engineering and protein expression engineering, and relates to a method for efficiently preparing sarcosine oxidase by a bioengineering technology. CGMCCNo.17306 is utilized as an original strain, a sarcosine oxidase gene of the strain CGMCC No.17306 is obtained by comparison, a target gene is obtained by gene synthesis, an expression vector SOX-pET is constructed, and the expression vector is introduced into escherichia coli BL21 (DE3) to construct and obtain a genetically engineered bacterium GLYH18 producing the sarcosine oxidase. The inventionalso provides an application of the genetically engineered bacterium GLYH18 producing the sarcosine oxidase used for fermentative production of the sarcosine oxidase. The method has the advantages ofsimple operation, low cost and high yield, the selected sarcosine oxidase is high in enzyme activity and good in thermal stability, and recombinant genetic engineering bacteria has industrial production capacity compared with a wild strain.

title: Helicobacter pylori infection resistant mRNA vaccine and application thereof
text: Helicobacter pylori infection resistant mRNA vaccine and application thereof. The invention belongs to the technical field of mRNA vaccine preparation, and particularly relates to a helicobacter pylori infection resistant mRNA vaccine and application thereof. A UreB antigen sequence is optimized through AI calculation, a sequence is synthesized through a gene synthesis technology after optimization, the mRNA vaccine for resisting helicobacter pylori infection is obtained through T7 in-vitro transcription, a vaccinia capping system and a lithium chloride purification mode, the mRNA vaccine can induce a body to generate T lymphocyte immune response and high-titer UreB specific antibody humoral immune response, and the helicobacter pylori infection resisting mRNA vaccine can be used for resisting helicobacter pylori infection. The composition can be used for preventing helicobacter pylori infection related diseases.

title: CIRCADIAN PROBIOTIC SYSTEMS
text: CIRCADIAN PROBIOTIC SYSTEMS. Among the various aspects of the present disclosure is the provision of a circadian probiotic system. The present teachings include compositions for a probiotic supplement to deliver a drug to a subject. The supplement can be modified to include a synthetic gene circuit to drive a therapeutic controlled gene output that can be delivered to the gut of the subject on a prescribed time basis, and the synthetic gene circuit can include at least one circadian clock gene that drives expression of at least one prescribed therapeutic or diagnostic gene target. The present teachings also include therapeutic methods to deliver at least one diagnostic and therapeutic gene target to a subject time-sensitively. The method can include modifying a probiotic supplement to include a synthetic circadian gene circuit that drives expression of the at least one gene target. In another aspect, the method can include delivering the probiotic supplement to the subject.

title: Method for high-throughput screening of L-amino acid deaminase mutant recombinant strain
text: Method for high-throughput screening of L-amino acid deaminase mutant recombinant strain. The invention belongs to the technical field of genetic engineering of enzymes, and particularly relates to a method for high-throughput screening of an L-amino acid deaminase mutant recombinant strain. The method comprises the following steps of (1) recombinant bacteria construction: conducting gene synthesis on a Proteus mirabilis derived L-amino acid deaminase (LAAD) gene (genbank accession number EU669819.1), constructing a plasmid LAAD and transforming and constructing the recombinant strain PMLAAD; (2) error-prone PCR; (3) establishment of a mutant library; (4) fermentation; (5) enzymatic reaction and color development; (6) microplate reader testing; (7) screening mutant fermentation and screening; (8) mutant strain fermentation and screening. By means of the high-throughput screening system combined with the error-prone PCR, the time and the cost are greatly saved, the screening efficiency is improved, and the chances of obtaining positive mutant strains are increased.

title: Bradyrhizobium diazoefficiens chitosan oligosaccharide deacetylase as well as preparation method and application thereof
text: Bradyrhizobium diazoefficiens chitosan oligosaccharide deacetylase as well as preparation method and application thereof. The invention discloses a Bradyrhizobium diazoefficiens chitosan oligosaccharide deacetylase as well as preparation method and application thereof. According to Pichia pastoris codon preference, the sequence of chitooligosaccharide deacetylase coding gene in Bradyrhizobium diazoefficiens is obtained by a whole gene synthesis method, the optimized nucleic acid sequence is shown as SEQ ID NO. 2. Further a Pichia pastoris expression system is used for performing secretory expression on the optimized chitooligosaccharide deacetylase coding gene, the Bradyrhizobium diazoefficiens chitosan oligosaccharide deacetylase is obtained and the amino acid sequence thereof is shown as SEQ ID NO. 1. The Bradyrhizobium diazoefficiens chitosan oligosaccharide deacetylase provided by the invention can removeacetyls from non-reducing ends of chitosan oligosaccharides so as to obtain the chitosan oligosaccharides with specific structures, the regulated chitosan oligosaccharides have new or higher bioactivity than that before regulation. Therefore, the enzyme and deacetylation products thereof have good prospect of industrial application.

title: Method for preparing PRMT2 protein by using baculovirus expression system and multi-mode chromatography
text: Method for preparing PRMT2 protein by using baculovirus expression system and multi-mode chromatography.
