The invention discloses a method for preparing PRMT2 protein by using a baculovirus expression system and multi-mode chromatography, which comprises the following steps: S1, cloning a base sequence SEQ ID NO.1 of PRMT2 into a plasmid in an artificial gene synthesis mode to form a recombinant expression plasmid; s2, transposing the recombinant expression plasmid obtained in S1 into an escherichia coli competent cell to obtain a recombinant baculovirus, transfecting an insect cell to obtain a baculovirus, and expressing a PRMT2 protein SEQ ID NO.2; and S3, obtaining the purified PRMT2 protein SEQ ID NO.3 through multi-mode chromatography. According to the method disclosed by the invention, the label-free and high-purity PRMT2 protein can be obtained, and a foundation is laid for the biological research of related structures in the future.

title: Gas vesicle expression systems, gas vesicle constructs and related genetic circuits, vectors, mammalian cells, hosts, compositions, methods and systems
text: Gas vesicle expression systems, gas vesicle constructs and related genetic circuits, vectors, mammalian cells, hosts, compositions, methods and systems. Provided herein are genetically engineered gas vesicle expression systems (GVES) that are configured to express gas vesicles (GVs) in a mammalian cell, related gas vesicle polynucleotide constructs, gas vesicle reporting genetic circuits, vectors, genetically engineered mammalian cells, non-human mammalian hosts, compositions, methods and systems, which in several embodiments can be used together with contrast-enhanced imaging techniques to detect and report biological events in an imaging target site comprising a mammalian cell and/or organism.

title: Designing and preparing method and application of novel alveolar echinococcosis subunit vaccine
text: Designing and preparing method and application of novel alveolar echinococcosis subunit vaccine. The invention provides a novel alveolar echinococcosis subunit vaccine. A polypeptide serves as an active ingredient of the alveolar echinococcosis subunit vaccine, which is mainly composed of a surface antigen Emy162 of alveolar echinococcosis and a mucosal immune adjuvant, namely cholera toxin B subunit (CTB). According to the invention, the gene sequence of the alveolar echinococcosis surface antigen Emy162 is synthesized by virtue of a gene synthesis technology, and then the gene is coupled with the gene sequence of the cholera toxin B subunit, so that a fusion gene is formed. The fusion gene is expressed by virtue of an escherichia coli prokaryotic expression system, and protein is purified, so that the alveolar echinococcosis subunit vaccine is obtained. The alveolar echinococcosis subunit vaccine is capable of inducing a body to generate alveolar echinococcosis targeted T cell and B cell immune response and high-titer specific antibody humoral immune response, and the subunit vaccine can be used for preventing and treating alveolar echinococcosis infection related diseases.

title: Zinc finger protein gene, synthesis method and application
text: Zinc finger protein gene, synthesis method and application. The invention belongs to the technical field of molecular biology gene and discloses a zinc finger protein gene, a synthesis method and application. The zinc finger protein BnBBX22 gene has gene sequences of SEQ ID NO:1 and SEQ ID NO:2 in salt-tolerant brassica napus 2205 and salt-sensitive brassica napus 1423. A specific primer is designed according to a coding region CDS sequence of a brassica napus BnaA07g34250D gene and cloned into the gene sequences in the salt-tolerant brassica napus 2205 and salt-sensitive brassica napus 1423 by a TA cloning method. By use of the gene, the influences ofthe gene on the salt tolerance of brassica napus can be researched by transgenosis, gene editing and other genetic transformation technologies. Meanwhile, a theoretical foundation is laid for creating salt-tolerant new varieties of the brassica napus in combination with breeding techniques.

title: Method and composition for electrochemically detecting cells
text: Method and composition for electrochemically detecting cells. The invention provides a composition for detecting cells. A DNA assembly formed by self-assembly of four oligonucleotides shown in SEQ ID No. 2-5 is used as a template to synthesize silver nanoclusters. After the cell surface is treated with TCEP, the oligonucleotide strands with maleimide groups at the 5' end are ligated to the cell surface by Michael addition reaction and bound to the DNA assembly containing the silver nanoclusters by base complementary pairing. Antibodies modified on the electrode are used for capturing the target cells, and silver nanoparticles are immobilized on the target cells and the target mesenchymal stem cells are quantitatively detected by dissolving metal silver ions in the nitric acid solution. According to the detection composition, the DNA assembly templatesilver nanoclusters are used as an electrochemical signal to clicks on the chemically mediated signal amplification system so as to realize qualitative and quantitative detection of the target cellsin the sample.

title: DNA self-assembly atomic force microscope detection method
text: DNA self-assembly atomic force microscope detection method. The invention provides a DNA self-assembly atomic force microscope detection method. The method comprises the steps of preparing a siRNA/DNA assembly nanometer material, representing the morphology of the siRNA/DNA assembly nanometer material through an atomic force microscope, and representing the morphology structure of siRNA/DNA assembly nanometer particles in a liquid phase tapping mode through the atomic force microscope. Damage caused by the probe to the sample can be eliminated, the transverse force influence of image resolution is reduced, and the scanning speed is moderate. And meanwhile, the liquid phase mode is beneficial to keeping the inherent morphology of the biological assembly in a solution state, so that the obtained morphology is closer to the real morphology.

title: D-lactate dehydrogenase gene b116812S as well as preparation method and application thereof
text: D-lactate dehydrogenase gene b116812S as well as preparation method and application thereof. The invention discloses a D-lactate dehydrogenase gene b116812S derived from a high-efficiency nitrogen fixation bradyrhizobium japonicum as well as a preparation method and application of the D-lactate dehydrogenase gene b116812S. A nucleotide sequence of the D-lactate dehydrogenase gene b116812S is shown in SEQ ID No.1, and an amino acid sequence coded by the D-lactate dehydrogenase gene b116812S is shown in SEQ ID No.2. The D-lactate dehydrogenase gene b116812S is prepared by reconstructing a D-lactate dehydrogenase gene b116812 derived from the high-efficiency nitrogen fixation bradyrhizobium japonicum according to a plant preferred codon and by using a gene synthesis method. The D-lactate dehydrogenase gene b116812S is converted into arabidopsis thaliana to obtain a transgenosis arabidopsis thaliana, and the performance of the transgenosis arabidopsis thaliana resisting glyphosate is remarkably superior to that of wild type arabidopsis thaliana, and can be applied to glyphosate-resisting crop breeding.

title: Gene for promoting tomato ascorbic acid synthesis and application thereof
text: Gene for promoting tomato ascorbic acid synthesis and application thereof. The invention belongs to the technical field of gene synthesis, and discloses a gene for promoting tomato ascorbic acid synthesis and an application thereof, and a DNA sequence of the gene for ascorbic acid synthesis is SEQ ID NO: 1; the invention also provides an expression vector constructed by using the gene for promoting tomato ascorbic acid synthesis. The MIOX gene for promoting tomato ascorbic acid synthesis has practical application significance in identifying MIOX functions. According to the invention, an inositol pathway is proved from the aspect of genetic transformation, and the function of MIOX is preliminarily identified; by regulating and controlling expression of ascorbic acid biosynthesis related enzyme genes, activity of related enzymes is changed, AsA content in plants isregulated and controlled, and resistance of the plants to oxygen adversity is improved. The regulation of ascorbic acid production is another feasible way for improving plant stress resistance, and plays an important role in plant stress resistance breeding.

title: Programmable DNA-Guided Artifical Restriction Enzymes
text: Programmable DNA-Guided Artifical Restriction Enzymes. Methods and compositions for specific cleavage of DNA molecules using single-stranded guides and Argonaute (Ago) proteins are described.

title: Acyl-coenzyme A synthetase mutant ACS01-Des4 as well as coding gene, expression strain and application of acyl-coenzyme A synthetase mutant ACS01-Des4
text: Acyl-coenzyme A synthetase mutant ACS01-Des4 as well as coding gene, expression strain and application of acyl-coenzyme A synthetase mutant ACS01-Des4. The invention discloses an acyl coenzyme A synthetase mutant ACS01-Des4 as well as a coding gene, an expression strain and application thereof. According to the invention, acyl-coenzyme A synthetase from marine uncultured microorganisms is taken as a basis, mutation sites are designed through PROSS, and then whole-gene synthesis is carried out, so that mutant genes are obtained. After the engineering bacteria containing the mutant plasmids are subjected to induced expression, the acyl-coenzyme A synthetase with improved stability and specific enzyme activity is obtained. Under the condition of 37 DEG C, the stability of the mutant is improved by 3 times. When palmitic acid is used as a substrate, the specific enzyme activity of the mutant is improved by 1.14 times. The mutant has potential application value in an in-vitro diagnostic kit for detecting free fatty acid by an enzyme method.

title: DNA synthesizer
text: DNA synthesizer. The utility model relates to the technical field of gene synthesis equipment, in particular to a DNA (Deoxyribose Nucleic Acid) synthesizer. The DNA synthesizer comprises a chamber; the synthesis assembly comprises a synthesis plate and a liquid injection assembly, the synthesis plate is arranged in the cavity, and the liquid injection assembly can be matched with the cavity to realize DNA synthesis reaction; the ammonolysis assembly comprises a sealing piece, and an air inlet and an air outlet are formed in the sealing piece; the driving part is connected with the cavity so as to be used for driving the cavity to move, the driving part can drive the cavity to move to the liquid injection assembly so as to achieve the synthesis process, the driving part can drive the cavity to move to the sealing part, and the cavity is matched with the sealing part so as to achieve the ammonolysis process. According to the DNA synthesizer, the ammonolysis device is integrated in the DNA synthesizer, so that not only can manual operation steps be reduced, but also the manufacturing cost can be reduced.

title: Application of needle mushroom genes Fvegt1, Fvegt2 and Fvegt3 to synthesis of ergothioneine
text: Application of needle mushroom genes Fvegt1, Fvegt2 and Fvegt3 to synthesis of ergothioneine. The invention discloses an application of needle mushroom genes Fvegt1, Fvegt2 and Fvegt3 to synthesis of ergothioneine, and belongs to the field of biosynthesis. By a homologous clone method and a gene synthesis method, 3 genes in an ergothioneine synthesis way are cloned from a needle mushroom mycelium, expression in vitro is performed, and in vitro biosynthesis of the needle mushroom ergothioneine is realized. The FvEgt1 can catalyze substrates of His, Cys, SAM, FeSO4 and the like to form AdoHcy and HerSul; FvEgt2 can be combined with the Cys, so that SH of cysteine is activated to form sulfur anions; the FvEgt3 can catalyze PLP and HerSul to form a ketimine compound; and the sulfur anions activated by the SH of the cysteine perform nucleophillic attack on the ketimine compound to obtain the ergothioneine. The invention firstly clarifies a biosynthesis way of the ergothioneine in basidiomycetes needle mushrooms except bacteria, ascomycetes and the like.

title: ND6 protein prepared from human mitochondrial ND6 gene recombinant vector and application
text: ND6 protein prepared from human mitochondrial ND6 gene recombinant vector and application. The invention discloses an ND6 protein prepared by using a human mitochondrial ND6 gene recombinant vector and application of the ND6 protein. According to the vector, an ND6 nucleotide sequence coded by a nuclear codon is synthesized and optimized through a whole-gene synthesis method, and a human ND6 gene reconstruction vector is constructed. The nucleotide sequence of the human ND6 gene recombinant vector is as shown in SEQ ID NO: 5. The invention also discloses an amino acid sequence, a mitochondrial targeting sequence, a CMV promoter sequence and an expression vector sequence of the prepared ND6 protein, and a preparation method and a preparation of the ND6 protein. The human ND6 gene recombinant vector can be efficiently expressed in cells, the prepared ND6 protein can target mitochondria, the function of the normal ND6 protein can be played, and m.14484Tgt can be relieved; the ND6 protein abnormity caused by C mutation has the influence on the cell function.

title: Plasmid capable of inducing soluble expression of ketoreductase at high temperature and application of plasmid
text: Plasmid capable of inducing soluble expression of ketoreductase at high temperature and application of plasmid. The invention discloses a plasmid for high-temperature induction of soluble expression of ketoreductase and application of the plasmid. The construction method of the plasmid disclosed by the invention comprises the following steps: acquiring sequences of temperature-induced double promoters pL/pR and Cits857 regulation and control elements from a vector pBV220; combining the 3'end of the sequence with a reverse antifreeze protein XXA sequence; and finally, fusing the polyclone region, the resistance fragment region and the replicon region from pET28a at the downstream of the reverse antifreeze protein. After the sequence is subjected to whole gene synthesis, the annular plasmid pSK-101 is obtained. Then ketoreductase which is difficult to express on a pET carrier is cloned into a plasmid pSK-101, under induction of 37 DEG C, expression of efficient soluble protein is obtained, efficient synthesis of the protein at high temperature is achieved, and the production cost is greatly reduced.

title: Set of base editing system based on suppurative streptococcus and application of set of basic group editing system in gene editing
text: Set of base editing system based on suppurative streptococcus and application of set of basic group editing system in gene editing. The invention discloses a set of precise basic group editing system based on suppurative streptococcus Cas9 and an application of the set of precise basic group editing system in mammalian cells and / or embryonic gene editing. The basic group editing system is composed of the two parts of components of a rAPOBEC1 : Cas9 : UGI expression vector and a gRNA expression vector, the rAPOBEC1 : Cas9 : UGI expression vector is obtained by cloning an encoding gene of rAPOBEC1 : Cas9 : UGI into a pcDNA3.1 (-) vector through a method of gene synthesis and molecular cloning, and the gRNA expression vector is obtained by cloning a gRNA sequence into a pDR274 vector including a T7 promoter through the mode of restriction enzyme ligation. The set of precise basic group editing system can be applied to construction of gene modification, a mammalian cell model and an animal model and used for gene therapy of mammalian cells and embryos, and has a good application prospect.

title: Detection method for legionella pneumoniae, neisseria meningitidis and mycoplasma pneumoniae
text: Detection method for legionella pneumoniae, neisseria meningitidis and mycoplasma pneumoniae. The invention discloses a detection method for legionella pneumoniae, neisseria meningitidis and mycoplasma pneumoniae in the technical field of bacterial detection. The legionella pneumoniae, the neisseria meningitidis and the mycoplasma pneumoniae include the following three pairs of primers: the legionella pneumoniae has the following detection primers: an uppstream primer: 5'-AGGGTTGATAGGTTAAGAGC-3', and a downstream primer: 5'-ABGGTCAGATAATACTGGTTGAC-3'; the neisseria meningitidis has the following detection primers: an upstream primer: 5'-GCTGGCGCCGCTGGCAACAAAATTC-3', and a downstream primer: 5'-CTTCTGCAGATTGCGGCGTGCCGT-3'; and the mycoplasma pneumoniae has the following detection primers: an upstream primer: 5'-GAAGCTTATGGTACAGGTTGG-3', and a downstream primer: 5'-ATTACCATCCTTTCTTGTAAGG-3'; and the three bacteria are detected by using a whole gene synthesis method, the conserved sequences of the legionella pneumoniae, neisseria meningitidis and mycoplasma pneumoniae are constructed into a pJET1.2 vector and then are transformed into DH5a competence, and single-colony shaking bacteria are selected and used as a standard for subsequent PCR detection experiments to improve thedetection accuracy of the bacteria.

title: Novel oligonucleotide synthesis device
text: Novel oligonucleotide synthesis device. The utility model relates to the technical field of gene synthesis, in particular to a novel oligonucleotide synthesis device which comprises a synthesis device body, the synthesis device body comprises a fixed base, a sealing bin and a top plate, a through groove is formed in the top plate, a movable cover is movably connected to the through groove, and a reaction column is arranged on the bottom face of the movable cover. According to the utility model, the lifting frame, the driving device and other structures are arranged, so that the electric automatic control can be realized, and the hard spring, the transmission device and other structures are arranged in the driving device, so that the safety of the device is improved, and the service life of the device is prolonged. The consistency and the stability of the motion trail are ensured in the folding process of the reaction column and the reaction tube, so that the insertion depth of the reaction column into the reaction tube is the same, and the volume of reaction liquid in the reaction tube is the same, so that the reaction process can be more accurately controlled, and the reaction quality in the reaction tube is improved.

title: Technological process of producing recombinant human histiotype plasminogen activator TNK mutant
text: Technological process of producing recombinant human histiotype plasminogen activator TNK mutant. The present invention discloses the technological process of producing recombinant human histiotype plasminogen activator TNK mutant. TNK-tPA gene containing said mutant sites is synthesized via full length gene synthesis process and expressed in Chinese hamster ovary cell (CHO-DHFR), and through cloning and screening high-level expressed TNK-tPA engineering cell strain is obtained. By means ofsustained culture of the engineering cell in cell culturing bioreactor, collection of the supernatant and serial separation and purification in chromatographic column, rhTNK-tPA product is obtained.The destination gene of the present invention has expression level in cell as high as 18000 IU/106 cell/d, and the prepared rhTNK-tPA product has single strand rate as high as 80 % and purity up to 95 %. As for industrial scale, the present invention has relatively simple requirement in apparatus, low cost and simple technological requirement.

title: Acyl-coenzyme A synthetase mutant as well as coding gene, expression strain and application thereof
text: Acyl-coenzyme A synthetase mutant as well as coding gene, expression strain and application thereof. The invention discloses an acyl coenzyme A synthetase mutant, and a coding gene, an expression strain and application thereof, and the acyl coenzyme A synthetase mutant is based on acyl coenzyme A synthetase from marine uncultured microorganisms, and a mutant gene is obtained through complete gene synthesis after a mutation site is designed through Funclib. After the engineering bacteria containing the mutant plasmids are subjected to induced expression, the acyl-coenzyme A synthetase with improved specific enzyme activity and improved stability is obtained. When palmitic acid is used as a substrate, the specific enzyme activity of the mutant is improved by 4.4 times. While the specific activity is improved, the stability of the mutant is also improved, and the mutant has potential application value in an in-vitro diagnostic kit for detecting free fatty acid by an enzyme method.

title: Anti-human ACE2 antibody, detection kit and application thereof
text: Anti-human ACE2 antibody, detection kit and application thereof. The invention discloses an anti-human ACE2 antibody, a detection kit and application thereof, and relates to the technical field of biomedicine. A mouse anti-human ACE2 monoclonal antibody is obtained through gene synthesis, vector construction, expression and purification of human ACE2 recombinant protein, mouse immunization with the recombinant protein as an antigen and preparation of a monoclonal antibody hybridoma cell strain, and the anti-human ACE2 monoclonal antibody capable of neutralizing and blocking human ACE2 is obtained through screening. In addition, the expressed humanized human-mouse chimeric antibody also has the activity of effectively neutralizing infection of novel coronavirus and coronavirus, and can block infection of the novel coronavirus (SARS-Cov-2), the coronavirus (SRAS-Cov) and all viruses entering cells through ACE2 to a human body. The monoclonal antibody can be used for detection of human ACE2 protein and diagnosis or auxiliary diagnosis of related diseases taking human ACE2 as a marker.

title: Methods and systems for chemoautotrophic production of organic compounds
text: Methods and systems for chemoautotrophic production of organic compounds. The present disclosure identifies pathways, mechanisms, systems and methods to confer chemoautotrophic production of carbon-based products of interest, such as sugars, alcohols, chemicals, amino acids, polymers, fatty acids and their derivatives, hydrocarbons, isoprenoids, and intermediates thereof, in organisms such that these organisms efficiently convert inorganic carbon to organic carbon-based products of interest using inorganic energy, such as formate, and in particular the use of organisms for the commercial production of various carbon-based products of interest.

title: Recombinant sea cucumber peptide with anti-aging effect as well as preparation method and application thereof
text: Recombinant sea cucumber peptide with anti-aging effect as well as preparation method and application thereof. The invention discloses a recombinant sea cucumber peptide with an anti-aging effect and a preparation method and application thereof, and the amino acid sequence with the anti-aging effect is SEQ ID NO.1. The preparation method of the recombinant sea cucumber peptide mainly comprises the following steps: gene synthesis; constructing a carrier plasmid: integrating the target gene sequence into the carrier plasmid, and introducing the plasmid into a competent gene engineering strain; fermenting and expressing a target product; high-purity sea cucumber peptide is obtained through purification means (solid-liquid separation, salting-out, isoelectric precipitation, chromatography technology and ultrafiltration), the sea cucumber peptide has excellent water solubility and high purity and can be widely applied to cosmetics, meanwhile, the invention provides a preparation method of the anti-aging lifting and repairing base liquid, and the product is mild in use, free of irritation and excellent in anti-aging effect.

title: Monomer streptavidin mutant and preparation method thereof
text: Monomer streptavidin mutant and preparation method thereof. The invention discloses a monomer streptavidin mutant and a preparation method thereof. The amino acid sequence of the mutant is shown as SEQ ID NO.1. The preparation method comprises the following steps: constructing a recombinant plasmid of a nucleotide sequence; transforming the recombinant plasmid into a host cell, and screening to obtain a positive engineering bacterium; culturing positive engineering bacteria and collecting thalli; crushing the thallus cells, and enriching and purifying the target protein; carrying out enzyme digestion after protein elution; and purifying the protein subjected to enzyme digestion again, wherein the purified protein is the monomer streptavidin mutant protein. The 110 < th > site of the core amino acid sequence of the artificial monomer streptavidin is mutated, the melting temperature is increased by 4.75 DEG C, and the stability of the monomer streptavidin mutant is remarkably improved. The monomer streptavidin mutant full-artificial gene synthesis is easy to prepare, the operation process of the method is simpler than that of multipoint mutation, the requirements for experimental equipment and the technical level are low, and the efficiency of the preparation method is high.

title: METHOD FOR PRODUCING DNA ENCODING POLYPEPTIDES THAT ARE COMPOSED OF SEVERAL SECTIONS, AND FOR PRODUCING POLYPEPTIDES BY EXPRESSING THE DNA THUS OBTAINED
text: METHOD FOR PRODUCING DNA ENCODING POLYPEPTIDES THAT ARE COMPOSED OF SEVERAL SECTIONS, AND FOR PRODUCING POLYPEPTIDES BY EXPRESSING THE DNA THUS OBTAINED. The invention relates to a method for producing novel modular enzyme systems by genetic engineering, which is characterized by a cyclic in vitro gene synthesis and which allows a specific recombination of the individual, gene- encoded modular components to novel enzyme systems. Examples of such modular enzyme systems are the non-ribosomal peptide synthetases (NRPS) or polyketid e synthases (PKS) of type 1, that is the amino acid sequence of such an enzyme is characterized by being composed of a repetitive sequence of identical sequence sections or sequence sections that are very similar to one another. Every single repetitive sequence is referred to as a module and every module allows the enzymatic incorporation of a specific substrate into the substanc e synthesized by the enzyme. The products synthesized by the NRPS and PKS enzymes are often highly valuable as pharmaceuticals, such as the penicillin s, vancomycins or erythromycins. The inventive method allows for an effective production of novel genes for modular enzymes, and the gene expression of sa id novel genes allows for the production of novel substances.

title: Aminopeptidase for catalytic synthesis of carnosine, and preparation method and application thereof
text: Aminopeptidase for catalytic synthesis of carnosine, and preparation method and application thereof. The invention provides aminopeptidase for catalytic synthesis of carnosine, and a preparation method and application thereof. The aminopeptidase has the amino acid sequence shown by SEQ ID NO:2. For the preparation of biological enzymes, firstly, a gene engineering strain of the biological enzymes is built; the biological enzyme gene fragment obtained through full gene synthesis is subjected to linearized plasmid recombination by using restriction enzymes SalI. The built gene-engineered strain is suitable for high-density culture; the exoenzyme is biologically synthesized; solid-liquid separation is performed; ultrafiltration concentration is performed to obtain enzyme liquid; histidine and beta-alanine can be catalyzed in normal-temperature and normal-pressure water solution to efficiently synthesize carnosine; the reaction environment is friendly; the production cost is low; the conversion time is short; the process operation is simple; the reaction system impurity content is low; the post treatment is easy; the wide prospects of large-scale industrial application are realized.

title: Pichia yeast recombinant bacteria for expressing Streptomyces sp. FA1 (fertilization antigen 1) source xylanase
text: Pichia yeast recombinant bacteria for expressing Streptomyces sp. FA1 (fertilization antigen 1) source xylanase. The invention discloses Pichia yeast recombinant bacteria for expressing Streptomyces sp. FA1 (fertilization antigen 1) source xylanase, and belongs to the field of gene engineering. Plasmid pMD18-T-XynA serves as a template, a primer is designed, PCR (polymerase chain reaction) amplification is performed to obtain a target gene, and the nucleotide sequence of the gene is as shown in SEQ ID NO: 1 in a sequence table. The invention further discloses a production method of recombinant Pichia yeast engineering bacteria. The production method includes the steps: (1) synthesizing an autonomously replicating sequence PARS by whole gene synthesis technology; (2) building an expression vector pGAPZ alpha A-PARS; (3) building a recombinant expression vector pGAPZ alpha A-PARS-XynA; (4) transforming Pichia yeast hosts and screening positive converters; (5) shake-flask induced cultivation; (6) horizontally inducing a recombinant Pichia yeast engineering bacteria fermentation tank to produce the xylanase.

title: Method for synthesizing DNA fragments and assembling synthetic genes in Escherichia coli through one-step method
text: Method for synthesizing DNA fragments and assembling synthetic genes in Escherichia coli through one-step method. The invention provides a method for synthesizing DNA fragments and assembling synthetic genes in Escherichia coli through a one-step method. The method mainly comprises the following steps: constructing LIC vector plasmid LIC-A; preparing a LIC-A linear vector; constructing a receptor vector puc-Kana for assembling the DNA fragments; selecting target genes, and dividing small fragments of about 300bp for synthesizing; and assembling multiple small-fragment DNA into large-fragment genes by utilizing a Jinmen cloning reaction. The method is simple in operation, the operations of cloning and sequencing are not needed to be repeatedly performed, direct annealing is realized, the cloning efficiency is high, luminous report genes such as sfgfp and gfp are used, the screening method is intuitive, the error rate of the synthesis method is extremely low, the period of the synthetic genes is short, the cost is greatly saved, and the time is saved. Moreover, the method is suitable for synthesizing various genes and is particularly effective for large-gene synthesis.

title: Production method of yeast recombinant collagen
text: Production method of yeast recombinant collagen. The invention belongs to the technical field of bioengineering, and particularly relates to a production method of yeast recombinant collagen, which comprises construction of recombinant pichia pastoris engineering bacteria and a method for constitutive secretory expression of recombinant collagen without methanol induction. The method comprises the following steps: firstly, optimizing a collagen gene sequence by using conventional codons of pichia pastoris on a gene level, carrying out artificial whole-gene synthesis, and then integrating into a yeast chromosome to construct a constitutive secretory expression recombinant pichia pastoris engineering bacterium. The fermentation process is simple to operate, high in yield and simple in product separation and purification; methanol induction is not needed, fireproof and explosion-proof indexes of production equipment and plant construction are low, and the production environment is environment-friendly and pollution-free; and no toxic substance is used, so that the product is safer. A specific affinity purification label carried by the biomedical material is easy to obtain a product with higher purity and can be used as a biomedical raw material.

title: Application of human ND4 gene recombinant vector in preparation of ND4 protein kit
text: Application of human ND4 gene recombinant vector in preparation of ND4 protein kit. The invention discloses application of a human ND4 gene recombinant vector in preparation of an ND4 protein kit. A nuclear codon-coded ND4 nucleotide sequence is synthesized by a whole-gene synthesis method, a human ND4 gene recombinant vector is constructed, the recombinant vector is introduced into a cell to synthesize mitochondria-targeted ND4 protein, and the mitochondria-targeted ND4 protein is positioned in the mitochondria and plays a role. The nucleotide sequence of the ND4 gene recombinant vector is as shown in SEQ ID NO: 5. The invention also discloses an amino acid sequence, a mitochondrial targeting sequence, a CMV promoter sequence and an expression vector sequence for preparing the ND4 protein. Comprising an ND4 protein preparation method and a used preparation. The human ND4 gene recombinant vector can be efficiently expressed in cells, the prepared ND4 fusion protein can target mitochondria, the normal ND4 protein function can be played, and the m.11778Ggt can be relieved; the influence of ND4 protein abnormity caused by A mutation on the cell function is avoided.

title: SYSTEMS AND METHODS FOR CONTROL OF GENE EXPRESSION
text: SYSTEMS AND METHODS FOR CONTROL OF GENE EXPRESSION. Embodiments disclosed herein provide artificial expression systems comprising multivalent transcription factor complexes for cooperative transcription factor assembly and modulating gene expression. More specifically, engineered synthetic transcription factors are recruited and structurally organized on synthetic gene circuits using molecular clamps, where the strength of intra-complex interactions can be modulated for fine tuning of gene expression as desired.

title: Genetic engineering bacterium for producing low-temperature glucose oxidase and preparation method thereof
text: Genetic engineering bacterium for producing low-temperature glucose oxidase and preparation method thereof. The invention belongs to the field of biological engineering, and especially relates to a genetic engineering bacterium for producing low-temperature glucose oxidase and a preparation method thereof.The genetic engineering bacterium producing low-temperature glucose oxidase is CGMCCNO. 17228 is taken as a starting strain, the gene is obtained by gene synthesis, an expression vector pET28a-GOD isconstructed, and an expression vector is dumped into E. coli BL21 to construct the genetic engineering bacterium E. coli BL21/pET28a producing the low-temperature glucose oxidase. The invention also provides the use of the GOD-producing genetic engineering bacterium for the fermentation production of GOD. The genetic engineering bacterium capable of greatly increasing the production of the low-temperature glucose oxidase and shortening the fermentation time is constructed, can solve the disadvantages of low vigor and long fermentation time of the existing wild strain, and has wide industrial application prospects.

title: Chemoautotrophic cells comprising an engineered carbon fixation pathway
text: Chemoautotrophic cells comprising an engineered carbon fixation pathway. The present disclosure identifies pathways, mechanisms, systems and methods to confer chemoautotrophic production of carbon-based products of interest, such as sugars, alcohols, chemicals, amino acids, polymers, fatty acids and their derivatives, hydrocarbons, isoprenoids, and intermediates thereof, in organisms such that these organisms efficiently convert inorganic carbon to organic carbon-based products of interest using inorganic energy, such as formate, and in particular the use of organisms for the commercial production of various carbon-based products of interest.

title: Method for rapidly and seamlessly preparing mRNA (messenger ribonucleic acid) containing long polyadenine and application
text: Method for rapidly and seamlessly preparing mRNA (messenger ribonucleic acid) containing long polyadenine and application. The invention discloses a method for rapidly and seamlessly preparing mRNA (messenger Ribonucleic Acid) containing long polyadenine and application of the mRNA. The method comprises two steps of rapid synthesis of synthesizing and decomposing a complex gene containing long poly (A) into a simple target gene through modular design and an IIS type restriction DNA (Deoxyribose Nucleic Acid) endonuclease system, and seamless connection with a pre-prepared leader plasmid. The mRNA synthesized based on the method can carry the poly (A) with fixed length without tailing operation, and the target gene synthesis does not involve long poly (A) complex fragments, so that the template plasmid preparation speed and efficiency are greatly improved. The plasmid can be used as a linear template to be directly used for in-vitro transcription synthesis of long poly (A) mRNA, an IIS type restriction DNA incision enzyme system does not need to introduce an additional splicing sequence between a target gene and poly (A), and a transcribed mRNA sequence completely meets expectation.

title: SYSTEMS AND METHODS FOR CONTROL OF GENE EXPRESSION
text: SYSTEMS AND METHODS FOR CONTROL OF GENE EXPRESSION. Embodiments disclosed herein provide artificial expression systems comprising multivalent transcription factor complexes for cooperative transcription factor assembly and modulating gene expression. More specifically, engineered synthetic transcription factors are recruited and structurally organized on synthetic gene circuits using molecular clamps, where the strength of intra-complex interactions can be modulated for fine tuning of gene expression as desired.

title: ISOLATION OF DNA FRAGMENTS
text: ISOLATION OF DNA FRAGMENTS. The present invention allows for the isolation of sequence correct DNA fragments from heterogeneous mixtures of fragments through the implementation of several steps for the purification of DNA molecules, the amplification of said DNA molecules, identification of sequence correct DNA fragments, and isolation of fragments as templates for PCR-based production of further downstream fragments.

title: AUTOCATALYTIC BASE EDITING FOR RNA-RESPONSIVE TRANSLATIONAL CONTROL
text: AUTOCATALYTIC BASE EDITING FOR RNA-RESPONSIVE TRANSLATIONAL CONTROL. Genetic circuits that control transgene expression in response to pre-defined transcriptional cues would enable the development of smart therapeutics. The present disclosure relates to engineered programmable single-transcript RNA sensors in which adenosine deaminases acting on RNA (ADARs) autocatalytically convert trigger hybridization into a translational output. This system amplifies the signal from editing by endogenous ADAR through a positive feedback loop. Amplification is mediated by the expression of a hyperactive, minimal ADAR variant and its recruitment to the edit site via an orthogonal RNA targeting mechanism. This topology confers high dynamic range, low background, minimal off-target effects, and a small genetic footprint. The circuits and systems disclosed herein leverage an ability to detect single nucleotide polymorphisms and modulate translation in response to endogenous transcript levels in mammalian cells.

title: Corn growth-promoting rhizosphere bacterium enterobacter cloacae LN01 and application thereof
text: Corn growth-promoting rhizosphere bacterium enterobacter cloacae LN01 and application thereof. The invention discloses a corn rhizosphere growth-promoting bacterium enterobacter cloacae LN01 and an application thereof. The growth-promoting rhizosphere bacterium is named as Enterobacter hormaechei LN01 and is preserved in the China Center for Type Culture Collection, and the preservation number of the growth-promoting rhizosphere bacterium is CCTCC (China Center for Type Culture Collection) NO.M 2024003. Researches find that LN01 has the capabilities of dissolving phosphorus and potassium, fixing nitrogen and secreting indole-3-acetic acid (IAA) and siderophores. The LN01 can promote biomass accumulation of the overground part of the corn and root morphological development such as root length, intersection number, node number and the like. Through antiSMASH analysis, biological gene synthesis clusters of four secondary metabolites, including non-ribosome peptide synthetase, terpene, thiopeptide and aryl polyene, for promoting plant growth are found in the whole genome of LN01. The invention provides a new technical means for research, development and application of the biological fertilizer for promoting corn growth.

title: Bacillus amyloliquefaciens chitosanase and preparation method and application thereof
text: Bacillus amyloliquefaciens chitosanase and preparation method and application thereof. The invention discloses bacillus amyloliquefaciens chitosanase and a preparation method and application thereof. A code gene sequence of chitosanase in bacillus amyloliquefaciens is acquired by usinga whole-gene synthesis method according to codon preference of pichia pastoris, and an optimized nucleotide sequence is as shown in SEQ ID NO.2. Further, a pichia pastoris expression system performs efficient secretion expression on the optimized chitosanase code gene so as to acquire the bacillus amyloliquefaciens chitosanase, and the nucleotide sequence is as shown in SEQ ID NO.1. The chitosanase obtained by the method has relatively high hydrolysis activity for a chitosan substrate, coarse enzyme liquid generated by shaking flask fermentation has the hydrolysis function of degrading 5g of chitosan with 1mL of coarse enzyme liquid (containing 0.23mg of protein), while the degradation of the same amount of chitosan needs 150mg of alpha-diastase, so that the efficiency is improved by 600 times or above theoretically. The bacillus amyloliquefaciens chitosanase has good industrial application prospect.

title: Establishment of prokaryotic expression and purification as well as test method of reconstruction influenza A H1N1 virus nonstructural protein NS1
text: Establishment of prokaryotic expression and purification as well as test method of reconstruction influenza A H1N1 virus nonstructural protein NS1. The invention belongs to the research field of zoonosis, relating to a differential diagnosis method. Nonstructural protein NS1 gene in a disclosed influenza A H1N1 California virus strain (A/California/08/2009(H1N1)) cDNA sequence is taken as the research content, a gene synthesis method is used to obtain a gene fragment construction prokaryotic expression carrier Pgex-6P-1-NS1; positive recombinant plasmid is converted to colibacillus to obtain a recombination strain (Escherichia coli BL21 rosseta/Pgex-6p-1-NS1); the nonstructural protein NS1 of the purified H1N1 is obtained by a plurality of chromatography methods, and an enzyme linked immunosorbent assay differential diagnosis method is established in a manner that the expression protein obtains specificity through a immunization host animal specific to polyclonal or monoclonal antibody of the H1N1 nonstructural protein NS1, which is used for distinguishing patients suffering from influenza A H1N1 virus infection and other patients suffering from influenza virus infections.

title: Establishment of method for preparing recombinant protein vaccine of type A H1N1 influenza virus
text: Establishment of method for preparing recombinant protein vaccine of type A H1N1 influenza virus. The invention belongs to the field of zoonosis researches, and relates to a method for preparing a vaccine of zoonosis. In the method, an HA gene in a cDNA sequence of a type A H1N1 California virus strain (A/California/08/2009(H1N1)) is selected as a research content; the method comprises the following steps of: firstly, analyzing a sequence of the part of an HA protein leaked outside an envelope according to structural biology software; secondly, selecting a part with high immunogenicity according to an antigenic analysis, and constructing a prokaryotic expression vector pGEX-6P-1-HA by using a genetic fragment obtained through a gene synthesis method; and thirdly, converting a masculine recombinant plasmid into Escherichia coli to obtain a recombinant strain (Escherichia coli BL21 rosseta/pGEX-6P-1-HA truncated protein). Through a plurality of chromatography methods, a purified HA truncated protein of the H1N1 is obtained; and by utilizing the expression vector and an adjuvant together to immune organisms, the protective effect on the H1N1 virus is achieved.

title: Hemicellulase-based engineering bacteria and realization method thereof
text: Hemicellulase-based engineering bacteria and realization method thereof. The invention relates to hemicellulase-based engineering bacteria in the technical field of genetic engineering and a realization method thereof. The realization method comprises the following steps: firstly, by taking a vector pUC57-XL obtained by whole gene synthesis of streptomyces griseorubens genome DNA (deoxyribonucleic acid) as a template, performing PCR (polymerase chain reaction) amplification on the vector and a primer with an enzyme cutting site to obtain a nucleotide sequence XL for coding xylanase and a nucleotide sequence XO for coding xylosidase correspondingly and respectively; secondly, connecting the gene sequences obtained by amplification to a coexpression vector pETDuet-1 in sequence, and finally recombining a coexpression vector pETDuet-XL-XO; thirdly, converting the recombinant coexpression vector to an escherichia coli expression strain so as to obtain a transgenic coexpression strain. Therefore, a large quantity of xylanase and xylosidase can be expressed and synthesized in vitro by using genetic engineering means, and finally hemicellulose can be quickly degraded in situ.

title: Virus envelope protein assembly system as well as method and application thereof
text: Virus envelope protein assembly system as well as method and application thereof. The invention provides a virus envelope protein assembly system as well as a preparation method and application thereof. The envelope protein assembly system comprises a secretory peptide signal sequence, an exogenous assembly target protein molecule and a transmembrane (TM) region which are connected in sequence. The method comprises the following steps: integrating multiple components containing the secretory peptide signal sequence, the exogenous assembly target protein molecule and the TM region into a gene expression vector by utilizing a gene synthesis technology to transfect cells, and then infecting the cells with viruses. The scheme provided by the invention can be used for modifying an oncolytic virus or a viral vaccine candidate vector, and the envelope protein assembly system can be correctly assembled on surfaces of the viruses. After exogenous target protein molecules are assembled on the virus envelope, the application way and application of the recombinant virus are expanded. The preparation method does not need to carry out complex internal editing modification on virus genes, and is simple and wide in application range.

title: Recombinant plasmid, construction method and prokaryotic expression method of mu-conotoxin peptide
text: Recombinant plasmid, construction method and prokaryotic expression method of mu-conotoxin peptide. The invention discloses a recombinant plasmid, a construction method and a prokaryotic expression method of mu-conotoxin peptide. The construction method of the recombinant plasmid for prokaryotic expression of the [mu]-conotoxin peptide comprises the following steps: 1) transforming an amino acid sequence of the [mu]-conotoxin peptide; 2) performing codon optimization on the modified amino acid sequence to obtain an optimized base sequence; 3) performing gene synthesis according to the base sequence, and cloning the gene to a vector to obtain a synthetic plasmid; 4) transforming the synthetic plasmid through escherichia coli to obtain a transformed plasmid; 5) amplifying the transformed plasmid through the first primer, the second primer and the third primer to obtain an amplified target product; and 6) carrying out homologous recombination on the linearized synthetic plasmid and the amplified target product to obtain a homologous recombination product, and converting the homologous recombination product into escherichia coli to obtain the recombinant plasmid. The recombinant plasmid is beneficial to prokaryotic expression of the mu-conotoxin peptide.

title: Gene for encoding LsGAD (Lactobacillus senmaizukei glutamic acid decarboxylase), recombinant bacterium and Kombucha fermentation viable bacteria preparation
text: Gene for encoding LsGAD (Lactobacillus senmaizukei glutamic acid decarboxylase), recombinant bacterium and Kombucha fermentation viable bacteria preparation. The invention belongs to the technical field of bioengineering, particularly relates to a gene for encoding LsGAD (Lactobacillus senmaizukei glutamic acid decarboxylase), a recombinant bacterium and aKombucha fermentation viable bacteria preparation. The gene for the encoding LsGAD has a sequence disclosed by SEQ ID NO.1. A novel glutamic acid decarboxylase gene excavated from the genome of Lactobacillus senmaizukei approved by GRAS (Generally recognized as safe) is taken as a target gene, a codon utilization frequency table of Lactococcus lactis MG1363 is taken as codon optimization, after gene synthesis is carried out, the engineering bacterium of the Lactococcus lactis capable of realizing the high yield of a Gamma-aminobutyric acid is successfully constructed in virtue of a food-gradelactobacillus expression plasmid, the research of the functional Kombucha produced by fermentation is carried out, and the content of the GABA (Gamma-aminobutyric acid) in the Kombucha can be obviously improved.

title: METHOD FOR SCREENING OF USEFUL ENZYMES FROM METAGENOME USING FORCED TRANSCRIPTION
text: METHOD FOR SCREENING OF USEFUL ENZYMES FROM METAGENOME USING FORCED TRANSCRIPTION. PURPOSE: A high efficiency detection method of metagenome-derived useful enzyme is provided to enhance the expression of a foreign gene using a forces transcription system, to sense a small amount of reactant, and to sort a target gene. CONSTITUTION: A high efficiency detection method for metagenome-derived enzyme through forced transcription comprises: a step of randomly inserting transposon into a metagenome library gene containing a gene encoding an enzyme to be detected; a step of transducing the metagenome library to a RNA polymerase-inserted host microbe and preparing a recombinant microbe library; a step of introducing a redesigned genetic circuit for sensing phenol compounds to the metagenome library; a step of treating an inducer to the recombinant microbe library and culturing; a step of treating the recombinant microbe library with a phenol releasing compound; a step of measuring and detecting activity of a reporter protein at high speed; and a step of collecting a gene of the enzyme which releases the phenol compounds from a detected microbe and performing sequencing of the gene. [Reference numerals] (AA) Redesigned genetic circuit searching system; (BB) Redesigned genetic circuit; (CC) Forced transcription introducing library; (DD) Forced transcription system; (EE) Metagenome library; (FF) Forced transcription unit;

title: Method for preparing aspartate aminotransferase and application of aspartate aminotransferase
text: Method for preparing aspartate aminotransferase and application of aspartate aminotransferase. The invention discloses a method for preparing aspartate aminotransferase and application of the aspartate aminotransferase. The method includes: modifying the coding gene sequence of humanized aspartate aminotransferase subjected to gene synthesis, and removing escherichia coli rare codons; inserting the modified aspartate aminotransferase coding gene sequence into an expression vector, and using an escherichia coli strain to transform expression; inoculating strain for transforming expression to LB (Luria-Bertani) medium for IPTG (isopropyl-beta-d-thiogalactoside) induction; and homogenizing and centrifuging obtained bacteria, and enabling the centrifuged bacteria to pass through the Ni-NTA column for purification. The method for preparing the aspartate aminotransferase is extremely simple in operation, low in preparation cost and high in finished product yield and purity; the aspartate aminotransferase has high biological activity and can be used for animal immunization and screening related antibodies by serving as antigen substances; and the method and the aspartate aminotransferase lay the foundation for establishing a badly-needed calibrator preparation platform as early as possible for domestic clinical chemistry diagnosis and developing diagnostic reagent standard substances and references.

title: ENGINEERED HERPES SIMPLEX VIRUS-1 (HSV-1) VECTORS AND USES THEREOF
text: ENGINEERED HERPES SIMPLEX VIRUS-1 (HSV-1) VECTORS AND USES THEREOF. Provided herein are engineered HSV-1 vectors comprising a modified HSV-1 genome. The engineered HSV-1 vectors can be used to deliver genetic circuits (e.g., up to 100 kb) to cells in vitro or in vivo. Methods of treating or diagnosing a disease (e.g., cancer) using the engineered HSV-1 vectors described herein are also provided.

title: Human long-acting erythropoietin for treating anemia
text: Human long-acting erythropoietin for treating anemia. The invention provides human long-acting erythropoietin for treating anemia. The human long-acting erythropoietin is characterized in that chimeric genes of erythropoietin (EPO) constructed by using a gene synthesis technology contain one or more CL sequences, wherein the CL sequences can be i) two at the C terminal, ii) one at the C terminal, the other at the N terminal, iii) one at the N terminal, the other two at the C terminal, iv) two at the C terminal and the two at the N terminal; and the chimeric genes are sequenced and cloned into eukaryotic expression vectors, the constructed vectors containing EPO and CL sequences are transfected into CHO or HEK293 cells, stable clones are selected, a culture medium of the stable clones is collected, and variants are purified. A carbohydrate linker is connected to a coding sequence of erythropoietin to develop long-acting human erythropoietin, the long-acting EPO analogue is connected to a cloning site of an eukaryotic expression vector, cells are transfected, and clones capable of stably secreting the EPO analogue are screened out.

title: Recombinant pichia pastoris for enhanced expression of xylanase from Streptomyces sp.FA1
text: Recombinant pichia pastoris for enhanced expression of xylanase from Streptomyces sp.FA1. Belonging to the field of genetic engineering, the invention discloses a recombinant pichia pastoris for enhanced expression of xylanase from Streptomyces sp.FA1. According to the invention, a target gene with a nucleotide sequence shown as SEQ ID NO:1 is acquired by amplification, the target gene is connected to the expression vector pGAPZ alpha A-PARS, and then the obtained recombinant vector is introduced into pichia pastoris KM71/pPIC9K-XynA so as to obtain the recombinant pichia pastoris. The invention also discloses a production method for the recombinant engineered pichia pastoris. The method includes the steps of: (1) synthesizing an autonomously replicating sequence PARS by whole gene synthesis technology; (2) constructing the expression vector pGAPZ alpha A-PARS; (3) constructing the recombinant expression vector pGAPZ alpha A-PARS-XynA; (4) converting a pichia pastoris host and screening a positive transformant; (5) conducting flask shaking induction culture; and (6) inducing recombinant engineered pichia pastoris in a fermentation tank to produce xylanase.

title: Application of SC8 gene in regulation and control of accumulation of soluble solids in tomato fruits and breeding method
text: Application of SC8 gene in regulation and control of accumulation of soluble solids in tomato fruits and breeding method. The invention belongs to the technical field of genetic engineering breeding, and particularly relates to application of an SC8 gene in regulating accumulation of soluble solids in tomato fruits and a breeding method. The SC8 gene provided by the invention plays an important role in tomato saccharic acid metabolism and soluble solid synthesis and accumulation, and plays a role in negative regulation of tomato fruit soluble solid synthesis and accumulation. The SC8 gene is applied to plant genetic engineering breeding, and a new functional site and a new breeding thought are provided for cultivating high-quality flavor tomato varieties. By inhibiting the expression of the SC8 gene, soluble solids and sugar acid metabolites thereof such as cane sugar, fructose, glucose, malic acid and citric acid in the red ripe tomato fruits can be increased, and by enhancing the expression level of the SC8 gene, synthesis and accumulation of the soluble solids in the tomato can be reduced; therefore, the implementation of the invention provides a feasible way for cultivating and producing tomatoes with various flavors and qualities, and plays an important role in improving the economic benefits of the tomato industry.

title: Method for producing DNA encoding polypeptides that are composed of several sections, and for producing polypeptides by expressing the DNA thus obtained
text: Method for producing DNA encoding polypeptides that are composed of several sections, and for producing polypeptides by expressing the DNA thus obtained. The invention relates to a method for producing novel modular enzyme systems by genetic engineering, which is characterized by a cyclic in vitro gene synthesis and which allows a specific recombination of the individual, gene-encoded modular components to novel enzyme systems. Examples of such modular enzyme systems are the non-ribosomal peptide synthetases (NRPS) or polyketide synthases (PKS) of type 1, that is the amino acid sequence of such an enzyme is characterized by being composed of a repetitive sequence of identical sequence sections or sequence sections that are very similar to one another. Every single repetitive sequence is referred to as a module and every module allows the enzymatic incorporation of a specific substrate into the substance synthesized by the enzyme. The products synthesized by the NRPS and PKS enzymes are often highly valuable as pharmaceuticals, such as the penicillins, vancomycins or erythromycins. The inventive method allows for an effective production of novel genes for modular enzymes, and the gene expression of said novel genes allows for the production of novel substances.

title: Flavin monooxygenase mutant with improved enzyme activity and application thereof
text: Flavin monooxygenase mutant with improved enzyme activity and application thereof. The invention belongs to the technical field of gene engineering of enzymes, and particularly relates to a flavin monooxygenase mutant with improved enzymatic activity and preparation and application thereof. The method comprises the following steps: obtaining a wild-type flavin monooxygenase gene from schizosaccharomyces pombe through whole-gene synthesis, transforming the wild-type flavin monooxygenase gene by using a key site amino acid residue site-directed mutagenesis technology to obtain a flavin monooxygenase mutant Q89H and a coding gene spfmom thereof, reconstructing a recombinant plasmid, and carrying out purification to obtain the flavin monooxygenase mutant Q89H and the coding gene spfmom thereof, thereby obtaining the flavin monooxygenase mutant Q89H and the coding gene spfmom of the flavin monooxygenase mutant Q89H. According to the present invention, the high-efficiency expression of the mutant in Escherichia coli is achieved, the relative enzyme activity of the mutant is 130% of the relative enzyme activity of the wild type, the yield of the catalytic alliin reaches 22.86%, and the important industrial value is provided.

title: Product expressed by housefly embryonic cell construction antibacterial peptide attacin eukaryotic expression system and construction method
text: Product expressed by housefly embryonic cell construction antibacterial peptide attacin eukaryotic expression system and construction method. The present invention relates to a product expressed by a housefly embryonic cell construction antibacterial peptide attacin eukaryotic expression system and a construction method, housefly antibacterial peptide attacin is used as a target gene, constructed housefly embryonic cell line MDEC-07114 is transfected by recombinant baculovirus Bacmid-pFastBacTM-attacin, and an antibacterial peptide attacin housefly embryonic cell eukaryotic expression system is constructed. The housefly antimicrobial peptide attacin is obtained by complete gene synthesis, the target gene attacin is cloned into PUC57plasmid, after double enzyme digestion, recombinant plasmid is connected with pFastBacTMHTA donor plasmid for recombination of baculovirus Bacmid DNA; the recombinant baculovirus Bacmid DNA is transfected into housefly embryonic cell MDEC-07114, culture supernatant and cell lysates crude protein are extracted, and purified by a nickel column, SDS-PAGE and Western-Blot are used for detection and identification of protein expression, and the antimicrobial activity of the expressed product can be detected.

title: COMPOSITION FOR SEQUENTIAL POLYMERASE CHAIN REACTION, AND GENE AMPLIFICATION METHOD USING SAME
text: COMPOSITION FOR SEQUENTIAL POLYMERASE CHAIN REACTION, AND GENE AMPLIFICATION METHOD USING SAME. The present invention relates to a composition for a sequential polymerase chain reaction and a gene amplification method using the same. The composition for a sequential polymerase chain reaction comprises a template gene, a DNA polymerase, dNTP, a first forward primer, a first reverse primer, a second reverse primer and a third reverse primer so that a sequential polymerase chain reaction can be induced in one container. Unlike conventional nested PCR, a reactant-adding step is omitted in the sequential polymerase chain reaction according to the present invention, and thus the inflow of contaminants from the outside is low, the yield of polymerase chain reaction (PCR) of genes can be increased, and highly-reproducible polymerase chain reaction results can be obtained even if the presence of genes is very low.

title: Engineered Herpes Simplex Virus-1 (HSV-1) vectors and uses thereof
text: Engineered Herpes Simplex Virus-1 (HSV-1) vectors and uses thereof. Provided herein are engineered HSV-1 vectors comprising a modified HSV-1 genome. The engineered HSV-1 vectors can be used to deliver genetic circuits (e.g., up to 100 kb) to cells in vitro or in vivo. Methods of treating or diagnosing a disease (e.g., cancer) using the engineered HSV-1 vectors described herein are also provided.

title: Leech tyrosine sulfonate transferase gene and application thereof
text: Leech tyrosine sulfonate transferase gene and application thereof. The invention discloses a leech tyrosine sulphonate transferase gene and application thereof, and belongs to the field of biomedicine. The leech tyrosine sulphonate transferase gene is characterized in that on the basis of a hirudo nipponia transcriptome database, a leech tyrosine sulphonate transferase cDNA sequence is excavated and identified through a bioinformatics means, and on this basis, the leech tyrosine sulfotransferase is synthesized through codon optimization, gene synthesis and microbial cell expression, and the recombinant hirudin is catalyzed by the leech tyrosine sulfotransferase to be subjected to sulfonation modification to obtain the natural hirudin. The catalytic ability of the transferase is greatly improved compared with that of acyl sulfonate transferase, human-derived tyrosine sulfonate transferase and bovine-derived tyrosine sulfonate transferase. Compared with other tyrosine sulfonic acid transferase, the leech tyrosine sulfonic acid transferase obtained in the invention has stronger substrate preference for recombinant hirudin, is suitable for large-scale preparation of natural hirudin, and provides a new way for synthesis of natural hirudin.

title: Preparation method of recombinant human collagen III
text: Preparation method of recombinant human collagen III. The invention is applicable to the technical field of gene engineering, and provides a recombinant human source III type collagen which comprises a nucleotide sequence of the recombinant human source III type collagen, and the nucleotide sequence of the recombinant human source III type collagen is shown as SEQ ID NO.2. The preparation method comprises the following steps: step 1, gene design; 2, gene synthesis is carried out, and expression vectors pPIC9K and pPICZA are constructed; step 3, preparing a pichia pastoris expression system containing the proline hydroxylase gene; and 4, carrying out induced expression on the recombinant human source III type collagen. The human-derived III-type collagen produced by the method is good in safety and stable in quality, and potential safety hazards existing in a traditional extraction method are eliminated. The hydrophilicity and immune exclusion of the collagen are improved, and meanwhile, the expression quantity and the effective activity are also improved.

title: Application of lactobacillus reuteri ZJ617 in improvement of non-alcoholic fatty liver and fat accumulation
text: Application of lactobacillus reuteri ZJ617 in improvement of non-alcoholic fatty liver and fat accumulation. The invention belongs to the technical field of microorganisms, and particularly relates to application of lactobacillus reuteri capable of improving non-alcoholic fatty liver and fat accumulation. The invention discloses an application of Lactobacillus reuteri ZJ617 (Lactobacillus reuteri ZJ617) in preparation of a product for improving non-alcoholic fatty liver disease and fat accumulation, and a preparation method of the Lactobacillus reuteri ZJ617 and the product for improving non-alcoholic fatty liver disease and fat accumulation. The lactobacillus reuteri ZJ617 can be used for relieving liver lipid accumulation, relieving liver injury and inflammation, reducing hyperlipidemia and improving glucose intolerance and insulin resistance. The fat accumulation improvement comprises the steps of reducing the size of fat cells, promoting heat production of organisms and white fat tissues and improving the expression level of fat heat production genes; the expression level of related gene synthesis of fat tissue mitochondria is promoted, and fat deposition is improved.

title: Recombinant expression and application of human myelin sheath oligodendrocyte glycoprotein
text: Recombinant expression and application of human myelin sheath oligodendrocyte glycoprotein. The invention discloses recombinant expression and application of human myelin sheath oligodendroglia glycoprotein, and particularly provides a preparation method of eukaryotic recombinant MOG protein which is high in expression quantity, good in immunogenicity and high in purity. According to the invention, the extracellular fragment of the MOG gene is obtained by cloning by using a gene engineering technology; the MOG antibody is connected to an expression vector and then transformed into a host cell to express and purify a large amount of stable MOG protein, and a basis is provided for in-vitro diagnosis of the MOG antibody. According to the method, codon optimization is carried out on the MOG gene in the gene synthesis process, optimization of optimal expression time of three different signal peptides is applied, the optimal sample collection time of supernatant expression is determined, 10% ethyl alcohol is added into a purified buffer solution, and the condition of protein aggregation is successfully avoided; and in the activity identification of final MOG expression, the expressed MOG protein can accurately distinguish positive and negative samples of MOG-IgG clinically.

title: 3-ketosteroid-delta1-dehydrogenase mutant and application thereof
text: 3-ketosteroid-delta1-dehydrogenase mutant and application thereof. The invention discloses a 3-ketosteroid-delta1-dehydrogenase mutant and application of the 3-ketosteroid-delta1-dehydrogenase mutant. The amino acid sequence of the 3-ketosteroid-delta1-dehydrogenase mutant is obtained by mutation of the amino acid sequence as shown in SEQ ID NO: 2. The invention further discloses application of a steroid drug and a steroid drug intermediate based on C1 and C2 site dehydrogenation catalytic synthesis of the mutant. According to the invention, a 3-ketosteroid-delta1-dehydrogenase gene derived from Mycobacterium smegmatics mc2155 is obtained through whole-gene synthesis, a recombinant escherichia coli expression strain is constructed by adopting a genetic engineering technology, and the enzyme activity is improved by adopting a molecular modification technology. A 3-sterone compound is used as a raw material, and a steroid drug or a drug intermediate is catalytically synthesized by adopting a whole-cell or zymoprotein catalysis method. The target product can be obtained through one-step reaction under mild conditions, and the method is environment-friendly and is a green biological catalytic synthesis approach.

title: Echinococcus multilocularis glucose transporter polypeptide vaccine GLEP as well as preparation method and application thereof
text: Echinococcus multilocularis glucose transporter polypeptide vaccine GLEP as well as preparation method and application thereof. The invention discloses an echinococcus multilocularis glucose transporter polypeptide vaccine GLEP as well as a preparation method and application thereof. The active ingredient of the vaccine GLEP is a polypeptide, and the polypeptide is mainly composed of an echinococcus multilocularis glucose transporter polypeptide sequence. The echinococcus multilocularis vaccine GLEP gene sequence is synthesized through a gene synthesis technology, double digestion is connected into an expression vector, and then the expression vector is converted into Arctic Express for expression of fusion protein. After the protein is purified through Ni-NTA affinity chromatography, the echinococcus multilocularis vaccine GLEP is obtained. The echinococcus multilocularis vaccine GLEP can induce an organism to generate immune response and high-titer specific antibody humoral immune response for echinococcus multilocularis T cells and B cells, therefore, mice are effectively prevented from being infected with echinococcus multilocularis, and the echinococcus multilocularis glucose transporter polypeptide vaccine GLEP can be used for preventing echinococcus multilocularis infection related diseases.

title: Expression vector of cyclodextrin glucosyltransferase and construction and application of bacillus subtilis
text: Expression vector of cyclodextrin glucosyltransferase and construction and application of bacillus subtilis. The invention discloses an expression vector of cyclodextrin glucosyltransferase and construction and application of bacillus subtilis, belongs to the technical field of enzyme engineering, and is characterized in that the amino acid sequence of the cyclodextrin glucosyltransferase is shown as SEQ ID NO. 1; the nucleotide sequence for coding the amino acid sequence of the cyclodextrin glucosyltransferase is as shown in SEQ ID NO. 2; the construction method of the expression vector of the cyclodextrin glucosyltransferase comprises the following steps: carrying out whole gene synthesis on an amino acid sequence as shown in SEQ ID NO.1 to obtain a target gene, and connecting the target gene with a vector plasmid pWB980-ori to obtain an expression vector pWB980-ori-CGT; the method has the beneficial effects that the expression vector pWB980-orii-CGT is obtained, the bacillus subtilis is constructed, the cyclodextrin glucosyltransferase can be stably and efficiently expressed, the bacillus subtilis can be directly treated by full-fermentation reaction enzyme liquid without separation, and the constructed genetically engineered bacterium is shortened in fermentation period and has high enzyme activity and high conversion rate.

title: Systems and methods for control of gene expression
text: Systems and methods for control of gene expression. Embodiments disclosed herein provide artificial expression systems comprising multivalent transcription factor complexes for cooperative transcription factor assembly and modulating gene expression. More specifically, engineered synthetic transcription factors are recruited and structurally organized on synthetic gene circuits using molecular clamps, where the strength of intra-complex interactions can be modulated for fine tuning of gene expression as desired.

title: Method for efficiently producing vanillin by utilizing vibrio natriureus
text: Method for efficiently producing vanillin by utilizing vibrio natriureus. The invention discloses a method for efficiently producing vanillin by utilizing vibrio natriticus, and relates to the field of biochemistry, chassis cells are vibrio natriticus, and plasmids are introduced into electrotransfection competent cells of the vibrio natriticus by an electrotransfection method to obtain recombinant bacteria; the plasmids are feruloyl coenzyme A synthase of syntrophic alkane degrading bacteria and enoyl coenzyme A hydratase from saccharomonospora vibrio, and recombinant plasmids are obtained by codon optimization of vibrio, gene synthesis and combined expression. The method for producing vanillin comprises the following steps: introducing the carrier into vibrio natrieti, carrying out whole-cell catalysis, and carrying out biotransformation by taking ferulic acid as an initial substrate to generate vanillin. The vibrio natriticus with higher protein synthesis efficiency and higher growth speed is introduced as a chassis cell, so that the cell culture time and the protein expression time can be further shortened, and the reaction efficiency is improved. Meanwhile, the reaction is carried out under a high-temperature condition, so that the vanillin synthesis rate is higher.

title: Establishment and application of detection method for in vitro evaluation of influenza A virus subtype H1N1 antibody titer
text: Establishment and application of detection method for in vitro evaluation of influenza A virus subtype H1N1 antibody titer. The invention belongs to the field of research on zoonosis, and relates to a differential diagnosis method for zoonosis. The method comprises: selecting a HA gene in a cDNA sequence of disclosed influenza H1N1 California virus strain (A/California/08/2009 (H1N1)) as research content; analyzing the sequence of the part of HA protein leaking out of peplos according to structural biology software; selecting the part with higher immunogenicity according to antigenicity analysis; obtaining a gene fragment by a gene synthesis method to build a prokaryotic expression vector pGEX-6P-1-HA; transforming positive recombinant into escherichia coli to obtain recombinant strain (Escherichia Coli BL21 Rosseta/pGEX-6P-1-HA truncated protein); obtaining HA truncated protein of purified H1N1 by a plurality of chromatography methods; and utilizing indirect-method experimental principles of expression protein establishment enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay to evaluate the antibody titer produced inside patients because of influenza virus infection or vaccine immunization.

title: BIOTECHNOLOGICAL PRODUCTION OF MONOMERS OF BISUCABERINS, DESFERRIOXAMINES AND ANALOGS THEREOF
text: BIOTECHNOLOGICAL PRODUCTION OF MONOMERS OF BISUCABERINS, DESFERRIOXAMINES AND ANALOGS THEREOF. The present invention relates to a recombinant microbial cell for producing N5-aminopentyl-N- (hydroxy)-succinamic acid of Formula I from at least one simple carbon source wherein the simple carbon source is selected from the group consisting of glucose, sucrose, xylose, arabinose, mannose, glycerol and combinations thereof and wherein the cell comprises a further genetic modification to increase production of L-lysine from at least one of the simple carbon sources.

title: SINGLE-CHAIN ANTIBODY TRAB1 AGAINST TRSV AND PREPARATION METHOD THEREOF
text: SINGLE-CHAIN ANTIBODY TRAB1 AGAINST TRSV AND PREPARATION METHOD THEREOF. Provided is a single-chain antibody TRAb1 against TRSV, having an amino acid sequence shown in SEQ ID No.1, and consisting of a 15aa histidine tag region, a 184aa TRSV heavy chain variable region, a 15aa linker peptide, and a reverse-linked 120aa TRSV light chain variable region, connected in sequence, with a length of 334 amino acid residues. The sequences of the 184aa heavy chain variable region and the 120aa light chain variable region are derived from the sequence of a monoclonal antibody produced by the hybridoma cell line CGMCCZO795. The TRAb1 gene is obtained by a whole gene synthesis method, and the codon composition is optimized. The yield of TRAb1 is 200-2000mg/L, with a purity of 82-95%. The single-chain antibody TRAb1 of the invention has the characteristics of selectively identifying the TRSV coat protein, and has a positive response to natural TRSV particles. The invention has broad application prospects in the Entry-Exit inspection and quarantine of the TRSV.

title: Application of PLEKHS1 protein in product preparation
text: Application of PLEKHS1 protein in product preparation. The application of the PLEKHS1 protein in product preparation mainly comprises the following four aspects: (1) constructing a lentiviral vector aiming at knocking down PLEKHS1 protein expression by using a gene synthesis technology, performing transformation and activation in a competent cell DH5alpha by using a gene cloning and molecular biological method, and performing plasmid extraction; (2) preparing a lentivirus for knocking down PLEKHS1 protein expression in an HEK293T engineering cell, and screening out an optimal infection condition in a lung adenocarcinoma cell strain; (3) a series of cell phenotype experiments find that the expression of p-AKT is obviously inhibited by knocking down the PLEKHS1 protein, so that the proliferation and invasion of lung cancer cells are further obviously inhibited; and (4) on the nude mouse animal level, compared with the nude mouse in the control group, the tumor volume of the nude mouse in the PLEKHS1 gene knock-down group is obviously reduced. Therefore, the PLEKHS1 protein can regulate the proliferation and transformation of the lung cancer. The method has a significant application value.

title: Fusion protein CRM197 and preparation method thereof
text: Fusion protein CRM197 and preparation method thereof. The invention discloses a fusion protein CRM197 and a preparation method thereof.
