The preparation method comprises the following steps: S1, performing whole-gene synthesis on a DNA sequence of the fusion protein; s2, connecting the target fragment with a vector through a genetic engineering technology to obtain a recombinant escherichia coli expression vector containing a DNA sequence for coding the fusion protein; and S3, transforming the recombinant escherichia coli expression vector in the step S2 into an escherichia coli competent state, and after successful sequencing, transforming the plasmid into a host expression system for expression to obtain the fusion protein, and S4, carrying out high-density fermentation on the screened high-yield strain, and carrying out enzyme digestion, first-step purification and second-step purification to obtain the high-purity natural CRM197 protein. According to the invention, the problem that low yield and high cost of commercially available natural CRM197 cause great limitation on application of the natural CRM197 is solved, and the natural CRM197 has a wide prospect in vaccine production.

title: COMPOSITION FOR SEQUENTIAL POLYMERASE CHAIN REACTION, AND GENE AMPLIFICATION METHOD USING SAME
text: COMPOSITION FOR SEQUENTIAL POLYMERASE CHAIN REACTION, AND GENE AMPLIFICATION METHOD USING SAME. The present invention relates to a composition for a sequential polymerase chain reaction and a gene amplification method using the same. The composition for a sequential polymerase chain reaction comprises a template gene, a DNA polymerase, dNTP, a first forward primer, a first reverse primer, a second reverse primer and a third reverse primer so that a sequential polymerase chain reaction can be induced in one container. Unlike conventional nested PCR, a reactant-adding step is omitted in the sequential polymerase chain reaction according to the present invention, and thus the inflow of contaminants from the outside is low, the yield of polymerase chain reaction (PCR) of genes can be increased, and highly-reproducible polymerase chain reaction results can be obtained even if the presence of genes is very low.

title: Engineering strain based on lignin metabolism channel key enzyme and implementation method thereof
text: Engineering strain based on lignin metabolism channel key enzyme and implementation method thereof. The invention relates to an engineering strain based on lignin metabolism channel key enzyme and an implementation method thereof. The implementation method comprises the following steps: carrying out complete gene synthesis on Streptomyces griseorubens genome (deoxyribonucleic acid) DNA to obtain a vector used as a template, respectively carrying out PCR (polymerase chain reaction) amplification with corresponding primers containing enzyme digestion sites to respectively obtain nucleotide sequence of coding laccase, nucleotide sequence of coding lignin peroxidase and nucleotide sequence of coding manganese peroxidase; sequentially connecting the amplified sequences to a co-expression vector and connecting the amplified sequences to a recombinant expression vector to finally obtain a recombinant co-expression vector and a recombinant expression vector; and sequentially transforming the recombinant co-expression vector and recombinant expression vector into an Escherichia coli expression strain to obtain a transgenic co-expression strain. The gene engineering means is utilized to implement abundant in-vitro expression synthesis of laccase, lignin peroxidase and manganese peroxidase, and finally used for implementing in-situ quick degradation of lignin.

title: Single-chain antibody for resisting canine parvovirus as well as preparation method and application of single-chain antibody
text: Single-chain antibody for resisting canine parvovirus as well as preparation method and application of single-chain antibody. The invention provides a single-chain antibody for resisting canine parvovirus as well as a preparation method and application thereof, belongs to the field of genetic engineering, and can solve the technical problem that the treatment effect of a conventional antibody on canine parvovirus is not ideal. The single-chain antibody for resisting canine parvovirus is a single-chain antibody coupled with signal peptide, the amino acid sequence of the single-chain antibody is shown as SEQ ID NO.1, and the nucleotide sequence of the single-chain antibody is shown as SEQ ID NO.3. The single-chain antibody for resisting canine parvovirus is prepared by the following steps: (1) gene synthesis and expression vector construction; (2) plasmid transformation and double enzyme digestion identification; (3) target protein induced expression; (4) purifying the target protein; and (5) detecting target protein antigen binding activity, cell proliferation, cytotoxicity and canine parvovirus inhibition effect. The invention can be applied to the aspects of resisting canine parvovirus and preparing anti-parvovirus medicine products.

title: Microbial subsurface soil sensors
text: Microbial subsurface soil sensors. Methods of detecting a target analyte in subsurface soil can providing a bacterial consortium embodying a genetic circuit. Such a consortium can include a sensor bacterium, a signal propagation bacterium, and a display bacterium, which are provided at a soil surface. The sensor bacterium and signal propagation can be allowed to migrate into subsurface soil below the soil surface. The sensor bacterium can be exposed to the target analyte in the subsurface soil. The sensor bacterium can produce a signal molecule in response to the presence of the target analyte. The signal propagation bacterium can amplify the signal molecule by producing additional signal molecules in response to the presence of the signal molecule. The display bacterium can produce an observable signal in response to presence of the signal molecule.

title: METHODS AND SYSTEMS FOR CHEMOAUTOTROPHIC PRODUCTION OF ORGANIC COMPOUNDS
text: METHODS AND SYSTEMS FOR CHEMOAUTOTROPHIC PRODUCTION OF ORGANIC COMPOUNDS. The present disclosure identifies pathways, mechanisms, systems and methods to confer chemoautotrophic production of carbon-based products of interest, such as sugars, alcohols, chemicals, amino acids, polymers, fatty acids and their derivatives, hydrocarbons, isoprenoids, and intermediates thereof, in organisms such that these organisms efficiently convert inorganic carbon to organic carbon-based products of interest using inorganic energy, such as formate, and in particular the use of organisms for the commercial production of various carbon-based products of interest.

title: Polyphenol oxidase as well as preparation method and application thereof
text: Polyphenol oxidase as well as preparation method and application thereof. The invention belongs to the technical field of bioengineering, and particularly relates to polyphenol oxidase as well as a preparation method and application thereof. A polyphenol oxidase AbPPO gene from agaricus bisporus is subjected to gene synthesis after being optimized by an aspergillus niger codon, an AbPPO gene expression cassette is constructed, a CRISPR/Cas9 technology is utilized, hygB is taken as a screening tag, the AbPPO gene expression cassette is transformed into aspergillus niger through a PEG-mediated protoplast transformation method, a transformed strain with the highest enzyme activity is obtained through screening, and the Agaricus bisporus-derived polyphenol oxidase AbPPO gene is obtained. The recombinase is used for catalyzing degradation of catechol, and the degradation rate of the recombinase can reach 99.6%. The operation method provided by the invention is simple and safe, the production cost is low, the preparation condition is mild, and the method has the advantages of greenness and high efficiency in the field of organic phenol pollutant treatment and shows relatively high economic value and social benefit.

title: Construction method of novel fruit fly intestinal cancer model
text: Construction method of novel fruit fly intestinal cancer model. The invention discloses a novel construction method of a fruit fly intestinal cancer model, shRNA (short hairpin ribonucleic acid) sequences of fruit fly APC1 and APC2 genes are obtained through gene synthesis, a novel transgenic fruit fly intestinal cancer model is constructed by utilizing a microinjection technology and a fruit fly hybridization technology, materials and thoughts are provided for screening intestinal cancer related drugs more quickly and conveniently, and the novel construction method of the fruit fly intestinal cancer model has a wide application prospect. The novel fruit fly intestinal cancer model has the advantages of simple operation, stable inheritance, strong repeatability and the like, and the novel fruit fly intestinal cancer model can be used for researching mechanisms causing intestinal tumors, can also be used for screening drugs with pertinence for treating intestinal cancers, and has a good application prospect.

title: Chicken GM-CSF protein and monoclonal antibody, preparation method and application thereof
text: Chicken GM-CSF protein and monoclonal antibody, preparation method and application thereof. The invention relates to a chicken granulocyte-macrophage colony-stimulating factor (GM-CSF) protein and a monoclonal antibody, a preparation method and an application thereof, and belongs to the technical field of genetic engineering. The preparation method of the chicken GM-CSF protein includes the following steps: synthesizing gene sequences shown in SEQ ID NO. 1; preparing a prokaryotic expression vector pGEX-4T-1-GM-CSF; inducing expressions of the chicken GM CSF protein by the prokaryotic expression vector pGEX-4T-1-GM-CSF; and carrying out purification to obtain the chicken GM-CSF protein. The invention has the following advantages: gene synthesis does not need any templates due to chemical synthesis of GM-CSF gene, so that the invention is not limited by gene sources, more economical, simple and convenient and time-saving and labor-saving; a prokaryotic expression system is used as an expression vector, so that the invention has advantages of cost saving, high yield and large-scale preparation.

title: Anti-chicken TLR15 protein polyclonal antibody and preparation method thereof
text: Anti-chicken TLR15 protein polyclonal antibody and preparation method thereof. The invention provides an anti-chicken TLR15 protein polyclonal antibody and a preparation method thereof. The preparation method comprises the following steps: analyzing antigenicity of chicken TLR15 protein, screening a gene sequence corresponding to a peptide fragment for preparing the antibody, optimizing codons, synthesizing genes, and performing prokaryotic expression and immunization. Chicken Toll-like receptor 15 (chTLR15) is a specific cell surface pattern recognition receptor for poultry, the mediated congenital immune response plays an important role in resisting bacteria and virus invasion for poultry, but the ligand and the activation mechanism of the chicken Toll-like receptor 15 (chTLR15) are not clear due to the lack of commercial antibodies of the chicken Toll-like receptor 15 (chTLR15). According to the present invention, the recombinant protein His-chTLR15 is obtained by analyzing the antigenicity, the codon optimization, the gene synthesis and the prokaryotic expression of the chTLR15 protein, and the anti-chTLR15 protein polyclonal antibody with high specificity is prepared, such that the important support is provided for the research on the effect of the chTLR15 on the poultry immunity aspect.

title: Goat corynebacterium pseudotuberculosis PLD recombinant protein as well as preparation method and application thereof
text: Goat corynebacterium pseudotuberculosis PLD recombinant protein as well as preparation method and application thereof. The invention relates to goat corynebacterium pseudotuberculosis PLD recombinant protein as well as a preparation method and an application thereof, and belongs to the technical field of gene engineering. According to the recombinant protein provided by the invention, a sequence of a goat corynebacterium pseudotuberculosis phospholipase D (PLD) that signal peptide is removed is obtained by virtueof a whole-gene synthesis method; the sequence is sub-cloned into a prokaryotic expression vector pET28a; IPTG induction is conducted, so that soluble expression protein is obtained; and two times ofpurification are implemented, so that the expression protein that purity is above 95% is obtained. Based upon western-blot analysis, it is indicated that the protein has bio-activity; and through a series of optimization, a complete set of ELISA detection kits are assembled. Through sensitivity and specificity analysis as well as serological testing and immunological prevention monitoring on goat,testing and monitoring results show that the kits provided by the invention are relatively good in accuracy, sensitivity and repeatability; and the kits are applicable to diagnosis and immunologicalmonitoring of infection caused by goat corynebacterium pseudotuberculosis.

title: Engineering bacteria for production of trans-4-hydroxyproline and application thereof
text: Engineering bacteria for production of trans-4-hydroxyproline and application thereof. The invention relates to engineering bacteria for production of trans-4-hydroxyproline and an application thereof; the engineering bacteria are obtained through the steps of carrying out complete gene synthesis of an optimized proline 4-hydroxylase gene and a tryptophane series promoter, then connecting the obtained product to an expression vector inserted with a vitreoscilla hemoglobin gene, and then introducing the expression vector into escherichia coli; the optimized proline 4-hydroxylase gene includes that low-utilization-rate codes in the proline 4-hydroxylase gene are replaced with high-utilization-rate codes in the escherichia coli, and the CG% of the proline 4-hydroxylase gene is adjusted to be close to that of host bacteria; an enzyme digestion site of the proline 4-hydroxylase gene is removed; and a second-level structure of the proline 4-hydroxylase gene is adjusted to prevent a 5' end of mRNA of the proline 4-hydroxylase gene from forming a second-level structure. The engineering bacteria can be used for mass and efficient production of trans-4-hydroxyproline.

title: Method for realizing high-accuracy gene homologous cloning in saccharomycetes
text: Method for realizing high-accuracy gene homologous cloning in saccharomycetes. The invention provides a method for realizing high-accuracy gene homologous cloning in saccharomycetes, comprising the steps: obtaining the upstream sequence and downstream sequence of a target cloning gene by polymerase chain reaction or full gene synthesis, connecting the upstream sequence and downstream sequence and inserting into a carrier plasmid for constructing an annular cloning vector, performing linearization treatment on the annular cloning vector to obtain linear double-stranded DNA, and performing 5' ->3' exonuclease treatment on the linear double-stranded DNA to form linear 3' protruding long cohesive end double-stranded DNA and converting a saccharomycete cell. The method makes use of a special extraneous DNA structure type and realizes high-accuracy gene homologous cloning in saccharomycetes. The homologous gene cloning accuracy can reach 50-95%, and the method can greatly reduce the homologous gene cloning workload of saccharomycetes, and has great application potential in study on saccharomycete functional genes, gene engineering strain construction, and the like.

title: Construction method and application of hepatitis A virus infected animal model
text: Construction method and application of hepatitis A virus infected animal model. The invention discloses a construction method and application of a hepatitis A virus infected animal model. The invention belongs to the technical field of animal models, and particularly discloses a method for constructing a hepatitis A virus infected animal model, which comprises the following steps: encapsulating a hepatitis A virus complete genome RNA in LNP to obtain LNP encapsulated hepatitis A virus genome RNA, and inoculating the LNP encapsulated hepatitis A virus genome RNA into a non-human mammal to obtain the hepatitis A virus infected animal model. The LNP-encapsulated HAV genome RNA is obtained through gene synthesis, the LNP-encapsulated HAV genome RNA is used as an inoculation substance of an animal infection model, the LNP-encapsulated HAV genome RNA is convenient to obtain and good in uniformity and repeatability, the infected animal model can be directly established without separating and culturing the virus and the virus is amplified for the first time, and the virus genome RNA is easier to obtain and safer to operate compared with a virus strain.

title: Construction and application of zebra fish cardiovascular specific transgenic line for potassium ion monitoring
text: Construction and application of zebra fish cardiovascular specific transgenic line for potassium ion monitoring. The invention provides construction and application of a zebra fish cardiovascular specific transgenic line for potassium ion monitoring. The construction of the zebra fish cardiovascular specific transgenic line comprises the following steps: 1) constructing by using a gene synthesis and connection transformation method to obtain a recombinant plasmid Ptol2243250Cmlc-GEPII 1.0; (2) co-injecting a recombinant plasmid Ptol2243250Cmlc-GEPII 1.0 and a polyA tail added Tol2 transposase mRNA (messenger Ribonucleic Acid) into a fertilized egg of the zebra fish; and 3) culturing the fertilized eggs to obtain the zebra fish cardiovascular specific transgenic line for potassium ion monitoring. The zebra fish cardiovascular specific transgenic line constructed by the invention can monitor the concentration of potassium ions in the zebra fish cardiovascular system in real time, can be applied to screening drugs for regulating the concentration of potassium ions in cardiac muscle cells, and can also be applied to researching the functions of genes related to abnormal metabolism of potassium ions in cardiac muscle cells.

title: Mycoplasma pneumonia mosaic antigen, antigen detection reagent, and preparation method of both
text: Mycoplasma pneumonia mosaic antigen, antigen detection reagent, and preparation method of both. The invention provides a mycoplasma pneumonia mosaic antigen amino acid sequence containing an amino acid sequence as shown in SEQ ID NO:1 as well as a full-gene synthesized mycoplasma pneumonia mosaic antigen full-gene sequence containing an amino acid sequence as shown in SEQ ID NO:2. The invention also provides a method for constructing the two gene sequences. The invention also provides a preparation method of the mycoplasma pneumonia mosaic antigen containing full-gene synthesis, a mycoplasma pneumonia detection kit and a preparation method thereof. Mp recombinant mosaic antigen is selected as a mark material and is applied to a gold immunochromatography system, and the detection system is directly marked and captured, so the sensitivity is greatly improved, the specificity of the antigen is high, the antigen is easy in cultivation and purification, and cost is reduced; and a new method for detecting mycoplasma pneumoniae IgG rapidly and accurately is provided for clinical use, and a good market prospect is achieved.

title: VvMT1S and VvMT2S double genes for improving cadmium tolerance of plants and application of VvMT1S and VvMT2S double genes
text: VvMT1S and VvMT2S double genes for improving cadmium tolerance of plants and application of VvMT1S and VvMT2S double genes. The invention relates to VvMT1S and VvMT2S double genes for improving cadmium tolerance of plants and application of the VvMT1S and VvMT2S double genes, VvMT1S and VvMT2S genes derived from grapes are modified by a gene synthesis method, the VvMT1S gene with the nucleotide sequence shown as SEQ ID No 1 and the VvMT2S gene with the nucleotide sequence shown as SEQ ID No 3 are obtained, a recombinant expression vector is constructed, cadmium-tolerant genes VvMT1S and VvMT2S are successfully introduced into rice, and the cadmium tolerance of the plants is improved. The obtained transgenic rice plants have very strong cadmium resistance. After being treated by a 30mM cadmium solution, the transgenic line grows well, and the cadmium content in the transgenic line is only 17%-48% of that of a control line, which indicates that the VvMT1S and VvMT2S genes modified and synthesized by the invention have the capabilities of improving the cadmium resistance of plants and reducing enrichment, and can be used for cultivating cadmium-tolerant crops.

title: SYSTEMS AND METHODS FOR CONTROL OF GENE EXPRESSION
text: SYSTEMS AND METHODS FOR CONTROL OF GENE EXPRESSION. Embodiments disclosed herein provide artificial expression systems comprising multivalent transcription factor complexes for cooperative transcription factor assembly and modulating gene expression. More specifically, engineered synthetic transcription factors are recruited and structurally organized on synthetic gene circuits using molecular clamps, where the strength of intra-complex interactions can be modulated for fine tuning of gene expression as desired.

title: DNA segment for efficient expression of egg white lysozyme by employing recombinant pichia pastoris and expression method thereof
text: DNA segment for efficient expression of egg white lysozyme by employing recombinant pichia pastoris and expression method thereof. The invention belongs to the field of bio-genetic engineering and provides a DNA segment for efficient expression of egg white lysozyme by employing recombinant pichia pastoris and an expression method thereof. The method comprises the following steps: directly obtaining a gene of egg white lysozyme by gene synthesis means; carrying out operations such as digestion and connection, and then introducing the gene into an eukaryotic expression vector pPIC9K; transforming into pichia pastoris GS115, screening high-copy recons, and then realizing efficient expression of the recombinant egg white lysozyme in vitro through methanol induction. The method is not limited by a raw material source; the copy number of the lysozyme can be increased; the expression amount and the activity of the enzyme are improved; the later separation and purification technologies are simplified; large-scale industrialized production of the egg white lysozyme is realized; and the DNA segment has the characteristics of being stable, pure, and high in biological activity, and can be widely applied to many fields such as pharmacy and feeds.

title: SBT5 engineering bacterium for producing phospholipase D as well as construction method and application of SBT5 engineering bacterium
text: SBT5 engineering bacterium for producing phospholipase D as well as construction method and application of SBT5 engineering bacterium. The invention relates to the technical field of gene engineering, and provides an SBT5 engineering bacterium for producing phospholipase D. The invention also provides a construction method of the SBT5 engineering bacterium for producing phospholipase D. The method comprises the following steps: site-specific mutagenesis of phospholipase D genes, screening of mutants, glycosylation site analysis of phospholipase D gene mutants, site-specific optimization and whole-gene synthesis of glycosylation sites, acquisition of preliminary engineering bacteria, extraction of pMS82-PLDAnti plasmid, and construction of a recombinant streptomyces strain. Meanwhile, the invention also provides an application of the SBT5 engineering bacterium for producing phospholipase D in the development of an sdLDL-C detection kit. The engineering bacterium has the advantages that the constructed engineering bacterium can efficiently express PLD protein, the hydrolase activity reaches 130.5 U/mL, PLD in an existing sdLDL-C detection kit is replaced, and the production cost is reduced to the maximum extent.

title: Application of human ND1 gene recombinant vector in preparation of ND1 fusion protein kit
text: Application of human ND1 gene recombinant vector in preparation of ND1 fusion protein kit. The invention discloses application of a human ND1 gene recombinant vector in preparation of an ND1 fusion protein kit. An ND1 nucleotide sequence coded by a nuclear codon is synthesized by a whole-gene synthesis method, a human ND1 gene recombinant vector is constructed, and after the recombinant vector is introduced into a cell, the mitochondria-targeted ND1 fusion protein is synthesized and plays a role. The nucleotide sequence of the ND1 gene recombinant vector is as shown in SEQ ID NO: 7. The invention further discloses an amino acid sequence, a mitochondrial targeting sequence, a CMV promoter sequence and an expression vector sequence of the prepared ND1 fusion protein, a preparation method of the ND1 fusion protein and a preparation used by the preparation method. The human ND1 gene recombinant vector disclosed by the invention can be efficiently expressed in cells, and the prepared ND1 fusion protein can target mitochondria, exert normal ND1 protein functions and relieve m.3460Ggt; the influence of ND1 protein abnormity caused by A mutation on the cell function is avoided.

title: HER3 nano antibody composition for tumor targeted therapy and preparation method of HER3 nano antibody composition
text: HER3 nano antibody composition for tumor targeted therapy and preparation method of HER3 nano antibody composition. The invention relates to the technical field of biological medicines, and discloses an HER3 nano-antibody composition for tumor targeted therapy and a preparation method thereof, according to an HER3 nano-antibody sequence, a gene synthesis technology is adopted to synthesize an HER3 nano-antibody gene, and the synthesized gene is subjected to sequencing verification; cloning the HER3 nano antibody gene subjected to sequencing verification into an expression vector, and carrying out enzyme digestion and sequencing verification on the constructed expression vector; the preparation method comprises the following steps: converting an expression vector into an expression system, expressing under a certain condition, purifying an expression product to obtain a high-purity HER3 nano antibody, and mixing the high-purity HER3 nano antibody with pharmaceutic adjuvants to prepare the HER3 nano antibody composition. The invention has the advantages of specific binding of HER3, small molecular weight, good stability, low immunogenicity and the like, and has wide application prospects in tumor targeted therapy.

title: Fluorescence detection device based on binding induced DNA Assembly and method for detecting small molecule content thereof
text: Fluorescence detection device based on binding induced DNA Assembly and method for detecting small molecule content thereof. The present invention relates to a fluorescence detection device based on binding induced DNA Assembly and a method for detecting small molecules content. The fluorescence detection device based on binding induced DNA Assembly comprises an identification element and a signal element. The identification element comprises colloidal gold, colloidal gold surface-modified specific antibodies corresponding to the small molecules which are to be detected, and a DNA sequence, i.e., an auxiliary probe AO. The signal element comprises a DNA sequence. The DNA sequence is labeled with a fluorescent groupon one end and is linked with small molecule antigen to be detected on the other end. A DNA sequence C1* at the end labeled with the fluorescent group is complementary to a DNA sequence C1 at a free end of the auxiliary probe AO. The middle of the signal element can be freely bent. The method of the invention is homogeneous detection, simple and convenient, and can be applied to detections of different small molecules by using different antibodies.

title: Synthetic post of small -size DNA
text: Synthetic post of small -size DNA. The utility model discloses a synthetic post of small -size DNA, including the synthetic hollow plate of CPG frits, void column pipe or 96 holes, diameter <= 1.5mm of CPG frits, thickness <= 1.5mm of CPG frits, the intraductal assembly position that is provided with of void column, the internal diameter <= 1.5mm of assembly position, the height <= 5mm of assembly position, the assembly position be the cylinder of standard, and assembly position internal diameter gradient in addition reduces. The utility model discloses a synthetic post of small -size DNA is suitable for micro - gene synthesis, can the DNA resultant quantity be hanged down to 0.1 does 10nmol have advantages such as saving time, economize reagent, quality are reliable, environmental protection, and synthetic chain reach 120 200bp, the aggregate velocity is fast, and high efficiency, mutation rate can be hanged down to 1 per mill, use technology simple and convenient, and the industrialization degree is high.

title: Recombinant human-derived collagen as well as preparation method and application thereof
text: Recombinant human-derived collagen as well as preparation method and application thereof. The invention discloses recombinant human-derived collagen as well as a preparation method and application thereof, and belongs to the field of bioengineering. The preparation method comprises the following steps: (1) obtaining a recombinant vector, specifically, selecting a human type-III collagen conserved region peptide fragment, repeating the sequence for 8 times to carry out artificial whole-gene synthesis of RHIIIC8, and constructing the RHIIIC8 into an expression vector of pET22b to obtain the recombinant vector; (2) constructing recombinant genetic engineering bacteria, specifically, extracting plasmids, transforming the plasmids into BL21-DE3-PLySs competent cells, picking monoclonal antibodies, and carrying out enlarged culture on the monoclonal antibodies to obtain the recombinant genetic engineering bacteria; and (3) preparing the recombinant human-derived collagen, specifically, carrying out induced expression on target protein, and then separating and purifying the expression product to obtain the high-purity water-soluble recombinant human-derived collagen. The recombinant human-derived collagen provided by the invention has the characteristic of high activity, and can be widely applied to the industries of biomedicine and cosmetics.

title: METHOD OF CREATING INDUSTRIAL STREPTOMYCES WITH CAPABILITY TO GROW ON CELLULOSIC POLYSACCHARIDE SUBSTRATES
text: METHOD OF CREATING INDUSTRIAL STREPTOMYCES WITH CAPABILITY TO GROW ON CELLULOSIC POLYSACCHARIDE SUBSTRATES. A heterologous gene cassette useful for creating Streptomyces species with enhanced capability of growing on a cellulosic polysaccharide substrate, wherein the cassette comprises at least two members of the following categories: a) a GH6 gene, b) an AA10 gene, c) a GH48 gene, d) a GH5 gene and e) either (i) a GH9 gene, (ii) a GH9 gene and a GH12 gene, or (iii) a GH12 gene is disclosed.

title: METHOD FOR PRODUCING DNA ENCODING POLYPEPTIDES THAT ARE COMPOSED OF SEVERAL SECTIONS, AND FOR PRODUCING POLYPEPTIDES BY EXPRESSING THE DNA THUS OBTAINED
text: METHOD FOR PRODUCING DNA ENCODING POLYPEPTIDES THAT ARE COMPOSED OF SEVERAL SECTIONS, AND FOR PRODUCING POLYPEPTIDES BY EXPRESSING THE DNA THUS OBTAINED. The invention relates to a method for producing novel modular enzyme systems by genetic engineering, which is characterized by a cyclic in vitro gene synthesis and which allows a specific recombination of the individual, gene-encoded modular components to novel enzyme systems. Examples of such modular enzyme systems are the non-ribosomal peptide synthetases (NRPS) or polyketide synthases (PKS) of type 1, that is the amino acid sequence of such an enzyme is characterized by being composed of a repetitive sequence of identical sequence sections or sequence sections that are very similar to one another. Every single repetitive sequence is referred to as a module and every module allows the enzymatic incorporation of a specific substrate into the substance synthesized by the enzyme. The products synthesized by the NRPS and PKS enzymes are often highly valuable as pharmaceuticals, such as the penicillins, vancomycins or erythromycins. The inventive method allows for an effective production of novel genes for modular enzymes, and the gene expression of said novel genes allows for the production of novel substances.

title: Nucleic acid and gene synthesis
text: Nucleic acid and gene synthesis. The present invention relates to a method of synthesizing a nucleic acid, comprising providing at least one overlapping primer oligonucleotide comprising a first and second single-stranded oligonucleotide, each comprising a first, second, third and fourth sequence, with the first and third sequences being self-complementary palindromic sequences, wherein the third sequence of the first oligonucleotide hybridises to the third sequence of the second oligonucleotide to provide an overhanging region and enzymatically extending both oligonucleotides to provide a duplex. The fourth sequence is complementary to the second sequence. The method further comprises denaturing the duplex, annealing the first and second single stranded polynucleotides to generate a 5’ overhang and extending, repeating these steps to increase the length of the nucleic acid sequence. Preferably the oligonucleotide can be either DNA or RNA and is immobilised on a surface. The first and third sequences are at least 6 bases in length, comprise at least one functional site and comprise tandem repeats. The second sequence comprises at least one modification, such as modified nucleotides, artificial base or loop structure.

title: Application of tomato SlHMGA3 gene in cultivation of tomatoes with delayed fruit maturity
text: Application of tomato SlHMGA3 gene in cultivation of tomatoes with delayed fruit maturity. The invention discloses application of a tomato S1HMGA3 gene in cultivation of tomatoes with delayed fruit maturity, and belongs to the technical field of tomato genetic engineering.The application method comprises the following steps: constructing a CRISPR/Cas9 expression vector of the tomato S1HMGA3 gene; designing two target sequences of an SlHMGA3 gene, performing gene synthesis on a promoter and an sgRNA sequence containing two targets, and inserting the promoter and the sgRNA sequence between two restriction enzyme cutting sites of a vector to obtain a SlHMGA3 gene mutation vector; and transferring the constructed vector with correct sequencing into host cells, infecting a cotyledon explant of tomato by using the vector, and screening a tomato plant with SlHMGA3 gene mutation but not containing a gene knockout vector sequence from positive transgenic tomato progeny, so as to obtain a tomato plant with delayed fruit maturity. In order to conveniently identify and screen tomato plants, the used vector can be processed, for example, a plant selective marker or a resistant antibiotic marker and the like are added.

title: ISOLATION OF DNA FRAGMENTS
text: ISOLATION OF DNA FRAGMENTS. The present invention allows for the isolation of sequence correct DNA fragments from heterogeneous mixtures of fragments through the implementation of several steps for the purification of DNA molecules, the amplification of said DNA molecules, identification of sequence correct DNA fragments, and isolation of fragments as templates for PCR-based production of further downstream fragments.

title: Production method of recombinant IL2 in pichia pastoris
text: Production method of recombinant IL2 in pichia pastoris. The invention discloses a production method of recombinant IL2 in pichia pastoris. The production method comprises the following steps: S1, constructing recombinant plasmids; S2, preparing competent cells; S3, linearizing the plasmids; S4, carrying out electric conversion; S5, carrying out PCR identification on bacterial colonies of positive clones and carrying out small-amount transfer of clones;S6, carrying out positive clone induction and a small-amount expression test; and S7, amplifying and purifying; codon optimization is carried out on genes of IL2 in the gene synthesis process, optimal clone screening and optimal expression time optimization are carried out on later expression lab tests, and therefore the expression quantity of the protein is greatly increased, a seamless cloningtechnology is used in vector construction, recombinant IL2 is made to express tag free protein in a pPICZ alpha A vector, hydrophobic interaction chromatography is used in the later period, the purification process is shortened, and low-temperature operation is performed, so that the protein activity is greatly guaranteed.

title: Method for preparing ssDNA by using biotin-streptavidin system
text: Method for preparing ssDNA by using biotin-streptavidin system. The invention discloses a method for preparing ssDNA by using a biotin-streptavidin system, and relates to the technical field of molecular biology and nucleic acid, and the method comprises the following steps: S01, constructing a target ssDNA sequence into a pUC57 vector through gene synthesis to obtain a circular DNA plasmid containing the ssDNA sequence; s02, by taking the circular DNA plasmid as a template, carrying out PCR (Polymerase Chain Reaction) amplification by adopting a tail primer containing a biotin label at the 5'end and an unlabeled first primer, so as to obtain dsDNA containing the biotin label; and S03, combining the amplification product of the dsDNA containing the biotin label with streptavidin magnetic beads, selectively separating a DNA chain containing the biotin label, and releasing the unlabeled ssDNA after alkali treatment to obtain a high-purity ssDNA product. According to the method, the biotin-streptavidin magnetic beads are specifically combined with the biotin labeled chain, and the non-labeled chain is dissociated through NaOH treatment, so that the purity of the prepared ssDNA is maximized.

title: PK-15 cell line capable of stably expressing CD63-GFP and construction and application thereof
text: PK-15 cell line capable of stably expressing CD63-GFP and construction and application thereof. The invention belongs to the technical field of biology, and particularly relates to a PK-15 cell line capable of stably expressing CD63-GFP and construction and application thereof, wherein the preservation number of the cell line is CCTCC NO: C2018253. The construction method comprises the following steps: target gene synthesis, target gene and lentivirus carrier double enzyme digestion, recombinant plasmid construction and identification, transfection of 293T cells, infection of PK-15 cells, and cell line screening verification. The PK-15 cell strain capable of stably expressing CD63-GFP can be used for evaluating a content of exudates inside and outside cells. The PK-15 cell line capable of stably expressing CD63-GFP fusion protein can be used for observing green fluorescent protein byfluorescence or conveniently evaluating the content of exudates by a flow cytometry. The establishment of the cell line provides a reliable material for researching the application of the exudates invirus infection of PK-15 cells.

title: A INHIBITING AGENT FOR INHIBITION OF ANGIOGENESIS, A METHOD FOR PREPARING THE AGENT, A METHOD FOR MODIFYING THE AGENT AND ITS USE FOR MANUFACTURING A MEDICAMENT FOR TREATING TUMOR
text: A INHIBITING AGENT FOR INHIBITION OF ANGIOGENESIS, A METHOD FOR PREPARING THE AGENT, A METHOD FOR MODIFYING THE AGENT AND ITS USE FOR MANUFACTURING A MEDICAMENT FOR TREATING TUMOR. The present invention relates to a highly efficient antiangiogenesis agent, which is a polypeptide for inhibition of angiogenesis Ile-Val-Arg-Arg-Ala-Asp-Arg-Ala-Ala-Val-Pro, connected with a polypeptide containing Arg-Gly-Asp on its one end or two ends. The inhibiting agent can be synthesized or gene engineered. The invention also relates to a physiochemical method for modifying the antiangiogenesis agent. Polypeptides with weight percentage of 1-70% preferably about 20-50% are mixed with 20%-95% polyethylene glycol, or heparin, or dextran, or polyvinylpyrrolidone, or polyethylene glycol - poly-amino acid copolymer, or palmitic acid or poly-sialic acid or liposomes solutions; preferably about 50-93% of the above modified substances are fully mixed even and shaken at a shaker at 4°C -40°C, preferably 25°C -37°C for more than 10 min, and the modified substances are separated through appropriate methods. The invention still relates to the use of the above polypeptides and the polypeptide modified substances for manufacturing medicaments for treating human solid tumors.

title: Novel coordination peptide QEY127 and application of novel coordination peptide QEY127 and rare earth ions assembled into luminescent material
text: Novel coordination peptide QEY127 and application of novel coordination peptide QEY127 and rare earth ions assembled into luminescent material. The invention discloses a novel coordination peptide QEY127 and application of the novel coordination peptide QEY127 to assembling of a luminescent material with rare earth ions. The coordination peptide QEY127 is composed of one initiation codon coded methionine (M), one 6-poly-histidine tag (6X His) and ten QSEPGDPGEPSY (QEY12), and the length of the coordination peptide QEY127 is 127 amino acid residues; the coordination peptide QEY127 gene is obtained through a whole-gene synthesis method, and codon constitution is optimized. The yield of the coordination peptide QEY127 is 100-1200mg/L fermentation liquor, and the purity of the coordination peptide QEY127 is 82-95%. The coordination peptide QEY127 disclosed by the invention has the characteristics of catalytic crosslinking of glutamine transaminase (TG enzyme), high elasticity and formation of a luminous coordination compound with rare earth metal, and has a wide application prospect in the fields of biomedicine and luminous materials.

title: MICROBIAL STEM CELL TECHNOLOGY
text: MICROBIAL STEM CELL TECHNOLOGY. The present disclosure relates to microbial stem cell technology that enables a growing microbial culture to stably maintain two or more distinct cell types in a ratio that can be genetically programmed and/or dynamically controlled during cultivation. It is contemplated that embodiments described herein can be utilized to increase product yield in microbial fermentations and advanced engineering of biomaterials using genetically engineered microbial cells, among others.

title: Pestalotiopsis fungus chitin deacetylase and preparation method and application thereof
text: Pestalotiopsis fungus chitin deacetylase and preparation method and application thereof. The invention discloses a pestalotiopsis fungus chitin deacetylase and a preparation method and application thereof. According to codon preference of pichia pastoris, an optimized nucleic acid sequence is shown as SEQ ID NO.2, and a chitin deacetylase encoding gene sequence of the pestalotiopsis fungus is obtained by using a whole-gene synthesis method. Furthermore, a pichia pastoris expression system is used for carrying out secretory expression on the optimized chitin deacetylase encoding gene to obtain chitin deacetylase, and the amino acid sequence of the chitin deacetylase is shown as SEQID NO.1. The chitin deacetylase obtained by the invention can be used for removing acetyl in chitin, chitosan and chitosan oligosaccharide, so that chitosan or chitosan oligosaccharide with a specific structure is obtained. These chitosan or chitosan oligosaccharides of specific structures may have a new or higher biological activity. Therefore, the pestalotiopsis fungus chitin deacetylase and the deacetylation product thereof have good industrial application prospects.

title: Preparation and application of insulin-like growth factor IGF3 recombinant protein of cyprinus carpio
text: Preparation and application of insulin-like growth factor IGF3 recombinant protein of cyprinus carpio. The invention relates to preparation and application of insulin-like growth factor IGF3 recombinant protein of cyprinus carpio and belongs to the molecular biology category in the technical field of biology. An amino acid sequence of IGF3 protein of FFRC strain common carp is optimized, IGF3 is inserted into an expression vector pET30a+ through full gene synthesis and a restriction enzyme cuttingsite Nde-Hind III, the accuracy of the final expression vector is confirmed by an enzyme cutting method and sequencing, the final expression vector is finally transformed to expression strains separately, the IGF3 protein is expressed through IPTG induction, and then, the IGF3 protein is purified through affinity chromatography Ni-IDA resin. Through preliminary verification, the obtained recombinant protein can significantly influence gonad indexes of female fishes, can influence the expression level of an igf3 gene in the hypothalamus-pituitary-gonadal axis of the FFRC strain common carp, andcan be applied to the genetic breeding process of aquatic animals such as fishes.

title: Preparation method of streptolysin O protein with double-high characteristic
text: Preparation method of streptolysin O protein with double-high characteristic. The invention relates to the technical field of biology, in particular to a preparation method of streptolysin O protein with double-high characteristics, which comprises the following steps: 1) gene synthesis; 2) carrier construction; the preparation method comprises the following steps: constructing a carrier by using tandem repeat SLO protein 77AA-571AA; the SLO protein 77AA-571AA represents the 77th amino acid to the 571th amino acid in the full-length sequence of the SLO protein, and the sequence is as shown in SEQ ID NO: 1; 3) strain transformation; and 4) purifying the streptolysin O protein. The streptolysin O protein SLO obtained by the method disclosed by the invention is simple, easy to purify, high in yield, small in batch difference, good in protein stability and high in reactivity; moreover, the tandem streptolysin O protein SLO with double high characteristics, which is separated and purified by the method disclosed by the invention, can be used for preparing quality control products and calibration products, and is beneficial to result comparison and quality control of various manufacturers and laboratories.

title: Novel beta-lactam antibiotic synthetase production method
text: Novel beta-lactam antibiotic synthetase production method. The present invention discloses a method for producing novel lactam antibiotics by using specific engineered bacteria transformant BL21(DE3)/PET28-ASPGA. According to the method, a penicillin acylase amino acid sequence represented by SEQIDNO:1 is adopted as basis, a mutation site is introduced, reverse design is performed to obtain a nucleotide sequence, optimization is performed, complete gene synthesis and recombinant vector construction are sequentially performed to construct a recombinant strain, and the constructed recombinant strain is treated through steps of fermentation, cell disruption, separation, purification, decoloration and immobilization to finally obtain the novel lactam antibiotic immobilized enzyme finished product capable of being directly used for industrial production. According to the present invention, the immobilized enzyme prepared by using the method has characteristics of high enzyme activity, good stability and excellent repeated use effect, and can be provided for catalyzing synthesis of a variety of lactam antibiotics such as amoxicillin, cephalexin and cefaclor.

title: Application of tomato SlULT1 gene in improvement of drought resistance of tomato
text: Application of tomato SlULT1 gene in improvement of drought resistance of tomato. The invention discloses application of a tomato SlULT1 gene in improvement of drought resistance of tomatoes, and relates to the technical field of tomato genetic engineering.The application method comprises the steps that two target sequences of the SlULT1 gene are designed firstly, then a promoter and an sgRNA sequence containing two target spots are subjected to gene synthesis and then inserted between two restriction enzyme cutting sites of a CRISPR-Cas9 gene editing carrier, and the promoter SlULT1 gene is obtained; the SlULT1 gene editing vector is obtained. Transferring the constructed vector with correct sequencing into a host cell, infecting a cotyledon explant of tomato by using the vector, and screening a tomato plant with SlULT1 gene mutation but not containing a gene editing vector sequence from positive transgenic tomato progeny, so as to obtain a tomato plant with enhanced drought resistance. In order to conveniently identify and screen tomato plants, the used carrier can be processed, for example, a plant selective marker or a resistant antibiotic marker and the like are added.

title: Nucleic acid and gene synthesis
text: Nucleic acid and gene synthesis. The present invention relates to a method of synthesizing a nucleic acid, comprising providing at least one overlapping primer oligonucleotide comprising a first and second single-stranded oligonucleotide, each comprising a first, second and third sequence, with the first and third sequences being self-complementary palindromic sequences, wherein the third sequence of the first oligonucleotide hybridises to the third sequence of the second oligonucleotide to provide an overhanging region and enzymatically extending both oligonucleotides to provide a duplex. Preferably the extension incorporates a fourth sequence, which is complementary to a second sequence. The method further comprises denaturing the duplex, annealing the first and second single stranded polynucleotides to generate a 5’ overhang and extending, repeating these steps to increase the length of the nucleic acid sequence. Preferably the oligonucleotide can be either DNA or RNA and is immobilised on a surface. Also disclosed is the overlapping primer oligonucleotide, wherein the first and third sequences are at least 6 bases in length, comprise at least one functional site and comprise tandem repeats.

title: Cryptococcus neoformans chitin deacetylase and preparation method and application thereof
text: Cryptococcus neoformans chitin deacetylase and preparation method and application thereof. The invention discloses cryptococcus neoformans chitin deacetylase and a preparation method and application thereof. According to codon preference of pichia pastoris, an optimized nucleic acid sequence is shown as SEQ ID NO.2, and a chitin deacetylase encoding gene sequence of cryptococcus neoformans is obtained by using a whole-gene synthesis method. Furthermore, a pichia pastoris expression system is used for carrying out secretory expression on the optimized chitin deacetylase encoding gene to obtain chitin deacetylase, and the amino acid sequence of the chitin deacetylase is shown as SEQ ID NO.1. The invention also discloses the preparation method of the chitin deacetylase. The chitin deacetylase obtained by the invention can be used for removing acetyl in chitin, chitosan and chitosanoligosaccharide, so that chitosan or chitosan oligosaccharide with a specific structure is obtained. These chitosan or chitosan oligosaccharides of specific structures may have a new or higher biological activity. Therefore, the cryptococcus neoformans chitin deacetylase and the deacetylation product thereof have good industrial application prospects.

title: Gene of coded recombined helicobacter pylori serine protease, egg yolk antibody and application
text: Gene of coded recombined helicobacter pylori serine protease, egg yolk antibody and application. The invention belongs to the technical field of biology, and specifically relates to a gene of coded recombined helicobacter pylori serine protease, an egg yolk antibody and application. The nucleotide sequence of the gene of the coded recombined helicobacter pylori serine protease is shown in SEQ ID NO.1. The invention further provides an egg yolk antibody inhibiting helicobacter pylori serine protease. The antibody is prepared through the following steps of performing gene synthesis to obtain the gene of the coded recombined serine protease; performing prokaryotic expression on the gene, purifying, acquiring recombined serine protease, using the recombined serine protease as an immunogen to immunize hens, collecting eggs laid by immunized hens, extracting the egg yolk antibody in the egg, purifying, and obtaining the egg yolk antibody inhibiting helicobacter pylori serine protease. The egg yolk antibody can be used as a food additive or added into healthcare products, or can be prepared into drugs preventing or treating gastric ulcer and gastric cancer.

title: Production method of recombinant hEGF in pichia pastoris
text: Production method of recombinant hEGF in pichia pastoris. The invention discloses a production method of recombinant hEGF in pichia pastoris. The production method comprises the following steps: S1, constructing recombinant plasmids; S2, preparing competentcells; S3, linearizing the plasmids; S4, carrying out electric conversion; S5, carrying out PCR identification on bacterial colonies of positive clones and carrying out small-amount transfer of clones; S6, performing positive clone induction and a small-amount expression test; and S7, amplifying and purifying; codon optimization is carried out on the gene of hEGF in the gene synthesis process, andoptimal clone screening and optimal expression time optimization are carried out on later expression lab tests, so that the expression quantity of the protein is greatly increased, the protein purification time is shortened to 2h, and the protein activity is greatly guaranteed; the recombinant hEGF plasmid is transformed into a pichia pastoris GS115 host cell, methanol induction is utilized, andfinally the high-purity target protein is obtained through ion cross-linking chromatography.

title: BIO-ENZYME FOR CATALYZING SYNTHESIS OF PHOSPHATIDYLSERINE, PREPARATION METHOD THEREFOR AND APPLICATION THEREOF
text: BIO-ENZYME FOR CATALYZING SYNTHESIS OF PHOSPHATIDYLSERINE, PREPARATION METHOD THEREFOR AND APPLICATION THEREOF. A bio-enzyme for catalyzing synthesis of phosphatidylserine, a preparation method therefor and application thereof. The bio-enzyme is an amino acid sequence represented by SEQ ID NO: 2. The preparation of the bio-enzyme comprises: first constructing a genetic engineering strain of a bio-enzyme; by using bio-enzyme gene fragments synthesized by whole gene synthesis, linearizing recombinant plasmids with restriction enzyme Sal I, for expressing the bio-enzyme; and performing further fermentation to obtain the bio-enzyme for catalyzing synthesis of phosphatidylserine. By utilizing high specificity of phosphatidylserine in synthesis of enzyme to catalyze the preparation of phosphatidylserine from lecithin and serine, the conversion rate reaches 80%, and thus the problem of being unable to obtain high-purity phosphatidylserine by using the existing extraction method and chemical synthesis method is effectively resolved. Moreover, the present invention is stable and reliable in process, high in efficiency and suitable for industrial production, and has great application potential in the fields of medicines and food healthcare products.

title: RNA-GUIDED ENDONUCLEASE-BASED DNA ASSEMBLY
text: RNA-GUIDED ENDONUCLEASE-BASED DNA ASSEMBLY. The invention provides a novel approach to facilitate assembly of DNA molecules. This approach utilizes RNA-guided endonucleases, capable of targeting any DNA sequence, which cleave DNA and generate DNA fragments characterized by single stranded overhangs. After annealing of complementary overhangs, DNA fragments are covalently connected, generating a single DNA molecule. In this way, the present invention combines the reliability of classic restriction-ligation techniques, removes all sequence constraints from the desired final DNA molecule, and expands the number of DNA pieces that can be assembled at once.

title: Auxin amide hydrolase RhILL1 gene and application of auxin amide hydrolase RhILL1 gene in regulation and control of China rose flower color
text: Auxin amide hydrolase RhILL1 gene and application of auxin amide hydrolase RhILL1 gene in regulation and control of China rose flower color. The invention relates to the technical field of molecular breeding, and discloses an auxin amide hydrolase RhILL1 gene and application of the auxin amide hydrolase RhILL1 gene in regulation and control of China rose flower color, and the nucleotide sequence of the auxin amide hydrolase RhILL1 gene is shown as SEQ ID NO.1. By silence or overexpression of the RhILL1 gene, synthesis of anthocyanin is effectively regulated and controlled, and accurate control of petal color is realized. Researches find that silencing of the RhILL1 gene leads to color fading of petals and reduction of anthocyanin content; and overexpression of the RhILL1 gene prolongs the color holding time of the petals. In addition, the invention also provides a kit containing the RhILL1 gene regulatory sequence, a recombinant plasmid and a corresponding gene editing and transgenic breeding method. The achievements provide a new molecular tool for Chinese rose breeding and flower color improvement, and have important ornamental value and economic significance.

title: Method for producing cis-4-hydroxy-L-proline through recombinant escherichia coli
text: Method for producing cis-4-hydroxy-L-proline through recombinant escherichia coli. The invention belongs to the field of genetic engineering and discloses a method for producing cis-4-hydroxy-L-proline through recombinant escherichia coli. The recombinant escherichia coli is constructed by a method comprising the following steps: optimizing the known gene sequence of L-proline-cis-4-hydroxylase; performing total gene synthesis of the optimized L-proline-cis-4-hydroxylase gene; connecting the gene to a carrier containing a proper promoter to construct a recombinant plasmid which can over-express L-proline-cis-4-hydroxylase; then introducing the recombinant plasmid into escherichia coli to obtain recombinant escherichia coli which can convert free L-proline into cis-4-hydroxy-L-proline. The invention further discloses application of the recombinant escherichia coli in production of cis-4-hydroxy-L-proline. The shake-flask fermentation result shows that the output of cis-4-hydroxy-L-proline produced by the recombinant escherichia coli is up to 47.33mg/L.

title: H13N8 recombinant influenza virus and application
text: H13N8 recombinant influenza virus and application. The invention belongs to the technical field of influenza vaccine, and particularly relates to a H13N8 recombinant influenza virus and application. The H13N8 recombinant influenza virus is constructed, PB2, PB1, PA, NP, M and NS genes of a PR8 virus are obtained through gene synthesis, and the synthesized genes are inserted into a pFluDD bidirectional expression vector by means of homologous recombination. Six PR8 virus internal gene infectious cloning plasmids and two H13N8 subtype influenza virus antigen gene infectious clones are co-transfected into 293T cells in proportion, a transfected cell suspension is inoculated into SPF chicken embryos, hemagglutination-positive chicken embryo allantoic fluid is harvested, and the H13N8 recombinant influenza virus is obtained. A H13N8 recombinantinfluenza virus inactivated vaccine is injected subcutaneously in the necks of 3-week-old SPF chickens for immunization, and at the 4<th> week after immunization, the hemagglutination inhibition titer against the H13N8 subtype influenza virus in the serum of chickens in an immunization group reaches 160-640.

title: Magnetic aperture-controllable nano-porous glass, preparation method and application
text: Magnetic aperture-controllable nano-porous glass, preparation method and application. The invention belongs to the technical field of material preparation and processing, and particularly relates to magnetic aperture-controllable nano-porous glass as well as a preparation method and application thereof. The invention provides nano-porous glass with a controllable magnetic aperture, a preparation method and application. The nano-porous glass with the controllable magnetic aperture is mainly prepared from silicon dioxide, boric acid, sodium carbonate, ferric oxide and zirconium oxide. The raw materials comprise, by mass, 55%-65% of silicon dioxide, 20%-30% of boric acid, 6%-7% of sodium carbonate, 5%-10% of ferric oxide and 2%-5% of zirconium oxide. According to the magnetic aperture-controllable nano-porous glass, the preparation method and the application provided by the invention, the magnetic aperture-controllable nano-porous glass prepared at the split-phase temperature of 550-650 DEG C has excellent corrosion resistance and mechanical strength, the aperture size and aperture distribution are uniform, and the porosity is relatively high; meanwhile, stable superparamagnetism is achieved, monodispersity is kept without an external magnetic field, and the performance requirement of a gene synthesis carrier material can be met.

title: Zinc-cadmium-resistant gene TmZRC1S derived from tuber melanosporum and application thereof
text: Zinc-cadmium-resistant gene TmZRC1S derived from tuber melanosporum and application thereof. The invention relates to a zinc-cadmium-resistant gene TmZRC1S derived from tuber melanosporum and application thereof, a predicted zinc transporter gene sequence GSTUMT00007113001 derived from tuber melanosporum is modified according to plant codon preference, gene artificial synthesis is carried out by adopting a gene synthesis method to obtain a TmZRC1S gene with a nucleotide sequence as shown in SEQ ID No.1, a recombinant expression vector is constructed and transferred into arabidopsis thaliana, and the transgenic arabidopsis thaliana plant with relatively strong zinc and cadmium resistance and zinc and cadmium enrichment capacity is obtained. After treatment with zinc and cadmium solutions, the growth of a transgenic line is better than that of a wild line, the zinc and cadmium content in the transgenic line is remarkably higher than that of the wild line, particularly, the zinc content of the overground part is 3.74 times that of the wild line, and the cadmium content is 3.36 times that of the wild line, so that the TmZRC1S gene synthesized by transformation has the capabilities of improving the resistance of plants and enriching zinc and cadmium, and can be used for cultivating plants rich in zinc and cadmium.

title: Hybridized tRNA (transfer ribonucleic acid) and application thereof to glutathione peroxidase preparation
text: Hybridized tRNA (transfer ribonucleic acid) and application thereof to glutathione peroxidase preparation. The invention discloses hybridized tRNA (transfer ribonucleic acid) and application thereof to glutathione peroxidase preparation and belongs to the field of biotechnology. The hybridized tRNA relates to sequences SEQ ID No: 1-16, consists of 90 basic groups, and not only can serve as substrate tRNA for selenocysteine synthesis, but also can be recognized by autologous elongation factor EF-Tu of escherichia coli. A hybridized tRNA gene is obtained by a gene synthesis method, then a GPX (glutathione peroxidase) gene is synthetized or amplified, the hybridized tRNA gene and the GPX gene are assembled on a secretory prokaryotic expression vector capable of expressing the hybridized tRNA, a TAG engineering strain is transformed and subjected to induced expression in presence of sodium selenite, and a catalytic group Sec of the GPX is introduced into a substrate binding site of protein in a way that conventional amino acids enter a peptide chain, so that the protein is given with high GPX activity. The hybridized tRNA and application thereof to glutathione peroxidase preparation have the advantages that the method is simple, and zymoprotein is high in activity, yield and stability, so that the problems that natural GPX is limited in source and instable in property are solved.

title: Streptomyces thermoviolaceus chitinase, as well as preparation method and application of streptomyces thermoviolaceus chitinase
text: Streptomyces thermoviolaceus chitinase, as well as preparation method and application of streptomyces thermoviolaceus chitinase. The invention discloses streptomyces thermoviolaceus chitinase (GH19 family), as well as a preparation method and application of streptomyces thermoviolaceus chitinase. According to the method, a chitinase gene sequence in streptomyces thermoviolaceus can be obtained by utilizing a gene synthesis method according to the codon preference of pichia pastoris, and the optimized nucleotide sequence isas shown in SEQ ID NO.2. The optimized chitinase coding gene is efficiently secreted and expressed by further utilizing a pichia pastoris expression system to obtain the streptomyces thermoviolaceus chitinase (GH19 family) having an amino acid sequence as shown in SEQ ID NO.1. The streptomyces thermoviolaceus chitinase (GH19 family) has relatively high hydrolytic activity for chitosan primer withlow degree of deacetylation, 1mL of crude enzyme (the content of protein is about 0.30mg) generated by fermentation of a shake flask has the hydrolysis ability for degrading 0.5g of chitosan, and theefficiency is improved by over 300 times theoretically since about 100mg of commercial cellulose is required for degrading the same amount of chitosan. The streptomyces thermoviolaceus chitinase has excellent industrial application prospect.

title: Codon-optimized glycosidase SWMU-F2-2 and preparation method and application thereof
text: Codon-optimized glycosidase SWMU-F2-2 and preparation method and application thereof. The invention discloses codon-optimized glycosidase SWMU-F2-2 and a preparation method and application thereof. The preparation method comprises the following steps of: performing whole-gene synthesis on a codon-optimized glycosidase gene SWMU-F2-2 to obtain a full-length fragment of the glycosidase gene SWMU-F2-2; preparing a recombinant plasmid: connecting the full-length fragment of the glycosidase gene SWMU-F2-2 with a vector pET-28a (+) to obtain a recombinant plasmid pET-28a (+)/SWMU-F2-2; preparing the glycosidase SWMU-F2-2: transforming the recombinant plasmid pET-28a (+)/SWMU-F2-2 into an E.coli BL21 competent cell to obtain a recombinant E.coli BL21 cell, then adding isopropyl thiogalactoside IPTG for inducible expression to obtain a bacterial solution, centrifuging, and taking supernatant for further purification to obtain an enzyme solution of the glycosidase SWMU-F2-2, wherein the DNA sequence of the glycosidase SWMU-F2-2 is shown as SEQ ID NO. 1, and the amino acid sequence is shown as SEQ ID NO. 2.

title: High-amplification-activity mutant Pfu DNA polymerase
text: High-amplification-activity mutant Pfu DNA polymerase. The invention discloses a mutant Pfu DNA polymerase with high amplification activity, and belongs to the technical field of gene engineering. Mutation sites are screened out from an amino acid sequence as shown in SEQ ID NO.1, mutation, reverse translation and gene synthesis are performed to obtain a template, then PCR amplification is performed, seamless cloning is performed on a PCR product and a linearized vector, plasmid extraction is performed, and the mutant Pfu DNA polymerase with high amplification activity is obtained. The method comprises the following steps: constructing an expression vector of the mutant Pfu DNA polymerase, transforming the expression vector into competent cells, performing enlarged culture, collecting thalli, performing low-temperature crushing, performing centrifugation, collecting supernate, performing magnetic bead purification, performing elution, and collecting purified protein, thereby obtaining the mutant Pfu DNA polymerase. The mutant Pfu DNA polymerase has reverse transcription activity, transcription and amplification can be simultaneously carried out in the PCR process, an additional reverse transcription step can be omitted, the mutant Pfu DNA polymerase has high amplification activity, and the amplification efficiency can be improved.

title: CHIMERIC ANTIGEN RECEPTOR CELL LIBRARY CARRYING GENE ELEMENT COMBINATION, PREPRATION AND SCREENING METHOD, AND USE THEREOF
text: CHIMERIC ANTIGEN RECEPTOR CELL LIBRARY CARRYING GENE ELEMENT COMBINATION, PREPRATION AND SCREENING METHOD, AND USE THEREOF. A chimeric antigen receptor (CAR) cell library is established through the fusion of a cell and a vector assembly. The vector assembly carries three genetic elements corresponding to a plurality of first genetic elements encoding one or more idiotype CARs, a second genetic element carrying one or more genetic circuits, and a third genetic element encoding one or more inducible proteins, respectively. The one or more genetic circuits are pre-programmed and are each a combination of a cis-regulatory factor and a transcription factor; and the one or more inducible proteins include one or two selected from the group consisting of a drug resistance protein and a suicide protein. By designing a CAR library-genetic circuit-inducible protein coupling scheme, the cell library construction and screening for complex and unknown disease target antigens are realized, such as to solve the problems that there are complex, diverse, and variable antigens.

title: Detection method for clostridium perfringens of intestinal tracts of meat ducks
text: Detection method for clostridium perfringens of intestinal tracts of meat ducks. The invention discloses a detection method for clostridium perfringens of intestinal tracts of meat ducks. The method comprises steps as follows: DNA extraction: a feces DNA extraction kit is adopted, and total DNA of chime of ceca of ducks is extracted into a 1.5-ml EP tube according to extraction steps and stored in a refrigerator at subzero 20 DEG C; design of primers; synthesis of standard plasmids: two ends of each designed primer extend dozens of bp and then handed over to the TaKaRa bioengineering company for complete-gene synthesis, genes are cloned to plasmids, and standard products are formed; production of a standard curve; detection of samples: to-be-detected sample DNA is subjected to a fluorescent quantitative PCR (polymerase chain reaction) according to a reaction system and reaction conditions. With the adoption of the method, harmful germs, namely, the clostridium perfringens, of the intestinal tracts of the ducks can be detected quickly and accurately through detection procedures; the absolute copy number of the germs can be determined through absolute quantification of the clostridium perfringens of the intestinal tracts of the ducks, and reference is provided for treatment of diseases. The method can be used for detection of clostridium perfringens of intestinal tracts of other animals.

title: Biological preparation method of R-3-aminobutanol
text: Biological preparation method of R-3-aminobutanol. The invention provides a biological preparation method of R-3-aminobutanol. The method includes: conducting whole-gene synthesis according to an artificially designed sequence shown as SEQ ID NO:1, and performing cloning into pET24a so as to obtain a recombinant expression vector; transferring the recombinant expression vector into E.coli to obtain a recombinant D-transaminase genetic engineering bacterium; culturing the bacterium to prepare recombinant D-transaminase; adding 3-carbonyl butanol with a final concentration of 10-300mmol/L, dimethyl sulfoxide or acetonitrile with a mass percentage concentration of 2-15%, pyridoxal phosphate with a final concentration of 0.1-1mmol/L, isopropylamine or D-alanine with a final concentration of 0.02-1mol/L, and recombinant D-transaminase with a mass percentage concentration of 0.01-1% are added into the reaction solution to compose a reaction system for reaction; and at the end of the reaction, extracting R-3-aminobutanol from the reaction solution. With a low cost, the method provided by the invention can achieve a conversion rate of more than 97% and a product yield of greater than 87%, and does not generate by-product, thus being more suitable for industrial application.

title: TMV infectious cloning vector and construction method thereof
text: TMV infectious cloning vector and construction method thereof. The invention provides a TMV infectious cloning vector and a construction method thereof. The TMV infectious cloning vector obtained by construction is pCB-TMV-SY of which the nucleotide sequence is disclosed by SEQ ID NO.3, the length of the TMV infectious cloning vector is 11275bp, and the TMV infectious cloning vector can be applied to research of aspects, including a mutual function between TMV and a host plant, a TMV functional genome, TMV molecular detection, medicine effect detection of an anti-TMV medicament and the like. The invention has the beneficial effects that the construction method is convenient and novel and overcomes the defects of a traditional double-digestion vector construction method that enzymes are likely to lose activity and the like, the infectious cloning vector obtained by the construction only infects plants and is relatively safe to animals and the human body, a condition is provided for gene synthesis site-directed mutation, a purpose that the vector efficiently expresses foreign protein and efficiently infects tobaccos, plants including Capsicum annuum L. and the like in a host is realized, and the TMV infectious cloning vector can be applied to multiple aspects, including medicine effect mechanism research, efficacy detection and the like.

title: Serum IV-type avian adenovirus penton protein, preparation method and application thereof
text: Serum IV-type avian adenovirus penton protein, preparation method and application thereof. The invention discloses a serum IV-type avian adenovirus penton protein, a preparation method and application thereof, and belongs to the field of amynology. The preparation method comprises the following steps: chemically synthesizing a penton gene sequence shown as SEQ ID NO.1; preparing a recombinant pFastBac-1-penton carrier; using the prepared recombinant pFastBac-1-penton carrier to transfect an sf9 cell, and obtaining a recombinant protein through expression of the sf9 cell; and carrying out Ni-NTA purification on the obtained recombinant protein to obtain purified serum IV-type avian adenovirus penton protein. The serum IV-type avian adenovirus penton protein is obtained by the preparation method. The invention discloses application of the serum IV-type avian adenovirus penton protein in the preparation of vaccines for preventing avian adenovirus. The serum IV-type avian adenovirus penton protein has the advantages that gene synthesis does not need a template, and is more economic, simpler and more convenient; the expression quantity of penton can be improved preferably; and the protein is easy for industrial scaled production.

title: Amonolysis instrument and automatic liquid discharging method
text: Amonolysis instrument and automatic liquid discharging method. The invention relates to the technical field of gene synthesis, in particular to an ammonolysis instrument and an automatic drainage method. The ammonolysis instrument comprises an ammonolysis pot, a heating mechanism and a driving mechanism, the ammonolysis pot comprises a pot body and a cover body, and the pot body is used for containing a sample to be ammonolyzed; the heating mechanism is used for heating the pot body to raise the temperature of the pot body; the driving mechanism is used for driving the cover body and the pot body to be relatively close to or away from each other, so that the cover body and the pot body are connected or disconnected. Compared with the mode that in the prior art, bolts on the cover body and the pot body are manually screwed to achieve sealing, the driving mechanism drives one of the cover body and the pot body to move close to or away from the other one, it is guaranteed that the pot body and the cover body are sealed and opened, and therefore automatic cover closing and opening are achieved, manual operation is not needed, and the working efficiency is improved. Therefore, the risks of scalding and eye fumigation caused by manual uncovering can be reduced, and the personal safety coefficient of operators is further improved.

title: Preparation method of fluorescence sensor based on cascade DNA assembly reaction of hairpin DNA chain
text: Preparation method of fluorescence sensor based on cascade DNA assembly reaction of hairpin DNA chain. The invention discloses a preparation method of a fluorescence sensor based on a cascade DNA assembly reaction of a hairpin DNA chain. The preparation method comprises the following steps: S1, preparing a first-stage catalytic hairpin self-assembly reaction system (CHA); s2, preparing a second-stage hybrid chain reaction (HCR) system; s3, carrying out fluorescence labeling on an HP3 chain in the hybridization chain reaction; and S4, forming the fluorescence sensor. According to the preparation method of the fluorescence sensor based on the cascade DNA assembly reaction of the hairpin DNA chain, a Trigger chain is replaced by HP2 through toehold3 to form a stable HP1-HP2 chain, and the replaced Trigger chain initiates a new catalytic hairpin self-assembly reaction, so that a large number of HP1-HP2 chains are formed. The HP1-HP2 chain also has a toehold terminal, and the free toehold terminal can trigger the hybridization chain reaction of HP3 and HP4 to form an HP2-HP1-[HP3-HP4] n long-chain compound. At the moment, a hairpin structure of a molecular beacon HP3 chain is opened, a fluorophore Cy3 and a quenching group BHQ2 are separated, a fluorescence signal is emitted, and specific quantitative analysis of helicobacter pylori is realized according to the intensity of the fluorescence signal.

title: GENETIC MANIPULATION METHOD IN BACTERIA
text: GENETIC MANIPULATION METHOD IN BACTERIA. The present disclosure relates to bacterium engineered to produce aromatic compounds or compounds with aromatic metabolites or intermediates using the CRISPR-CAS transcriptional activation (CRISPRa) and/or transcriptional repression (CRISPRi). Accordingly, in an aspect the present disclosure relates to an engineered bacterium comprising genetic elements supporting programmable transcriptional activation and/or repression. The present disclosure also provides methods and systems for producing aromatic compounds or compounds with aromatic metabolites or intermediates using the engineered bacterium disclosed herein.

title: Preparation method of recombinant engineering bacterium for expressing XVII type collagen COL17A1
text: Preparation method of recombinant engineering bacterium for expressing XVII type collagen COL17A1.
