The invention discloses a preparation method of a recombinant engineering bacterium for expressing XVII type collagen COL17A1, and relates to the technical field of gene engineering, in particular to a preparation method of a recombinant engineering bacterium for expressing XVII type collagen COL17A1, which comprises the following steps: gene synthesis and vector construction: synthesizing an optimized sequence SEQ ID No.1 of human source XVII type collagen COL17A1; adding proper restriction enzyme cutting sites at two ends of the sequence; the synthesized gene sequence is cloned to a pGAPZ A-alpha A vector, and the expression of the gene under the control of a GAP promoter is ensured. According to the preparation method of the recombinant engineering bacterium for expressing the XVII type collagen COL17A1, by using a GAP promoter, the constructed recombinant engineering bacterium can keep a stable expression level under different environmental conditions, the GAP promoter serving as a constitutive promoter can continuously and stably express recombinant protein in pichia pastoris, an inducer does not need to be added, the production process is simplified, and the production cost is reduced. The production cost is reduced, and the production efficiency is improved.

title: CROSS-SPECIES-SPECIFIC PSCAXCD3, CD19XCD3, C-METXCD3, ENDOSIALINXCD3, EPCAMXCD3, IGF-1RXCD3 OR FAPALPHAXCD3 BISPECIFIC SINGLE CHAIN ANTIBODY
text: CROSS-SPECIES-SPECIFIC PSCAXCD3, CD19XCD3, C-METXCD3, ENDOSIALINXCD3, EPCAMXCD3, IGF-1RXCD3 OR FAPALPHAXCD3 BISPECIFIC SINGLE CHAIN ANTIBODY. The present invention relates to a bispecific single chain antibody molecule comprising a first binding domain capable of binding to an epitope of human and non-chimpanzee primate CD3 epsilon chain, wherein the epitope is part of an amino acid sequence comprised in the group consisting of SEQ ID NOs. 2, 4, 6, and 8, and a second binding domain capable of binding to an antigen selected from the group consisting of Prostate Stem Cell Antigen (PSCA), B-Lymphocyte antigen CD19 (CD19), hepatocyte growth factor receptor (C-MET), Endosialin, the EGF-like domain 1 of EpCAM, encoded by exon 2, Fibroblast activation protein alpha (FAP alpha) and Insulin-like growth factor I receptor (IGF-IR or IGF-1R). The invention also provides nucleic acids encoding said bispecific single chain antibody molecule as well as vectors and host cells and a process for its production. The invention further relates to pharmaceutical compositions comprising said bispecific single chain antibody molecule and medical uses of said bispecific single chain antibody molecule.

title: Method for rapidly removing protecting group from oligonucleotide and purifying primer
text: Method for rapidly removing protecting group from oligonucleotide and purifying primer. The invention discloses a method for rapidly removing a protecting group from oligonucleotide and purifying a primer, which comprises the following steps: ammonolysis: adding an ammonolysis reagent and conducting operation by a microwaves heating lamp; and purification: conducting purification through operations including acetonitrile cleaning suction filtration and ultrapure water centrifugal elution. According to the method, primer purification is achieved through simple desalting and purification. The oligonucleotide primer capable of being used for conventional PCR and sequencing can be rapidly obtained within 1 h, primer purification is achieved by simple desalting and purification, and a synthesized carrier is in full contact with a reagent containing ammonium ions, then microwave heating is conducted in an ammonia-containing environmental glass lunch box microenvironment by a microwave oven, so that ions in the closed microenvironment are rapidly heated to a high-temperature environment through high-speed movement friction, thereby achieving the purpose of carrying out rapid ammonolysis deprotection on protecting groups of oligonucleotides on the carrier. The method has the advantages that the device is simple, easy to obtain, simple to operate, economical and applicable; and the method has great application value for large-scale gene synthesis enterprises.

title: Method for increasing content of beneficial glucosinolates in radishes based on gene editing technology
text: Method for increasing content of beneficial glucosinolates in radishes based on gene editing technology. The invention discloses a method for increasing the content of beneficial glucosinolates in radishes based on a gene editing technology, and relates to the technical field of radish genetic engineering.The application method comprises the steps that firstly, two target sequences of an RsGRS1 gene are designed, then a promoter and an sgRNA sequence containing two target spots are subjected to gene synthesis and then inserted into a Ubi-CRISPR/Cas9-WPR carrier stored in a laboratory, and the RsGRS1 gene is obtained; and finally obtaining the gene editing vector of the RsGRS1. And transferring the constructed and sequenced plasmid into a host cell, and infecting a rootless seedling explant of the 'NAU-RG' radish by using the plasmid to obtain a transgenic regenerated plant. An rsgrs1 gene mutant is screened from a radish positive transgenic plant, the content of glucosinolate in the mutant plant is further measured, and it is found that the content of beneficial glucosinolate 4-methylsulfinyl butyl glucosinolate (GRA) in the rsgrs1 mutant is increased by 39.66-46.38 times compared with that of a wild type. The invention provides a new method for germplasm creation and genetic improvement of the radish high beneficial thioglycoside component.

title: Method for exogenous gene expression optimization in salmonella
text: Method for exogenous gene expression optimization in salmonella. The present invention provides a method for exogenous gene expression optimization in salmonella. The method comprises the following specific steps: carrying out statistics from a NCBI database to obtain a salmonella codon usage frequency table; analyzing usage frequency of every codon; cloning or synthesizing a target gene, determining a nucleotide sequence of the target gene, adopting a molecular mutation manner or a gene synthesis manner to carry out mutation of the codon having a lowest salmonella codon usage frequency and the codon having a higher salmonella codon usage frequency in the target gene into the codon having a highest usage frequency under a condition of no change of amino acids according to the salmonella codon usage frequency table and an amino acid codon corresponding table to obtain a new nucleotide segment encoding the target gene; adopting a molecular biology method to insert the new nucleotide segment encoding the target gene into an expression vector, transforming the obtained vector to gene engineering escherichia coli, and identifying the obtained clones; and transforming the target gene-containing expression vector into salmonella, wherein the target gene-containing expression vector is identified, and a correct result is obtained.

title: Genetically engineered bacterium for producing phospholipase D, construction method of genetically engineered bacterium, and application of genetically engineered bacterium in development of sdLDL-C detection kit
text: Genetically engineered bacterium for producing phospholipase D, construction method of genetically engineered bacterium, and application of genetically engineered bacterium in development of sdLDL-C detection kit. The invention relates to the technical field of gene engineering, and provides a genetically engineered bacterium for producing phospholipase D. The invention also provides a construction method of the genetically engineered bacterium for producing the phospholipase D. The method comprises the following steps: optimizing phospholipase D gene codons, analyzing glycosylation sites of phospholipase D genes, carrying out site-specific mutagenesis and whole-gene synthesis on the glycosylation sites, obtaining primary engineering bacteria, extracting plasmids, transforming pichia pastoris cells, and identifying recombinant positive transformants. Meanwhile, the invention also provides application of the genetically engineered bacterium for producing the phospholipase D in the development of an sdLDL-C detection kit. The invention has the advantages that: the constructed genetically engineered bacterium for producing the phospholipase D can efficiently express PLD protein; the activity of hydrolase reaches 115.2 U/mL; PLD in an existing sdLDL-C detection kit is replaced; and the production cost of the corresponding kit is greatly reduced.

title: METHOD FOR CONSTRUCTING SEQUENCING LIBRARY AND APPLICATION THEREOF
text: METHOD FOR CONSTRUCTING SEQUENCING LIBRARY AND APPLICATION THEREOF. Provided in the present application are a method for constructing a sequencing library and an application thereof. The method comprises the following steps: (1) separately transferring M samples to be sequenced into competent bacteria, streaking and culturing overnight; (2) picking N colonies from the competent bacteria of each sample to be sequenced respectively, and separately culturing same; (3) mixing bacterial liquids containing different samples to be sequenced to form N parts of a mixed bacterial liquid, wherein each part of the mixed bacterial liquid contains M kinds of competent bacteria of the samples to be sequenced; (4) separately extracting plasmids from the N parts of the mixed bacterial liquid, and linearizing enzyme digestion; (5) adding different tag sequences to N parts of the linearized mixed plasmid, and obtaining a sequencing library after mixing, wherein M and N are positive integers. The present application employs mixing, extraction and enzyme digestion steps, simultaneously eliminates the monoclonal screening process and reduces the plasmid extraction work by 99% while increasing the sample volume, and facilitates replacing first generation sequencing with third-generation sequencing for use in gene synthesis.

title: RhFN recombinant human chemical fibronectin process of genetic engineering technology
text: RhFN recombinant human chemical fibronectin process of genetic engineering technology. The invention discloses an rhFN recombinant human chemical fibronectin process of a genetic engineering technology, and particularly relates to the technical field of genetic engineering, and the rhFN recombinant human chemical fibronectin process comprises target gene acquisition, gene cloning and vector construction, host cell selection and transformation, cell culture and screening, protein expression and optimization, and protein separation and purification. According to the rhFN recombinant human chemical fibronectin process of the genetic engineering technology provided by the invention, high-quality target genes are accurately obtained through technologies such as new-generation sequencing and gene synthesis, and the gene expression efficiency and stability are enhanced by virtue of optimized vector construction and regulatory elements; protein expression ability and specificity are improved in host cell selection and transformation, efficient culture and accurate screening are realized in cell culture and screening links, total yield is increased in a protein expression optimization link, purity and efficiency are improved in a protein separation and purification link, and product quality and safety are ensured through comprehensive quality control. The requirements in the fields of medical treatment and scientific research are expected to be better met.

title: Method and apparatus for evolutionary data driven design of protein and other sequence defined biomolecules using machine learning
text: Method and apparatus for evolutionary data driven design of protein and other sequence defined biomolecules using machine learning. A method and apparatus are provided for designing sequence-defined biomolecules, such as proteins, using a data-driven evolution-based process. To design a protein, an iterative method established over the composition of an unsupervised sequence-based model and a supervised functionality-based model may select candidate amino acid sequences that may have a desired functionality. Measurement of feedback of candidate proteins using high throughput gene synthesis and protein screening processes is used to refine and improve a model that directs candidate selection to the most desirable region in a very large amino acid sequence search space. The unsupervised sequence-based model may be, for example, a restricted Boltzmann machine, a variational auto-encoder, a generative adversarial network, a statistical coupling analysis, or a direct coupling analysis model. A supervised functionality-based model may be based on generating functional prospects in a dimensionality-reduced potential space of an unsupervised model by fitting measurements of the high-throughput assay using, for example, multivariate linear regression, support vector regression, Gaussian process regression, random forest, or an artificial neural network.

title: Method for constructing prokaryotic expression vector of human neurofilament light polypeptide and purifying protein
text: Method for constructing prokaryotic expression vector of human neurofilament light polypeptide and purifying protein. The invention discloses a method for constructing a prokaryotic expression vector of a human neurofilament light polypeptide and purifying a protein. The method comprises the following steps: optimizing a recombinant NEFL codon through a gene synthesis technology, and subcloning the optimized recombinant NEFL codon into pET-22b (+), pET-32a (+) and pSmart-I to construct a heavy NEFL plasmid; preparing an expression vector by using the constructed heavy NEFL plasmid to transform competent cells of expression bacteria; identifying whether the target protein is massively expressed in escherichia coli or not by using a bacterial colony SDS-PAGE method; according to the SDS-PAGE result, judging whether the target protein is generated in the form of an inclusion body or a soluble protein, and determining the optimal supernatant expression inducing condition; and finally, purifying the target protein, and dialyzing and concentrating the purified target protein. According to the invention, codon optimization is carried out on the gene, screening of optimal clone and optimization of optimal expression time are carried out by using different expression vectors, and the supernatant expression quantity of the protein is improved.

title: Bioluminescent reporter gene for monitoring DNA (Deoxyribonucleic Acid) synthesis phase and application thereof
text: Bioluminescent reporter gene for monitoring DNA (Deoxyribonucleic Acid) synthesis phase and application thereof. The invention relates to gene synthesis in molecular biology, mainly relates to a bioluminescent reporter gene for monitoring a DNA (Deoxyribonucleic Acid) synthesis phase, and also relates to application of the gene to anti-tumor medicament screening. According to the invention, a plasmid PBS (pBluescript) containing a full-length human cyclinA2 gene is taken as a template, primers are designed on two ends of the cyclinA2 gene, cDNA (Complementary DNA) full length of the cyclinA2 is amplified and cloned to an end 5' of a Luc gene of a pGL3-control vector to form an eukaryon recombinant expression vector, namly pGL3-CYCA-Luc, which is capable of expressing fusion protein. The vector can express the fusion protein CYCA-Luc after being transfected into a tumor cell. In-vitro experiments show that after being degraded through an ubiquitination dependency pathway, the fusion protein changes in a cell cycle dependency manner and can be used for monitoring the action effect of a specific anti-tumor medicament of a phase S. The invention plays an important role in the fields of screening the anti-tumor medicaments specific to the phase S, and the like.

title: METHOD FOR ENCODING DIGITAL DATA ON NUCLEIC ACIDS USING BIOLOGICAL PROCESSES
text: METHOD FOR ENCODING DIGITAL DATA ON NUCLEIC ACIDS USING BIOLOGICAL PROCESSES. The present invention relates to a nucleic acid-based data storage method for storing information, and to a data storage nucleic acid molecule.

title: Codon-optimized danio rerio g-type lysozyme-1 gene and recombinant expression protein thereof
text: Codon-optimized danio rerio g-type lysozyme-1 gene and recombinant expression protein thereof. The invention discloses a codon-optimized danio rerio g-type lysozyme-1 gene and a recombinant expression protein thereof. The danio rerio has three lysozyme genes in total, namely c type lysozyme, g1 type lysozyme and g2 type lysozyme. The danio rerio g-type lysozyme-1 gene is obtained through optimization and is obtained by adopting a whole-gene synthesis mode after codon optimization, the nucleotide sequence of the danio rerio g-type lysozyme-1 gene is shown as SEQ ID NO.2, and the coded amino acid sequence of the danio rerio g-type lysozyme-1 gene is shown as SEQ ID NO.3. The gene is connected with a carrier pET-28a (+) to obtain a recombinant plasmid 28a-Lys-g1, the recombinant plasmid 28a-Lys-g1 is further transformed into escherichia coli BL21 (DE3) competent cells, and the danio rerio g-type lysozyme-1 recombinant protein with high purity and high bacteriolytic activity is obtained through induced expression, purification, SDS-PAGE verification and renaturation.

title: Recombinant pichia pastoris engineering bacteria and production method thereof
text: Recombinant pichia pastoris engineering bacteria and production method thereof. The invention discloses recombinant pichia pastoris engineering bacteria. The recombinant pichia pastoris engineering bacteria comprises a codon-optimized Richter trichoderma xylanase gene, and the nucleotide sequence of the gene is shown in SEQ:1 in a sequence table. The invention also discloses a production method of the recombinant pichia pastoris engineering bacteria. The production method comprises the following steps: (1) performing synonymous mutation on the Richter trichoderma xylanase gene according to pichia pastoris flavored codons so that the Richter trichoderma xylanase gene is completely mutated into the pichia pastoris favored codons; (2) synthesizing an optimized xylanase gene by utilizing a total-gene synthesis technology, and connecting the optimized xylanase gene with a pMD20-T vector to build a cloning vector pMD20T-Xynopti; (3) building a recombinant expression vector pPICZalpha-Xynopti; (4) transforming a pichia pastoris host; (5) performing induction culture of a positive converter; (6) producing xylanase by small-scale fermentation by adopting the recombinant pichia pastoris engineering bacteria. The recombinant pichia pastoris engineering bacteria disclosed by the invention can be used for secreting a great amount of xylanase.

title: Chemically-synthesized porcine circo-virus (PCV) protein gene, modification method and applications thereof
text: Chemically-synthesized porcine circo-virus (PCV) protein gene, modification method and applications thereof. The invention discloses a chemically-synthesized porcine circo-virus (PCV) protein gene, a modification method, and applications thereof. The preparation of the gene comprises the following steps: obtaining and modifying a PCV2ORF2 gene sequence, designing and synthesizing a primer, synthesizing PCV2ORF2 short-section DNA, synthesizing the full-length gene, determining the sequence of the full-length gene, constructing an expression vector, and expressing a target gene in pichia pastoris. The method takes the PTDS method and PAS method as the references, and reduces the gene synthesis cost, moreover, the procedure of the method is simple, and only comprises two steps of PCR, the error rate is low, and the synthesis period is short. During the whole chemical synthesis process, high fidelity Taq enzyme is used, and thus the mistakenly matched base sequences can be corrected during the synthesis process so as to maximally reduce the generation of mistakenly matched base pair. The gene is cloned to a yeast expression vector to construct a recombinant expression plasmid, and the expression product extracted from large-scale fermentation of the recombinant expression plasmid can be used as the diagnosis antigen for PCV2 antibody detection, and can also be used to prepare subunit vaccine or nucleic acid vaccine.

title: METHOD FOR RNA-GUIDED ENDONUCLEASE-BASED DNA ASSEMBLY
text: METHOD FOR RNA-GUIDED ENDONUCLEASE-BASED DNA ASSEMBLY. The invention provides a novel approach to facilitate assembly of DNA molecules. This approach utilizes RNA-guided endonucleases, capable of targeting any DNA sequence, which cleave DNA and generate DNA fragments characterized by single stranded overhangs. After annealing of complementary overhangs, DNA fragments are covalently connected, generating a single DNA molecule. In this way, the present invention combines the reliability of classic restriction-ligation techniques, removes all sequence constraints from the desired final DNA molecule, and expands the number of DNA pieces that can be assembled at once.

title: Preparation method and application of brain natriuretic peptide
text: Preparation method and application of brain natriuretic peptide. The invention discloses a preparation method and application of a brain natriuretic peptide. The preparation method comprises the following steps: transforming a whole-gene synthesis humanization brain natriuretic peptide coding deoxyribonucleic acid sequence, and removing escherichia coli rare codon; inserting the transformed brain natriuretic peptide coding deoxyribonucleic acid sequence into an expression carrier, and carrying out transformation and expression by using an escherichia coli strain; inoculating the strain subjected to transformation and expression into a luria-bertani (LB) culture medium, and carrying out isopropyl-beta-d-thiogalactoside (IPTG) induction; and breaking the obtained thallus, centrifugally separating and purifying through an Ni-nitrilotriacetic acid (NTA) column. The method is simple in brain natriuretic peptide preparation operation, low in preparation cost, high in finished product yield, high in purity and high in biological activity, can be used as an antigen material for animal immunity, screens related antibodies and lays a foundation for quickly building a calibrator preparation platform which is urgently required by domestic clinical chemical diagnosis and researching diagnostic reagent standard substances and references.

title: Method for realizing high-accuracy fixed-point gene knockout in yeast
text: Method for realizing high-accuracy fixed-point gene knockout in yeast. The invention provides a method for realizing high-accuracy fixed-point gene knockout in yeast. The method comprises the following steps: obtaining upstream and downstream sequences of a target knockout gene by a polymerase chain reaction or total-gene synthesis; connecting the upstream and downstream sequences in a direction opposite to the protogene, and inserting into a vector plasmid to construct an annular cloning vector; performing linear treatment on the annular cloning vector to obtain linear double-stranded DNA; performing 5'-to-3' exonuclease treatment on the linear double-stranded DNA to form linear 3' protruding long-cohesive end double-stranded DNA; and then converting yeast cells. In the invention, by use of the special exogenous DNA structure type, high-accuracy fixed-point gene knockout is realized in yeast. The accuracy of fixed-point gene knockout can reach 45-92%, the fixed-point gene knockout workload for yeast can be remarkably reduced, and the method is of relatively great application potential in yeast function gene study, gene engineering strain construction and the like.

title: METHOD FOR ENCODING DIGITAL DATA ON NUCLEIC ACIDS USING BIOLOGICAL PROCESSES
text: METHOD FOR ENCODING DIGITAL DATA ON NUCLEIC ACIDS USING BIOLOGICAL PROCESSES. A nucleic acid-based data storage method for storing information, and to a data storage nucleic acid molecule.

title: Establishment method of clostridium difficile exotoxin A carboxyl-terminal sequence codon optimization gene segment and expression vector and application of expression protein thereof
text: Establishment method of clostridium difficile exotoxin A carboxyl-terminal sequence codon optimization gene segment and expression vector and application of expression protein thereof. The invention relates to an establishment method of a codon-transformed clostridium difficile exotoxin A carboxyl-terminal gene segment and an expression vector and high-efficiency expression thereof in escherichia coli. The codon-transformed gene sequence integrates the escherichia coli codon usage preference and reduces the influence of the original gene duplication sequence on gene synthesis and expression. The high-efficiency expression vector involved in the study consists of a codon-transformed clostridium difficile exotoxin A carboxyl-terminal gene and a prokaryotic expression vector pET32b(+); after IPTG induction, the high-efficiency expression vector can express the fusion protein with a His tag with high efficiency. The fusion protein can release about 872 amino acid segments at the clostridium difficile exotoxin A carboxyl terminal after thrombin digestion. In the study, the codon-transformed clostridium difficile exotoxin A carboxyl-terminal gene segment can be subjected to high-efficiency fusion expression in escherichia coli; the purified protein can be used as an antigen for preparing a clostridium difficile exotoxin A antibody and an anti-clostridium difficile infection vaccine.

title: Recombinant human keratinocyte growth factor-2 as well as preparation method and application thereof
text: Recombinant human keratinocyte growth factor-2 as well as preparation method and application thereof. The invention relates to a recombinant human keratinocyte growth factor-2 as well as a preparation method and application thereof, and the preparation method comprises the following steps: carrying out artificial whole-gene synthesis on a nucleotide sequence of the recombinant human keratinocyte growth factor-2, and inserting the nucleotide sequence between NcoI and XhoI restriction enzyme cutting sites of a pET-28a (+) vector to obtain a recombinant vector pET-28a (+)-rhKGF-2; introducing the recombinant vector into escherichia coli BL21, and screening a high-expression activated transformant; and culturing the transformant, inducing the expression of the recombinant human keratinocyte growth factor-2, and after the expression is completed, carrying out post-treatment to obtain the rhKGF-2. The invention solves the problem that the N terminal of the target protein obtained by expression in the existing method has one more Met or even more amino acid residues and needs to be processed and modified, can obtain the rhKGF-2 with high physicochemical properties and biological activity and higher activity and purity, and can be used for preventing and treating acute lung injury, respiratory distress syndrome and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.

title: Method for establishing an agrobacterium-mediated Dryopteris fragrans (L.) Schoot. gametophyte genetic transformation system
text: Method for establishing an agrobacterium-mediated Dryopteris fragrans (L.) Schoot. gametophyte genetic transformation system. The invention discloses a method for establishing an agrobacterium-mediated Dryopteris fragrans (L.) Schoot. gametophyte genetic transformation system and belongs to the technical field of Dryopterisfragrans (L.) Schoot genetic transformation. The method includes the following steps of: transforming agrobacterium with a plant binary expression vector by utilizing a Dryopteris fragrans (L.) Schoot. gametophyte as a receptor; soaking a pre-cultured Dryopteris fragrans (L.) Schoot. gametophyte in a transformed agrobacterium bacteria liquid for infection; performing delay culture after sterilization; and finally detecting target genes, and obtaining a plant of Dryopteris fragrans (L.) Schoot. having a heritable target gene after screening. The method is simple in process, is convenient in operation and low in cost, can complete the establishment of the genetic transformation system of the Dryopteris fragrans (L.) Schoot. in a short time, and lays a foundation for further studying the characteristics of relative gene synthesis and regulation and control, trangenosis of resistance genes, and innovations in germplasm resources and genetic transformation of pteridophytes. The method is suitable for genetic transformation of the Dryopteris fragrans (L.) Schoot.

title: Prokaryotic 2-component signaling pathways for use as logic gates in mammalian cells
text: Prokaryotic 2-component signaling pathways for use as logic gates in mammalian cells. The invention relates to mammalian cells comprising at least one prokaryotic two-component signaling (TCS) pathway comprised of an activator protein A, a response regulator (RR) protein B activated by said protein A, such activation leading to an activated RR protein B, and an output gene C operably linked to a promoter. Transcription from said promoter is activated by activated RR protein B, and the expression of output gene C defines at least a first state (0, no transcription) and a second state (1, detectable transcription). The invention further relates to logic gates designed from such cells, and methods for integrating a plurality of output signals based on the cells and logic gates of the invention.

title: Construction method and application of hepatitis E virus infected animal model
text: Construction method and application of hepatitis E virus infected animal model. The invention discloses a construction method and application of a hepatitis E virus infected animal model. The invention belongs to the technical field of animal models, and particularly discloses a construction method of a hepatitis E virus infected animal model, which comprises the following steps: encapsulating a complete hepatitis E virus genome RNA in LNP to obtain an LNP-encapsulated hepatitis E virus genome RNA, and inoculating the LNP-encapsulated hepatitis E virus genome RNA into a non-human mammal to obtain the hepatitis E virus infected animal model. The animal infection model can be established when the RNA dosage is as low as 1ug. The technical barrier that the hepatitis E virus is not suitable for in-vitro culture is solved, the virus does not need to be separated from natural or clinical specimens, the LNP-encapsulated HEV genome RNA can be obtained through gene synthesis, and the LNP-encapsulated HEV genome RNA serving as an inoculation substance of an animal infection model is convenient to obtain, good in uniformity and repeatability and safer to operate.

title: Method for highly expressing and preparing exocrine type human-derived AFP (alpha-fetoprotein)
text: Method for highly expressing and preparing exocrine type human-derived AFP (alpha-fetoprotein). A method for highly expressing and preparing exocrine type human-derived AFP (alpha-fetoprotein) is disclosed and mainly comprises the following three aspects of content: (1) utilizing a gene synthesis technology to synthesize a DNA sequence of AFP, then employing gene cloning and a molecular biological method to construct yeast recombinant expression plasmid PpICZ alpha A-AFP, and performing plasmid extraction; (2) using a pichia pastoris expression system for in vitro inducing expression of secreting type AFP recombinant protein, and optimizing the expression time, inducer addition amount and other conditions, so as to obtain optimum induction conditions; and (3) optimizing ammonium sulfate graded precipitate, and performing separation purification on the supernatant of a fermentation liquid to obtain AFP recombinant protein secreted outside cells. The target protein AFP almost completely precipitates at the ammonium sulfate concentration of 55%, and the purity of the protein AFP is up to 98.844%. The biological activity of the exocrine type recombinant protein AFP obtained through purification is detected to be 3500 ng/mL by using a clinic detection electrochemiluminescence method.

title: Tandem co-expression double visual enhanced fluorescent protein reporter gene plant expression vector and application thereof
text: Tandem co-expression double visual enhanced fluorescent protein reporter gene plant expression vector and application thereof. The invention provides a tandem co-expression double visual enhanced fluorescent protein reporter gene plant expression vector, which is characterized in that after an enhanced expression framework is obtained through gene synthesis on the basis of a starting vector, DsRed2 and eGFP are respectively inserted through homologous recombination reaction, and an enhanced DsRed2 expression cassette is inserted in the same direction on the basis of an enhanced eGFP expression cassette to obtain a final vector. A to-be-analyzed target gene sequence is cloned into the MCS site of the vector through homologous recombination reaction, and the obtained recombinant plasmid can be used for plant genetic transformation. The red fluorescence signal and the green fluorescence signal are clear and stable in imaging effect through observation, and are mutually independent and mutually characterized. The homonymous tandem co-expression dual-visual enhanced fluorescent protein reporter gene plant expression vector can improve the expression efficiency of a fusion gene, and can conveniently and directly identify the genetic transformation through a visual enhanced fluorescent signal.

title: FTCD and CYP2D6 fusion protein as well as construction method and application thereof
text: FTCD and CYP2D6 fusion protein as well as construction method and application thereof. The invention discloses FTCD and CYP2D6 fusion protein as well as a construction method and application thereof. The construction method comprises the following steps of S1, recombining a whole-gene synthesis sequence: connecting nucleotide sequences of FTCD and CYP2D6 protein sections to obtain a target gene; S2, constructing recombinant plasmids: constructing the recombinant whole-gene synthesissequence obtained in the step S1 to an expression vector so as to obtain the recombinant plasmids; S3, performing primary transfection: transfecting host cells with the recombinant plasmids in the step S2 to obtain recombinant bacmids; S4, performing secondary transfection: transfecting the host cells with the recombinant bacmids in the step S3 again to obtain first-generation baculoviruses; S5,performing expression: expressing the first-generation baculoviruses in the step S4; and S6, obtaining the fusion protein: screening and purifying a cell culture obtained by expression in the step S5to obtain the fusion protein. The fusion protein constructed by the method provided by the invention can improve the sensitivity and specificity of a detection kit and reduce missing detection and wrong detection.

title: Mutation coprinus cinereus peroxidase and preparing method and application thereof
text: Mutation coprinus cinereus peroxidase and preparing method and application thereof. The invention discloses mutation coprinus cinereus peroxidase and the preparing method and application thereof and belongs to the technical fields of genetic engineering and the environment. CIPmt4 is synthesized by means of a gene synthesis site-specific mutagenesis platform, error-prone PCR and resistance screening are conducted three times to obtain the coprinus cinereus peroxidase CIPmt5 containing five amino acid mutations (I49S, V53A, T121A, M166F and Y272F), and the amino acid sequence is shown in SEQ ID NO:3. The optimal reaction temperature of the mutation coprinus cinereus peroxidase is increased to 45 DEG C from 25 DEG C corresponding to the wild type, the optimal reaction pH is increased to 6.5 from the original 5.0, the high-activity reaction pH becomes larger, stability is improved, and the mutation coprinus cinereus peroxidase is more suitable for industrialized application. Except methylene blue, the optimal pH of the mutation coprinus cinereus peroxidase CIPmt5 for degradation of dye amino black, methyl orange, Congo red, aniline blue, crystal violet and bromophenol blue tends to be neutral and alkali, and the degradation ability on the five kinds of dye is remarkably higher than that of wild-type enzyme.

title: Design and preparation method and application of novel fasciola hepatica multi-epitope vaccine
text: Design and preparation method and application of novel fasciola hepatica multi-epitope vaccine. The invention provides a novel fasciola hepatica multi-epitope vaccine. An active ingredient of the novel fasciola hepatica multi-epitope vaccine is a polypeptide. The polypeptide is mainly formed by fasciola hepatica sphingolipid activated mucin-like protein-2 Th, a multicopy body of B-cell epitope and a mucosal immunologic adjuvant cholera toxin B subunit. An artificial gene is mainly synthesized through the gene synthesis technology and comprises Th of sphingolipid activated mucin-like protein-2 and a gene sequence of the multicopy body of the B-cell epitope, then the artificial gene is coupled with the gene sequence of the cholera toxin B subunit, and a fusion gene is formed. An escherichia coli prokaryotic expression system is utilized for expressing the fusion gene, and the fasciola hepatica multi-epitope vaccine is obtained after protein purification. The fasciola hepatica multi-epitope vaccine can induce an organism to generate sphingolipid activated protein T cell immune responses and high-titer specific antibody humoral immune responses, and can be used for preventing and treating fasciola hepatica infection related diseases.

title: Helicobacter pylori tetravalent adhesion multi-epitope vaccine and preparation method thereof
text: Helicobacter pylori tetravalent adhesion multi-epitope vaccine and preparation method thereof. The invention provides a multi-epitope vaccine for a helicobacter pylori tetravalent adhesion, wherein the activity of the multi-epitope vaccine is presented as a polypeptide which mainly consists of urease A and B subunits, superior Th and B cell epitopes or fragments of three outer membrane proteins (Lpp20, HpaA and CagL) as well as cholera toxin B subunit. According to the invention, an artificial gene is synthesized by virtue of gene synthesis technology, wherein the synthesized artificial gene consists of urease A and B subunits, and superior Th and B cell epitopes or fragments of three outer membrane proteins (Lpp20, HpaA and CagL), and the artificial gene is coupled with gene sequence of the cholera toxin B subunit, so as to form a fusion gene. The fusion gene is expressed by escherichia coli, and upon protein purification, the tetravalent adhesion multi-epitope vaccine is obtained. The vaccine can be used for inducing a body to generate T cellullar immunologic response and specific antibody humoral immune response in accordance with the urease A and B subunits and the three outer membrane proteins (Lpp20, HpaA and CagL); and the vaccine is suitable for preventing and controlling helicobacter pylori infection related diseases.

title: Bovine viral diarrhea virus (BVDV) E2 protein antigen multi-epitope fusion peptide as well as preparation and application thereof
text: Bovine viral diarrhea virus (BVDV) E2 protein antigen multi-epitope fusion peptide as well as preparation and application thereof. The invention discloses a bovine viral diarrhea virus (BVDV) E2 multi-epitope fusion peptide and a preparation method thereof. The preparation method comprises the following steps: performing gene synthesis on two respective epitope tandem sequences of optimized E2 proteins such as BVDV-1 and BVDV-2, cloning the sequences to a pET-28a (+) vector, transforming Escherichia coli DH-5alpha, transforming Escherichia coli BL21 from an accurate recombinant vector, performing IPTG inducible expression, and performing Ni-NTA purification, so as to obtain the BVDV E2 multi-epitope fusion peptide. Afterbeing subjected to animal immunization, the E2 multi-epitope fusion peptide is capable of producing a neutralizing antibody of the BVDV, and can be used for research and development of BVDV epitope vaccines; an ELISA (Enzyme-linked Immuno Sorbent Assay) detection kit prepared by utilizing the E2 multi-epitope fusion peptide can be used for BVDV antibody detection of cattle, and has the advantagesof being high in specificity, high in sensitivity, excellent in repeatability and easy to operate.

title: Method for screening cellulase gene from rumen macrotranscriptome data of ruminants for cloning
text: Method for screening cellulase gene from rumen macrotranscriptome data of ruminants for cloning. The invention discloses a method for screening a cellulase gene from rumen macrotranscriptome data of ruminants for cloning and mainly provides a cellulase gene and a method for screening a cellulasegene from rumen macrotranscriptome data of ruminants for cloning. The method mainly comprises the steps of providing a cellulase gene and providing a method for screening cellulase genes from rumen macrotranscriptome data of ruminants for cloning. The main method comprises the following steps: a nucleic acid sequence of the rumen cellulase gene is sequenced, a gene library of Dulong cattle rumen microorganism macro-transcriptome is constructed, the data of Dulong cattle rumen microorganism macro-transcriptome is analyzed, and the whole gene synthesis, cloning and enzymatic properties of cellulase gene are studied. By adopting the method, two cellulase genes from a GH48 family are finally selected and successfully cloned, and it is showed through enzyme property research that compared withtwo cellulases purchased in the market, the two cellulases of the invention still maintain higher enzyme activity under the conditions of high temperature and high pH value, and have a certain application value.

title: Pseudo-virus standard substance for nucleic acid diagnosis of novel coronavirus 2019-nCov and application thereof
text: Pseudo-virus standard substance for nucleic acid diagnosis of novel coronavirus 2019-nCov and application thereof. The invention relates to a pseudo-virus standard substance for nucleic acid diagnosis of novel coronavirus 2019-nCov and application thereof. The pseudo-virus standard substance is prepared through the steps of gene synthesis, plasmid extraction, pseudo-virus packaging and the like. Wherein the synthesized gene comprises a novel coronavirus 2019-nCov gene RdRp, a Gene E and a Gene N; the 2019-nCovpseudo-virus standard substance is provided for the first time, is different from RNA synthesized in vitro and clinical positive living virus in the past, perfectly overcomes the defects of RNA and clinical positive living virus, is really suitable for performance evaluation of test kit, comprises limit of detection, specificity, repeatability and the like, and is further applied to clinical diagnosis of 2019-nCov; the pseudo-virus standard substance has the advantages of no pathogenicity, reproducibility, reliable quality control method and stable batch-to-batch, can be stably prepared and supplied for a long time, requires the biological safety level of P2 in a laboratory, and meets the safety requirements of many units.

title: Method for preparing base mismatched specific binding protein and related biological material and application thereof
text: Method for preparing base mismatched specific binding protein and related biological material and application thereof. The invention discloses a method for preparing a base mismatched specific binding protein as well as a related biological material and application thereof. The invention provides a method for preparing a base mismatched specific binding protein, which comprises the following step: expressing a coding gene of the base mismatched specific binding protein in organisms to obtain the base mismatched specific binding protein, the organisms are microorganisms, plants or non-human animals; the base mismatch specific binding protein comprises a base mismatch specific endonuclease and a structural domain which contributes to the affinity adsorption of the zymoprotein to the matrix carrier or promotes the expression of the zymoprotein. The base mismatch specific endonuclease is a CEL I zymoprotein or a CEL II zymoprotein of apios graveolens, and the structural domain is an MBP protein. The fusion protein disclosed by the invention is applied to methods for detecting DNA base errors or mutation, correcting base errors in a gene synthesis process and the like, and has the advantages of high efficiency, rapidness, convenience in automatic operation, low cost and the like.

title: GPC3-targeting fourth-generation CART cell with high expression of CCL-19 and preparation method and application of GPC3-targeting fourth-generation CART cell
text: GPC3-targeting fourth-generation CART cell with high expression of CCL-19 and preparation method and application of GPC3-targeting fourth-generation CART cell. The invention discloses a fourth-generation CART cell targeting GPC3 and highly expressing CCL-19 as well as a preparation method and application of the fourth-generation CART cell. According to an amino acid sequence of a GPC3 monoclonal antibody, scFv is spliced according to a VL-Linker-VH mode, the scFv is converted into a nucleotide sequence according to codon preference, a BamHI restriction enzyme cutting site and a signal peptide are added to a 5'end, an NheI restriction enzyme cutting site is added to a 3 'end, then whole-gene synthesis is performed, and then a CAR vector enters T lymphocytes through virus transfection. According to the invention, aiming at the characteristic that tumor cells have immune escape, a single-chain variable region of a high-specificity GPC3 antibody is fused and constructed into a CAR vector, so that liver cancer cells positively expressed by GPC3 can be specifically recognized and killed; the effective killing performance of the CART is guaranteed by adjusting the hinge area with the proper length and flexibility; meanwhile, the high expression CCL-19 has higher activity.

title: CROSS-SPECIES-SPECIFIC PSCAxCD3, CD19xCD3, C-METxCD3, ENDOSIALINxCD3, EPCAMxCD3, IGF-1RxCD3 OR FAPALPHAxCD3 BISPECIFIC SINGLE CHAIN ANTIBODY
text: CROSS-SPECIES-SPECIFIC PSCAxCD3, CD19xCD3, C-METxCD3, ENDOSIALINxCD3, EPCAMxCD3, IGF-1RxCD3 OR FAPALPHAxCD3 BISPECIFIC SINGLE CHAIN ANTIBODY. The present invention relates to a bispecific single chain antibody molecule comprising a first binding domain capable of binding to an epitope of human and non-chimpanzee primate CD3 epsilon chain, wherein the epitope is part of an amino acid sequence comprised in the group consisting of SEQ ID NOs. 2, 4, 6, and 8, and a second binding domain capable of binding to an antigen selected from the group consisting of Prostate Stem Cell Antigen (PSCA), B-Lymphocyte antigen CD19 (CD19), hepatocyte growth factor receptor (C-MET), Endosialin, the EGF-like domain 1 of EpCAM, encoded by exon 2, Fibroblast activation protein alpha (FAP alpha) and Insulin-like growth factor I receptor (IGF-IR or IGF-1R). The invention also provides nucleic acids encoding said bispecific single chain antibody molecule as well as vectors and host cells and a process for its production. The invention further relates to pharmaceutical compositions comprising said bispecific single chain antibody molecule and medical uses of said bispecific single chain antibody molecule.

title: COMPOSITIONS AND METHODS FOR NUCLEIC ACID ASSEMBLY
text: COMPOSITIONS AND METHODS FOR NUCLEIC ACID ASSEMBLY. The present disclosure generally relates to compositions and methods for the assembly of nucleic acid molecules into larger nucleic acid molecules. Also provided are compositions and methods for seamlessly connection of nucleic acid molecules with high sequence fidelity.

title: METHOD FOR THE FERMENTATIVE PRODUCTION OF GUANIDINOACETIC ACID USING A MICROORGANISM COMPRISING A HETEROLOGOUS L-THREONINE 3-DEHYDROGENASE GENE (TDH) AND A GLYCINE C-ACETYLTRANSFERASE GENE (KBL)
text: METHOD FOR THE FERMENTATIVE PRODUCTION OF GUANIDINOACETIC ACID USING A MICROORGANISM COMPRISING A HETEROLOGOUS L-THREONINE 3-DEHYDROGENASE GENE (TDH) AND A GLYCINE C-ACETYLTRANSFERASE GENE (KBL). The present invention concerns a microorganism comprising at least one heterologous gene coding for a L-arginine:glycine amidinotransferase (AGAT) and at least one heterologous L-threonine 3-dehydrogenase gene (tdh) and at least one heterologous glycine C-acetyltransferase (2-amino-3-oxobutanoate coenzyme A ligase) gene (kbl) and a method for the fermentative production of guanidinoacetic acid (GAA) using such microorganism. The present invention also relates to a method for the fermentative production of creatine.

title: EB (Epstein-Barr) virus detection method for improving sensitivity by combining culture amplification with PCR (Polymerase Chain
text: EB (Epstein-Barr) virus detection method for improving sensitivity by combining culture amplification with PCR (Polymerase Chain. The invention provides an EB (Epstein-Barr) virus detection method for improving sensitivity by combining culture amplification with PCR (Polymerase Chain Reaction). The method provided by the invention comprises the following steps: splitting cells possibly infected with EB virus, taking splitting supernatant, and inoculating human B lymphocytes; inoculating B lymphocytes, continuously culturing, adding a chemical agent TPA for promoting EB virus gene synthesis in the culture process, and screening to obtain the optimal TPA use concentration so as to fully amplify potential EB viruses; and detecting the cultured B cells by using a PCR (Polymerase Chain Reaction) method, and judging whether the infectious EB virus exists or not. The EB virus PCR detection method disclosed by the invention has the excellent effects that an effective method for detecting infectious EB viruses is provided for a cell line which cannot be directly detected by a PCR method due to EB virus sequence expression in a cell nucleus, so that false positive is avoided, and meanwhile, the overall sensitivity of EB virus detection is also improved.

title: Construction method and application of sequencing library
text: Construction method and application of sequencing library. The invention provides a construction method and application of a sequencing library. The method comprises the following steps: (1) respectively transferring M samples to be sequenced into competent bacteria, and carrying out streak culture for one night; (2) respectively picking N bacterial colonies from competent bacteria of each to-be-sequenced sample, and carrying out independent culture; (3)mixing the bacterium solutions containing different samples to be sequenced to form N mixed bacterium solutions, each mixed bacterium solution containing M competent bacteria of the samples to be sequenced; (4) respectively extracting plasmids from the N mixed bacterium solutions, and carrying out linear enzyme digestion; and (5) adding different tag sequences into the N linear mixed plasmids, andmixing to obtain a sequencing library; wherein M and N are positive integers. According to the method, a step of mixed extraction and enzyme digestion is adopted, the monoclonal screening process isomitted while the sample size is increased, 99% of plasmid extraction work is reduced, and three-generation sequencing can replace one-generation sequencing and is applied to gene synthesis.

title: Expression and purification method of virus-derived chitin synthetase and application of virus-derived chitin synthetase
text: Expression and purification method of virus-derived chitin synthetase and application of virus-derived chitin synthetase. The invention belongs to the technical field of biological enzymes, and particularly relates to an expression and purification method and application of virus-derived chitin synthetase. The invention relates to an expression and purification method of virus-derived chitin synthetase, which comprises the following steps: carrying out codon optimization on a virus-derived chitin synthetase gene sequence according to escherichia coli codon preference to obtain a gene sequence with a nucleotide sequence as shown in SEQ ID NO.1; performing whole-gene synthesis on the optimized gene sequence to an expression vector; and culturing, inducing, splitting and centrifuging the host containing the expression vector to obtain the membrane component containing the virus-derived chitin synthetase. The invention provides a purification method of virus-derived chitin synthetase, which utilizes a strategy of escherichia coli heterologous expression and biofilm-like system separation and purification, and the high-purity virus-derived chitin synthetase can be obtained through the strategy. The invention also provides a novel method for synthesizing the oligomeric chitosan oligosaccharide, the composition of a reaction system is simple, and the product is easy to separate and purify.

title: Synthetic method of DNA
text: Synthetic method of DNA. The invention relates to the technical field of DNA synthesis, in particular to a synthetic method of DNA. The synthetic method comprises the following steps: dividing a sequence of to be synthesized DNA into n sequence fragments, n being an even number larger than or equal to 2, the length of each sequence fragment being 50-150 bp; designing a primer for each sequence fragment, the primer comprising all sequences of the sequence fragment or reverse complementary sequences of the sequence fragment, the primer of the ith sequence fragment being a forward primer, the primer of the i+1th sequence fragment being a reverse primer, the 3'end of the primer of the ith sequence fragment and the 3 'end of the primer of the i+1th sequence fragment having a reverse complementary pairing region, i being all odd numbers in the range of 1-n-1; and using the primer pair to perform template-free PCR to obtain a PCR product. According to the method, the PCR product can be rapidly obtained through a round of PCR and used for vector construction and gene synthesis; and the method has the advantages of being easy, convenient and rapid to operate and high in synthesis efficiency and accuracy.

title: Genetically engineered bacterium for producing bacterial laccase as well as construction method and application of genetically engineered bacterium
text: Genetically engineered bacterium for producing bacterial laccase as well as construction method and application of genetically engineered bacterium. The invention relates to the technical field of genetic engineering, and provides a genetically engineered bacterium for producing bacterial laccase. The invention also provides a construction method of the genetically engineered bacterium for producing the bacterial laccase. The method comprises the following steps: optimizing bacterial laccase gene codons, performing whole-gene synthesis and performing escherichia coli cell transformation. Meanwhile, the invention also provides an application of the genetically engineered bacterium for producing bacterial laccase in decoloration of indigo and azo dyes. The genetically engineered bacterium for producing the bacterial laccase has the advantages that the genetically engineered bacterium for producing the bacterial laccase can efficiently express the bacterial laccase protein Lac1 under an illumination condition. When no mediator is added, the decolorization rate of the Lac1 to indigo dye carmine red can reach 99.7% within one hour, the decolorization efficiency of the Lac1 to azo dye Congo red can reach 99.5% within 24 hours, secondary pollution of the mediator is avoided through mediator-free decolorization application of the Lac1, and the Lac1 has large industrial application potential and economic value.

title: GAS BIOSENSORS
text: GAS BIOSENSORS. Microbial biosensors that generate gas outputs in hard-to-image materials using exogenous methyl halide transferase (MHT) genes. By varying the promoter that is fused to the MHT gene, biosensors for different triggers can be made.

title: MRNA vaccine for echinococcosis as well as preparation method and application of mRNA vaccine
text: MRNA vaccine for echinococcosis as well as preparation method and application of mRNA vaccine. The invention discloses an echinococcosis mRNA (messenger Ribonucleic Acid) vaccine as well as a preparation method and application thereof. The preparation method of the mRNA vaccine for the echinococcosis comprises the following steps: carrying out codon optimization on a modified target antigen protein, then assembling the modified target antigen protein with 5 'UTR, 3' UTR and Poly (A) tail, carrying out gene synthesis, then cloning the synthesized gene into a pUC57 plasmid, and sequentially carrying out plasmid linearization, in-vitro transcription and purification on the constructed recombinant plasmid to prepare an mRNA molecule, thereby obtaining the mRNA vaccine for the echinococcosis. Finally, mRNA molecules are wrapped in lipid nanoparticles through a microfluidic method to form the mRNA vaccine for the echinococcosis, and immune effect evaluation is carried out on the mRNA vaccine for the echinococcosis. Experiments prove that the prepared mRNA vaccine for the echinococcosis can activate humoral immunity and cellular immunity of mice at the same time, can provide an effective protection effect for the mice attacking insects, and has a wide application prospect in the aspect of preventing and/or treating the echinococcosis.

title: Helicobacter pylori tetravalent virulence factor multi-epitope vaccine and preparation method thereof
text: Helicobacter pylori tetravalent virulence factor multi-epitope vaccine and preparation method thereof. The invention provides a helicobacter pylori tetravalent virulence factor multi-epitope vaccine and a preparation method thereof. The active constituent of the vaccine is polypeptide. The vaccine mainly comprises the dominant Th and B cell epitopes or sections of a urease A subunit, a urease B subunit, cytotoxin associated protein A and vacuolating cytotoxin associated protein A, and neutrophil activating protein. The gene synthesis and molecular cloning technology is used for building a fused gene containing the dominant Th and B cell epitopes or sections of ureases, the cytotoxin associated protein A and the vacuolating cytotoxin associated protein A, and the neutrophil activating protein. Escherichia coli is used for expressing the fused gene, and the tetravalent virulence factor multi-epitope vaccine is obtained after protein purification. The vaccine can stimulate a body to generate T cell immunity response and specific antibody humoral immunity response aiming at the ureases, the cytotoxin associated protein A, the vacuolating cytotoxin associated protein A and the neutrophil activating protein and can be used for preventing and treating diseases related to helicobacter pylori infection.

title: SaRNA vaccine for echinococcosis as well as preparation method and application of SaRNA vaccine
text: SaRNA vaccine for echinococcosis as well as preparation method and application of SaRNA vaccine. The invention discloses an SaRNA vaccine for echinococcosis as well as a preparation method and application of the SaRNA vaccine. The preparation method of the SaRNA vaccine comprises the following steps: carrying out codon optimization on a modified target antigen protein through a genetic engineering technology, then assembling the modified target antigen protein with a self-replicating protein sequence, 5 'UTR, 3' UTR and Poly (A) tail, carrying out gene synthesis, then cloning the synthesized gene into a plasmid, and carrying out purification to obtain the SaRNA vaccine. The preparation method comprises the following steps: constructing recombinant plasmids, sequentially carrying out plasmid linearization, in-vitro transcription and purification on the constructed recombinant plasmids to prepare SaRNA molecules, and finally wrapping the SaRNA molecules in lipid nanoparticles to form the SaRNA vaccine for the echinococcosis. Experiments prove that the SaRNA vaccine can activate humoral immunity and cellular immunity of mice at the same time, high-level EG95 specific antibodies and cytokines can be generated through low-dose immunity, and the SaRNA vaccine has wide application prospects in the aspect of preventing and/or treating the echinococcosis.

title: Enhanced double expression cassette vector containing naked eye visible fluorescent protein reporter gene and application thereof
text: Enhanced double expression cassette vector containing naked eye visible fluorescent protein reporter gene and application thereof. The invention provides an enhanced double-expression cassette vector containing a naked eye visible fluorescent protein reporter gene, an enhanced expression framework eYGFPuv and 3 * Flag + 6 * MYC are respectively obtained through gene synthesis, the expression framework is inserted on the basis of a starting vector, and an enhanced eYGFPuv expression cassette is inserted in the same direction on the basis of a modified 3 * Flag + 6 * MYC intermediate vector, so that a final vector pCAMBIA1300-eYGFPuv-Flag-MYC is obtained. A to-be-analyzed target gene sequence is cloned into the MCS site of the vector, and the obtained recombinant plasmid is used for plant genetic transformation. Compared with a traditional plant expression vector, the expression efficiency of the fusion gene can be remarkably improved, whether the plant is a transgenic plant or not can be clearly, visually and rapidly judged by observing a fluorescence signal excited by ultraviolet light with naked eyes, and meanwhile Western blot identification and downstream immunoprecipitation research can be conducted on the fusion protein of the plant through an antibody tag.

title: Carica papaya chitinase as well as preparation method and application thereof
text: Carica papaya chitinase as well as preparation method and application thereof. The invention discloses carica papaya chitinase as well as a preparation method and the application thereof. According to the preference of a pichia pastoris codon, chitinase coding gene sequence in the carica papaya is obtained by using a whole-gene synthesis method, and an optimized nucleotide sequence is as shown in SEQ ID NO.2; a pichia pastoris expression system is further used for performing efficient secretion expression on the optimized chitinase coding gene, so as to obtain the carica papaya chitinase with the amino acid sequence as shown in SEQ ID NO.1. The carica papaya chitinase disclosed by the invention is relatively high in hydrolytic activity for a chitosan substrate with low deacetylation degree; for crude enzyme generated by flask shaking fermentation, 1 mL of the crude enzyme (with the protein content of about 0.18 mg) has the hydrolysis capacity of degrading 0.5 g of chitosan, but 20 mg of the carica papaya chitinase is needed to degrade the chitosan with the same amount, so that the efficiency is theoretically improved by 100 times or more; the carica papaya chitinase has a good industrial application prospect.

title: Target protein expression with laetiporus sulphureus mushroom lectin N-acetyllactosamine binding domain as fusion tag and purification method thereof
text: Target protein expression with laetiporus sulphureus mushroom lectin N-acetyllactosamine binding domain as fusion tag and purification method thereof. The invention discloses an artificially-synthesized LSL gene for encoding a laetiporus sulphureus mushroom lectin N-acetyllactosamine binding domain and an expression vector and host cell containing the LSL gene. The nucleotide sequence of the LSL gene is SEQ ID NO. 1. The invention further discloses a target protein expression with LSL used as the fusion tag and a purification method thereof. The method specifically relates to LSL gene synthesis, construction of the expression vector containing the LSL gene and the target protein gene, construction of the expression vector containing the LSL gene and a protease gene, expression and purification of fusion protein, LSL protein tag removal, target protein purification and the like. The method is simple and easy to implement, one-step purification of target protein is achieved, high-purity target protein can be obtained, the protein purification cost is lowered, the method can be widely applied to activity protein in the fields of biological medicine, veterinary medicine and the like and large-scale preparation of vaccine antigen in the industry of biological products, and high practical value is achieved.

title: SYNTHETIC BIOLOGICAL CIRCUITS FOR THE DETECTION OF TARGET ANALYTES USING A GLUCOSE METER IN A CELL-FREE SYSTEM
text: SYNTHETIC BIOLOGICAL CIRCUITS FOR THE DETECTION OF TARGET ANALYTES USING A GLUCOSE METER IN A CELL-FREE SYSTEM. Described are methods for generating a reporter molecule in response to a target analyte in a cell-free system. A synthetic biological circuit is used to modify the level of the reporter molecule in response to the presence of the target analyte. The reporter molecule may be glucose or another molecule readily detected using a device such as glucose monitor or other portable sensor. Also provided are kits comprising a cell-free system with a synthetic biological circuit that generates or consumes a reporter molecule in response to a target analyte.

title: PR3, MPO and GBM fusion protein as well as construction method and application thereof
text: PR3, MPO and GBM fusion protein as well as construction method and application thereof. The invention discloses PR3, MPO and GBM fusion protein as well as a construction method and application thereof. The construction method comprises the following steps of S1, recombining a whole-genesynthesis sequence: sequentially connecting nucleotide sequences of PR3, MPO and GBM protein sections to obtain a target gene; S2, constructing recombinant plasmids: constructing the recombinant whole-gene synthesis sequence obtained in the step S1 to an expression vector so as to obtain the recombinant plasmids; S3, performing primary transfection: transfecting host cells with the recombinant plasmids in the step S2 to obtain recombinant bacmids; S4, performing secondary transfection: transfecting the host cells with the recombinant bacmids in the step S3 again to obtain first-generation baculoviruses; S5, performing expression: expressing the first-generation baculoviruses in the step S4; and S6, obtaining the fusion protein: screening and purifying a cell culture obtained by expressionin the step S5 to obtain the fusion protein. The fusion protein constructed by the method provided by the invention can improve the sensitivity and specificity of a detection kit and reduce missing detection and wrong detection.

title: Biological preparation method for R-3-aminopiperidine
text: Biological preparation method for R-3-aminopiperidine. The invention discloses a biological preparation method for R-3-aminopiperidine. The biological preparation method comprises the following steps: carrying out gene synthesis according to a sequence as shown in SEQ ID NO:1, cloning the gene into an expression vector pET22a to prepare a recombinant expression vector, and transferring the vector into escherichia coli to prepare a genetically engineered bacterium of recombinant D-transaminase; culturing the bacterium to prepare recombinant D-transaminase genetically engineered bacterium; adding 3-ketopiperidine with final concentration of 10-300mmol/L, 2-15wt% dimethyl sulfoxide or acetonitrile, phosphopyridoxal with final concentration of 0.1-1mmol/L, isopropylamine or D-alanine with final concentration of 0.02-1mol/L and 0.2-0.4wt% recombinant D-transaminase to form a reaction system to carry out reaction; and extracting the R-3-aminopiperidine in the reaction liquor after the reaction is ended. According to the biological preparation method, the cost is low, the conversion ratio is over 97%, the product yield is greater than 85% and byproducts are not produced, so that the biological preparation method is more suitable for industrial application.

title: Novel helicobacter pylori multiepitope vaccine and preparation method thereof
text: Novel helicobacter pylori multiepitope vaccine and preparation method thereof. The invention provides a helicobacter pylori multiepitope vaccine. The active ingredient of the helicobacter pylori multiepitope vaccine is a piece of polypeptide; and the helicobacter pylori multiepitope vaccine mainly comprises a multi-copy body of Th and B cell antigen epitopes of helicobacter pylori urease A and B bi-subunit and a mucosal immune adjuvant of a cholera toxin B subunit. The preparation method mainly comprises the following steps of: synthesizing an artificial gene by using a gene synthesis technology, wherein the artificial gene comprises a gene sequence of the multi-copy body of the Th and B cell antigen epitopes of the helicobacter pylori urease A and B bi-subunit; coupling the artificial gene with the gene sequence of the cholera toxin B subunit to form a fusion gene;and expressing the fusion gene by using an escherichia coli prokaryotic expression system, and performing protein purification to obtain the helicobacter pylori multiepitope vaccine. The helicobacterpylori multiepitope vaccine can induce an organism to generate T cell immune response and high-titred specific antibody humoral immune response aiming at the urease A and B bi-subunit, and can be used for preventing and treating helicobacter pylori infection related diseases.

title: MEDICAL DIAGNOSTIC DEVICE
text: MEDICAL DIAGNOSTIC DEVICE. The present invention relates to a medical diagnostic device with a cellular biosensor which detects urea and uric acid by means of a synthetic genetic circuit essentially consisting of transcriptional regulator and bio-sensing module.

title: CK20 antigen, hybridoma cell strain, monoclonal antibody, and application thereof
text: CK20 antigen, hybridoma cell strain, monoclonal antibody, and application thereof. The invention relates to a CK20 antigen, a hybridoma cell strain, a monoclonal antibody, and application thereof. High-variation head and tail sequences of CK20 are selected according to the characteristics of a cytokeratin sequence and connected by adopting connecting components, gene synthesis is carried out according to the sequence of head-connecting-components-tail, and a prokaryotic expression vector is inserted, expressed and purified to obtain the CK20 antigen; and the hybridoma cell strain C7C11 capable of stably secreting the mouse anti-human CK20 monoclonal antibody is finally obtained by using the CK20 antigen as an immunogen to immunize a BALB/c mouse, fusing spleen cells of the mouse with SP2/0 cells, obtaining monoclonal cells by using a limited dilution method and screeningpositive hybridoma cells by using an indirect ELISA method, and the preservation number is CCTCC NO: C2020201. The monoclonal antibody is prepared through induction in an animal body, and the specificity and the sensitivity of the monoclonal antibody are verified through Western Blot and immunohistochemistry (IHC). The CK20 monoclonal antibody provided has better specificity and sensitivity, andcan be used for preparing a CK20 in-vitro immunoassay reagent or a kit.

title: Recombinant protein of paralichthys olivaceus complement C1q-C chain and application thereof
text: Recombinant protein of paralichthys olivaceus complement C1q-C chain and application thereof. The invention relates to the field of molecular biology, in particular to a recombinant protein of a complement C1q-C chain (Complement C1q-C chain, PoC1q-C) of fish (paralichthys olivaceus) and an application of the recombinant protein of the complement C1q-C chain (PoC1q-C) of the fish (paralichthys olivaceus). The amino acid sequence of the recombinant protein is as shown in SEQ ID No. 1, and the nucleotide sequence for coding the recombinant protein is as shown in SEQ ID No. 2. The preparation method of the recombinant protein comprises the following steps: carrying out prokaryotic codon optimization on a paralichthys olivaceus complement C1q-C chain coding sequence to obtain a nucleotide sequence Seq-PoC1q-C, carrying out gene synthesis on the nucleotide sequence Seq-PoC1q-C to construct a PoC1q-C expression plasmid, transforming the PoC1q-C expression plasmid to escherichia coli BL21 (DE3), and carrying out induced expression and purification to obtain the recombinant protein. Bacteria are inhibited from infecting fish bodies, and the phagocytic ability of peripheral blood leucocytes is promoted. The obtained protein has application potential in bacterial infection resistance.

title: Helicobacter pylori multivalent epitope vaccine and preparation method thereof
text: Helicobacter pylori multivalent epitope vaccine and preparation method thereof. The present invention provides a Helicobacter pylori multivalent epitope vaccine, wherein the activity is a polypeptide, and the polypeptide comprises urease subunit A, urease subunit B, adhesin HpaA, heat shock protein HSP60 advantage Th, B cell epitope or segment, neutrophil activating protein NAP and cholera toxin subunit B. According to the present invention, the artificial gene is synthesized through the gene synthesis technology, and comprises the gene sequences of urease subunit A, urease subunit B, adhesin HpaA, heat shock protein HSP60 advantage Th, B cell epitope or segment and neutrophil activating protein NAP, the artificial gene is coupled to cholera toxin subunit B gene to form a fusion gene, the fusion gene is expressed through escherichia coli, and protein purification is performed to obtain the multivalent epitope vaccine; and the multivalent epitope vaccine can stimulate the body to produce the T cell immune response and the antibody humoral immunoresponse against urease, adhesin HpaA, heat shock protein HSP60 and neutrophil activating protein NAP, and can be used for prevention and treatment of Helicobacter pylori infection-related diseases.

title: CROSS-SPECIES-SPECIFIC PSCAXCD3, CD19XCD3, C-METXCD3, ENDOSIALINXCD3, EPCAMXC D3, IGF-1RXCD3 OR FAPALPHA XCD3 BISPECIFIC SINGLE CHAIN ANTIBODY
text: CROSS-SPECIES-SPECIFIC PSCAXCD3, CD19XCD3, C-METXCD3, ENDOSIALINXCD3, EPCAMXC D3, IGF-1RXCD3 OR FAPALPHA XCD3 BISPECIFIC SINGLE CHAIN ANTIBODY

title: Recombinant mycoplasma hyopneumoniae IgG binding protein, expression vector, engineering bacterium, application and subunit vaccine
text: Recombinant mycoplasma hyopneumoniae IgG binding protein, expression vector, engineering bacterium, application and subunit vaccine. The invention provides a recombinant mycoplasma hyopneumoniae IgG binding protein, an expression vector, an engineering bacterium, application and a subunit vaccine, and relates to the technical field of biological medicines. According to the invention, a sequence which is most related to a classical MIB sequence is selected for codon optimization and then complete gene synthesis is carried out, and the preparation of soluble MIB protein is finally realized in a prokaryotic expression system by optimizing expression conditions, so that the obtained recombinant MIB protein is proved to be capable of identifying in vitro and being combined with IgG protein and has biological activity; the recombinant MIB protein is purified and then quantitatively prepared into a 1mg/mL protein solution, the protein solution and a Freund's adjuvant are mixed according to the ratio of 1: 1 to immunize BALB/c mice, through humoral immunity and cellular immunity related detection, the result proves that after animals are immunized by the recombinant MIB protein, the organisms can be stimulated to generate specific humoral immunity and cellular immunity effects; therefore, the protein can be used as a swine mycoplasmal pneumonia gene engineering subunit vaccine antigen.

title: Bacteriostatic protein and encoding gene and application thereof and strain method
text: Bacteriostatic protein and encoding gene and application thereof and strain method. The present invention discloses a bacteriostatic protein and an encoding gene and an application thereof and a strain method. Chicken lysozyme is used as a starting template, an amino acid sequence ofscorpion-derived antimicrobial peptide IsCT is superposed to an N terminal of the chicken lysozyme protein through rational design, and meanwhile, an antioxidant peptide joint and an amino acid jointare directionally introduced to obtain a mutant LyIc; then according to a codon preference of pichia pastoris, a whole-gene synthesis technology is adopted to obtain a gene lysIs corresponding to themutant LyIc and a nucleotide sequence of the lysIs is shown as SEQ ID NO.1; then the lysozyme mutant gene lysIs is linked into an expression vector pPICZ alpha A, the vector is transferred into pichia pastoris X33, the recombinant microorganisms are subjected to fermentation culture and the lysozyme mutant LyIc is separated and purified; and finally, bacteriostatic effects of the lysozyme mutantLyIc and original lysozyme on common pathogenic bacteria are compared and analyzed. The lysozyme mutant LyIc effectively improves the bacteriostatic effect on common pathogenic bacteria (escherichia coli, salmonella and staphylococcus aureus) and lays foundation for wide application of the chicken lysozyme.

title: Bacillus circulans chitoanase as well as preparation method and application thereof
text: Bacillus circulans chitoanase as well as preparation method and application thereof. The invention discloses bacillus circulans chitoanase as well as a preparation method and application thereof. According to the preference of pichia pastoris codons, a chitoanase coding gene sequencein bacillus circulans is obtained by utilizing a whole gene synthesis method; an optimized nucleic acid sequence is shown as SEQ ID NO. 2. Furthermore, efficient secreting expression is carried out onan optimized chitoanase coding gene by utilizing a pichia pastoris expression system to obtain the bacillus circulans chitoanase; an amino acid sequence of the bacillus circulans chitoanase is shownas SEQ ID NO. 1. The chitoanase disclosed by the invention has relatively high hydrolysis activity on a chitosan substrate and a crude enzyme solution produced by shake-flask fermentation has a hydrolysis capability of degrading 5g of chitosan by utilizing 1mL of the crude enzyme solution (with the protein content of about 0.77mg); when the chitosan with the same amount is degraded, about 150mg ofneutral proteinase obtained from bacillus subtilis is needed; the efficiency is theoretically improved by about 200 times; the bacillus circulans chitoanase has a very good industrial application prospect.

title: A SEAMLESS CLONING PROCEDURE WITH STATIC RECOVERY PERIOD
text: A SEAMLESS CLONING PROCEDURE WITH STATIC RECOVERY PERIOD.
