The invention relates to a seamless cloning method, comprising a single assembly step of two or more polynucleotides, e.g., a plasmid vector and a gene insert, conducted at 58 to 100°C, the preferred temperature(s) being the same or greater than a particular one of the following temperatures: 58 °C, 59 °C, 60 °C, 61 °C, 62 °C, 63 °C, 64 °C, 65 °C, 66 °C, 67 °C, 68 °C, 69 °C, 70 °C, 71 °C, 72 °C, 73 °C, 74 °C, 75 °C, 76 °C, 77 °C, 78 °C, 79 °C, 80 °C, 84 °C, 88 °C, 92 °C, 96 °C and 100 °C., and a transformation into chemically competent cells for covalent linking, preferably including a static recovery period.

title: Recombinant vector and porcine fibroblast line for resisting African swine fever virus transgene as well as construction method and application of recombinant vector and porcine fibroblast line
text: Recombinant vector and porcine fibroblast line for resisting African swine fever virus transgene as well as construction method and application of recombinant vector and porcine fibroblast line. The invention belongs to the technical field of gene editing, and particularly relates to an anti-African swine fever virus transgene recombinant vector and a porcine fibroblast line as well as a construction method and application thereof. The preparation method comprises the following steps: carrying out double enzyme digestion on pCAG-pH11 and px330 vectors by using NOTI and SnaBI, and connecting two fragments subjected to double enzyme digestion to obtain a pCAG-intermediate vector; according to the present invention, the pCAG-intermediate vector is subjected to double enzyme digestion by using ASCI and SnaBI, the gene synthesis vector containing four gRNAs such as ASFV CP204L, pS273R, B646L and I215L is connected with the pCAG-intermediate vector subjected to double enzyme digestion to obtain the vector pX330-pH11-gRNA-anti-AFSV, and the vector pX330-pH11-gRNA-anti-AFSV is transfected into the porcine primary fibroblast to obtain the successfully constructed ASFV resistant transgenic porcine fibroblast cell line, and the important significance is provided for the research of ASFV and the research and development of drugs.

title: Self-assembled calcium chelate keratin RK35DE and preparation method thereof
text: Self-assembled calcium chelate keratin RK35DE and preparation method thereof. The invention relates to the technical field of recombinant protein, in particular to self-assembled calcium chelate keratin RK35DE and a preparation method thereof. According to the invention, the self-assembled calcium-chelated keratin RK35DE is obtained through protein design, whole-gene synthesis, vector construction and escherichia coli expression. The keratin RK35DE is subjected to self-assembly research, and the TEM image of the keratin RK35DE shows that the keratin RK35DE has self-assembly capability. In a calcium chelating ability experiment, compared with that before recombination, the self-assembled calcium-chelated keratin RK35DE disclosed by the invention has a remarkable chelating effect on calcium ions. In the prior art, keratin with self-assembly capability lacks effective coordination capability of forming stable chelates with calcium ions in hydroxyapatite, so that stable compounds are difficult to form and exert effects. The self-assembled calcium chelate keratin researched and developed by the scheme provides a new thought for researching a new hydroxyapatite tooth restoration material, and has an ideal popularization and application prospect.

title: Coronavirus fusion protein, and preparation method and application thereof
text: Coronavirus fusion protein, and preparation method and application thereof. The invention belongs to the technical field of antigen preparation processes, and particularly relates to a coronavirus fusion protein, and a preparation method and application thereof. According tothe coronavirus fusion protein provided by the invention, the nucleotide sequence of the RBD structural domain of a 2019-nCoV S protein and the nucleotide sequence of a 2019-nCoV N protein are subjected to complete gene synthesis and then are constructed into an insect cell expression vector to prepare a recombinant plasmid containing the RBD structural domain sequence of the 2019-nCoV N protein and the RBD structural domain sequence of the 2019-nCoV S protein, escherichia coli is transformed by the recombinant plasmid to obtain a recombinant plasmid containing the gene sequence of the fusionprotein, and host cells are transfected by the recombinant plasmid to complete then fusion expression of the RBD structural domains of the 2019-nCoV N protein and the 2019-nCoV S protein. The expression process of the fusion protein is simple, meanwhile, and when the fusion protein is applied to a 2019-nCoV antibody detection kit, the sensitivity of the detection kit is remarkably improved, the specificity is also improved, the detection rate is efficiently increased, and the overall use performance is more optimized.

title: Process preparation method for optimizing yeast
text: Process preparation method for optimizing yeast. The invention discloses a process preparation method for yeast optimization, which comprises the following steps: constructing a carrier: predicting a target gene signal peptide, removing a signal peptide sequence, performing codon optimization according to a pichia pastoris expression system, and performing gene synthesis to pPICZ alpha A; the signal peptide of the target gene is predicted by adopting SignalP4.0 and SignalP5.1, and the signal peptide of the target gene is predicted by adopting SignalP5.1; when codon optimization is carried out on a pichia pastoris expression system, a SacI restriction enzyme cutting site is avoided; when the gene is synthesized to pPICZ alpha A, the target gene is close to a carrier alpha-factor, and 6 * His is carried at the C end; the defects of an escherichia coli expression system are overcome through yeast expression, rapid breeding and high-density fermentation can be achieved, and protein can be modified and processed after translation. Secondly, the target protein is secreted into the culture medium, and the subsequent purification process is greatly simplified. Finally, the method is suitable for protein expression of eukaryotes, the human epidermal growth factor is eukaryotic protein, and yeast expression can improve the yield and reduce the cost.

title: Prokaryotic recombinant expression and purification method of procalcitonin mutant protein
text: Prokaryotic recombinant expression and purification method of procalcitonin mutant protein. The invention relates to a prokaryotic recombinant expression and purification method of procalcitonin mutant protein. The method comprises: gene cloning of recombinant procalcitonin protein, construction of a recombinant PCT expression vector, construction of a recombinant PCT expression bacterium, induced expression of the recombinant PCT expression bacterium and separation and purification of recombinant PCT. A PCT protein structure is analyzed, a natural PCT protein sequence is modified, sequence replacement on an easily degradable site is carried out (-Ala58-Ala59- and -Ala92-Ala93-Ala94-Ala95- replace -Lys58-Arg59- and -Gly92-Lys93-Lys94-Arg95-), the high-purity recombinant PCT protein with natural immunogenicity is prepared, the stability of the PCT protein is improved, and meanwhile, the original immunogenicity is maintained; a gene synthesis technology is used for replacing an original PCR technology to prepare a target gene, so that the accuracy and the working efficiency ofgene operation are improved, and the experimental period of expression bacterium construction is shortened; and a complete protein expression and purification system is designed, and the obtained mutant protein can be used as a standard antigen for preparing a monoclonal antibody or used as a standard substance for immunological detection of PCT.

title: MICROBIAL STEM CELL TECHNOLOGY
text: MICROBIAL STEM CELL TECHNOLOGY. The present disclosure relates to microbial stem cell technology that enables a growing microbial culture to stably maintain two or more distinct cell types in a ratio that can be genetically programmed and/or dynamically controlled during cultivation. It is contemplated that embodiments described herein can be utilized to increase product yield in microbial fermentations and advanced engineering of biomaterials using genetically engineered microbial cells, among others.

title: Preparation method of anti-AIDS drug Azanavir intermediate
text: Preparation method of anti-AIDS drug Azanavir intermediate. The invention discloses a preparation method of an anti-AIDS drug Azanavir intermediate and belongs to the technical field of medical biology. The method comprises the steps of adjusting the secondarystructure and codon preference of a gene by means of a full-gene synthesis approach, designing the length of a primer, synthesizing the primer, dissolving the obtained primer in double-distilled water, and adding the obtained solution into a following reaction system; amplifying the prepared PCR reaction system, performing gel cutting purification on DNA fragments obtained by PCR, and selecting amonoclonal sample for sequencing, wherein a correct DNA sequence is shown as SEQ ID NO.1 and named as Sst-1, and an amino acid sequence corresponding to the DNA sequence is shown as SEQ ID NO.2. Thepreparation method has the advantages that the reaction condition is mild, the requirement for equipment is low, high temperature or cooling is not required in a production process, the energy consumption is low, purification is convenient, single-enzyme catalysis is applied in the whole system, the ratio of substrate consumption to NAD consumption reaches 1,540:1, coenzyme circulation frequency is high, and the reaction conditions are mild.

title: Biosynthesis of sulfur-containing amino acid protein CMd and preparation method of high-fluorescence gold nanocluster of sulfur-containing amino acid protein CMd
text: Biosynthesis of sulfur-containing amino acid protein CMd and preparation method of high-fluorescence gold nanocluster of sulfur-containing amino acid protein CMd. The invention mainly relates to a biosynthesis method of sulfur-containing amino acid protein CMd and a preparation method of a high-fluorescence gold nano-cluster of the sulfur-containing amino acid protein CMd, which comprises the following steps: a) obtaining CMd protein granules through gene synthesis, and expressing and purifying the CMd protein; and b) reacting the CMd with sodium chloroaurate to prepare the high-fluorescence CMd-gold nano-cluster. And c) the storage stability of the obtained high-fluorescence CMd-gold nano-cluster is relatively good. And D) applying the high-fluorescence CMd-gold nanocluster to detection of heavy metal ions Hg < 2 + > and Cu < 2 + >. According to the CMd-gold nano-cluster prepared by the method, the range of raw materials for synthesizing the gold nano-cluster by taking protein as a template is widened, and meanwhile, the CMd-gold nano-cluster is good in fluorescence effect, small in protein dosage, long in luminescence time, high in storage stability and capable of carrying out sensitive and specific detection on Hg < 2 + > and Cu < 2 + >.

title: Anti-inflammatory CD209 polypeptide fragment and application thereof in resisting fatty liver disease inflammation
text: Anti-inflammatory CD209 polypeptide fragment and application thereof in resisting fatty liver disease inflammation. The invention discloses construction of a CD209 active polypeptide and fatty liver disease inflammation resisting activity of the CD209 active polypeptide. The construction sequence of the CD209 active polypeptide comprises an amino acid sequence and a nucleotide sequence, the amino acid sequence is as shown in SEQ ID NO: 1, and the nucleotide sequence is as shown in SEQ ID NO: 2; a construction method of a CD209 active polypeptide expression vector comprises the following steps: carrying out gene synthesis on a CD209 active polypeptide coding cDNA sequence, and connecting the CD209 active polypeptide coding cDNA sequence with a linearized pcDNA-Flag eukaryotic expression vector by using T4DNA ligase to obtain a recombinant plasmid; and transfecting macrophages separated from the liver with the recombinant plasmid. The antagonism of the CD209 active polypeptide on the proinflammatory activity of macrophages is clarified by utilizing a plurality of functional means, and the mechanism of the CD209 active polypeptide is clarified to be related to the inhibition of the expression of TLR4 and downstream proinflammatory factors. The CD209 active polypeptide is proved to have the therapeutic value of antagonizing the inflammatory activity of macrophages and inhibiting the inflammatory injury of fatty liver diseases on the aspect of cell biology.

title: Design and preparation method and application of echinococcus multilocularis subunit vaccine LTB-Emy162
text: Design and preparation method and application of echinococcus multilocularis subunit vaccine LTB-Emy162. The invention relates to a design and preparation method and an application of an echinococcus multilocularis subunit vaccine LTB-Emy162. The active component of the echinococcus multilocularis subunit vaccine LTB-Emy162 is a polypeptide, which is mainly composed of an echinococcus multilocularis antigen protein Emy162 amino acid sequence and a mucosal immunoadjuvant E. coli heat-labile enterotoxin B subunit (LTB) amino acid sequence. In the invention, the gene sequence of the echinococcus multilocularis subunit vaccine LTB-Emy162 is synthesized through gene synthesis technology, the gene sequence is then linked to an expression vector through dual enzyme-cut, the expression vector then is converted into Arctic Express to perform expression of fusion protein, after purification of the protein, the echinococcus multilocularis subunit vaccine LTB-Emy162 is produced. The echinococcus multilocularis subunit vaccine can induce body to generate T-cell and B-cell immunologic response of the echinococcus multilocularis and humoral immune response of a high-titer specific antibody, and can be applied in prevention and therapy of echinococcus multilocularis infection related diseases.

title: Method for efficiently expressing and preparing external-secretion-type human CA125
text: Method for efficiently expressing and preparing external-secretion-type human CA125. The invention provides a method for efficiently expressing and preparing external-secretion-type human CA125. The method comprises the following steps: (1) synthesizing a DNA sequence of CA125 by utilizing a gene synthesis technology, constructing yeast recombinant expression plasmid PpICZ alpha A-CA125 by adopting gene cloning and a molecular biological method, and carrying out plasmid extraction; (2) carrying out in-vitro expression induction on secretion-type CA125 recombinant protein by adopting a pichia yeast expression system, and optimizing the conditions including the expression time, the inducer addition amount and the like to obtain the optimal induction condition; (3) optimizing ammonium sulfate fractional precipitation, and carrying out separation and purification on the supernate of the fermentation liquor to obtain the CA125 recombinant protein secreted to the extracellular part, wherein the interest protein CA125 nearly completely precipitates out under the 55% of ammonium sulfate concentration. The biological activity of the secretion recombinant protein CA125 prepared by purification is detected by adopting the clinical test electrochemiluminescence method, and the value achieves 802100U/mL.

title: Application of tomato SlAVT6A gene in regulating amino acid transport to influence terpene content and fruit yield of tomato
text: Application of tomato SlAVT6A gene in regulating amino acid transport to influence terpene content and fruit yield of tomato. The invention belongs to the technical field of plant genetic engineering, and particularly relates to application of a tomato SlAVT6A gene in regulating amino acid transport to influence the terpene content and fruit yield of tomatoes. The tomato SlAVT6A gene regulates amino acid to enter a secondary metabolic pathway by influencing amino acid transport in tomato vacuoles, and further influences volatile terpene accumulation and fruit yield. Through a whole genome association analysis (mGWAS) method, the SlAVT6A gene significantly related to the tomato amino acid content is located, further experimental verification finds that the SlAVT6A gene is located in vacuole and has the function of excreting amino acid, the gene is subjected to excess and knock-down expression in a tomato plant, and the tomato amino acid content is remarkably improved. It is found that the amino acid content in tomatoes can be changed by changing the expression quantity of the SlAVT6A gene, synthesis of secondary volatile matter terpenoids and the fruit yield are affected, and the biological function and the application way of the gene are proved.

title: CpG-ODN with specific immunity stimulation function for PRRSV, and application of CpG-ODN
text: CpG-ODN with specific immunity stimulation function for PRRSV, and application of CpG-ODN. The invention relates to the technical field of bioengineering, in particular to CpG-ODN with a specific immunity stimulation function for PRRSV, and application of the CpG-ODN. A nucleotide sequence is disclosed by SEQ ID NO:1, 11 pieces of screened CpG-ODN fragments are used for randomly forming 10 groups of immune activation units, and in addition, the position information of each CpG-ODN fragment in each group of immune activation unit is different; and the 10 groups of immune activation units are inserted into a multi-copy cloning vector Puc19 deriving vector FVX through two digestion sites Xbal and EcoRI, a recombinant plasmid FVX-CpG is constructed through a gene synthesis means, and a preparation containing a recombinant plasmid FVX-CpG is prepared. The preparation has a low production cost, the defects of instability, sequence diversity, individual differences and the like of the CpG-ODN in the body are overcome, the in vivo natural immunologic function of a pig can be induced to be activated, the in vivo acquired immunity function of the pig for PRRSV can be activated, the in vivo immunoreaction induction function of the pig for the PRRSV can be improved, a PRRSV incidence rate is lowered, and a pig breeding cost is lowered.

title: Multifunctional flea salivary protein FS58, preparation method and application of multifunctional flea salivary protein FS58 in preparation of medicine for treating psoriasis
text: Multifunctional flea salivary protein FS58, preparation method and application of multifunctional flea salivary protein FS58 in preparation of medicine for treating psoriasis. The invention discloses a multifunctional flea salivary protein FS58, a preparation method and application thereof in preparation of drugs for treating psoriasis, and belongs to the technical field of biological medicines. The invention provides a multifunctional protein FS58. The amino acid sequence of the multifunctional protein FS58 is as shown in SEQ ID NO. 2. The invention also provides a preparation method, which comprises the following steps: 1, gene synthesis; 2, plasmid construction and amplification; 3, recombinant expression; carrying out 4Ni < + > column affinity chromatography purification; 5, carrying out enzyme digestion and separation on the FS58 fusion protein; 6, carrying out Superdex 75 gel purification; and 7, removing endotoxin. The invention further provides application of the protein FS58 in preparation of drugs for treating psoriasis. The preparation method provided by the invention provides a way and means for efficiently and massively producing FS58. Meanwhile, the FS58 protein disclosed by the invention has the functions of inhibiting angiogenesis, resisting inflammation and regulating immunity in vivo and in vitro, and a new way and means are provided for treating psoriasis.

title: Anchoring polypeptide and photodecarboxylase fusion protein as well as preparation method and application thereof
text: Anchoring polypeptide and photodecarboxylase fusion protein as well as preparation method and application thereof. The invention provides an anchoring polypeptide and photodecarboxylase fusion protein as well as a preparation method and application thereof. The method comprises the following steps: on the basis that McFAP is obtained through gene synthesis, replacing a thylakoid membrane positioning sequence at the 5'end of an McFAP gene with an anchoring polypeptide LCI sequence derived from bacillus subtilis to obtain LCI-McFAP; in addition, an LCI sequence is inserted into the 3'end of the McFAP to obtain McFAP-LCI, and the McFAP-LCI is compared with the previous fusion protein. Escherichia coli BL21 (DE3) is used as a host to express the two fusion proteins. LCI polypeptide configuration and surface amino acid residues are analyzed, a membrane material with negative charges on the surface and a large specific surface area is synthesized, and zymoprotein can be induced and firmly combined to the material without pre-purification of protein. Then, the catalytic preference of the immobilized enzyme to fatty acids with different chain lengths is investigated, the catalytic utilization times of the optimal substrate under experimental conditions are tested, and finally, amplification catalysis is successfully realized by utilizing a continuous flow photoreaction device.

title: Streptomyces thermoviolaceus chitinase as well as preparation method and application thereof
text: Streptomyces thermoviolaceus chitinase as well as preparation method and application thereof. The invention discloses streptomyces thermoviolaceus chitinase (GH18 family) as well as a preparation method and application thereof. According to the streptomyces thermoviolaceus chitinase disclosedby the invention, a full-gene synthesis method is utilized to obtain an encoding gene sequence of chitinase in streptomyces thermoviolaceus, and an optimized nucleotide sequence is shown by SEQ ID NO.2; furthermore, a pichia pastoris expression system is utilized to perform high-efficiency secretory expression on an optimized chitinase encoding gene to obtain the streptomyces thermoviolaceus chitinase (GH18 family), and an amino acid sequence of the streptomyces thermoviolaceus chitinase is shown by SEQ ID NO. 1. The obtained streptomyces thermoviolaceus chitinase (GH18 family) disclosed by the invention has higher hydrolytic activity on a low deacetyiation degree chitosan substrate; crude enzyme liquid generated by fermentation in a shaking flask has hydrolytic capacity of degrading 0.5gof chitosan by 1mL crude enzyme liquid (a protein content is about 0.20mg); however, about 100mg of commercial cellulase is needed to degrade the same amount of chitosan; theoretically, an efficiencyis improved by about 500 times; the streptomyces thermoviolaceus chitinase (GH18 family) has good industrial application prospect.

title: ENGINEERED HERPES SIMPLEX VIRUS-1 (HSV-1) VECTORS AND USES THEREOF
text: ENGINEERED HERPES SIMPLEX VIRUS-1 (HSV-1) VECTORS AND USES THEREOF. Provided herein are engineered HSV-1 vectors comprising a modified HSV-1 genome. The engineered HSV-1 vectors can be used to deliver genetic circuits (e.g., up to 100 kb) to cells in vitro or in vivo. Methods of treating or diagnosing a disease (e.g., cancer) using the engineered HSV-1 vectors described herein are also provided.

title: INHIBITORS OF CRISPR-CAS ASSOCIATED ACTIVITY
text: INHIBITORS OF CRISPR-CAS ASSOCIATED ACTIVITY. The invention relates to Acr proteins capable of regulating CRISPR-Cas associated activity, and their use in regulating CRISPR-Cas associated activity within a biological cell. The invention further provides nucleic acid molecules and self-replications genetic elements adapted for expression of said Acr proteins, or for their co-expression with a cognate CRISPR-Cas system in a biological cell.

title: Synthetic board waste liquid discharge device and gene synthesizer
text: Synthetic board waste liquid discharge device and gene synthesizer. The utility model relates to the technical field of gene synthesis, in particular to a synthetic board waste liquid discharge device and a gene synthesizer, which comprise a liquid pumping plate, the liquid pumping plate is used for being installed at the bottom of a synthetic board, the liquid pumping plate comprises a liquid discharge groove with an opening at the upper end, and lower ports of synthetic holes of the synthetic board are communicated with the liquid discharge groove. The liquid discharging groove is divided into at least two mutually independent sub-grooves with openings in the upper ends, each sub-groove is communicated with a liquid discharging channel, and each liquid discharging channel is provided with a valve piece for controlling the liquid discharging channel to be opened/closed; the discharging device further comprises a negative pressure unit, and the negative pressure unit is communicated with the liquid discharging channel and used for applying negative pressure to the liquid discharging channel. In an assembly state, the synthesis holes in the synthesis plate are divided into at least two groups, and one group of synthesis holes corresponds to one sub-groove; in the liquid discharging process, normal liquid adding operation can still be carried out; in addition, the sub-tanks can be used for independently extracting liquid, and normal synthesis of the synthesis hole groups corresponding to other sub-tanks cannot be interfered.

title: Digital circuit for implementing genetic algorithm, comprises circuit generation algorithm and genetic circuit evaluator, where circuit generation includes random number generator, logic function, two multiplexers and recorder
text: Digital circuit for implementing genetic algorithm, comprises circuit generation algorithm and genetic circuit evaluator, where circuit generation includes random number generator, logic function, two multiplexers and recorder. The circuit comprises a circuit generation algorithm and a genetic circuit evaluator. The circuit generation to understand genetic algorithms includes a random number generator, a logic function, two multiplexers and a recorder. The circuit assessment includes a circuit estimation adaptor, a multiplexer, a recorder and a comparator. The genetic algorithm provides a new configuration in each unit time and estimates the degree of adaptation of the circuit.

title: MYROTHECIUM SP. TRANSFORMATION AND EXPRESSION SYSTEM
text: MYROTHECIUM SP. TRANSFORMATION AND EXPRESSION SYSTEM. The present invention is related to a transformation and an expression system in which Myrothecium sp. host cells are used to express homologous or heterologous proteins or are used to genetically engineer metabolic pathways.

title: D- D- D- D-XYLONATE-RESPONSIVE PROMOTER ARTIFICIAL GENETIC CIRCUITS COMPRISING D-XYLONATE-RESPONSIVE PROMOTER AND METHOD FOR DETECTION OF D-XYLONATE USING ARTIFICIAL GENETIC CIRCUIT
text: D- D- D- D-XYLONATE-RESPONSIVE PROMOTER ARTIFICIAL GENETIC CIRCUITS COMPRISING D-XYLONATE-RESPONSIVE PROMOTER AND METHOD FOR DETECTION OF D-XYLONATE USING ARTIFICIAL GENETIC CIRCUIT. 본 발명은 D-자일론산 반응성 프로모터, 이를 포함하는 D-자일론산 감지용 재설계 유전자 회로 및 이를 이용한 D-자일론산의 감지방법에 관한 것으로, 본 발명에 따른 서열번호 1의 염기서열로 표시되는 프로모터는 D-자일론산에 대한 반응성이 높아 대장균에서 D-자일론산을 감지하기 위한 합성 회로에 적용할 경우, D-자일론산 축적을 조절함으로써 pH 조절을 통해 최종 산물의 생산 수율을 증가시킬 수 있다.

title: Vector capable of continuously secreting LAL, umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell as well as construction method and application of umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell
text: Vector capable of continuously secreting LAL, umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell as well as construction method and application of umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell. The invention belongs to the technical field of biology, and particularly relates to a carrier capable of continuously secreting LAL, an umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell and a construction method and application of the umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell. The vector capable of continuously secreting the LAL comprises a CMV enhancer, a CMV promoter, an HSA gene, an LAL gene, a T2A gene and a PuroR gene. The construction method comprises the following steps: constructing a pLVSO2-HSA-LAL plasmid, carrying out gene synthesis on an HSA-LAL sequence, and inserting the HSA-LAL sequence into a pLVSO2 vector. The method comprises the following steps: constructing a pT2-HB-CMV-HSA-LAL-T2A-PuroR plasmid, and amplifying a CMV enhancer, a CMV promoter, an HSA gene, an LAL gene, a T2A gene and a PuroR gene by taking a pLVSO2-HSA-LAL plasmid as a template, and recombining to a pT2-HB plasmid. The pT2-HB-CMV-HSA-LAL-T2A-PuroR vector disclosed by the invention is electrically transferred to umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells, so that a cell line capable of continuously secreting and expressing LAL is obtained, and a foundation is laid for further application of the vector in cell therapy of lysosomal acid lipase deficiency (LAL-D).

title: Construction and identification method for taenia solium recombinant lactococcus lactis secretory expression system
text: Construction and identification method for taenia solium recombinant lactococcus lactis secretory expression system. The invention discloses a construction and identification method for a taenia solium recombinant lactococcus lactis secretory expression system. The method comprises the steps of gene synthesis and primer design, construction and identification of recombinant plasmid pMG36e-SP-TSOL18, construction and identification of taenia solium recombinant pMG36e-SP-TSOL18/L.lactis, and protein expression and identification. The method comprises the following specific steps: performing gene optimization by taking lactococcus lactis MG1363 as a host system; synthesizing a target gene SP-TSOL18; connecting to a pMG36e carrier through double enzyme digestion; constructing an intracellular expression vector pMG36e-SP-TSOL18; performing transformation by electroporation to obtain L.lactis MG1363; successfully constructing the taenia solium lactococcus lactis expression system pMG36e-SP-TSOL18/L.lactis MG1363. PCR (Polymerase Chain Reaction) identification, SDS-PAGE and Western bolt analysis SP-TSOL18 are performed, so that intracellular and extracellular target protein expressions are realized; through AKTA protein purification identification and stability analysis, pMG36e-SP-TSOL18 plasmids are continuously passed for 20 generations in the L.lactis MG1363, so that higher genetic stability is achieved, and an effective reference is provided for the development and utilization of cysticercosis cellulosae vaccines.

title: Method for transforming thermal stability of bFGF (basic fibroblast growth factor)
text: Method for transforming thermal stability of bFGF (basic fibroblast growth factor). The invention belongs to the technical field of fibroblast growth factors, and discloses a bFGF thermal stability modification method, which comprises the following steps: S1, target gene synthesis; the method comprises the following steps: selecting bFGF protein, and mutating four heparin binding sites from K to N to obtain a bFGFK mutant; s2, designing and synthesizing a primer; the method comprises the following steps: designing a primer by taking a gene sequence of a bFGFK mutant as a template; respectively inserting two enzyme cutting points at two ends of the primer; the primer comprises an upstream primer and a downstream primer; s3, target gene amplification; carrying out PCR (Polymerase Chain Reaction) amplification by taking a gene sequence of the bFGFK mutant as a DNA (Deoxyribose Nucleic Acid) template, and purifying for later use; s4, constructing a recombinant plasmid vector; and S5, expressing and purifying the recombinant bFGFK protein. According to the invention, lysine (K) at heparin binding sites 128, 134, 138 and 144 of bFGF is mutated into asparagine (N) through a method of carrying out amino acid replacement at a specific site, so that bFGF K128/134/138/144N recombinant protein with improved thermal stability is obtained, and the biological activity of the bFGF K128/134/138/144N recombinant protein is not changed.

title: Method for preparing centipede peptide toxin SSD609 with physiological function similar to natural state
text: Method for preparing centipede peptide toxin SSD609 with physiological function similar to natural state. The invention relates to a method for preparing centipede peptide toxin SSD609 with a physiological function similar to a natural state. The method comprises the following steps: preparing a DNA (Deoxyribonucleic Acid) sequence of polypeptide toxin SSD609 according to a gene synthesis method, inserting a Factor-Xa enzyme cutting site in front of the DNA sequence according to a PCR (Polymerase Chain Reaction) method and cloning to a pET28 carrier with an His6 affinity tag and an SUMO solubilization tag; converting plasmid into escherichia coli and inducing a high-efficiency expression through isopropyl thiogalactoside; purifying by using a nickel column after bacteria is broken and cutting an His6-SUMO fusion tag and the SSD609 by using Factor-Xa enzyme, reversely hanging the nickel column to remove His6-SUMO; further purifying the SSD609 by using an HPLC (High Performance Liquid Chromatography), adding a certain proportion of GSH and GSSG, so that a disulfide bond in the SSD609 is newly connected to a thermodynamic stable state, and the conformation becomes uniform; then purifying the SSD609 by using the HPLC and freeze-drying for standby use. The method provided by the invention is simple and convenient to operate, high in yield and purity, and relatively low in cost, and the centipede peptide toxin SSD609 has the physiological function similar to the natural state. Therefore, the method can be used for large-scale preparation of the centipede toxin.

title: ULTRA-HIGH CAPACITY DNA SYNTHESIS TO POWER FUNCTIONALLY GENERATIVE BIOLOGICAL DESIGN
text: ULTRA-HIGH CAPACITY DNA SYNTHESIS TO POWER FUNCTIONALLY GENERATIVE BIOLOGICAL DESIGN. The present disclosure includes methods and compositions that provide ultra-low cost DNA synthesis in order to facilitate the scale of functional testing of diverse proteins. In some aspects, the present methods and compositions comprise design and synthesis of desired product polynucleotides.

title: DNA assembly method and application thereof
text: DNA assembly method and application thereof. The invention relates to a plasmid, a DNA assembly method and application of the method in recombinant bacteria. The plasmid comprises single contiguous IIP and IIS type restriction endonuclease recognition sites. The plasmid comprehensively uses the characteristics of IIP and IIS type restriction endonucleases to realize recursive cycle assembly, traceless assembly and repetitive sequence assembly.

title: Multi-antigen expressed circRNA vaccine and preparation method thereof
text: Multi-antigen expressed circRNA vaccine and preparation method thereof. The invention provides a circRNA vaccine expressed by multiple antigens and a preparation method of the circRNA vaccine, and belongs to the technical field of biological medicines, and the circRNA vaccine comprises a circular RNA (circRNA) sequence capable of simultaneously expressing two or more antigen sequences. The circRNA vaccine provided by the invention can be used for simultaneously expressing any two or three of HBV antigens HBsAg and HBcAg and therapeutic polypeptide, and also can be used for expressing a corresponding RSV (Respiratory Syndrome Virus) trimer F antigen. The circRNA vaccine for mammal injection is obtained through in-vitro gene synthesis, in-vitro transcription reaction, linear RNA (Ribonucleic Acid) cyclization, hydrogel/amino acid nanoparticle preparation and the like. HBVamp can be reduced for healthy people; the traditional Chinese medicine composition can be used for relieving and eliminating the symptoms, stabilizing the illness state, reducing the virus transmission, reducing the dosage, shortening the treatment course, delaying/reversing the disease course and improving the prognosis and life quality aiming at infected persons, and can be used for relieving and eliminating the symptoms, stabilizing the illness state, reducing the virus transmission, reducing the dosage, shortening the treatment course and improving the prognosis and life quality.

title: METHODS OF PURIFYING DNA FOR GENE SYNTHESIS
text: METHODS OF PURIFYING DNA FOR GENE SYNTHESIS. Provided herein are methods of purifying nucleic acids (e.g., DNA) for gene synthesis using combinations of nucleases. Also provided are improved products for use in the production of RNA.

title: MODULAR DNA ASSEMBLY SYSTEM
text: MODULAR DNA ASSEMBLY SYSTEM. A modular and hierarchical DNA assembly platform for synthetic biology is described. This enabling technology, termed MIDAS (for Modular Idempotent DNA Assembly System), can precisely assemble multiple DNA fragments in a single reaction using a standardised assembly design. It can be used to build genes from libraries of sequence-verified, reusable parts and to assemble multiple genes in a single vector. We describe the design and use of MIDAS, and its application in the reconstruction of the metabolic pathway for production of paspaline, a key intermediate in the biosynthesis of a range of indole diterpenes — a class of economically important secondary metabolites produced by several species of filamentous fungi.

title: Constructing very long DNA sequences from synthetic DNA molecules
text: Constructing very long DNA sequences from synthetic DNA molecules. A method of fabricating a DNA molecule of user-defined sequence. A multiplicity of DNA sequence segments that will comprise the DNA molecule of user-defined sequence are preselected using computational techniques to break said user-defined sequence into fragments of defined size. The multiplicity of DNA sequence segments are combined with at least one polymerase enzyme. The multiplicity of DNA sequence segments join to produce the DNA molecule of user-defined sequence.

title: HIGH-THROUGHPUT IN VIVO DNA RECOMBINEERING
text: HIGH-THROUGHPUT IN VIVO DNA RECOMBINEERING. Provided herein are methods and compositions for truncating and, optionally, joining polynucleotides. The methods bypass inefficient cloning methods and the requirement for expensive enzymes by using in vivo homologous recombination in conjunction with specific endonuclease digestion. The methods may further be used to assemble long fragments of DNA from existing DNA constructs or libraries or to generate novel DNA libraries.

title: Nicotinamide nucleoside kinase whole yeast cells and process for biocatalytically synthesizing NMN from nicotinamide nucleoside kinase whole yeast cells
text: Nicotinamide nucleoside kinase whole yeast cells and process for biocatalytically synthesizing NMN from nicotinamide nucleoside kinase whole yeast cells. The invention provides nicotinamide nucleoside kinase whole yeast cells and a process for biocatalytically synthesizing NMN by the nicotinamide nucleoside kinase whole yeast cells, and relates to the technical field of enzyme engineering. The process for biocatalytically synthesizing the NMN comprise the following steps: S1, constructing a nicotinamide nucleoside kinase recombinant display expression vector of saccharomyces cerevisiae: designing an encoding DNA sequence of NrK1 according to an amino acid sequence (SEQ ID NO: 1) of an NrK1 mutant, adopting preferred codons of the saccharomyces cerevisiae as codons, and synthesizing encoding DNA (SEQ ID NO: 2) of NrK1 in a whole-gene synthesis manner; and according to the nicotinamide nucleoside kinase whole yeast cell and the NMN biocatalytic synthesis process thereof, the nicotinamide nucleoside kinase whole yeast cell and the NMN biocatalytic synthesis process thereof can anchor and express high-activity enzymes capable of catalyzing NMN synthesis from different biological sources on the cell wall surface of saccharomyces cerevisiae through a genetic engineering technology; and the whole-yeast engineering cell enzyme capable of efficiently synthesizing the NMN is formed.

title: Molecular improved apoptin (30-121) as well as preparation method and application thereof
text: Molecular improved apoptin (30-121) as well as preparation method and application thereof. The invention discloses molecular improved apoptin (30-121) as well as a preparation method and application thereof. The apoptin sequence has an amino acid sequence shown in SEQ ID No.2 and a nucleotide sequence shown in SEQ ID No.1. In the early stage of the method, by virtue of a gene splicing method, an oligonucleotide sequence (Oligo1-Oligo20) and PCR (polymerase chain reaction) amplificationprimers p1 and p2, which are used for gene synthesis, are designed according to a VP3 gene sequence in an NCBI (National Center for Biotechnology Information) gene bank; totally 20 primers of the Oligo1 to the Oligo20 are overlapped in pairs to cover the whole VP3 gene coding area; by virtue of a serial steps such as a step of designing primers of VP3 (30-121), namely -N terminals and -C terminalprimers, according to the gene sequence of the VP3, a step of designing the primer sequence according to a complementary base pairing principle, a gas chromatography (GC) ratio and restriction enzymecutting site related factors, and the like, the required amplification product, namely the molecular improved apoptin VP3 (30-121) is prepared. Experimental results indicate that the molecular improved apoptin has an excellent application prospect in the aspect of preparing anti-tumor medicaments.

title: M-cell targeted echinococcus multilocularis lactic acid bacteria carrier trivalent multi-epitope vaccine as well as preparation method and application thereof
text: M-cell targeted echinococcus multilocularis lactic acid bacteria carrier trivalent multi-epitope vaccine as well as preparation method and application thereof. The invention relates to the technical field of biological medicine. The invention provides an M cell targeted echinococcus multilocularis lactic acid bacteria carrier trivalent multi-epitope vaccine as well as a preparation method and application thereof. A nucleotide sequence is shown as SEQ ID NO.1. The invention further provides a preparation method of the M cell targeted echinococcus multilocularis lactic acid bacteria carrier trivalent multi-epitope vaccine. On the basis of an echinococcus multilocularis multi-epitope vaccine E14L, an SAM gene sequence is added, recombinant plasmids are synthesized through a gene synthesis technology, and the recombinant plasmids are electrically transferred to lactococcus lactis NZ9000, so that the recombinant lactic acid bacteria vaccine is constructed. The recombinant lactic acid bacteria vaccine prepared by the method can secrete and express the recombinant antigen SAM-E14L, can display the recombinant antigen SAM-E14L on the surface of lactic acid bacteria, and delivers the recombinant antigen SAM-E14L to gastrointestinal tract M cells in a targeting manner to generate specific mucosal immune response aiming at echinococcosis multilocularis; and the lactic acid bacteria delivery carrier can protect the recombinant antigen protein from being degraded by a severe environment of the gastrointestinal tract, so that the immune effect of the vaccine is further improved.

title: Method of achieving high-accuracy site-directed gene knock-out in coprinus cinereus
text: Method of achieving high-accuracy site-directed gene knock-out in coprinus cinereus. The invention provides a method of achieving high-accuracy site-directed gene knock-out in coprinus cinereus. The method includes the steps of: 1) acquiring upstream and downstream sequences of a target knock-out gene through methods of polymerase chain reaction or total gene synthesis, and connecting the upstream and downstream sequences in the opposite direction of the original gene, and performing insertion onto a vector plasmid to construct an annular clone vector; 2) performing linear treatment to the annular clone vector to obtain linear double-chain DNA; and 3) processing the linear double-chain DNA through a 5'-to-3' exonuclease to form a linear 3'-terminal protruded long cohesive end double-chain DNA, thereby converting cells of coprinus cinereus. In the method, through structure and type of a special exogenous DNA, high-accuracy site-directed gene knock-out is achieved in the coprinus cinereus with site-directed gene knock-out accuracy rate reaching 25-70%. The method greatly reduces work load of the site-directed gene knock-out of the coprinus cinereus and has huge application potential in the fields of functional gene researching of coprinus cinereus and construction of a gene engineering bacterial strain.

title: Fusion protein LSDV090-133 and application thereof in detection of bovine nodular skin disease virus antibody
text: Fusion protein LSDV090-133 and application thereof in detection of bovine nodular skin disease virus antibody. The invention belongs to the field of molecular biology, and discloses a fusion protein LSDV090-133 and application thereof in detection of bovine nodular skin disease virus (LSDV) infection antibodies and identification of vaccine immune antibodies. Specific polypeptides aiming at bovine nodular skin disease virus infection serum and goat pox attenuated vaccine immune serum are screened by utilizing a phage display library, three polypeptides specific to the bovine nodular skin disease virus infection serum are selected through bioinformatics analysis, are connected to a pET28a vector in series through flexible peptides and are sent to a company for gene synthesis, and the specific polypeptides are obtained. The LSDV090-133 fusion protein is obtained through prokaryotic expression, and the molecular weight is about 20 kDa. Through verification, the protein has strong reactogenicity with LSDV infected bovine positive serum, the gray value of the protein is obviously stronger than that of immune serum immunized with goat pox attenuated vaccine for 60 dpi, and the protein is a potential new target for differential diagnosis and can be used for differential diagnosis of antibody reaction of LSDV infection and goat pox attenuated vaccine immunization. And a reference tool is provided for serological diagnosis and prevention and control of bovine nodular skin diseases.

title: Vector capable of continuously secreting ultra-high molecular weight hyaluronic acid, umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell as well as construction method and application of umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell
text: Vector capable of continuously secreting ultra-high molecular weight hyaluronic acid, umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell as well as construction method and application of umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell. The invention discloses a carrier capable of continuously secreting ultra-high molecular weight hyaluronic acid, an umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell and a construction method and application thereof, and belongs to the technical field of biology. The carrier capable of continuously secreting the ultra-high molecular weight hyaluronic acid comprises a CMV (cytomegalovirus) promoter and a human HAS2 gene or a naked mole rat Has2 gene. The construction method comprises the following steps: carrying out gene cloning on a human HAS2 sequence, carrying out gene synthesis on a Has2 sequence of a naked mole rat, inserting the Has2 sequence into a pLVSIN-CMV-HAS2 vector, constructing a human pLVSIN-CMV-HAS2 virus shuttle plasmid and a naked mole rat pLVSIN-CMV-Has2 virus shuttle plasmid, co-transferring a virus packaging plasmid (gag, VSV-G) and the shuttle plasmid (pLVSIN-CMV-HAS2 or pLVSIN-CMV-Has2) into a virus packaging cell (HEK-293T), carrying out virus packaging, and collecting a virus supernatant. Umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells are infected with the human HAS2 virus or the Has2 virus of naked mole rats respectively, the umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells capable of secreting a large amount of ultra-high molecular weight hyaluronic acid (the molecular weight is larger than 6 MDa) are obtained, and a foundation is laid for further application of the method to anti-inflammatory cell treatment of clinical diseases.

title: RNA (Ribonucleic Acid) internal reference for sequencing and quantifying human transcriptome as well as preparation method and application of RNA internal reference
text: RNA (Ribonucleic Acid) internal reference for sequencing and quantifying human transcriptome as well as preparation method and application of RNA internal reference. The invention discloses an RNA (Ribonucleic Acid) internal reference for human transcriptome sequencing quantification as well as a preparation method and application thereof, the RNA internal reference comprises an RNA internal reference mixture 1 and an RNA internal reference mixture 2, and the RNA internal reference is formed by gradient mixing of RNA fragments designed aiming at 51 personal genes and simulating human mRNA (Messenger Ribonucleic Acid). The preparation method comprises the following steps: carrying out gene synthesis on a coding sequence of a human gene after mirror image inversion, carrying out in-vitro transcription as a template to obtain 51 RNA internal references, carrying out valuing on each RNA internal reference by using an isotope dilution mass spectrometry, and taking the determined copy number concentration and proportion of the internal reference as the magnitude of the RNA internal reference. Performance verification is carried out on the RNA internal reference obtained through the method, and it is indicated that the RNA internal reference can be used for gene expression quantification and differential expression gene correction in conventional human transcriptome sequencing under PolyA and Ribozero library building, and therefore the accuracy of an RNA-seq result is improved.

title: Yeast recombined human-source I-type collagen alpha1 chain protein, synthesis method and application thereof
text: Yeast recombined human-source I-type collagen alpha1 chain protein, synthesis method and application thereof. The invention discloses yeast recombined human-source I-type collagen alpha1 chain protein, a synthesis method and an application thereof. The recombinant human-source I-type collagen alpha1 chain protein is composed of N-terminal affinity purification marker, human-source I-type collagen alpha1 chain mature peptide sequence and C-terminal affinity purification marker, and the double specific affinity purification markers are designed at two ends of the protein, so that purification of the recombinant protein is facilitated, and detection and full-length identification of the recombinant protein are facilitated. The synthesis method includes utilizing pichia pastoris idiomatic codon to optimize a collagen gene sequence and performing artificial whole-gene synthesis on a gene level; building a recombinant vector through PCR, enzyme digestion and connection, and integrating the recombinant vector into yeast chromosome to build a recombinant pichia pastoris engineered strain secreting andexpressing the recombined human-source I-type collagen alpha1 chain protein. The double specific affinity purification markers carried by the protein is supportive of two-step specific purification,and easiness in acquiring a high-purity product is realized.

title: NY-ESO-1 specific TCR and preparation method thereof
text: NY-ESO-1 specific TCR and preparation method thereof. The invention relates to an NY-ESO-1 specific TCR and a preparation method thereof. The method comprises the following steps: loading an NY-ESO-1 antigen peptide by using an artificial fruit fly antigen presenting cell, activating CD8+T cells to generate CTL cells, carrying out fluorescence double staining and flow detection by using HLA-A*02:01 NY-ESO-1 Tetramer-SLLMWITQC-APC (MBL, TS-M011-2) andCD8-FITC on the produced cytotoxic CTL cells by a flow sorter, screening and collecting double positive CTL cells, carrying out single-cell TCR gene sequencing on the CTL cells obtained by sorting, measuring gene sequences of TCR alpha and beta variable regions, optimizing and designing the TCR gene, changing a constant region of the TCR gene into a mouse source, adding a signal peptide to obtainwhole-genome sequences of TCR alpha and beta chains, performing gene synthesis on the TCR alpha and TCR beta chains, connecting the TCR alpha and TCR beta chains through an IRES sequence for co-expression on the TCR alpha and TCR beta chains in a plasmid to obtain a TCR gene, and performing eukaryotic expression on the TCR gene.

title: Trichogramma japonicum ecdysone regulatory protein recombinant protein, and preparation and application thereof
text: Trichogramma japonicum ecdysone regulatory protein recombinant protein, and preparation and application thereof. The invention provides trichogramma japonicum ecdysone regulatory protein recombinant protein as well as preparation and application thereof. The amino acid sequence of the trichogramma japonicum ecdysone regulatory protein recombinant protein is as shown in SEQ ID NO.1, and the nucleotide sequence for coding the recombinant protein is as shown in SEQ ID NO.2. The ecdysone regulatory protein of the trichogramma japonicum is identified for the first time, the recombinant protein with biological activity is prepared, and a preparation method of the recombinant protein comprises the following steps: performing prokaryotic codon optimization on a coding sequence of the ecdysone regulatory protein TjERP of the trichogramma japonicum to obtain a nucleotide sequence, performing gene synthesis on the nucleotide sequence, constructing and expressing plasmids to obtain the recombinant protein. And the recombinant protein is transformed to escherichia coli BL21 (DE3), and is subjected to induced expression and purification to obtain the recombinant protein. The invention also identifies the regulation and control of the ecdysone regulatory protein on the growth and development of trichogramma japonicum. The TjERP recombinant protein provided by the invention can regulate the growth and development and life of adult trichogramma japonicum, and the obtained protein has important application potential in prevention and control of rice pests by utilizing the trichogramma japonicum.

title: Innovative 96-pore plate CPG carrier for synthesizing trace DNA (Deoxyribonucleic Acid)
text: Innovative 96-pore plate CPG carrier for synthesizing trace DNA (Deoxyribonucleic Acid). The invention discloses an innovative 96-pore plate CPG carrier for synthesizing trace DNA (Deoxyribonucleic Acid). The innovative 96-pore plate CPG carrier comprises a 96-pore hollow plate and a CPG carrier, wherein two cylindrical holes are additionally formed in each of two sides of the pore plate and are matched with corresponding fixed clamping positions on an instrument; 96 pores are uniformly distributed on a plate body at equal intervals; each hole adopts a cylindrical or square cavity design firstly and then has a cylindrical cavity assembling position from a conical cavity; a conical or cylindrical cylinder design is adopted from the lower side of the assembling position to a sharp mouth part of the bottom end. The 96-pore plate CPG carrier for synthesizing the trace DNA, provided by the invention, is specially designed for trace DNA synthesis; the dosage of a synthesizing reagent can be effectively saved, the consumption of reagents is less and the cost is controlled to be the lowest; the innovative 96-pore plate CPG carrier has the advantages of super-trace gene synthesis, short synthesis period, rapid synthesis speed, high efficiency and time saving; the innovative 96-pore plate CPG carrier also has the advantages of extremely low mutation rate, more reliable product quality, environment protection, simple and convenient utilization technology and high automation degree and industrialization degree.

title: Duplex PCR (Polymerase Chain Reaction) detection method for SARS-CoV and COVID-19 viruses
text: Duplex PCR (Polymerase Chain Reaction) detection method for SARS-CoV and COVID-19 viruses. The invention discloses a duplex PCR (Polymerase Chain Reaction) detection method for SARS-CoV and COVID-19 viruses. The duplex PCR detection method comprises the following steps: S1, preparing a virus cDNA (Complementary Deoxyribose Nucleic Acid) template; S2, preparing reagent consumables; S3, designing a primer; S4, synthesizing a virus gene; S5, performing single PCR (Polymerase Chain Reaction) amplification; S6, performing duplex PCR amplification and condition optimization; S7, performing duplex PCR specific detection; and S8, performing sensitivity detection of a duplex PCR method. According to the method, single PCR amplification and duplex amplification are performed on viruses after virus gene synthesis, duplex optimization of primer concentration conditions and annealing temperature conditions is performed on the viruses during amplification, and finally other common respiratory system disease viruses are used for PCR, and the specificity for establishing the duplex PCR method is determined. The result shows that designed primers can only specifically detect two virus plasmid templates, namely the SARS-CoV and the COVID-19, but cannot detect other experimental viruses, so that the effectiveness of detecting the SARS-CoV and the COVID-19 viruses from a plurality of viruses is improved to a certain extent.

title: Building and identification method of recombinant lactococcus lactis intracellular type expression system for taenia solium
text: Building and identification method of recombinant lactococcus lactis intracellular type expression system for taenia solium. The invention discloses a building and identification method of recombinant lactococcus lactis intracellular type expression system for taenia solium. The method comprises the following steps of TSOL18 gene synthesis and primer design, building and identification of recombinant plasmid pMG36e-TSOL18, building and identification of recombinant pMG36e-TSOL18/L.lactis of the taenia solium, protein expression identification and the like. By using lactococcus lactis MG1363 as a host system, gene optimization is carried out, and a target gene TSOL18 is synthesized; the target gene TSOL18 is connected to a pMG36e carrier through double enzyme restriction to build an intracellular type expression carrier pMG36e-TSOL18, and the intracellular type expression carrier pMG36e-TSOL18 is electrically perforated and transformed to L.lactis MG1363, so that the lactococcus lactis intracellular type expression system pMG36e-TSOL18/L.lactis MG1363 for the taenia solium is built. PCR identification, and analysis on target protein expression detected by the TSOL18 gene through SDS-PAGE and Western blot are carried out; through AKTA protein purification identification and stability analysis, the plasmid pMG36e-TSOL18 is continuously passed for 20 generations in L.lactis MG1363; therefore, relatively good genetic stability is facilitated, and a powerful reference evidence for developing and utilizing cysticercosis cellulosae vaccines is provided.

title: APPLICATION OF DEEP LEARNING FOR INFERRING PROBABILITY DISTRIBUTION WITH LIMITED OBSERVATIONS
text: APPLICATION OF DEEP LEARNING FOR INFERRING PROBABILITY DISTRIBUTION WITH LIMITED OBSERVATIONS. A method for application of a deep learning neural network (NN) for predicting the probability distribution of a biological phenotype does not require any assumption or prior knowledge of the probability distributions. The NN may be a recurrent neural network (RNN) or a long short-term memory (LSTM) network. The NN includes a loss function, which is trained on limited observations, as low as one observation, which is obtained from a large data set related to a biological system. The NN with the trained loss function is capable of calculating if readings that are outside of the mean for the data set are inherent to the biological system or are outlier readings. The output of the method is a continuous probability distribution of the biological phenotypes for each input parameter or set of parameters from the biological data set.

title: METHODS FOR IMPROVING THE SEQUENCE FIDELITY OF SYNTHETIC DOUBLE-STRANDED OLIGONUCLEOTIDES
text: METHODS FOR IMPROVING THE SEQUENCE FIDELITY OF SYNTHETIC DOUBLE-STRANDED OLIGONUCLEOTIDES. Synthetic oligonucleotides, such as synthetic DNA, often contain sequence errors due to synthetic failures (e.g., side products and/or truncated products). Methods are provided herein for improving the sequence fidelity of synthetic double-stranded oligonucleotides by separative depletion of synthetic failures. Separation is effected by utilization of methodologies in a preparative mode under denaturing conditions. A preferred use of the methods relates to gene synthesis.

title: METHODS OF GLYCOENGINEERING PROTEOGLYCANS WITH DISTINCT GLYCAN STRUCTURES
text: METHODS OF GLYCOENGINEERING PROTEOGLYCANS WITH DISTINCT GLYCAN STRUCTURES. Disclosed herein are methods of generating proteoglycans with distinct glycan structures in engineered, non-naturally occurring eukaryotic cells. These methods make accessible a dynamic range of protein glycosylation. Compositions of engineered, non-naturally occurring cells capable of generating these proteoglycans are also disclosed herein.

title: Construction method and application of gamma-cyclodextrin glucosyltransferase
text: Construction method and application of gamma-cyclodextrin glucosyltransferase. The invention discloses a construction method and application of gamma-cyclodextrin glucosyltransferase. The method comprises the following steps: gene synthesis and recombinant bacterium construction: taking a gene sequence from Bacillus Clarkii as a template design primer, and constructing a gene engineering strain E.coli[gamma]-CGT-hfut by taking pET-28a as a carrier through a seamless cloning technology; expression of zymoprotein: inoculating 2% of the constructed engineering strain into an LB liquid culture medium of 100 [mu]g/mL kanamycin, culturing under the conditions of 37 DEG C and 220 r/min until OD600 reaches 1.8-2.0, adding IPTG with the final concentration of 0.3 mmol/L for induction, performing expression culture in the environment of 25 DEG C and 200 r/min for 8 hours, centrifuging under the conditions of 4 DEG C and 8000 r/min for 15 minutes to obtain thalli, washing cells with 0.05 mol/L of glycine-sodium hydroxide with the pH value of 11, carrying out ultrasonic crushing on a resuspended bacterial liquid in an ice bath for 15 minutes, and finally centrifuging for 15 minutes under the conditions of 4 DEG C and 6000 r/min to obtain crude enzyme, namely the gamma-cyclodextrin glucosyltransferase. The gamma-cyclodextrin glucosyltransferase can be applied to specific preparation of high-purity gamma-cyclodextrin by taking starch as a substrate.

title: CRISPR-CAS9 SYSTEM FOR DNA ASSEMBLY AND DNA ASSEMBLY METHOD DNA CRISPR-CAS9 DNA
text: CRISPR-CAS9 SYSTEM FOR DNA ASSEMBLY AND DNA ASSEMBLY METHOD DNA CRISPR-CAS9 DNA

title: CpG-ODN with specific immunostimulation effect on PRRSV (porcine reproductive and respiratory syndrome virus) and application of CpG-ODN
text: CpG-ODN with specific immunostimulation effect on PRRSV (porcine reproductive and respiratory syndrome virus) and application of CpG-ODN. The invention relates to the technical field of bioengineering, in particular to a CpG-ODN with a specific immunostimulation effect on PRRSV (porcine reproductive and respiratory syndrome virus) and application of the CpG-ODN. The nucleotide sequence of the CpG-ODN is shown as SEQ ID NO:1. Eleven CpG-ODN fragments are screened out, the screened eleven CpG-ODN fragments are connected end to end toform an immune activating unit containing multiple DNA sequences with -CG-dinucleotide as one CpG element, and the immune activating unit is named as Multi-CpG. the DNA sequence TCGTCGTTTTGTCGTTTTGTCGTTTTGTCGTTTTGTCGTT with -GTCGTT- as a CpG element is used to replace one section of DNA sequence with the same length in Multi-CpG, and the DNA sequence is named as Multi-CpG-Mix. A plurality of Multi-CpG-Mix are connected end to end to form a DNA sequence of a CpG element, then the DNA sequence of the CpG element is inserted into a multi-copy cloning vector Puc19 derived vector FVX through two restriction enzyme cutting sites of XbaI and EcoRI, recombinant plasmids FVX-CpG containing a plurality of Multi-CpG-Mix are constructed respectively by means of gene synthesis, then test screening is carried out to obtain CpG-ODN, the innate immunity function in a pig body can be induced to be activated, and the acquired immune function of the PRRSV in the pig body can also be activated.

title: Method for simultaneously expressing bFGF (basic fibroblast growth factor) and EGF (epidermal growth factor) in bombyx mori and facilitating rapid purification of protein
text: Method for simultaneously expressing bFGF (basic fibroblast growth factor) and EGF (epidermal growth factor) in bombyx mori and facilitating rapid purification of protein. The invention discloses a method for simultaneously expressing bFGF (basic fibroblast growth factor) and EGF (epidermal growth factor) in bombyx mori and facilitating rapid purification of protein. According to the method, gene fusion and gene synthesis technologies are utilized, baculovirus coding protein codon preference is utilized, two genes of bFGF and EGF are re-optimized and designed, the two genes are fused, a TEV restriction enzyme cutting site is arranged between the two genes, then a bombyx mori baculovirus expression system is utilized to construct recombinant baculovirus, and the recombinant baculovirus is obtained. The bFGF-EGF fusion protein is expressed in bombyx mori cells and bombyx mori, the protein is rapidly separated and purified by using a heparin affinity chromatography column, finally the two proteins are separated by using TEV enzyme, and effective separation of the two proteins is realized after the two proteins pass through the affinity chromatography column again. According to the method disclosed by the invention, the purification efficiency of the recombinant protein is remarkably improved, the production cost of the recombinant bFGF and EGF is greatly reduced, and a remarkably innovative technology is provided for large-scale production and medical application of the recombinant bFGF and EGF.

title: Gene encoding truncated human keratinocyte growth factor and preparation method thereof
text: Gene encoding truncated human keratinocyte growth factor and preparation method thereof. The present invention provides a gene encoding truncated human keratinocyte growth factor. The gene is any one selected trom the group consisting of, 1) the sequence of SEQ ID No: i in the sequence list; 2) a DNA sequence having a sequence honology of 90% or more to the DNA sequence of SEQ ID No: I and encoding proteins with the same functions as that encoded by SEQ ID No: L Also provided is a method of preparing a truncated human keratinocyte growth factor, comprising the following Steps: (1) preparation of plasmids by using a sequence of a fusion gene of a truncated human keratinecyte growth factor and a mature signal peptide, prepared through artil total gene synthesis; (2) obtaining a product by the steps of fermentation. cultivation. induction and expression, The method according to the Oresent invention is simple and can efficiently express genetic recombinant truncated human keratinocyte growth factor. The expression quantity of the truncated hunan keratinocyte growth factor in a fermenter is 250mg /L or more, w hich is more than three times of that of the wild type. title: CONSTRUCTING VERY LONG DNA SEQUENCES FROM SYNTHETIC DNA MOLECULES
text: CONSTRUCTING VERY LONG DNA SEQUENCES FROM SYNTHETIC DNA MOLECULES. A method of fabricating a DNA molecule of user-defined sequence. A multiplicity of DNA sequence segments that will comprise the DNA molecule of user-defined sequence into fragments of defined size. The multiplicity of DNA sequence segments are combined with at least one polymerase enzyme. The multiciplicity of DNA sequence segments join to produce the DNA molecule of user-defined sequence.

title: Dna nanocage by self-organization of dna and process for producing the same, and dna nanotube and molecule carrier using the same
text: Dna nanocage by self-organization of dna and process for producing the same, and dna nanotube and molecule carrier using the same. The invention provides a process for producing DNA nanocages, characterized by comprising a step of two-dimensionally assembling three types of oligonucleotides by hybridization to form tridirectionally branched double-strand DNA having self-complementary chains in the terminals, and a step of three-dimensionally self-organizing the resulting tridirectionally branched double-strand DNAs so as to consume all of the self-complementary terminals. Since the DNA nanocages according to the invention can easily be formed from DNAs by one-step procedure and include nanoparticles in the interior, the DNA nanocages are significantly effective for the development of functional materials using DNAs.

title: Construction and preparation method of difunctional fusion enzyme system for catalyzing steviol glycosylation based on substrate diversion principle
text: Construction and preparation method of difunctional fusion enzyme system for catalyzing steviol glycosylation based on substrate diversion principle. The invention provides a method for constructing UGT91C1-UGT76G1 fusion enzyme on the basis of a substrate diversion principle and computer assistance and recombining and preparing the fusion enzyme with the performance of efficiently catalyzing steviol glycosylation by combining a bioengineering means, and determines that a (GGGGS) 2 polypeptide sequence is suitable for mediating UGT91C1 to be fused at the C tail end of UGT76G1 and is beneficial for directly converting an intermediate product into a product, so that the GGGGS 2 polypeptide sequence is suitable for mediating UGT91C1 to be fused at the C tail end of UGT76G1, and the GGGGS 2 polypeptide sequence is suitable for mediating UGT91C1 to be fused at the C tail end of UGT76G1. And the intermediate is prevented from diffusing to a bulk phase. The gene sequence of the UGT91C1-UGT76G1 fusion enzyme is Seq ID (Identity) NO.1, the UGT91C1-UGT76G1 fusion enzyme is named as ugt91C1-ugt76G1, and the primary sequence of the protein is Seq ID NO.2. The invention further discloses a method for ugt91C1-C-ugt76G1 gene synthesis, recombinant plasmid construction, heterologous expression, purification and catalytic performance characterization, the obtained UGT91C1-C-UGT76G1 is high in catalytic cascade reaction efficiency, preparation of the UGT91C1-C-UGT76G1 is more intensive and economical, unit operation such as cascade reaction intermediate product separation and purification can be reduced, less waste is generated, the method better conforms to the green chemical synthesis concept, and the method is suitable for industrial production. And the catalyst has a good industrial catalytic conversion application prospect.

title: Arthrobotrys oligospora chitinase AO-59opt mesoporous silica nano-particles for killing nematodes and preparation method of Arthrobotrys oligospora chitinase AO-59opt mesoporous silica nano-particles
text: Arthrobotrys oligospora chitinase AO-59opt mesoporous silica nano-particles for killing nematodes and preparation method of Arthrobotrys oligospora chitinase AO-59opt mesoporous silica nano-particles. The invention discloses arthrobotrys oligospora chitinase AO-59opt mesoporous silica nanoparticles for killing nematodes and a preparation method of the mesoporous silica nanoparticles. The preparation method comprises the following steps: optimization and gene synthesis of arthrobotrys oligospora chitinase AO-59 gene codons, construction of an arthrobotrys oligospora AO-59opt gene eukaryotic expression recombinant vector, preparation of the mesoporous silica nanoparticles for killing nematodes, preparation of the mesoporous silica nanoparticles for killing nematodes, and preparation of the mesoporous silica nanoparticles for killing nematodes. The invention relates to induction expression of arthrobotrys oligospora chitinase AO-59opt in pichia pastoris and preparation of the chitinase AO-59opt mesoporous silica nanoparticles. The preparation method comprises the following steps: carrying out induction expression on arthrobotrys oligospora chitinase AO-59opt in pichia pastoris; compared with common chitinase AO-59, the prepared chitinase AO-59opt mesoporous silica nanoparticles (AO-59opt-MSNs) have the advantages that the enzymatic stability is remarkably improved, the decomposition activity on chitin is remarkably improved, the killing activity on haemonchus contortus L3 larvae in sheep manure and the splitting activity on worm eggs are also remarkably improved, and the application prospect is broad. And a foundation is laid for research and development of novel efficient nematode-killing biological prevention and control preparations.

title: ANTIBODY PREPARATION METHOD, AND ANTIBODY AND ANTIBODY LIBRARY THUS PREPARED
text: ANTIBODY PREPARATION METHOD, AND ANTIBODY AND ANTIBODY LIBRARY THUS PREPARED. Provided is a method for preparing antibodies against a protein of interest, through which highly specific antibodies against all proteins can be effectively and rapidly prepared with low cost, and the epitope to which the antibody is directed can be determined, so that a library covering epitopes on the surface of the proteins of interest and a library of antibodies against all the epitopes can be established. The antibodies are proved to be useful in detection, protein function investigation and antibody pharmaceuticals.

title: METHODS OF GLYCOENGINEERING PROTEOGLYCANS WITH DISTINCT GLYCAN STRUCTURES
text: METHODS OF GLYCOENGINEERING PROTEOGLYCANS WITH DISTINCT GLYCAN STRUCTURES. Disclosed herein are methods of generating proteoglycans with distinct glycan structures in engineered, non-naturally occurring eukaryotic cells. These methods make accessible a dynamic range of protein glycosylation. Compositions of engineered, non-naturally occurring cells capable of generating these proteoglycans are also disclosed herein.

title: DNA nanocage by self-organization of DNA and process for producing the same, and DNA nanotube and molecule carrier using the same
