The invention provides a process for producing DNA nanocages, characterized by comprising a step of two-dimensionally assembling three types of oligonucleotides by hybridization to form tridirectionally branched double-strand DNA having self-complementary chains in the terminals, and a step of three-dimensionally self-organizing the resulting tridirectionally branched double-strand DNAs so as to consume all of the self-complementary terminals. Since the DNA nanocages according to the invention can easily be formed from DNAs by one-step procedure and include nanoparticles in the interior, the DNA nanocages are significantly effective for the development of functional materials using DNAs.

title: PLASMID VECTORS FOR IN VIVO SELECTION-FREE USE WITH THE PROBIOTIC E. COLI NISSLE
text: PLASMID VECTORS FOR IN VIVO SELECTION-FREE USE WITH THE PROBIOTIC E. COLI NISSLE. Disclosed are methods for producing genetically modified bacteria, comprising introducing into said bacteria at least one engineered cryptic plasmid comprising a heterologous nucleic acid, wherein the heterologous nucleic acid comprises a nucleic acid sequence encoding a recombinant protein and a polypeptide secretion system for directing the recombinant protein to the outer membrane for secretion, wherein the bacteria do not comprise any native cryptic plasmids.

title: Enhanced mycoplasma ovipneumoniae antigen protein TU-sheep pox virus recombinant multivalent vaccine and construction method thereof
text: Enhanced mycoplasma ovipneumoniae antigen protein TU-sheep pox virus recombinant multivalent vaccine and construction method thereof. The invention belongs to the field of immune engineering, and particularly discloses an enhanced type mycoplasma ovipneumoniae antigen protein TU-sheep pox virus recombinant multivalent vaccine and a construction method thereof.An antigen gene TU of mycoplasma ovipneumoniae is selected, and two non-specific immune genes HSP70 and I FN-gamma are added to enhance the immune effect of the antigen gene TU; the method comprises the following steps: respectively carrying out biological information analysis on goat pox virus fragments, selecting regions with strong antigenicity for gene synthesis, connecting the three gene fragments together, and respectively inserting the three gene fragments into unmarked goat pox virus expression vectors for expressing exogenous genes by using a homologous recombination method, a sheep pox virus vaccine strain recombinant multivalent vaccine expression vector for expressing three antigen genes of the mycoplasma ovipneumoniae is constructed, and then the recombinant sheep pox virus capable of expressing the mycoplasma ovipneumoniae HSP70-I FN-gamma-TU is obtained through transfection. The sheep pox virus vaccine strain is taken as a carrier, the recombinant multivalent vaccine is constructed by recombining the primary antigen gene of the mycoplasma hyopneumoniae, and the recombinant multivalent vaccine has an immune protection effect on both sheep pox and mycoplasma hyopneumoniae.

title: Label-free OGP self-assembled polypeptide as well as preparation method and application thereof
text: Label-free OGP self-assembled polypeptide as well as preparation method and application thereof. The invention discloses a label-free OGP self-assembly polypeptide and a preparation method and application thereof, and belongs to the technical field of biology, a design sequence is SEQ ID NO: 1, the label-free OGP self-assembly polypeptide is composed of amino acids, CTBtriOGP composed of three parts of elements, namely CTB, tri and OGP, and the label-free OGP self-assembly polypeptide can have the osteogenesis promoting function of the OGP. The preparation method comprises the following steps: firstly, obtaining a self-assembled OGP gene by using a gene synthesis method, assembling the self-assembled OGP gene on an expression vector containing a TAC promoter, transforming BL21 (DE3) competent bacteria, carrying out IPTG induced expression, secreting the self-assembled OGP into a periplasmic cavity of escherichia coli, extracting CTBtriOGP from escherichia coli by using an osmotic shock method, and carrying out centrifugal purification by using polyethylene glycol to obtain the CTBtriOGP, so that the CTBtriOGP has an osteogenesis promoting function of the OGP, is stable in activity, and can be used for preparing a medicine for treating osteogenesis. The preservation time is long, so that the problem that the OGP is unstable when being independently used is solved.

title: L-amino acid ligase and application thereof in catalytic synthesis of L-valine dipeptide and derivatives thereof
text: L-amino acid ligase and application thereof in catalytic synthesis of L-valine dipeptide and derivatives thereof. The invention discloses an L-amino acid ligase and application thereof in catalytic synthesis of L-valine dipeptide and derivatives thereof, the amino acid sequence of the L-amino acid ligase is selected from any one sequence of SEQ ID No: 2-9, the L-amino acid ligase is prepared by firstly constructing a genetic engineering strain of a biological enzyme, and synthesizing a biological enzyme gene segment obtained by whole-gene synthesis into L-valine dipeptide and a derivative of the L-valine dipeptide. Using restriction endonuclease to linearize recombinant plasmids, and then performing activation and fermentation induction culture on the positive clones to prepare the recombinant plasmid. The invention also provides a method for catalytically synthesizing the L-valine dipeptide and the derivative thereof by using the L-amino acid ligase disclosed by the invention, the synthesis route is simple, the raw materials are cheap and easy to obtain, the use amount of the enzyme is less than 0.5%, the cost is low, and the conversion rate can reach more than 80%. The method has the advantages of simple post-treatment process, high product purity, high safety and small environmental pollution, and has extremely high economic value and industrial application prospect.

title: Method used for synthesizing plurality of sesquiterpenoid compounds using salviae miltiorrhizae SmTPS3 gene
text: Method used for synthesizing plurality of sesquiterpenoid compounds using salviae miltiorrhizae SmTPS3 gene. The invention discloses a terpene synthases gene sequence (SmTPS3) mainly participating in synthesis of a plurality of salviae miltiorrhizae secondary metabolites sesquiterpenoids such as zingiberene.The SmTPS3 gene possesses a nucleotide sequence represented by SEQ ID No.1, and the protein encoded by the gene possesses an amino acid sequence represented by SEQ ID No.2. The SmTPS3 gene sequence is obtained based on salviae miltiorrhizae genome and transcriptome data, a pET28a-SmTPS3 prokaryotic expression vector is constructed, the catalysis products of SmTPS3 are detected in Escherichia coli, so that the gene functions of SmTPS3 are verified. It is shown by research results that: SmTPS3 is capable of catalyzing synthesis of a plurality of sesquiterpenoid components including zingiberene,alpha-bergamot oil alkene, beta- sesquiphellandrene; zingiberene possesses anti-virus activity, anti-fertility activity, and antiulcer activity, and can be taken as food flavoring agent and cosmeticraw material; bergamot essential oil possesses excellent curative effects in alleviating anxiety and depression. The method is capable of providing salviae miltiorrhizae terpene synthases gene synthesis of sesquiterpenoid compounds with foundation, and promoting medicine development and industry development of sesquiterpenoid compounds, such as zingiberene and alpha-bergamot oil alkene, based onSmTPS3 catalysis synthesis.

title: Rheumatoid factor IgG, IgM and IgA composite quality control product as well as preparation method and application thereof
text: Rheumatoid factor IgG, IgM and IgA composite quality control product as well as preparation method and application thereof. The invention discloses a rheumatoid factor IgG, IgM and IgA composite quality control product as well as a preparation method and application thereof. The preparation method comprises the following steps: immunizing a mouse with a rheumatoid factor antigen to obtain a mouse RF antibody; sequencing an antibody amino acid sequence to obtain a variable region amino acid sequence of the mouse RF antibody; connecting an RF antibody variable region-human IgA constant region-human IgM constant region in parallel with a base sequence through sequence design and whole-gene synthesis, introducing the sequence into a pFUSE-hIgG1-Fc2 vector containing a human IgG Fc fragment gene expression sequence, preparing a recombinant plasmid containing human IgG, IgM, IgA Fc fragments and the RF antibody variable region at the same time, transfecting HEK293 mammalian cells with the recombinant plasmid, obtaining the recombinant antibody simultaneously containing human IgG, IgM, IgA Fc segments and RF antibody variable regions, and preparing the recombinant antibody into the quality control product. The quality control product can be simultaneously used for four types of detection kits such as RF-IgG, RF-IgM, RF-IgA and RF-IgG/M/A, the uniformity, the precision and the stability are good, the clinical operation is convenient, the cost is substantially saved, and the batch difference between the quality control products is substantially controlled.

title: Fluorescent cell strain for detecting drug activity and preparation method thereof
text: Fluorescent cell strain for detecting drug activity and preparation method thereof. The invention belongs to the field of drug screening, and particularly discloses a fluorescent cell strain for detecting drug activity and a preparation method, the preparation method comprises the following steps: 1) PLX-301-GFP plasmid construction: PLX-301 is used, the PLX-301-GFP plasmid is constructed, a double enzyme digestion site is BamHI/NotI, a GFP sequence is synthesized by a gene synthesis method, the PLX-301 is connected to the GFP sequence through double enzyme digestion, and the PLX-301-GFP plasmid is obtained; (2) transferring the constructed plasmids into competent escherichia coli DH5alpha and extracting the plasmids; (3) carrying out amplification on the escherichia coli DH5 alpha transfected with the PLX-301-GFP plasmid; 4) packaging and harvesting the GFP lentivirus liquid; (5) constructing an HCT116-GFP report cell strain and screening a stably transfected cell strain; the fluorescent cell strain is an HCT116-GFP (Green Fluorescent Protein) cell line capable of stably expressing GFP (Green Fluorescent Protein); in the invention, a lentivirus system is used for carrying out green fluorescence (GFP) gene modification on human colorectal cancer HCT116 cells, so that the human colorectal cancer HCT116 cells stably express GFP proteins, a monoclonal screening method for the HCT116 cells after stable green fluorescence transfection is established, and an HCT116-GFP cell line for stably expressing GFP is obtained and is applied to drug screening and research.

title: Protein(s), useful for combating arthropods, preferably insects, where the protein(s) is/are related to naturally occurring proteins in bacteria and has structural characteristic based on the toxic effect of the protein(s) on arthropods
text: Protein(s), useful for combating arthropods, preferably insects, where the protein(s) is/are related to naturally occurring proteins in bacteria and has structural characteristic based on the toxic effect of the protein(s) on arthropods. Protein(s) (I), which individually or in combination exhibit(s) toxicity, preferably insecticidal activity, on arthropods, is claimed, where (I): is/are related to naturally occurring proteins in bacteria, based on 16S rRNA gene coding sequence, that are related to the taxonomic system of the Gammaproteobacteria order Legionellales, or related to naturally occurring proteins in bacteria of specific taxon; and exhibits structural characteristics that are based on the toxic effect of (I) on the arthropods. Protein(s) (I), which individually or in combination exhibit(s) toxicity, preferably insecticidal activity, on arthropods, is claimed, where (I): is/are related to naturally occurring proteins in bacteria, based on 16S rRNA gene coding sequence, that are related to the taxonomic system of the Gammaproteobacteria order Legionellales represented as criterion for taxonomic classification according to second edition of Bergey's Manual of Systemic Bacteriology (Garrity GM, Brenner DJ, Krieg NR and Staley JT (Hrsg.), Vol. II: The Proteobacteria. Springer, New York, 2005), or related to naturally occurring proteins in bacteria of specific taxon; and exhibits structural characteristics that are based on the toxic effect of (I) on the arthropods. An independent claim is included for use of gene(s) (which naturally occurs in the genome of the above bacteria, or naturally occurs as its orthology in the genome of other organisms, or obtained by modification of the specified naturally occurring gene or prepared by de novo gene synthesis by which it codes for the structural characteristics, based on the insecticidal activity of the specified naturally occurring gene, associated with the gene products) for producing bioinsecticides or for combating arthropods. ACTIVITY : Arthropodicide; Insecticide. MECHANISM OF ACTION : None given.

title: INHIBITING AGENT FOR INHIBITION OF ANGIOGENESIS, A METHOD FOR PREPARING THE AGENT, A METHOD FOR MODIFYING THE AGENT AND ITS USE FOR MANUFACTURING A MEDICAMENT FOR TREATING TUMOR
text: INHIBITING AGENT FOR INHIBITION OF ANGIOGENESIS, A METHOD FOR PREPARING THE AGENT, A METHOD FOR MODIFYING THE AGENT AND ITS USE FOR MANUFACTURING A MEDICAMENT FOR TREATING TUMOR. a highly efficient antiangiogenesis agent, which is a polypeptide for inhibition of angiogenesis Ile-Val-Arg-Arg-Ala-Asp-Arg-Ala-Ala-Val-Pro, connected with a polypeptide containing Arg-Gly-Asp on its one end or two ends is provided. The inhibiting agent can be synthesized or gene engineered. It also relates to a physiochemical method for modifying the antiangiogenesis agent. Polypeptides with weight percentage of 1-70% preferably about 20-50% are mixed with 20%-95% polyethylene glycol, or heparin, or dextran, or polyvinylpyrrolidone, or polyethylene glycol-poly-amino acid copolymer, or palmitic acid or poly-sialic acid or liposomes solutions; preferably about 50-93% of the above modified substances are fully mixed even and shaken at a shaker at 4° C.-40° C., preferably 25° C.-37° C. for more than 10 min, and the modified substances are separated through appropriate methods. Furthermore, it still relates to the use of the above polypeptides and the polypeptide modified substances for manufacturing medicaments for treating human solid tumors.

title: Method for the synthesis of DNA sequences
text: Method for the synthesis of DNA sequences. A method is disclosed for the direct synthesis of double stranded DNA molecules of a variety of sizes and with any desired sequence. The DNA molecule to be synthesis is logically broken up into smaller overlapping DNA segments. A maskless microarray synthesizer is used to make a DNA microarray on a substrate in which each element or feature of the array is populated by DNA of a one of the overlapping DNA segments. The DNA segments are released from the substrate and held under conditions favoring hybridization of DNA, under which conditions the segments will spontaneously hybridize together to form the desired DNA construct. This method makes possible the remote assembly of DNA sequence, through a process analogous to facsimile transmission of documents, since the information on DNA to be made can be transmitted remotely to an instrument which can then synthesize any needed DNA sequence from the information.

title: Taenia solium Ts14-3-3.3 DNA vaccine preparation and identification method
text: Taenia solium Ts14-3-3.3 DNA vaccine preparation and identification method. The invention discloses a taenia solium Ts14-3-3.3 DNA vaccine preparation and identification method, and belongs to the technical field of genetic engineering vaccines. The taenia solium Ts14-3-3.3 DNA vaccine preparation and identification method comprises the following steps of 1) adopting a PAS method to perform whole-gene synthesis of Ts14-3-3.3 and sp-Ts14-3-3.3; 2) cloning a target gene to an eukaryotic expression vector, transferring into a cloning strain, and selecting positive clones for sequencing identification and double enzyme digestion identification of recombinant plasmids; 3) then preparing plasmids of two transfection levels, transfecting human 293F embryo kidney cells correspondingly, and identifying by adopting a Western blot method; 4) dividing the mice into four groups; and 5) collecting mouse serum and splenic lymphocytes at 0w, 2w, 4w, 6w and 8w after the first immunization, and identifying the mouse serum and splenic lymphocytes. According to the taenia solium DNA vaccine disclosed by the invention, two DNA vaccines of the taenia solium pMZ-X3-Ts14-3-3.3 and the taenia solium pMZ-X3-sp-Ts14-3-3.3 are successfully prepared. The expression of the taenia solium Ts14-3-3.3 protein in human 293F embryo kidney cells is realized. The taenia solium DNA vaccine pMZ-X3-Ts14-3-3.3 and the taenia solium DNA vaccine pMZ-X3-sp-Ts14-3-3.3 can be used for inducing a mouse to generate specific cellular immune response and humoral immune response.

title: Microbial stem cell technology
text: Microbial stem cell technology. The present disclosure relates to microbial stem cell technology that enables a growing microbial culture to stably maintain two or more distinct cell types in a ratio that can be genetically programmed and/or dynamically controlled during cultivation. It is contemplated that embodiments described herein can be utilized to increase product yield in microbial fermentations and advanced engineering of biomaterials using genetically engineered microbial cells, among others.

title: SYSTEMS FOR CELL PROGRAMMING TOWARDS CHONDROGENIC LINEAGE AND METHODS THEREOF
text: SYSTEMS FOR CELL PROGRAMMING TOWARDS CHONDROGENIC LINEAGE AND METHODS THEREOF. Provided herein are systems of modulating gene expression, methods of use thereof, and cells engineered thereof for the purpose of differentiating cells, for example chondroprogenitor cells.

title: Ebola virus N protein-containing armor RNA (Ribonucleic Acid) standard substance and preparation method thereof
text: Ebola virus N protein-containing armor RNA (Ribonucleic Acid) standard substance and preparation method thereof. The invention discloses an armor RNA (Ribonucleic Acid) standard substance containing Ebola virus N protein and a preparation method thereof, and belongs to the technical field of molecular diagnostics. Ebola virus L protein sequence information is obtained, then gene synthesis is performed, and then the Ebola virus L protein sequence information is constructed into armor RNA plasmids in a seamless cloning manner; after the recombinant plasmid is extracted, converting the armor RNA plasmid containing the Ebola virus L protein sequence into a BL21 competent cell, and performing induced expression and purification to obtain the armor RNA standard substance containing the Ebola virus L protein, wherein the armor RNA standard substance is good in stability and high in purity; compared with the RNA nucleic acid detection positive standard substance in the conventional nucleic acid detection, the positive standard substance provided by the invention has the advantages that the wild form of the Ebola virus and the essential nucleic acid extraction link in the nucleic acid detection process can be better simulated, and the positive quality control standard substance for the nucleic acid detection is provided for the rapid and accurate detection of the Ebola virus.

title: Difunctional antioxidant enzyme having glutathione peroxidase (GPX) and superoxide dismutase (SOD) activities at the same time and preparation method thereof
text: Difunctional antioxidant enzyme having glutathione peroxidase (GPX) and superoxide dismutase (SOD) activities at the same time and preparation method thereof. The invention relates to a difunctional antioxidant enzyme having glutathione peroxidase (GPX) and superoxide dismutase (SOD) activities at the same time and a preparation method thereof and belongs to the biotechnology field. Sequences SEQ ID No:1-26 are related, and the difunctional antioxidant enzyme is composed of amino acid sequences of two enzymes, namely GPX and SOD, can decompose an SOD substrate and also can decompose a GPX substrate. The preparation method comprises the following steps: firstly obtaining a hybrid tRNA gene by adopting a gene synthesis method, then synthesizing or amplifying GPX and SOD genes, assembling the two genes onto the same secretory prokaryotic expression vector which can express hybrid tRNA, transforming TAG engineering bacteria, inducing expression in presence of sodium selenite, introducing a GPX catalytic group Sec into a substrate binding site of a protein in the conventional way that an amino acid enters a peptide chain, and giving high GPX and SOD activities to the protein. The difunctional antioxidant enzyme provided by the invention is simple in preparation method, high in enzyme protein activity and yield and good in stability and has an efficient antioxidant function.

title: High-throughput screening method for stress-resistant DNA polymerase by using PCR enrichment amplification method
text: High-throughput screening method for stress-resistant DNA polymerase by using PCR enrichment amplification method. The invention relates to the technical field of mutant screening methods, in particular to a high-throughput screening method for stress-resistant DNA (deoxyribonucleic acid) polymerase by using a PCR (polymerase chain reaction) enrichment amplification method, which comprises the following steps of: firstly, determining an original sequence of Taq enzyme, performing gene synthesis according to an amino acid sequence, constructing a target fragment into a pET30a vector, and then mutating nucleotide of a target gene by using error-prone PCR to obtain the stress-resistant DNA polymerase. The method comprises the following steps of: constructing a mutant expression library, mutating nucleotide of a target gene by adopting error-prone PCR (Polymerase Chain Reaction), transferring 1ml of a mutant library strain into 50ml of an LB culture medium, carrying out 160rpm shaking culture on a shaking table at 37 DEG C, and cooling to 30 DEG C when OD600 of a bacterial solution reaches about 0.5. According to the method, detection by a fluorescent probe method is not required, and only conventional PCR conditions are required to be adopted, so that the method is low in requirement on equipment, simple in operation and convenient to implement. The method is simple and easy to implement, and meanwhile, the method does not need to target to a specific single cell during screening, and a target gene or a target mutation library can be obtained by extracting and analyzing a final product mainly through advantage accumulation.

title: Targeting selenium-coupled anti-inflammatory fusion protein and application thereof in acute coronary syndrome reperfusion injury repair
text: Targeting selenium-coupled anti-inflammatory fusion protein and application thereof in acute coronary syndrome reperfusion injury repair. The invention discloses targeted selenium-coupled anti-inflammatory fusion protein and application thereof in acute coronary syndrome reperfusion injury repair, and belongs to the technical field of biomedicine. A GPIb-Annexin A1 fusion protein is constructed firstly, specifically, a human GPIb active fragment serving as an N-terminal targeting element and a human Annexin A1 active fragment serving as a C-terminal anti-inflammatory element are connected in series through a (Gly4Ser) 3 flexible linker, the fusion protein sequence is shown as SEQ ID NO: 3, and the GPIb-Annexin A1 fusion protein is obtained through gene synthesis, vector construction, prokaryotic expression and Ni-NTA purification; and chelating selenomethionine serving as a selenium source with the fusion protein according to the mass ratio of 3: 1 in a phosphate buffer solution with the pH value of 7.5, and dialyzing, freezing and drying to obtain the selenium chelate. Experiments prove that the selenium chelate can significantly improve the left ventricular ejection fraction and the left ventricular shortening fraction of MI/RI mice, reduce the myocardial infarction area, inhibit the expression of TNF-alpha, IL-1beta and IL-6, and reduce the long-term myocardial collagen volume fraction, and can be used for preparing acute coronary syndrome reperfusion injury repair drugs.

title: BIOTECHNOLOGICAL PRODUCTION OF BISUCABERINS, DESFERRIOXAMINES AND ANALOGS THEREOF
text: BIOTECHNOLOGICAL PRODUCTION OF BISUCABERINS, DESFERRIOXAMINES AND ANALOGS THEREOF. The present invention relates to a recombinant microbial cell for producing at least one compound having structural Formula III from at least one simple carbon source: Formula (III) m = 1-3, wherein the simple carbon source is selected from the group consisting of glucose, sucrose, xylose, arabinose, mannose and glycerol; and wherein the cell comprises a further genetic modification to increase production of L-lysine in the cell from at least one of the simple carbon sources.

title: PROGRAMMABLE DNA-GUIDED ARTIFICIAL RESTRICTION ENZYMES
text: PROGRAMMABLE DNA-GUIDED ARTIFICIAL RESTRICTION ENZYMES. Methods and compositions for specific cleavage of DNA molecules using single-stranded guides and Argonaute (Ago) proteins are described.

title: METHOD FOR PRODUCING POLYMERS
text: METHOD FOR PRODUCING POLYMERS. The invention relates to a method for producing polymers, in particular synthetic nucleic acid double strands of optional sequence, comprising the steps: (a) provision of a support having a surface area which contains a plurality of individual reaction areas, (b) location-resolved synthesis of nucleic acid fragments having in each case different base sequences in several of the individual reaction areas, and (c) detachment of the nucleic acid fragments from individual reaction areas.

title: OPTOGENETIC CONTROL OF MICROBIAL CO-CULTURE POPULATIONS
text: OPTOGENETIC CONTROL OF MICROBIAL CO-CULTURE POPULATIONS. Microbial consortia exert great influence over the physiology of humans, animals, plants, and ecosystems. However, difficulty in controlling their composition and population dynamics have limited their application in medicine, agriculture, biotechnology, and the environment. The approach disclosed herein provides an effective method to dynamically control population compositions in microbial consortia, which we demonstrate in the context of co-culture fermentations for chemical production. Co-culture fermentations can improve chemical production from complex biosynthetic pathways over monocultures by distributing enzymes across multiple strains, thereby reducing metabolic burden, overcoming endogenous regulatory mechanisms, or exploiting natural traits of different microbial species. However, stabilizing and optimizing microbial sub-populations for maximal chemical production remains a major obstacle in the field. An optogenetic circuit, called OptoTA, is disclosed for regulating a toxin-antitoxin system, which enables tunability of, e.g., Escherichia coli growth using only blue light. With the disclosed system, one can control population ratios of co-cultures of, e.g., E. coli and Saccharomyces cerevisiae containing different metabolic modules of biosynthetic pathways. Results reveal that intermediate light duty cycles improve chemical production by establishing optimal co-culture populations.

title: CpG-ODN with specific immunostimulation effect on PRRSV and application of CpG-ODN
text: CpG-ODN with specific immunostimulation effect on PRRSV and application of CpG-ODN. The invention relates to a CpG-ODN with a specific immunostimulation effect on PRRSV and an application of the CpG-ODN. 11 CpG-ODN fragments are screened out and then connected end to end to form a DNA sequence containing a plurality of -CG-dinucleotide as a CpG element, and the DNA sequence is named as "Multi-CpG". A section of the DNA sequence is replaced with the same length in the "Multi-CpG"with the DNA sequence taking -GTCGTT- as the CpG element, and the DNA sequence is named as "Multi-CpG-Mix". Two "Multi-CpG" and one "Multi-CpG-Mix" are connected end to end to obtain the DNA sequencecontaining two immune activation units, and the DNA sequence is named as "Multi-CpG-Mixture". The "Multi-CpG-Mixture" is inserted into a multi-copy clone vector Puc19 derived vector FVX through two restriction enzyme cutting sites, i.e., XbaI and EcoRI, and a recombinant plasmid FVX-Multi-CpG-Mixture is constructed by virtue of a gene synthesis means. The CpG-ODN is capable of inducing natural immune function activation in a pig body, activating acquired immune function of the PRRSV in the pig body, improving the immune response induction effect of the PRRSV in the pig body, reducing the PRRSVmorbidity and reducing the pig breeding cost.

title: MICROBIAL STEM CELL TECHNOLOGY
text: MICROBIAL STEM CELL TECHNOLOGY. The present disclosure relates to microbial stem cell technology that enables a growing microbial culture to stably maintain two or more distinct cell types in a ratio that can be genetically programmed and/or dynamically controlled during cultivation. It is contemplated that embodiments described herein can be utilized to increase product yield in microbial fermentations and advanced engineering of biomaterials using genetically engineered microbial cells, among others.

title: PLASMID VECTORS FOR IN VIVO SELECTION-FREE USE WITH THE PROBIOTIC E. COLI NISSLE
text: PLASMID VECTORS FOR IN VIVO SELECTION-FREE USE WITH THE PROBIOTIC E. COLI NISSLE. Disclosed are methods for producing genetically modified bacteria, comprising introducing into said bacteria at least one engineered cryptic plasmid comprising a heterologous nucleic acid, wherein the heterologous nucleic acid comprises a nucleic acid sequence encoding a recombinant protein and a polypeptide secretion system for directing the recombinant protein to the outer membrane for secretion, wherein the bacteria do not comprise any native cryptic plasmids.

title: ACOUSTIC REMOTE CONTROL OF MICROBIAL IMMUNOTHERAPY
text: ACOUSTIC REMOTE CONTROL OF MICROBIAL IMMUNOTHERAPY. Disclosed herein include methods, compositions, and kits suitable for use in spatiotemporal regulation of probiotic cells. There are provided, in some embodiments, thermal bioswitches that allow probiotic cells to sense small changes in temperature and use them as inputs for the actuation of genetic circuits. Genetic circuits capable of inducing expression of a payload upon thermal stimulation are provided. Thermally actuated probiotic cells and methods of use for the treatment of diseases or disorders are also provided.

title: HIGH-THROUGHPUT SEQUENCING OF POLYNUCLEOTIDES
text: HIGH-THROUGHPUT SEQUENCING OF POLYNUCLEOTIDES. Provided herein are methods, compositions, and kits for simultaneously sequencing polynucleotides from a plurality of samples in a single sequencing run. In an embodiment, the present invention improves efficiency of the next-generation sequencing process, in part, by reducing reaction volumes to a sub-microliter range and generating and using a set of novel barcode sequences to tag a plurality of polynucleotides. In addition, the sample preparation processes have been simplified to save time and cost, while providing high-quality sequence coverage for all samples.

title: Gene synthesis method
text: Gene synthesis method. The invention discloses a DNA fragment splicing synthesis method. The method comprises the following steps of: a) designing a series of single strand DNA fragments comprising a forward direction single strand f located on the 5' end of the plus strand of a to-be-synthesized DNA fragment and a plurality of reverse direction single strands rn located on the inverse chain of the to-be-synthesized DNA fragment; b) adding DNA polymerase into the reaction system containing the series of single strand DNA fragments of the step a) for extension at 72 DEG C; and c) adding corresponding incision enzyme and 5'-3' excision enzyme into the reaction system of the step b) for 10s at 70 DEG C, 10s at 50 DEG C and 10s at 37 DEG C for 60 cycles, and obtaining the to-be-synthesized DNA fragment from the reaction product. The 5' end of the forward direction single strand f is connected with hydroxyl, the 3' end of the forward direction single strand f is matched with the 3' end sequence of a reverse direction single strand r1, the 5' end of the reverse direction single strand r1 is provided with an incision enzyme sequence, the sequence downstream of the enzyme digestion sequence of r1 is consistent with the 3' end sequence of a reverse direction single strand r2, the sequence downstream of the enzyme digestion sequence of r2 is consistent with the 3' end sequence of r3, the sequence downstream of the enzyme digestion sequence of r3 is consistent with the 3' end of r4, and the sequence downstream of the enzyme digestion sequence of r4 is consistent with the 3' end of r5. The method integrates the advantages of PCR and the ligase reaction, the system is simple, the condition is easy to control, the intermediate product is less, and the synthesis efficiency is high.

title: PCR method for detecting absolute telomere length
text: PCR method for detecting absolute telomere length. The invention provides a PCR method for detecting absolute telomere length, and belongs to the field of molecular biological techniques and medical detection. According to the method, the absolute length of the telomere of the human can be conveniently and accurately detected. A gene synthesis method is adopted for synthesizing oligonucleotide sequences of the telomere and a single-copy gene 36B4respectively, the sequences are connected to a plasmid vector to construct a standard product, escherichia coli is utilized for in-vitro amplification of the plasmid standard product, the plasmid DNAis extracted, the concentration is measured, and the copy number and the total length of the telomere are calculated. The initial mass of the standard product is determined, gradient dilution is conducted, a fluorescence quantitative PCR method (Q-PCR) is adopted for obtaining standard curves of the telomere and the 36B4 separately, through the standard curves, the total length of the telomere andthe copy number of the 36B4 of the to-be-tested sample DNA are subjected to absolute quantification, and thus the average absolute telomere length of the to-be-tested sample DNA is calculated. The length of the telomere is detected through the absolute telomere length PCR method, and the PCR method has the advantages of being low in cost, short in period and high in accuracy, and is suitable forhigh-throughput detection, easy to clinically apply and popularize, and high in practicability.

title: T CELL ACTIVATION RESPONSIVE CONSTRUCTS FOR ENHANCED CAR-T CELL THERAPY
text: T CELL ACTIVATION RESPONSIVE CONSTRUCTS FOR ENHANCED CAR-T CELL THERAPY. The present disclosure generally relates to genetic circuits including response elements engineered from the NR4A1 that augment chimeric antigen receptor activation with the delivery of a bioactive molecule. Particularly, the disclosure provides nucleic acid constructs including response elements operably linked to a nucleic acid sequence of interest such that are responsive to activation of CAR-T cells. The disclosure also relates to vectors and cells including the genetic circuits and the nucleic acid constructs. Also provided are methods of inducing an immune response, methods of treatment of subjects, and methods for delivering a bioactive molecule by an activated T cell having a chimeric antigen receptor and the response element constructs of the disclosure.

title: ENGINEERED VECTORS AND ORGANISMS CONTAINING THE SAME FOR ISOFLAVONE CONVERSION IN THE GUT
text: ENGINEERED VECTORS AND ORGANISMS CONTAINING THE SAME FOR ISOFLAVONE CONVERSION IN THE GUT. Generally, the present disclosure contemplates engineered vectors, organisms (e.g., bacteria or bacteriophage) containing the same, and methods for treating the gut of mammalian species by providing the organism containing the engineered vector to the gut of a mammal. Example engineered organisms can include one or more genes isolated from microbial populations for promoting isoflavone metabolism.

title: Bioengineered Microbial Spores
text: Bioengineered Microbial Spores. Phenotypically modified bioengineered microbial spores are provided. The microbial spores may be used in various spore-based technologies such as probiotics, biomaterials and vaccines.

title: Digital circuit for implementing genetic algorithm, comprises circuit generation algorithm and genetic circuit evaluator, where circuit generation includes random number generator, logic function, two multiplexers and recorder
text: Digital circuit for implementing genetic algorithm, comprises circuit generation algorithm and genetic circuit evaluator, where circuit generation includes random number generator, logic function, two multiplexers and recorder. The circuit comprises a circuit generation algorithm and a genetic circuit evaluator. The circuit generation to understand genetic algorithms includes a random number generator, a logic function, two multiplexers and a recorder. The circuit assessment includes a circuit estimation adaptor, a multiplexer, a recorder and a comparator. The genetic algorithm provides a new configuration in each unit time and estimates the degree of adaptation of the circuit.

title: Preparation and application of PT-IRAKH-1 anti-inflammatory recombinant protein
text: Preparation and application of PT-IRAKH-1 anti-inflammatory recombinant protein. The invention discloses preparation and application of PT-IRAKH-1 anti-inflammatory recombinant protein, and relates to the technical field of medicinal chemistry, and the key points of the technical scheme are as follows: a PT-IRAKH-1 active peptide coding sequence is a sequence which is derived from circular RNA (Ribonucleic Acid) and has coding potential, the size of the PT-IRAKH-1 active peptide coding sequence is 324 basic groups, and the nucleotide sequence of the PT-IRAKH-1 active peptide coding sequence is shown as SEQ ID NO: 1. A section of sequence with polypeptide coding potential is found in circular RNA, plasmids for expressing the polypeptide sequence are constructed through gene synthesis and plasmids, the protein is successfully expressed through a prokaryotic expression system to be prepared into recombinant protein, meanwhile, a lipopolysaccharide-induced mouse lung tissue inflammation model is constructed, and the mouse lung tissue inflammation model can be used for treating the lung tissue inflammation. The PT-IRAKH-1 recombinant protein is applied to the model, and evaluation is performed through methods of HE dyeing, lung tissue Smith scoring, flow cytometry and the like to obtain a conclusion that the PT-IRAKH-1 recombinant protein can relieve inflammation and injury of mouse lung tissue.

title: High-efficiency expression and application of VP28 protein fragment having binding activity with litopenaeus vannamei protein molecule Rab7
text: High-efficiency expression and application of VP28 protein fragment having binding activity with litopenaeus vannamei protein molecule Rab7. The invention relates to 84 amino acids bound with a litopenaeus vannamei protein molecule Rab7 at the VP28 c-terminus of prawn white spot syndrome virus, a high-efficiency expression of the 84 amino acids at the c-terminus of prawn white spot syndrome virus VP28, and application of anti-WSSV (White Spot Syndrome Virus) infection. In the invention, an omni-gene synthesis technology is utilized to transform a codon for encoding the amino acids at the VP28 c-terminus (VP28C) to an escherichia coli bias codon; the escherichia coli bias codon is constructed in a prokaryotic expression vector pBAD/gIIIA to constitute a high-efficiency expression recombinant plasmid pBAD/gIIIA-VP28C, and then transformed to escherichia coli; the escherichia coli is induced by arabinose; the VP28C expression quantity is more than 40% of the total proteins in the bacteria, which is greatly higher than the expression level reported in literature, so that the expression provides basis for producing VP28C for treating the prawn white spot syndrome virus and reducing the production cost. The invention further relates to application of VP28C in control of the prawn white spot syndrome virus.

title: Synthetic board waste liquid discharge device and gene synthesizer
text: Synthetic board waste liquid discharge device and gene synthesizer. The invention relates to the technical field of gene synthesis, in particular to a synthetic plate waste liquid discharging device which comprises a liquid pumping plate, the liquid pumping plate is used for being installed at the bottom of a synthetic plate, the liquid pumping plate comprises a liquid discharging groove with an opening in the upper end, and lower ports of synthetic holes of the synthetic plate are all communicated with the liquid discharging groove. The liquid discharging groove is divided into at least two mutually independent sub-grooves with openings in the upper ends, each sub-groove is communicated with a liquid discharging channel, and each liquid discharging channel is provided with a valve piece for controlling the liquid discharging channel to be opened/closed; the discharging device further comprises a negative pressure unit, and the negative pressure unit is communicated with the liquid discharging channel and used for applying negative pressure to the liquid discharging channel. In an assembly state, the synthesis holes in the synthesis plate are divided into at least two groups, and one group of synthesis holes corresponds to one sub-groove; in the liquid discharging process, normal liquid adding operation can still be carried out; in addition, the sub-tanks can be used for independently extracting liquid, and normal synthesis of the synthesis hole groups corresponding to other sub-tanks cannot be interfered.

title: GENE EXPRESSION IN BACTEROIDES
text: GENE EXPRESSION IN BACTEROIDES. Provided herein, in some aspects, are tools (e.g., methods, compositions and nucleic acids) for building genetic circuits in Bacteroides and Parabacteroides bacteria, as well as the bacteria containing the genetic circuits.

title: Chinese herbal medicine composition with skin whitening effect as well as preparation method and application of Chinese herbal medicine composition
text: Chinese herbal medicine composition with skin whitening effect as well as preparation method and application of Chinese herbal medicine composition. The invention discloses a Chinese herbal medicine composition with a skin whitening effect. The Chinese herbal medicine composition is prepared from the following components in parts by weight: 8-40 parts of bletilla striata, 8-40 parts of rhizome of chuanxiong, 8-40 parts of radix puerariae, 8-40 parts of green tea and 5-20 parts of rhizome nardostachyos. The invention also discloses a preparation method of the Chinese herbal medicine composition with the skin whitening effect and an application of the Chinese herbal medicine composition with the skin whitening effect to cosmetics. The Chinese herbal medicine composition with the skin whitening effect has the beneficial effects that the Chinese herbal medicine composition capable of mainly inhibiting the synthesis of melanin and expression of relevant mRNA, synergically promoting metabolization of skin and promoting blood circulation can be used for effectively preventing and reducing the synthesis of skin melanin; the Chinese herbal medicine composition is a pure Chinese herbal extract so as to be safe in use, conform to the hygienic code of cosmetics and be used in a skin whitening cosmetic formula such as skin whitening and moisturizing lotions and skin whitening cleaning milk; researches on the three mechanisms including biological chemistry, cell biology and molecule biology prove that the Chinese herbal medicine composition with the skin whitening effect has the multilevel skin whitening effect through remarkably inhibiting tyrosinase, inhibiting melanin and inhibiting the gene synthesis of melanin correlated factor mRNA.

title: DNA assembly method using DNA polymerase having exonuclease activity
text: DNA assembly method using DNA polymerase having exonuclease activity. The present invention relates to a DNA assembly method using a DNA polymerase having exonuclease activity. The invention also provides a design of a fragment to be assembled for implementing the method, and a method and a primer for preparing the fragment to be assembled.

title: Method for Producing Plasmid, and Plasmid
text: Method for Producing Plasmid, and Plasmid. Provided are a method for producing a novel plasmid in heterologous expression of a gene and a plasmid. The method includes preparing a first gene cluster containing the plurality of genes by a DNA assembly method using a plasmid transformation system of Bacillus subtilis; and linking, to the first gene cluster, a second gene cluster containing a replication origin of Bacillus subtilis, a replication origin of Escherichia coli, and an initiation sequence for conjugation to an actinomycete.

title: Even DNA molecular weight standard and preparation method thereof
text: Even DNA molecular weight standard and preparation method thereof. Two recombinant vectors p2186 and P1000 are obtained by gene engineering and gene synthesis methods. A p2186DNA vector is prepared by nucleic acid fermentation, and EcoRI is used for complete enzyme digestion to obtain a mixture-Mix1 of 2kb, 1kb, 800bp and 600bp DNA fragments with equivalent molar numbers; plasmid P1000 is used as a PCR template, a specific primer is used for amplification to obtain a 1000bp PCR product, and the EcoRI is used for partly incomplete enzyme digestion to obtain a mixture-Mix2 of five DNA fragments; and a 200bp ladder DNA molecular weight standard with evenly distributed bands is prepared by proper mixing of the Mix1 and the Mix2, and the 200bp ladder DNA molecular weight standard comprises totally six kinds of 200bp, 400bp, 600bp, 800bp, 1000bp and 2000bp double-stranded DNA fragments. The two 200bp ladder preparation nucleic acid vectors p2186 and P1000 for and a method for preparation of the 200bp ladder DNA molecular weight standard from the two 200bp ladder preparation nucleic acid vectors p2186 and P1000 have larger technical and cost advantages, is high in efficiency, and time and labor-saving, and fragment of the200bp ladder DNA molecular weight standard is much better than similar DNA molecular weight standards of single vector enzyme digestion or PCR amplification sourcing.

title: SYSTEMS FOR CELL PROGRAMMING TOWARDS PANCREATIC LINEAGE AND METHODS THEREOF
text: SYSTEMS FOR CELL PROGRAMMING TOWARDS PANCREATIC LINEAGE AND METHODS THEREOF. Provided herein are systems of modulating gene expression, methods of use thereof, and cells engineered thereof for the purpose of differentiating cells, for example beta cells.

title: Seamless Cloning Method with Static Recovery Period
text: Seamless Cloning Method with Static Recovery Period. The invention relates to a seamless cloning method, comprising a single assembly step of two or more polynucleotides, e.g., a plasmid vector and a gene insert, conducted at 58 to 100° C., the preferred temperature(s) being the same or greater than a particular one of the following temperatures: 58° C., 59° C., 60° C., 61° C., 62° C., 63° C., 64° C., 65° C., 66° C., 67° C., 68° C., 69° C., 70° C., 71° C., 72° C., 73° C., 74° C., 75° C., 76° C., 77° C., 78° C., 79° C., 80° C., 84° C., 88° C., 92° C., 96° C. and 100° C., and a transformation into chemically competent cells for covalent linking, preferably including a static recovery period.

title: BIOSENSORS FOR DETECTING AND/OR NEUTRALIZING BIOAVAILABLE URANIUM AND RELATED U-SENSITIVE GENETIC MOLECULAR COMPONENTS, GENE CASSETTES, VECTORS, GENETIC CIRCUITS, COMPOSITIONS, METHODS AND SYSTEMS
text: BIOSENSORS FOR DETECTING AND/OR NEUTRALIZING BIOAVAILABLE URANIUM AND RELATED U-SENSITIVE GENETIC MOLECULAR COMPONENTS, GENE CASSETTES, VECTORS, GENETIC CIRCUITS, COMPOSITIONS, METHODS AND SYSTEMS. U biosensors, and related U-sensing genetic molecular components, genetic circuits, compositions, methods and systems are described, which in several embodiments can be used to detect and/or neutralize uranium and in particular bioavailable U.

title: DNA NANOCAGE BY SELF-ORGANIZATION OF DNA AND PROCESS FOR PRODUCING THE SAME, AND DNA NANOTUBE AND MOLECULE CARRIER USING THE SAME
text: DNA NANOCAGE BY SELF-ORGANIZATION OF DNA AND PROCESS FOR PRODUCING THE SAME, AND DNA NANOTUBE AND MOLECULE CARRIER USING THE SAME. The invention provides a process for producing DNA nanocages, characterized by comprising a step of two-dimensionally assembling three types of oligonucleotides by hybridization to form tridirectionally branched double-strand DNA having self-complementary chains in the terminals, and a step of three-dimensionally self-organizing the resulting tridirectionally branched double-strand DNAs so as to consume all of the self-complementary terminals. Since the DNA nanocages according to the invention can easily be formed from DNAs by one-step procedure and include nanoparticles in the interior, the DNA nanocages are significantly effective for the development of functional materials using DNAs. <IMAGE>

title: Mitochondrial regulatory factor targeting TGF-B and TNF pathways and immunoregulation method of mitochondrial regulatory factor
text: Mitochondrial regulatory factor targeting TGF-B and TNF pathways and immunoregulation method of mitochondrial regulatory factor. The invention discloses a mitochondrial regulatory factor targeting TGF-B and TNF pathways and an immunoregulation method thereof, and relates to the technical field of biologication.The immunoregulation method includes the steps of S1, molecular design and gene synthesis, S2, recombinant plasmid construction and verification, S3, mammalian cell expression and purification, S4, preparation freeze-drying and redissolving, and S5, preparation freeze-drying and redissolving. According to the mitochondrial targeting fusion protein disclosed by the invention, two inflammatory pathways of TGF-beta and TNF-alpha are targeted at the same time, so that collaborative regulation and control on immunoreaction are realized, and the structural domain A blocks excessive activation of a downstream SMAD signal pathway by inhibiting kinase activity of a TGF-beta receptor I, so that abnormal amplification of fibrosis-promoting and immunosuppressive cells is reduced, and the immunosuppressive effect of the mitochondrial targeting fusion protein is improved. The structural domain B simulates a soluble TNF receptor, neutralizes free TNF-alpha and inhibits an NF-kappa B inflammation cascade reaction triggered after the structural domain B is combined with a membrane receptor, the synergistic effect of the structural domain B and the soluble TNF-alpha can intervene in unbalance of proinflammatory and anti-inflammatory signals at the same time, and compensatory signal rebound possibly caused by single-channel inhibition is avoided.

title: Microbial stem cell technology
text: Microbial stem cell technology. The present disclosure relates to microbial stem cell technology that enables a growing microbial culture to stably maintain two or more distinct cell types in a ratio that can be genetically programmed and/or dynamically controlled during cultivation. It is contemplated that embodiments described herein can be utilized to increase product yield in microbial fermentations and advanced engineering of biomaterials using genetically engineered microbial cells, among others.

title: Mothod for the synthesis of DNA sequences
text: Mothod for the synthesis of DNA sequences. A method is disclosed for the direct synthesis of double stranded DNA molecules of a variety of sizes and with any desired sequence. The DNA molecule to be synthesis is logically broken up into smaller overlapping DNA segments. A maskless microarray synthesizer is used to make a DNA microarray on a substrate in which each element or feature of the array is populated by DNA of a one of the overlapping DNA segments. The DNA segments are released from the substrate and held under conditions favoring hybridization of DNA, under which conditions the segments will spontaneously hybridize together to form the desired DNA construct. This method makes possible the remote assembly of DNA sequence, through a process analogous to facsimile transmission of documents, since the information on DNA to be made can be transmitted remotely to an instrument which can then synthesize any needed DNA sequence from the information.

title: BACTERIAL BIOSENSORS FOR MONITORING AND DETECTION OF NAPHTHENIC ACIDS IN THE ENVIRONMENT
text: BACTERIAL BIOSENSORS FOR MONITORING AND DETECTION OF NAPHTHENIC ACIDS IN THE ENVIRONMENT. A bacterial biosensor comprises a modified Pseudomonas species carrying a plasmid or chromosomally encoding a genetic circuit having a reporter gene fused with a promoter responsive to the presence of naphthenic acids. The genetic circuit is expressed proportionally in the presence of naphthenic acids in the environment. Methods of detecting the presence of naphthenic acids in an environment or monitoring degradation of NAs by a bacterial strain using the bacterial biosensor are provided.

title: T CELL ACTIVATION RESPONSIVE CONSTRUCTS FOR ENHANCED CAR-T CELL THERAPY
text: T CELL ACTIVATION RESPONSIVE CONSTRUCTS FOR ENHANCED CAR-T CELL THERAPY. The present disclosure generally relates to genetic circuits including response elements engineered from the NR4A1 that augment chimeric antigen receptor activation with the delivery of a bioactive molecule. Particularly, the disclosure provides nucleic acid constructs including response elements operably linked to a nucleic acid sequence of interest such that are responsive to activation of CAR-T cells. The disclosure also relates to vectors and cells including the genetic circuits and the nucleic acid constructs. Also provided are methods of inducing an immune response, methods of treatment of subjects, and methods for delivering a bioactive molecule by an activated T cell having a chimeric antigen receptor and the response element constructs of the disclosure.

title: Engineering bacterium based on cellulose metabolic pathway key enzyme and implementation method of engineering bacterium based on cellulose metabolic pathway key enzyme
text: Engineering bacterium based on cellulose metabolic pathway key enzyme and implementation method of engineering bacterium based on cellulose metabolic pathway key enzyme. The invention discloses an engineering bacterium based on a cellulose metabolic pathway key enzyme and an implementation method of the engineering bacterium based on the cellulose metabolic pathway key enzyme. The method comprises the following steps: building a vector as a template by whole-gene synthesis of streptomyces griseorubens genome DNA; respectively carrying out PCR amplification on the template and corresponding primers containing enzyme digestion sites, thereby respectively obtaining a nucleotide sequence of encoding exo-beta-1,4-glucanase, a nucleotide sequence of encoding endo-beta-1,4-glucanase, and a nucleotide sequence of encoding beta-1,4-glucosaccharase; sequentially connecting the amplified sequences to obtain a coexpression vector, thereby finally obtaining a recombinant coexpression vector and a recombinant expression vector; and successively transforming the recombinant coexpression carrier into an escherichia coli expression strain, so as to obtain a transgenic coexpression strain. A large number of in vitro expression synthesis of the exo-beta-1,4-glucanase, the endo-beta-1,4-glucanase and the beta-1,4-glucosaccharase is achieved by using genetic engineering means, and in-situ rapid degradation of cellulose is finally achieved.

title: METHOD FOR PRODUCING PLASMID, AND PLASMID
text: METHOD FOR PRODUCING PLASMID, AND PLASMID. Provided are a method for producing a novel plasmid in heterologous expression of a gene and a plasmid. The method includes preparing a first gene cluster containing the plurality of genes by a DNA assembly method using a plasmid transformation system of Bacillus subtilis; and linking, to the first gene cluster, a second gene cluster containing a replication origin of Bacillus subtilis, a replication origin of Escherichia coli, and an initiation sequence for conjugation to an actinomycete.

title: Innovative preparation process of recombinant human adipocyte peptide (BMP-2 stem cell for short)
text: Innovative preparation process of recombinant human adipocyte peptide (BMP-2 stem cell for short). The invention discloses an innovative preparation process of recombinant human adipocyte peptide (BMP-2 stem cells for short). The process comprises the following steps of BMP-2 stem cell gene synthesis, construction of an expression system of mature peptide, induction expression, separation and purification, amplification and the like. A novel PCR amplification method is adopted, and an efficientexpression vector is constructed while the target gene is synthesized rapidly; a promoter adopted in the process is a Trc promoter which is weaker than a T7 promoter commonly adopted in a prokaryoticexpression system, and soluble expression at a high temperature can be achieved; the molecular chaperone PDI protein originating from saccharomyces cerevisiae is added at the C end of the gene, correct folding of the protein is better facilitated, and the solubility and high activity of the BMP-2 stem cells are maintained; a 6*HIS label is added at the N end of the gene to achieve rapid isolationand purification of the BMP-2 stem cells. The method is simple and flexible, the product separation and purification process is simple, the purity is high, the activity is high, the production cost is low, and the process has broad application prospects in clinical virus prevention, cell aging resistance, wound healing promotion, repair in epidermis, dermis and mucosa and in the fields of medicaltreatment, beautifying, health care and the like.

title: Anticancer drug target screening method and system based on hypergraph-knowledge graph double channels
text: Anticancer drug target screening method and system based on hypergraph-knowledge graph double channels. The invention relates to the technical field of bioinformatics and computational biology, and provides an anti-cancer drug target screening method and system based on hypergraph-knowledge graph dual-channel, a hypergraph-knowledge graph dual-channel architecture is constructed through a feature extraction module, a self-adaptive multi-modal fusion module dynamically integrates multi-source heterogeneous data, and the anti-cancer drug target screening method and system based on the hypergraph-knowledge graph dual-channel architecture are obtained. The method comprises the following steps: adaptively fusing high-order interaction features and semantic association features of a gene, generating a high-quality negative sample by using a generative adversarial network to optimize a training process, finally optimizing feature representation through a discriminator, and calculating a synthetic lethal probability score between any gene pair. According to the application, firstly, the extracted multi-dimensional features are normalized through the feature extraction module, then the importance of different features is dynamically weighted based on the attention mechanism, finally, the synthesis lethal relationship prediction probability of the gene pair is output through the multi-layer sensor, and under the theoretical framework of the gene synthesis lethal effect, the synthesis lethal effect of the gene pair is predicted. And an innovative solution is provided for anti-cancer drug target screening.

title: Method for producing polymers
text: Method for producing polymers. The invention relates to a method for producing polymers, in particular synthetic nucleic acid double strands of optional sequence, comprising the steps: (a) provision of a support having a surface area which contains a plurality of individual reaction areas, (b) location-resolved synthesis of nucleic acid fragments having in each case different base sequences in several of the individual reaction areas, and (c) detachment of the nucleic acid fragments from individual reaction areas.

title: ENGINEERED HYPOXIA BIOSENSORS AND METHODS OF USING THE SAME
text: ENGINEERED HYPOXIA BIOSENSORS AND METHODS OF USING THE SAME. The present disclosure relates generally to hypoxia biosensors. In particular, the present disclosure relates to hypoxia biosensors that employ engineered genetic circuits for enhanced biosensor sensitivity and response. Some engineered genetic circuits employ positive feedback and amplification of expression in order to promote high-efficiency reporting of hypoxia.

title: PHOSPHORYLATION-BASED MIRNA SENSOR
text: PHOSPHORYLATION-BASED MIRNA SENSOR. Provided herein are genetic circuits and cell state classifiers for detecting the microRNA profile of a cell. The cell state classifiers of the present disclosure utilize phosphorylation state of a transcription factor to control classifier output. Kinases and phosphatase pairs that function in phosphorylating or dephosphorylating the transcription factor are integrated into the circuit, their expression tuned by the presence of microRNAs of interest (e.g., in a cell). The genetic circuits and cell state classifiers may be used in various applications (e.g., therapeutic or diagnostic applications).

title: METHODS OF PURIFYING DNA FOR GENE SYNTHESIS
text: METHODS OF PURIFYING DNA FOR GENE SYNTHESIS. Provided herein are methods of purifying nucleic acids (e.g., DNA) for gene synthesis using combinations of nucleases. Also provided are improved products for use in the production of RNA.

title: High performance multi-input microRNA sensors and uses thereof
text: High performance multi-input microRNA sensors and uses thereof. Provided herein are genetic circuits and cell state classifiers for detecting the microRNA profile of a cell. The cell state classifiers of the present disclosure are designed to incorporate multiple genetic circuits integrated together by transcriptional or translational control. Multiple inputs can be sensed simultaneously by coupling their detection to different portions of the genetic circuit such that the output molecule is produced only when the correct input profile of miRNAs is detected. The genetic circuits and cell state classifiers may be used in various applications (e.g., therapeutic or diagnostic applications).

title: Cross-species-specific single domain bispecific single chain antibody
text: Cross-species-specific single domain bispecific single chain antibody. The present invention relates to a bispecific single chain antibody molecule comprising a first binding domain consisting of one antibody variable domain capable of binding to an epitope of the human and non-chimpanzee primate CD3 epsilon chain, wherein the epitope is part of an amino acid sequence comprised in the group consisting of SEQ ID NOs. 2, 4, 6, and 8, and a second binding domain capable of binding to an epitope of a human and a non-chimpanzee primate tumor target antigen. The invention further relates to a bispecific single chain antibody molecule comprising a first binding domain capable of binding to an epitope of human and non-chimpanzee primate CD3ϵ (epsilon) chain, wherein the epitope is part of an amino acid sequence comprised in the group consisting of SEQ ID NOs. 2, 4, 6, and 8, and a second binding domain consisting of one antibody variable domain capable of binding to an epitope of a human and a non-chimpanzee primate tumor target antigen. The invention also provides nucleic acids encoding said bispecific single chain antibody molecule as well as vectors and host cells and a process for its production. The invention further relates to pharmaceutical compositions comprising said bispecific single chain antibody molecule and medical uses of said bispecific single chain antibody molecule.

title: MODULAR, INDUCIBLE REPRESSORS FOR THE CONTROL OF GENE EXPRESSION
text: MODULAR, INDUCIBLE REPRESSORS FOR THE CONTROL OF GENE EXPRESSION. A modular, inducible repressor for control of gene expression is disclosed. Generally, the repressor comprises a DNA binding protein fused to a protein that binds to an exogenous inducer molecule, wherein the repressor is directly inducible by the exogenous inducer molecule. In particular embodiments, the DNA binding protein comprises a transcription activator-like effector (TALE) and the protein that binds to an exogenous inducer molecule comprises glycine betaine binding protein (GBBP). In such embodiments, the exogenous inducer molecule comprises glycine betaine.

title: Process for reducing the number of mismatches in double stranded polynucleotides
text: Process for reducing the number of mismatches in double stranded polynucleotides. The present invention relates to a process for reducing the number of mismatches in a population of double stranded polynucleotides. Furthermore, the present invention relates to a process of producing double stranded polynucleotides. Also, the invention is directed to the use of an enzymes that cleaves and/or removes cleaved mismatches in double stranded polynucleotides in a process according to the invention as well as a kit of parts. <IMAGE>

title: PROCESS FOR THE FERMENTATIVE PRODUCTION OF PROTEINS
text: PROCESS FOR THE FERMENTATIVE PRODUCTION OF PROTEINS. The present invention relates to a process for producing a heterologous protein by means of an E. coli strain in a fermentation medium, in which an E. coli strain which has a mutation in the lpp gene or in the promoter region of the lpp gene, and contains a gene coding for a heterologous protein which is functionally linked to a signal sequence coding for a signal peptide, is fermented on an industrial scale in the fermentation medium, with the E. coli strain secreting the heterologous protein into the fermentation medium, and the protein being removed from the fermentation medium, wherein the heterologous protein comprises more than 70 amino acids.

title: Methods for improving the sequence fidelity of synthetic double-stranded oligonucleotides
text: Methods for improving the sequence fidelity of synthetic double-stranded oligonucleotides. Synthetic oligonucleotides, such as synthetic DNA, often contain sequence errors due to synthetic failures (e.g., side products and/or truncated products). Methods are provided herein for improving the sequence fidelity of synthetic double-stranded oligonucleotides by separative depletion of synthetic failures. Separation is effected by utilization of methodologies in a preparative mode under denaturing conditions. A preferred use of the methods relates to gene synthesis.

title: Devices and Methods for Detection of Biomolecular Interactions
text: Devices and Methods for Detection of Biomolecular Interactions. Devices, systems, and methods are provided for the detection of biomolecular interactions. The interactions between one or more target DNA strands, one or more receptor DNA strands, and one or more probe DNA strands, if necessary, are used to detect the one or more target DNA strands. The one or more target DNA strands or the one or more probe DNA strands may be coupled to a magnetic bead, and the one or more receptor strands may be coupled to the Hall device.

title: CRISPR-Based Synthetic Gene Circuits as Next Generation Gene Therapy of Inner Ear
text: CRISPR-Based Synthetic Gene Circuits as Next Generation Gene Therapy of Inner Ear. Aspects of the disclosure relate to synthetic regulatory systems comprising a multifunctional Cas nuclease and at least two guide RNAs (gRNAs) including a truncated gRNA and an multilayered regulatory control element. The synthetic regulatory system modulates endogenous gene expression, including transcriptional repression and transcriptional activation of one or more endogenous genes of a mammalian inner ear cell with multiple safety switches. Also provided herein are methods for modulating hearing sensitivity in damaged cells of the organ of corti.

title: Recombinant strain E. coli GPE10 based on recombinant vector pGPE57, construction method and applications thereof
text: Recombinant strain E. coli GPE10 based on recombinant vector pGPE57, construction method and applications thereof. The invention discloses a recombinant vector pGPE57, and construction and applications of a recombinant strain E. coli GPE10, and belongs to the field of biotechnology, wherein the plasmid pGPE57 andthe recombinant strain E. coli GPE10 contain gene elements such as Amp, AraC, Pbad, gam, pfu-Ssod7 and exo. According to the present invention, the recombinant vector pGPE57 and the recombinant strainE. coli GPE10 can induce the expression of a recombinase system through arabinose, wherein the intracellular DNA assembly is completed through the recombination between homologous sequences; the application of the recombinant strain E. coli GPE10 to perform multi-segment DNA assembly is the simple, rapid and efficient DNA-directed cloning technology without enzyme digestion and enzyme linkage, wherein the DNA fragment can be directly transformed into E. Coli GPE10 competent cells to achieve molecular cloning without the restriction by the enzyme digestion sites, the target DNA fragment can becloned into any sites of any vectors in a directed manner, the method is the rapid cloning technology without the linkage reaction, and the efficient and sequential splicing of the multi-fragment DNAcan achieved at the same time.

title: System and method for loop-iteration assembly of DNA
text: System and method for loop-iteration assembly of DNA. The invention relates to the technical field of biology and particularly relates to a system and method for loop-iteration assembly of DNA. According to the system and the method, a loop iteration policy employs cyclic utilization of four donor plasmids, and a design of four Cas9 cleavage sites and two screening markers is employed; and A gene Cas9, a [lambda]Red system and a gene telN are implemented in the form of plasmid, and the same DNA assembled effect can be achieved by integrating the gene Cas9, the [lambda]Red system and the gene telN to a genome of Escherichia coli. According to themethod, on one hand, the DNA is not required to be subjected to tedious operations such as in-vitro extraction, enzyme digestion and recovery, and on the other hand, the condition that the traditionallarge-fragment DNA assembly needs a process for producing competent cells such as protoplasts is avoided, so that the method has the advantages of simplicity in operation, high degree of automation,low cost and the like. By the method, after continuous 4 rounds of assembly, the positive rate of assembly of 5 DNA fragments can reach 98.4%, and thus, the method has the advantage of high positive rate of DNA assembly.

title: DNA data storage method and device based on assembly optimization and terminal equipment
text: DNA data storage method and device based on assembly optimization and terminal equipment. The invention discloses a DNA data storage method and reading method based on assembly optimization and a terminal, and the method comprises the steps: converting data information into DNA module assembly data through coding, the data information comprising or being converted into multiple pieces of binary information; the DNA modules containing the same sticky tail ends are formed into the same kind of module combinations from the DNA module assembly data, so that a plurality of same kind of module combinations are obtained; carrying out degeneracy calculation on a plurality of same-type module combinations to obtain optimized DNA module assembly data; and performing DNA assembly reaction on the optimized DNA module assembly data to form a DNA molecular long chain, thereby completing the storage of the data information. The storage speed is high, the occupied space of the reaction is small, and the method can be applied to other high-throughput biochemical reactions including but not limited to DNA assembly, cloning, plasmid construction, PCR amplification and the like besides the DNA data storage method described in the invention.

title: Derivatized versions of ligase enzymes for constructing DNA sequences
text: Derivatized versions of ligase enzymes for constructing DNA sequences. A method of making very long, double-stranded synthetic poly-nucleotides. A multiplicity of short oligonucleotides is provided. The short oligonucleotides are sequentially hybridized to each other. Enzymatic ligation of the oligonucleotides provides a contiguous piece of PCR-ready DNA of predetermined sequence.

title: D- D- D- D-XYLONATE-RESPONSIVE PROMOTER ARTIFICIAL GENETIC CIRCUITS COMPRISING D-XYLONATE-RESPONSIVE PROMOTER AND METHOD FOR DETECTION OF D-XYLONATE USING ARTIFICIAL GENETIC CIRCUIT
text: D- D- D- D-XYLONATE-RESPONSIVE PROMOTER ARTIFICIAL GENETIC CIRCUITS COMPRISING D-XYLONATE-RESPONSIVE PROMOTER AND METHOD FOR DETECTION OF D-XYLONATE USING ARTIFICIAL GENETIC CIRCUIT. The present invention relates to a D-xylonic acid reactive promoter, a redesigned gene circuit for detecting D-xylonic acid including the same, and a D-xylonic acid detection method using the same. The promoter represented by nucleotide sequences of SEQ ID NO: 1 according to the present invention has high reactivity to D-xylonic acid, so when applied to a synthetic circuit for detecting D-xylonic acid in E. coli, it is possible to increase the production yield of a final product through the pH control by adjusting the accumulation of D-xylonic acid.

title: Method of creating industrial streptomyces with capability to grow on cellulosic polysaccharide substrates
text: Method of creating industrial streptomyces with capability to grow on cellulosic polysaccharide substrates. A heterologous gene cassette useful for creating Streptomyces species with enhanced capability of growing on a cellulosic polysaccharide substrate, wherein the cassette comprises at least two members of the following categories: a) a GH6 gene, b) an AA10 gene, c) a GH48 gene, d) a GH5 gene and e) either (i) a GH9 gene, (ii) a GH9 gene and a GH12 gene, or (iii) a GH12 gene is disclosed.

title: Biosensor as well as preparation method and application
text: Biosensor as well as preparation method and application. The invention discloses a biosensor as well as a preparation method and application thereof. The biosensor disclosed by the invention is an assembly comprising gold and silver core-shell nanoparticle-tetrahedral structure DNA containing azobenzene molecules. The preparation method of the biosensor comprises the following steps of: preparing the gold and silver core-shell nanoparticles; preparing tetrahedral structure DNA containing the azobenzene molecules; and (3) assembling the gold and silver core-shell nanoparticle-tetrahedron DNA assembly containing the azobenzene molecules. According tothe biosensor which is the assembly comprising the gold and silver core-shell nanoparticle-tetrahedral structure DNA containing azobenzene molecules, high-sensitivity detection of miR-21 can be achieved on the single-nanoparticle scale, the concentration of miR-21 and the red shift amount of a scattering spectrum of the gold-silver core-shell nanoparticle-tetrahedral structure DNA assembly containing the azobenzene molecules are in a linear relation, and important significance is achieved for diagnosis and prognosis treatment of early-stage cancer.

title: METHODS FOR CIRCULARIZING LINEAR DOUBLE STRANDED NUCLEIC ACIDS

Also, a circularized double stranded DNA nucleic acid obtainable by the method. Another aspect pertains to a host cell comprising a circularized double stranded DNA nucleic acid obtainable by the method. Further, therapeutic and non-therapeutic uses of a circularized double stranded DNA nucleic acid obtainable by the method. Finally, a kit for the circularization of a double stranded DNA nucleic acid.
